# Mods, Arcanes & Rivens
FORMAT: Each ## entry is one mod/arcane. Meta line: Rarity | Polarity | Drain | Compatible | Max Rank. ### Stats by Rank lists per-rank values. ### Drop Locations lists enemies with %.
INDEX: Mods (1533 entries) | Arcanes (164 entries) | Riven Mod Tags & Dispositions (7 entries)

# Mods
Complete mod database for Warframe. Each mod entry includes its name, polarity, drain, compatibility, all rank-up stat progression, drop locations, and whether it is an augment or prime variant.

## Abating Link
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Trinity | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
Can be acquired by attaining the rank of Partner under The Perrin Sequence, or the rank of Flawless under New Loka, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Link Augment: Reduces Armor Rating by 30% on enemies targeted by Link.
- **Rank 1:** Link Augment: Reduces Armor Rating by 40% on enemies targeted by Link.
- **Rank 2:** Link Augment: Reduces Armor Rating by 50% on enemies targeted by Link.
- **Rank 3:** Link Augment: Reduces Armor Rating by 60% on enemies targeted by Link.

### Drop Locations
- New Loka, Flawless — Common (100.00%)
- The Perrin Sequence, Partner — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Abating Link is a Warframe Augment Mod for Trinity's Link that reduces the Armor of all connected enemies.
**Notes:**
Armor reduction is affected by Ability Strength, fully removing Armor at 167% Ability Strength.
Armor reduction is temporary, enemies will regain their Armor when no longer tethered.
**Tips:**
With one player using Corrosive Projection, 100% Armor reduction can be achieved at 137% Ability Strength.
**Trivia:**
The image of the mod depicts Trinity surrounded by basic Corpus units that don't have armor to begin with.

## Abundant Mutation
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Nidus | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired by attaining the rank of General under the Steel Meridian, or the rank of Partner under The Perrin Sequence, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Passive Augment: Nidus gains an additional 50 max stacks of Mutation. Undying has a 30s cooldown.
- **Rank 1:** Passive Augment: Nidus gains an additional 100 max stacks of Mutation. Undying has a 30s cooldown.
- **Rank 2:** Passive Augment: Nidus gains an additional 150 max stacks of Mutation. Undying has a 30s cooldown.
- **Rank 3:** Passive Augment: Nidus gains an additional 200 max stacks of Mutation. Undying has a 30s cooldown.

### Drop Locations
- Steel Meridian, General — Common (100.00%)
- The Perrin Sequence, Partner — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Abundant Mutation is a Warframe Augment Mod for Nidus's passive that increases the amount of max stacks that he can carry but adds a cooldown of 30 seconds to his Undying passive.
**Notes:**
Damage from Virulence and maggots spawned from Ravenous scales with the increased max stacks.
Not affected by Ability Strength.
If Nidus reaches 0 Health during the 30 second cooldown period on Undying, Nidus will go into Bleedout and lose all stacks of Mutation.

## Accelerated Blast
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** madurai | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Shotgun | **Max Rank:** 3

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +15% Fire Rate, +15% Puncture
- **Rank 1:** +30% Fire Rate, +30% Puncture
- **Rank 2:** +45% Fire Rate, +45% Puncture
- **Rank 3:** +60% Fire Rate, +60% Puncture

### Drop Locations
- Nightmare Mode Rewards, Rotation A — Uncommon (18.97%)

### Wiki
Accelerated Blast is a mod that increases the Fire Rate of shotguns by 15% and Puncture damage by 15% per rank for a maximum of 60% at max rank for both values.
**Notes:**
Can be paired with Shotgun Barrage and Frail Momentum to provide a 240% increase in Fire Rate.
Stacks with Breach Loader and Flechette for a maximum of 270% Puncture damage.
There are only five shotguns that deal primarily Puncture damage; the Hek, Vaykor Hek, Corinth, Corinth Prime, and Cedo. All other shotguns, including the Sweeper for Sentinels, deal less than 20% of their base damage as Puncture.
The Phage deals no physical damage whatsoever, so it only benefits from the increase in Fire Rate.
**Trivia:**
Prior to Update 12.4, at base level the mod was increasing Puncture damage by 22% rather than 15% and adding the Puncture damage based on the weapon's total base damage, causing unusually large boosts in Puncture damage as a result. These bugs have since been fixed.
The mod picture is misleading, as it implies that it adds punch through in order to puncture the Shield Lancer's shield, when in reality it increases the puncture damage type. while also, the shotgun featured in the image is a Sobek. which deals mostly Impact damage.

### Wiki
Accelerated Deflection increases the shield recharge rate and reduces the shield recharge delay for Sentinels, MOAs and Hounds.

## Accumulating Whipclaw
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Khora | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired by attaining the rank of General under Steel Meridian, or the rank of Exalted under the Red Veil, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.
This mod can periodically be acquired from The Circuit.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Whipclaw Augment: Hitting 3 enemies will grant a 10% stacking Damage Bonus to subsequent Whipclaws. Bonus will decay after 10s.
- **Rank 1:** Whipclaw Augment: Hitting 3 enemies will grant a 15% stacking Damage Bonus to subsequent Whipclaws. Bonus will decay after 10s.
- **Rank 2:** Whipclaw Augment: Hitting 3 enemies will grant a 20% stacking Damage Bonus to subsequent Whipclaws. Bonus will decay after 10s.
- **Rank 3:** Whipclaw Augment: Hitting 3 enemies will grant a 35% stacking Damage Bonus to subsequent Whipclaws. Bonus will decay after 10s.

### Drop Locations
- Red Veil, Exalted — Common (100.00%)
- Steel Meridian, General — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Accumulating Whipclaw is a Warframe Augment Mod for Khora's Whipclaw that grants a stacking damage bonus to subsequent Whipclaws upon hitting at least 3 enemies in a single cast, which will decay after 10 seconds.
**Notes:**
Damage bonus is additive to the one provided by Ability Strength.
This diminishes the effectiveness of modding for Ability Strength alongside the Augment.
Damage bonus caps at 350%, or after hitting 3 or more enemies 10 times.
Whipclaw itself must hit the enemies to get a bonus: hitting them through Strangledome's range extension alone does not count.
Only one stack is granted per cast of Whipclaw; hitting 6 or more enemies will not grant additional stacks.
Damage bonus decays at a rate of 2.5% per second.
Damage bonus and decay rate are not affected by mods.
Damage bonus resets after falling or being nullified.
The -2.5% per second decay starts after the 10 second "grace period" you get after hitting 3 enemies with one Whipclaw. Meaning that, after those first 10 seconds, it would take another 14 seconds for it to gradually remove 35% worth of buffs.
The long decay time makes it very easy to keep this buff maxed or near maxed for an entire mission in most cases.
9pRoKzxd_dw
23.7

## Acid Shells
**Rarity:** Uncommon | **Polarity:** madurai | **Drain:** 4 | **Compatible:** Sobek | **Max Rank:** 5

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Enemies explode on death, dealing 75 Corrosive Damage (+8% Enemy Max Health) in a 2.5m radius.
- **Rank 1:** Enemies explode on death, dealing 150 Corrosive Damage (+15% Enemy Max Health) in a 5m radius.
- **Rank 2:** Enemies explode on death, dealing 225 Corrosive Damage (+23% Enemy Max Health) in a 7.5m radius.
- **Rank 3:** Enemies explode on death, dealing 300 Corrosive Damage (+30% Enemy Max Health) in a 10m radius.
- **Rank 4:** Enemies explode on death, dealing 375 Corrosive Damage (+38% Enemy Max Health) in a 12.5m radius.
- **Rank 5:** Enemies explode on death, dealing 450 Corrosive Damage (+45% Enemy Max Health) in a 15m radius.

### Drop Locations
- Kela De Thaym — Uncommon (11.11%)

### Wiki
Acid Shells is a Weapon Augment Mod exclusive to the Sobek and Kuva Sobek that causes enemies killed by the Sobek to explode, dealing a flat amount of Corrosive damage plus a percentage of the enemy's maximum Health as Blast damage in an area of effect.
**Notes:**
Explosion has linear damage falloff from 100% to 0% from the central enemy.
Explosion requires Line of Sight.
The explosion itself cannot apply Corrosive procs.
The explosion caused by Acid Shells can proc into itself and create another explosion on kill, creating a chain reaction that lead to very large areas being cleared.
The explosion damage does not account for the bonus Health given to enemies in The Steel Path, Archon Hunt, Deep Archimedea, and similar modes.
Enemies killed by Acid Shells will have their corpses disintegrate.
**Tips:**
Due to the nature of this mod, prioritizing heavy units will result in other enemies taking high damage or dying due to the resulting explosion, reducing the time to kill entire crowds significantly.
Modding the Sobek for Corrosive or Viral damage and punch through can be effective to soften up enemies at higher levels with procs before the explosion occurs, thus amplifying the damage inflicted by Acid Shells' burst.
Against the Infested, this mod scales tremendously well since most of them have no armor.
Acid Shells explosion will cause an additional damage instance from Cyte-09's Resupply, Garuda's Seeking Talons, Oraxia's Silken Stride, Saryn's Toxic Lash, Uriel's Vythelas, and Xaku's Xata's Whisper.
Because these effects are considered Sobek's damage, they can trigger Acid Shells again on kill.
Faction damage mods and Rhino's Roar directly affect the mod's explosion damage.
Abilities that increase damage on enemies or debuff them such as Banshee's Sonar, Nova's Molecular Prime, or Saryn's Spores can indirectly enhance it.
The AOE affects breakable containers as well, giving an effect similar to Looter as long as there are enemies around to proc th

## Acidic Spittle
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** penjaga | **Drain:** 0 | **Compatible:** Vizier Predasite | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
Acidic Spittle is automatically acquired upon obtaining a Vizier Predasite.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Every 8s spit acidic tar at an enemy within 7.5m, blinding them for 3s and dealing 30 Corrosive Damage.
- **Rank 1:** Every 7s spit acidic tar at an enemy within 15m, blinding them for 6s and dealing 60 Corrosive Damage.
- **Rank 2:** Every 6s spit acidic tar at an enemy within 22.5m, blinding them for 9s and dealing 90 Corrosive Damage.
- **Rank 3:** Every 5s spit acidic tar at an enemy within 30m, blinding them for 12s and dealing 120 Corrosive Damage.

### Wiki
Acidic Spittle allows the Vizier Predasite to spit Corrosive projectiles at nearby targets, Blind them for a brief period.
**Notes:**
Innate damage type is 100% Corrosive.
Damage is affected by elemental mods, such as Venom Teeth.
Damage is not affected by Maul or Bite.
Can apply Status Effects normally, according to damage type distribution.
Status Chance is affected by Mecha Overdrive and other status chance mods.
Blind does not open enemies to melee finishers.

## Adaptation
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** vazarin | **Drain:** 2 | **Compatible:** WARFRAME | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 10

### Acquisition
Awarded from Arbitrations missions.
Can be purchased from the Arbitrations vendor in the Arbiters of Hexis room in Relays for 20 Vitus Essence.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** When Damaged: +5% Resistance to that Damage Type for 10s. Stacks up to 90%.
- **Rank 1:** When Damaged: +5.5% Resistance to that Damage Type for 11s. Stacks up to 90%.
- **Rank 2:** When Damaged: +6% Resistance to that Damage Type for 12s. Stacks up to 90%.
- **Rank 3:** When Damaged: +6.5% Resistance to that Damage Type for 13s. Stacks up to 90%.
- **Rank 4:** When Damaged: +7% Resistance to that Damage Type for 14s. Stacks up to 90%.
- **Rank 5:** When Damaged: +7.5% Resistance to that Damage Type for 15s. Stacks up to 90%.
- **Rank 6:** When Damaged: +8% Resistance to that Damage Type for 16s. Stacks up to 90%.
- **Rank 7:** When Damaged: +8.5% Resistance to that Damage Type for 17s. Stacks up to 90%.
- **Rank 8:** When Damaged: +9% Resistance to that Damage Type for 18s. Stacks up to 90%.
- **Rank 9:** When Damaged: +9.5% Resistance to that Damage Type for 19s. Stacks up to 90%.
- **Rank 10:** When Damaged: +10% Resistance to that Damage Type for 20s. Stacks up to 90%.

### Drop Locations
- Arbitrations, Rotation A — Rare (2.00%)
- Arbitrations, Rotation B — Rare (2.50%)

### Wiki
Adaptation is a Warframe mod that grants damage resistance to damage types received recently, stacking up to 90%.
**Notes:**
Due to the nature of Damage Type Modifiers, the mod's effect stacks multiplicatively with other sources of Damage Reduction, including, but not limited to:
Armor damage mitigation
Ancient Healer aura damage mitigation
Warframe Abilities granting damage mitigation
Other resistance mods, such as Lightning Rod or Aviator
Impact, Puncture, Slash, Cold, Electricity, Heat, Toxin, Blast, Corrosive, Magnetic, Radiation, Viral, and Tau damage types stack their respective resistances separately, and will have different icons in the status bar.
Will not stack or refresh from self-inflicted damage.
One attack from one source of damage can only increase one resistance type, with the resistance type given reflecting the highest damage component of an attack. Follow up attacks will grant additional resistance to that damage type and will refresh the duration.
For example, if you are hit by one bullet dealing 15 Impact, 15 Puncture, 30 Slash damage - you will receive a Slash damage resistance from Adaptation.
If multiple damage types from different sources are dealt to the player, a separate instance of the buff will stack for each respective damage type.
For example, if there are two enemies, one of which deals Slash damage while the other deals Puncture damage to the player, two different damage resistance buffs will be seen, each representing the two damage types the player was dealt. So a stack of up to 90% damage resistance can be achieved for both the Slash and Puncture damage.
Enemy weapons almost always have different stats from the player-wielded variant. For example, a normal Heavy Gunner will only give Impact resistance as the Gorgon mostly deals Impact.

### Acquisition
Adept Surge can be acquired by reaching the rank of Mistral from the Conclave Syndicate, and spending 5,000 Standing to purchase.

- **Rank 2:** +7.5% Mobility, -18.7 Health
- **Rank 3:** +10% Mobility, -25 Health

### Drop Locations

### Wiki
Adept Surge is a Conclave-exclusive Warframe mod that increases Mobility at the cost of Health.

## Adhesive Blast
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** madurai | **Drain:** 4 | **Compatible:** Unique | **Max Rank:** 5

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Grenades have 17% chance to stick to surfaces.
- **Rank 1:** Grenades have 33% chance to stick to surfaces.
- **Rank 2:** Grenades have 50% chance to stick to surfaces.
- **Rank 3:** Grenades have 67% chance to stick to surfaces.
- **Rank 4:** Grenades have 83% chance to stick to surfaces.
- **Rank 5:** Grenades have 100% chance to stick to surfaces.

### Drop Locations
- Ancient Disruptor — Rare (0.03%)
- Deimos Therid — Rare (0.03%)
- Tar Mutalist Moa — Rare (0.03%)
- Duviri/Endless: Tier 6 (Normal) — Rare (1.03%)
- Duviri/Endless: Tier 4 (Normal) — Rare (1.49%)

### Wiki
Adhesive Blast is a mod that allows weapon-fired grenades to stick to surfaces.
**Notes:**
This mod can be equipped on the following weapons:
Ogris
Penta
Secura Penta
Carmine Penta
Tenet Ferrox
Tenet Envoy
Tonkor
Torid
Zarr
Helstrum
When equipped, this removes the grenade's immediate explosion; it will explode after the 'fuse' duration of 4s ends.
Does almost nothing for Penta weapons:
Normal grenades will stick but will not explode on their own after the 'fuse' duration ends.
Tether Grenades are intangible and will not stick to enemies, normally sticking to surfaces even without Adhesive Blast.
Napalm Grenades will stick and will explode once the 'fuse' duration ends, but can still be detonated manually.
In Update 17.10, Adhesive Blast now gives 17% per rank and reaches 100% at full rank.
As of Update 21.3.1, Adhesive Blast cannot be equipped on Javlok nor Scourge.
**Trivia:**
Pre-Update 19.10, with the exception of the Zarr and Javlok, Adhesive Blast possessed the ability to deal damage over time and proc Status Effects, this was later revealed that the ability of stuck projectiles to deal a DoT was unintentional and was removed.
Additionally, if Adhesive Blast was equipped upon a manually detonated projectile such as the Penta's grenades, the damage over time effect would apply indefinitely due to the grenades' lack of fuse time.
During this time, although its description stated "and deal +60% extra damage", it reportedly never did.
jnywNZ8axa4

## Aegis Gale
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Hildryn | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired by attaining the rank of Partner under The Perrin Sequence, or the rank of Genius under Cephalon Suda, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Aegis Storm Augment: Balefire has an Alternate Fire during Aegis Storm. The blast deals 9% of Hildryn's Max Shield as additional damage.
- **Rank 1:** Aegis Storm Augment: Balefire has an Alternate Fire during Aegis Storm. The blast deals 11% of Hildryn's Max Shield as additional damage.
- **Rank 2:** Aegis Storm Augment: Balefire has an Alternate Fire during Aegis Storm. The blast deals 13% of Hildryn's Max Shield as additional damage.
- **Rank 3:** Aegis Storm Augment: Balefire has an Alternate Fire during Aegis Storm. The blast deals 15% of Hildryn's Max Shield as additional damage.

### Wiki
Aegis Gale is a Warframe Augment Mod for Hildryn's Aegis Storm that grants Balefire an Alternate Fire during flight, firing a volley of projectiles whose damage scales with Hildryn's max Shields.
**Notes:**
Alternate Fire is instantaneous and no charge time, burst-firing 4 projectiles.
Base damage per shot is 500 (same as Balefire's uncharged shots) plus the bonus damage scaling from 15% of max shields.
Does not take into consideration Overshields.
Bonus damage scales with Ability Strength and is applied before mods.
When combined with Primed Redirection, Precision Intensify can add a very significant amount of damage to the alternate fire at the cost of having less strength in the rest of Hildryn's kit.
Precision Intensify has better damage scaling as Maximum Shields increase, surpassing Umbral Intensify after 3189 Maximum Shield capacity, which is easily achievable even on a non-prime Hildryn with a rank 10 Redirection.

## Aerial Ace
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** naramon | **Drain:** 4 | **Compatible:** Rifle | **Max Rank:** 5

### Acquisition
Obtainable from the Arbitrations vendor in the Arbiters of Hexis room in Relays for Vitus Essence 30 Vitus Essence.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** On Kill: Refresh Double Jump up to 1x while Airborne.
- **Rank 1:** On Kill: Refresh Double Jump up to 2x while Airborne.
- **Rank 2:** On Kill: Refresh Double Jump up to 3x while Airborne.
- **Rank 3:** On Kill: Refresh Double Jump up to 4x while Airborne.
- **Rank 4:** On Kill: Refresh Double Jump up to 5x while Airborne.
- **Rank 5:** On Kill: Refresh Double Jump up to 6x while Airborne.

### Wiki
Aerial Ace is an Exilus mod for rifles that refreshes Double Jump on kills while airborne.
**Notes:**
With a maxed-out mod a total of 8 consecutive jumps (7 while mid-air) are possible.
While Double Jump is specifically mentioned, all aerial parkour maneuvers are refreshed on kill. This includes Aim Glide and Bullet Jump.
Cannot be equipped on Exalted Weapons.

## Aerial Bond
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** vazarin | **Drain:** 2 | **Compatible:** COMPANION | **Max Rank:** 5

### Acquisition
The mod can be bought from The Business for 20,000 Standing after reaching Rank 3 - Doer with Solaris United.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Airborne kills decrease Companion Recovery Time by 0.5s and 1.5s for headshot kills. Companion creates a field of cold that increases up to 10% and 2.5m radius while Warframe is airborne, lasting for 3s after returning to ground.
- **Rank 1:** Airborne kills decrease Companion Recovery Time by 1s and 3s for headshot kills. Companion creates a field of cold that increases up to 15% and 4m radius while Warframe is airborne, lasting for 3s after returning to ground.
- **Rank 2:** Airborne kills decrease Companion Recovery Time by 1.5s and 4.5s for headshot kills. Companion creates a field of cold that increases up to 20% and 5.5m radius while Warframe is airborne, lasting for 3s after returning to ground.
- **Rank 3:** Airborne kills decrease Companion Recovery Time by 2s and 6s for headshot kills. Companion creates a field of cold that increases up to 25% and 7m radius while Warframe is airborne, lasting for 3s after returning to ground.
- **Rank 4:** Airborne kills decrease Companion Recovery Time by 2.5s and 7.5s for headshot kills. Companion creates a field of cold that increases up to 30% and 8.5m radius while Warframe is airborne, lasting for 3s after returning to ground.
- **Rank 5:** Airborne kills decrease Companion Recovery Time by 3s and 9s for headshot kills. Companion creates a field of cold that increases up to 35% and 10m radius while Warframe is airborne, lasting for 3s after returning to ground.

### Drop Locations
- Solaris United (The Business), Doer — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Aerial Bond is a Companion mod that reduces the companion recovery timer on airborne kills. The companion will also generate a Cold field when the owner is airborne, which remains for 3 seconds upon landing.
**Notes:**
The AoE Cold field can reach up to 10 Cold stacks and freeze enemies.

## Aerial Prospectus
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** penjaga | **Drain:** 0 | **Compatible:** Hound | **Max Rank:** 5

### Acquisition
Aerial Prospectus is automatically acquired upon obtaining a Hound built with the Wanz Stabilizer.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Launches a beacon at an enemy within 12m that calls down an Orbital Strike dealing 200 Blast Damage in a 2m radius.
- **Rank 1:** Launches a beacon at an enemy within 15m that calls down an Orbital Strike dealing 400 Blast Damage in a 3m radius.
- **Rank 2:** Launches a beacon at an enemy within 18m that calls down an Orbital Strike dealing 600 Blast Damage in a 4m radius.
- **Rank 3:** Launches a beacon at an enemy within 21m that calls down an Orbital Strike dealing 800 Blast Damage in a 5m radius.
- **Rank 4:** Launches a beacon at an enemy within 24m that calls down an Orbital Strike dealing 1000 Blast Damage in a 6m radius.
- **Rank 5:** Launches a beacon at an enemy within 27m that calls down an Orbital Strike dealing 1200 Blast Damage in a 7m radius.

### Wiki
Aerial Prospectus is a precept mod for Hounds that tosses a beacon at a target within range to call down an orbital strike, dealing radial Blast damage.
**Notes:**
Orbital Strike hits after a ~2s delay and has a 7m radius.
Damage is not affected by equipped mods on Hound or Hound Weapon.
Hit enemies are flung away.
Cooldown of ~10s.
Targeted enemy may move out of strike zone.
Cannot be equipped with Focused Prospectus and Synergized Prospectus.

## Aero Agility
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** naramon | **Drain:** 4 | **Compatible:** Sniper | **Max Rank:** 3

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** 25% Reload Speed while Aim Gliding
- **Rank 1:** 50% Reload Speed while Aim Gliding
- **Rank 2:** 75% Reload Speed while Aim Gliding
- **Rank 3:** 100% Reload Speed while Aim Gliding

### Drop Locations
- Jupiter/Ganymede (Disruption), Rotation C — Uncommon (10.00%)

### Wiki
Aero Agility is a set mod that increases reload speed during Aim Glide.
**Notes:**
Stacks additively with other reload speed mods such as Fast Hands.
**Trivia:**
It was first seen on Devstream 124, under the name Kestrel Finesse.
This version also featured a different set bonus, which allowed the player to deal Blast damage on landing, instead of putting enemies to sleep.

### Wiki
Aero Vantage is a set mod that grants reduced gravity during Aim Glide.
**Notes:**
Stacks with Boreal's Anguish for a total of -175% Gravity. This has several odd effects on aerial momentum; when starting an Aim Glide while falling, the user will stop immediately and slightly rise, while when starting an Aim Glide while rising(such as from a Jump, Double Jump, or Bullet Jump) the user will briefly fall before stabilizing in midair.
Slide attacking with melee weapons while aim gliding can make the character do a normal walking animation while still midair.
**Trivia:**
It was first seen on Devstream 124, under the name Kestrel Vantage.
This version also featured a different set bonus, which allowed the player to deal Blast damage on landing, instead of putting enemies to sleep.
Performing a slide attack with melee weapons while aim gliding can make the character do the normal walking and sprinting animations while still midair.

## Aerodynamic
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** vazarin | **Drain:** -2 | **Compatible:** AURA | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 5

### Acquisition
Awarded from Arbitrations missions.
Can be purchased from the Arbitrations vendor in the Arbiters of Hexis room in Relays for 30 Vitus Essence.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Squad takes 4% reduced damage while airborne, gains +1s Aim Glide and +2s Wall Latch
- **Rank 1:** Squad takes 8% reduced damage while airborne, gains +2s Aim Glide and +4s Wall Latch
- **Rank 2:** Squad takes 12% reduced damage while airborne, gains +3s Aim Glide and +6s Wall Latch
- **Rank 3:** Squad takes 16% reduced damage while airborne, gains +4s Aim Glide and +8s Wall Latch
- **Rank 4:** Squad takes 20% reduced damage while airborne, gains +5s Aim Glide and +10s Wall Latch
- **Rank 5:** Squad takes 24% reduced damage while airborne, gains +6s Aim Glide and +12s Wall Latch

### Drop Locations
- Arbitrations, Rotation A — Rare (2.00%)

### Wiki
Aerodynamic is an aura mod that increases the duration of Aim Glide and Wall Latch, as well as granting Damage Reduction while airborne.
* Aura mods increase the amount of Mod Capacity
**Notes:**
Archwing mode does not count as airborne.
Airborne damage reduction is applied during the following warframe abilities:
Titania's Razorwing form
Hildryn's Aegis Storm form
Jade's Glory on High form
Oraxia wall latching with her Silken Stride active
Valkyr while using Rip Line
Styanax casting Final Stand
Garuda channeling Seeking Talons while in the air
Zephyr casting Tail Wind
Unlike most other increases to Aimglide/Wall Latch duration, Aerodynamic increases the duration by a flat amount, by up to 6 seconds at Rank 5, for a total of 9 Seconds without other duration increases.

## Afterburn
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Chroma | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired by attaining the rank of Genius under Cephalon Suda, the rank of Partner under The Perrin Sequence, or the rank of Tempest in Conclave, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Spectral Scream Augment: Upon deactivation, Chroma will launch an elemental projectile dealing 25 Damage for each second the ability was active, to a maximum of 200 Damage.
- **Rank 1:** Spectral Scream Augment: Upon deactivation, Chroma will launch an elemental projectile dealing 50 Damage for each second the ability was active, to a maximum of 300 Damage.
- **Rank 2:** Spectral Scream Augment: Upon deactivation, Chroma will launch an elemental projectile dealing 75 Damage for each second the ability was active, to a maximum of 400 Damage.
- **Rank 3:** Spectral Scream Augment: Upon deactivation, Chroma will launch an elemental projectile dealing 100 Damage for each second the ability was active, to a maximum of 500 Damage.

### Drop Locations
- Cephalon Suda, Genius — Common (100.00%)
- Conclave, Tempest — Common (100.00%)
- The Perrin Sequence, Partner — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Afterburn is a Warframe Augment Mod usable in both PvE and Conclave for Chroma's Spectral Scream that launches a single damaging elemental attack upon deactivating the ability.
**Notes:**
Afterburn's damage is determined by how long Spectral Scream had been active, with a maximum cap on the damage that can be dealt.
Damage is affected by Ability Strength.
The damage dealt at max rank is enough to instantly kill a player in the Conclave.
**Trivia:**
Along with Ice Wave Impedance, Iron Shrapnel, and Mesa's Waltz, this mod is one of the few Augments usable in Conclave.

## Afterburner
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Elytron | **Max Rank:** 3

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Core Vent Augment: Blast Damage ignites exhaust fumes for 6s. Enemies passing through the flames take 200 Heat Damage.
- **Rank 1:** Core Vent Augment: Blast Damage ignites exhaust fumes for 8s. Enemies passing through the flames take 300 Heat Damage.
- **Rank 2:** Core Vent Augment: Blast Damage ignites exhaust fumes for 10s. Enemies passing through the flames take 400 Heat Damage.
- **Rank 3:** Core Vent Augment: Blast Damage ignites exhaust fumes for 12s. Enemies passing through the flames take 500 Heat Damage.

### Wiki
Afterburner is an Elytron Augment Mod for Core Vent that allows any sort of Blast damage to ignite the fuel cloud for a given duration, dealing Heat damage to any enemy inside the cloud.
Obtained by defeating Eximus units in Archwing missions.
**Notes:**
Duration is affected by Ability Duration.
Damage is affected by Ability Strength.
Detonating a cloud will also detonate all other clouds that were produced during the length of Core Vent's activation.
However, activating Core Vent a second time will require the new chain of clouds to be ignited separately. Thus, an ignited line can not ignite another line made by a separate Core vent use.
The cloud can be triggered by anything generating Blast damage, including enemies and enemy projectiles. This can be used to add a buffer around players when facing enemies using missiles.
Each cloud can hit multiple times, thus stacking clouds in the same area can be used to create an area of denial at choke points.
Alternatively, activating Core Vent and flying around the local area can create a wall of explosions to weaken the enemy.

## Agility Drift
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** vazarin | **Drain:** 4 | **Compatible:** WARFRAME | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 5

### Acquisition
Agility Drift is rewarded upon completing the Agility Test in the Orokin Moon. It is the only way to obtain this mod outside of Trading.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Reduced damage by 2% while airborne, -1% Enemy Accuracy when targeting Warframe
- **Rank 1:** Reduced damage by 4% while airborne, -2% Enemy Accuracy when targeting Warframe
- **Rank 2:** Reduced damage by 6% while airborne, -3% Enemy Accuracy when targeting Warframe
- **Rank 3:** Reduced damage by 8% while airborne, -4% Enemy Accuracy when targeting Warframe
- **Rank 4:** Reduced damage by 10% while airborne, -5% Enemy Accuracy when targeting Warframe
- **Rank 5:** Reduced damage by 12% while airborne, -6% Enemy Accuracy when targeting Warframe

### Wiki
Agility Drift is a rare dual-stat Exilus Mod that reduces damage taken while airborne and reduces enemy accuracy when targeting the player.
**Notes:**
Evasion refers to the ability to passively reduce the accuracy of enemy units attacking the player from range.
Damage reduction does affect Titania during Razorwing and Hildryn during Aegis Storm.
Can be used with Aviator to grant 1 - [ (1 - Aviator) * (1 - Agility Drift) ] damage reduction while airborne.
Can be used in conjunction with rolling in the air for an even greater damage reduction.

## Airburst Rounds
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Zephyr | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired by attaining the rank of Flawless in New Loka or the rank of Exalted under Red Veil and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Airburst Augment: Each enemy hit by Airburst increases secondary damage by 10% for 5s.
- **Rank 1:** Airburst Augment: Each enemy hit by Airburst increases secondary damage by 20% for 8s.
- **Rank 2:** Airburst Augment: Each enemy hit by Airburst increases secondary damage by 30% for 11s.
- **Rank 3:** Airburst Augment: Each enemy hit by Airburst increases secondary damage by 40% for 14s.

### Drop Locations
- New Loka, Flawless — Common (100.00%)
- Red Veil, Exalted — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Airburst Rounds is a Warframe Augment Mod for Zephyr that provides a damage bonus for secondaries for every enemy hit with Airburst.
**Notes:**
Total maximum damage bonus is capped at 500%.
Maximum damage bonus is not affected by Ability Strength.
Damage bonus per enemy hit is affected by Ability Strength.
Enemies struck by both the Airburst projectile directly and the explosion will provide twice the bonus per enemy hit this way.
Damage bonus additively stacks with base damage mods such as Hornet Strike.
Duration is affected by Ability Duration.
Duration can be refreshed by recasting the ability and hitting at least one enemy.
Refreshing keeps the highest bonus between the old and new Airbursts.
Applies to Hildryn's Balefire Charger, Dante's Noctua, and Jade's Glory.
Does not apply to Mesa's Regulators and Titania's Dex Pixia.
**Tips:**
Cast an Airburst vortex once to group enemies to strike them all directly with a second Airburst, maximizing the bonus per enemy.

## Amalgam Argonak Metal Auger
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** naramon | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Argonak | **Max Rank:** 5

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +0.5 Punch Through, Damage from Daggers reduces Armor by 1. Enemies are revealed by Punch Through.
- **Rank 1:** +1 Punch Through, Damage from Daggers reduces Armor by 2. Enemies are revealed by Punch Through.
- **Rank 2:** +1.5 Punch Through, Damage from Daggers reduces Armor by 3. Enemies are revealed by Punch Through.
- **Rank 3:** +2 Punch Through, Damage from Daggers reduces Armor by 4. Enemies are revealed by Punch Through.
- **Rank 4:** +2.5 Punch Through, Damage from Daggers reduces Armor by 5. Enemies are revealed by Punch Through.
- **Rank 5:** +3 Punch Through, Damage from Daggers reduces Armor by 6. Enemies are revealed by Punch Through.

### Drop Locations
- Jupiter/The Ropalolyst (Assassination) — Uncommon (20.00%)

### Wiki
Amalgam Argonak Metal Auger is an Amalgam Weapon Augments mod exclusive to the Argonak. It provides increased Punch Through, armor stripping when using a dagger, and allows the Argonak's enemy highlighting to work through walls.
**Notes:**
The special effect is only accessible while aiming Argonak and has a range of 100m.
Only enemies that are near your aiming reticle will be highlighted.
Enemies will be highlighted through walls even if you do not have enough punchthrough to hit them.
Armor reduction works like Shattering Impact and stacks additively with it.
All damage effects from daggers, including Status damage (Slash, Heat, Toxin, Electricity, Gas and Blast procs) will reduce armor.
Electricity's chain damage, Gas's damage cloud and Blast's AoE explosion will trigger the armor reduction, allowing them to remove armor on multiple targets.
Damage from lingering Gas clouds after an enemy dies does not trigger the armor reduction.
Damage from status effect transferred via Melee Influence will trigger the armor reduction.
Not tradeable.
**Tips:**
The Pointed Wind stance contains the two highest hit/sec combos of dagger stances (~3.2 hits/sec), however lacks the slashing of Stinging Thorn (~2.7 h/s on forward combo, ~2.5 h/s on neutral).

## Amalgam Barrel Diffusion
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** madurai | **Drain:** 10 | **Compatible:** Pistol | **Max Rank:** 5

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired by accumulating at least 50 points during the recurring Thermia Fractures event.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +18% Multishot, +10% Dodge Speed
- **Rank 1:** +37% Multishot, +20% Dodge Speed
- **Rank 2:** +55% Multishot, +30% Dodge Speed
- **Rank 3:** +73% Multishot, +40% Dodge Speed
- **Rank 4:** +91% Multishot, +50% Dodge Speed
- **Rank 5:** +110% Multishot, +60% Dodge Speed

### Wiki
Amalgam Barrel Diffusion is the Amalgam variant of Barrel Diffusion, sacrificing a small amount of Multishot to increase dodge speed.
**Notes:**
Dodge Speed increases the speed the player moves at when rolling.
Has a hidden Acrobatic Speed bonus, which increases the rate at which you roll, resulting in a shorter roll duration.
Together, the Dodge Speed and Acrobatic Speed bonuses result in a roll that goes approximately the same distance as a normal roll while taking less time.
The quicker roll animation can benefit Rolling Guard, maximizing the invulnerability period by reducing the time wasted while rolling.
The both bonuses are applied universally so long as Amalgam Barrel Diffusion is installed on the currently carried Secondary weapon; you do not need to be aiming, or even have your secondary wielded, to make use of the bonuses.
Amalgam Barrel Diffusion is essentially the Barrel Diffusion mod with altered stats. It is considered the same mod as its normal counterpart, and thus both mods cannot be equipped together.
Cannot be equipped on Sentinel weapons.
Synergizes well with Limbo, allowing him to slide between the material plane and Rift Plane more quickly.
The mod in reality grants 109.50% multishot, however the text and tooltip round up to 110%.
**Trivia:**
Before release, this mod was known as Tactical Diffusion.

## Amalgam Daikyu Target Acquired
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** madurai | **Drain:** 10 | **Compatible:** Daikyu | **Max Rank:** 5

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +12.5% to Headshot Multiplier, +0.5% Life Steal on Nikanas, 10% chance to pickup used arrows.
- **Rank 1:** +25% to Headshot Multiplier, +1% Life Steal on Nikanas, 20% chance to pickup used arrows.
- **Rank 2:** +37.5% to Headshot Multiplier, +1.5% Life Steal on Nikanas, 30% chance to pickup used arrows.
- **Rank 3:** +50% to Headshot Multiplier, +2% Life Steal on Nikanas, 40% chance to pickup used arrows.
- **Rank 4:** +62.5% to Headshot Multiplier, +2.5% Life Steal on Nikanas, 50% chance to pickup used arrows.
- **Rank 5:** +75% to Headshot Multiplier, +3% Life Steal on Nikanas, 60% chance to pickup used arrows.

### Drop Locations
- Jupiter/The Ropalolyst (Assassination) — Uncommon (20.00%)

### Wiki
Amalgam Daikyu Target Acquired is an Amalgam mod exclusive to the Daikyu and Daikyu Prime. It provides an increased headshot multiplier, Life Steal when using Nikanas, and a chance to gather used arrows.
**Notes:**
With this mod equipped, arrows stuck in enemies or on surfaces can sometimes be picked up when physically touched, along with a unique sound upon pickup. Multiple arrows fired from multishot may also be picked up, occasionally refunding more ammo than what was expended.
Since headshots normally reward 3.0x damage, this mod will increase that to 5.25x damage. This is further increased with Primary Deadhead for a total headshot damage multiplier of 6.15x.
Not tradeable.
The life steal from Nikanas applies to all of their attacks and Damage over Time status effects, such as Slash damage.
Lifesteal does not apply to Two-Handed Nikanas or Dual Nikanas.
**Tips:**
The mod can synergize very well with Ivara's Prowl ability as that also has innate bonus damage to headshots, and the Daikyu is innately silent.

## Amalgam Furax Body Count
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** naramon | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Furax | **Max Rank:** 5

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +2.5s Combo Duration +8% Fire Rate for Secondary Weapons Melee kills Blast and stagger enemies in 2.5m
- **Rank 1:** +5s Combo Duration +15% Fire Rate for Secondary Weapons Melee kills Blast and stagger enemies in 5m
- **Rank 2:** +7.5s Combo Duration +23% Fire Rate for Secondary Weapons Melee kills Blast and stagger enemies in 7.5m
- **Rank 3:** +10s Combo Duration +30% Fire Rate for Secondary Weapons Melee kills Blast and stagger enemies in 10m
- **Rank 4:** +12.5s Combo Duration +38% Fire Rate for Secondary Weapons Melee kills Blast and stagger enemies in 12.5m
- **Rank 5:** +15s Combo Duration +45% Fire Rate for Secondary Weapons Melee kills Blast and stagger enemies in 15m

### Drop Locations
- Jupiter/The Ropalolyst (Assassination) — Uncommon (20.00%)

### Wiki
Amalgam Furax Body Count is an Amalgam mod exclusive to the Furax, Mk1-Furax and Furax Wraith. It provides increased combo duration, increases secondary fire rate, and causes melee kills to inflict Blast and Stagger procs to nearby enemies.
Melee kills tend to apply ~ 6 Blast and Impact procs, though amount varies up to the limit of 10 stacks.
Blast procs only deal 1 damage per stack.
Does not trigger Slash procs via Furax Incarnon Genesis's Flashing Bleed.
Does trigger the armor reduction from Shattering Impact.
The effect has line of sight and does not go through walls.
Kills from melee status damage-over-time trigger this effect.
Kills by Exalted Weapons or pseudo-exalted abilities like Landslide do not trigger this effect.
Range mods such as Reach do not affect the 15m radius.
This effect alerts affected enemies, making this mod unsuitable for stealth play.
The +45% Fire Rate to Secondary Weapons applies to Mesa's Regulators, Hildryn's Balefire Charger, Dante's Noctua and Jade's Glory, when equipped.
The +45% Fire Rate to Secondary Weapons does not apply to Titania's Dex Pixia, when equipped.
**Trivia:**
Prior to Update 31.1, the "knockdown" term written on the mod is an effect of the old pre-Update 27.2 Blast proc, where enemies in a radius would be knocked down. The term was replaced by a stagger effect to restore the mod's original functionality.

Amalgam Javlok Magazine Warp is an Amalgam mod exclusive to the Javlok that increases its magazine capacity. It also increases Combo Count when parrying with a Sword and Shield.
**Notes:**
This mod increases the Javlok's magazine size by 3 (or 2.7 rounded up), thereby increasing the maximum damage of the thrown alternate fire by 60%.
If the Warframe has an invulnerability buff active (such as through Iron Skin or Elude, for example) the Warframe will not automatically block and thus, cannot take advantage of the Parrying bonuses from this mod, unless manually blocking in melee-only mode.
Not tradeable.

## Amalgam Organ Shatter
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** madurai | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Melee | **Max Rank:** 5

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired by accumulating at least 50 points during the recurring Thermia Fractures event.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +14% Critical Damage, +10% Heavy Attack Wind Up Speed
- **Rank 1:** +28% Critical Damage, +20% Heavy Attack Wind Up Speed
- **Rank 2:** +42% Critical Damage, +30% Heavy Attack Wind Up Speed
- **Rank 3:** +56% Critical Damage, +40% Heavy Attack Wind Up Speed
- **Rank 4:** +71% Critical Damage, +50% Heavy Attack Wind Up Speed
- **Rank 5:** +85% Critical Damage, +60% Heavy Attack Wind Up Speed

### Wiki
Amalgam Organ Shatter is the Amalgam variant of Organ Shatter, increasing the amount of critical damage while decreasing the time it takes to charge a Heavy Attack as well.
**Notes:**
Amalgam Organ Shatter does not increase the attack speed of the weapon during a Heavy Attack, as may be interpreted from the mod's description. Instead, it will simply reduce the 1 second charge up time required to make a heavy attack to 1/(1+60%) seconds at max rank. The swing itself will still remain the same speed and is only affected by normal attack speed mods/bonuses.
Speeds up the animation speed of Glaives when performing a throw attack, similar to Killing Blow.
Amalgam Organ Shatter is essentially the Organ Shatter mod with altered stats. It is considered the same mod as its normal counterpart, and thus both mods cannot be equipped together.
Synergizes well with melees that have unique behaviours on heavy attacks such as Sibear and Zenistar.
**Trivia:**
Before release, this mod was known as Swift Shatter.

## Amalgam Ripkas True Steel
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** madurai | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Ripkas | **Max Rank:** 5

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +31% Critical Chance (x2 for Heavy Attacks), +3% Reload Speed on Shotguns, +17% Gore Chance
- **Rank 1:** +62% Critical Chance (x2 for Heavy Attacks), +7% Reload Speed on Shotguns, +33% Gore Chance
- **Rank 2:** +93% Critical Chance (x2 for Heavy Attacks), +10% Reload Speed on Shotguns, +50% Gore Chance
- **Rank 3:** +125% Critical Chance (x2 for Heavy Attacks), +13% Reload Speed on Shotguns, +67% Gore Chance
- **Rank 4:** +156% Critical Chance (x2 for Heavy Attacks), +17% Reload Speed on Shotguns, +84% Gore Chance
- **Rank 5:** +187% Critical Chance (x2 for Heavy Attacks), +20% Reload Speed on Shotguns, +100% Gore Chance

### Drop Locations
- Jupiter/The Ropalolyst (Assassination) — Uncommon (20.00%)

### Wiki
Amalgam Ripkas True Steel is an Amalgam mod exclusive to the Ripkas. It provides increased critical chance, increased reload speed for shotguns, and increased gore chance.
**Notes:**
Cannot be equipped alongside True Steel or Galvanized Steel.
The "gore" keyword appears to describe the dismemberment mechanic; having this mod equipped will allow many weapons, abilities, and companions (even temporary ones) to dismember, even if they don't otherwise meet the requirements.
This synergizes well with Nekros's Desecrate, as bisected body parts are treated as distinct "corpses", and have their own chance to yield additional loot.
Tentacle Swarm consistently bisects enemies that it kills, though the low damage means that it scales poorly into higher-end content without additional sources of damage. Running it with Pilfering Swarm alongside a Nekros's Desecrate allows for up to 4 drops per applicable enemy.
This may include the Silver Grove Specters; at most, two drops from Pilfering Swarm, and one from Desecrate have been consistently observed.
The following exceptions exist:
This mod can allow killing blows from your damaging status effects to bisect a large variety of enemies, but it's inconsistent:
Slash, Heat, and Toxin will consistently bisect many common humanoids, but not newer or Infested enemies.
Be aware that Heat and Blast will make corpses despawn after 5 sec, compared to the default 15 sec.

## Amalgam Serration
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** madurai | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Rifle | **Max Rank:** 10

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired by accumulating at least 25 points during the recurring Thermia Fractures event.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +14% Damage, +2% Sprint Speed
- **Rank 1:** +28% Damage, +5% Sprint Speed
- **Rank 2:** +42% Damage, +7% Sprint Speed
- **Rank 3:** +56% Damage, +9% Sprint Speed
- **Rank 4:** +71% Damage, +11% Sprint Speed
- **Rank 5:** +85% Damage, +14% Sprint Speed
- **Rank 6:** +99% Damage, +16% Sprint Speed
- **Rank 7:** +113% Damage, +18% Sprint Speed
- **Rank 8:** +127% Damage, +20% Sprint Speed
- **Rank 9:** +141% Damage, +23% Sprint Speed
- **Rank 10:** +155% Damage, +25% Sprint Speed

### Wiki
Amalgam Serration is the Amalgam variant of Serration, sacrificing a small amount of damage to increase sprint speed.
**Notes:**
Amalgam Serration's sprint speed bonus always applies, regardless of whether or not you are holding the weapon it is equipped to.
Amalgam Serration's sprint speed bonus stacks additively with other speed mods like Rush.
Amalgam Serration is essentially the Serration mod with altered stats. It is considered the same mod as its normal counterpart, and thus both mods cannot be equipped together.
This mod cannot be equipped on Sentinel weapons.
While equipped, your Warframe's mod loadout will display the modified sprint speed, including the bonus from this mod, unlike Sprint Boost.
Affects Archwing speed when deployed in Landscapes.
**Trivia:**
Before release, this mod was known as Serrated Rush.

## Ammo Case
**Rarity:** Common | **Polarity:** penjaga | **Drain:** 2 | **Compatible:** Carrier | **Max Rank:** 5

### Acquisition
Ammo Case is automatically acquired upon obtaining Carrier or Carrier Prime.
The extra 25% ammo maximum is a multiplicative bonus to modded ammo maximum, rather than being additive with other bonuses.
For example, a weapon with 120 ammo maximum normally, and 228 after Trick Mag is slotted, will have 285 ammo maximum with Ammo Case.
Mutation Efficiency is the same for all ranks and equivalent to a Rank 3 Ammo Mutation Mod.
The amount of ammo received depends on the equipped weapon and follows the respective mutation mod for the equipped weapon.
Other mutation mods stack with this effect, however the mutation delay will always be applied.
Conversion effect does not work with Atmospheric Archguns.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Increases Ammo Capacity by 5% and converts Ammo Pickups into ammo for equipped weapons after 5s.
- **Rank 1:** Increases Ammo Capacity by 10% and converts Ammo Pickups into ammo for equipped weapons after 4s.
- **Rank 2:** Increases Ammo Capacity by 13% and converts Ammo Pickups into ammo for equipped weapons after 3.5s.
- **Rank 3:** Increases Ammo Capacity by 15% and converts Ammo Pickups into ammo for equipped weapons after 3s.
- **Rank 4:** Increases Ammo Capacity by 17% and converts Ammo Pickups into ammo for equipped weapons after 2.5s.
- **Rank 5:** Increases Ammo Capacity by 25% and converts Ammo Pickups into ammo for equipped weapons after 2s.

### Drop Locations
- Corrupted Butcher — Common (1.14%)
- Corrupted Lancer — Common (1.14%)

### Wiki
Ammo Case is a precept mod for Carrier and Carrier Prime to convert any unused ammo type into ammo for the currently equipped weapon and provides increased ammunition capacity.
Converted ammo is denoted by a Mutate symbol.
Ammo Conversion
**Trivia:**
Ammo Case was added as a replacement mod for Carrier's Vacuum precept, with all copies of Vacuum converted to Ammo Case. However, copies of Vacuum acquired after Update The Vacuum Within 2 will not be converted.

### Wiki
Ammo Drum increases the maximum amount of rifle ammunition your Warframe can carry for that weapon.
**Notes:**
For any non-integer maximum ammo capacity values the capacity is rounded down to the nearest whole number. For example, a Lanka modded with a max rank Ammo Drum will have a calculated maximum ammo capacity of 72 (1 + 90%), which will round down to 72 (1 + 90%).
**Trivia:**
The damaged version of Ammo Drum, along with 1 credit, were offered as a reward for a one hour long Infested Survival alert on the 2016 April Fools' Day.

## Ammo Stock
**Rarity:** Uncommon | **Polarity:** naramon | **Drain:** 2 | **Compatible:** Shotgun | **Max Rank:** 3

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +5% Magazine Capacity
- **Rank 1:** +10% Magazine Capacity
- **Rank 2:** +15% Magazine Capacity
- **Rank 3:** +20% Magazine Capacity

### Drop Locations
- Kuva Napalm — Rare (0.17%)
- Napalm — Rare (0.17%)
- Narmer Napalm — Rare (0.17%)
- Tusk Napalm — Rare (0.17%)
- Duviri/Endless: Tier 1 (Normal) — Legendary (0.67%)
- Duviri/Endless: Tier 3 (Normal) — Legendary (0.67%)

### Wiki
The Ammo Stock mod increases shotgun magazine capacity by 10% per rank for a maximum of 60% at rank 5.
**Notes:**
For any non-integer magazine values the magazine size is simply rounded up to the nearest whole number. For example, a Strun modded with a max rank will have a calculated magazine size of 6 (1 + 60%), which will simply round to 10.
A shotgun with only 1 base magazine capacity (such as Exergis) will have 1 (1 + 60%), rounded to 2 magazine capacity.

## Anabolic Pollination
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** penjaga | **Drain:** 0 | **Compatible:** Pharaoh Predasite | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
Anabolic Pollination is automatically acquired upon obtaining a Pharaoh Predasite.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Release a cloud of spores that persists for 10s increasing Toxin Damage by +25% for 15s.
- **Rank 1:** Release a cloud of spores that persists for 10s increasing Toxin Damage by +50% for 15s.
- **Rank 2:** Release a cloud of spores that persists for 10s increasing Toxin Damage by +75% for 15s.
- **Rank 3:** Release a cloud of spores that persists for 10s increasing Toxin Damage by +100% for 15s.

### Wiki
Anabolic Pollination is a precept mod for the Pharaoh Predasite to leave a trail of spores that add Toxin damage to allies for a brief period when touched.
**Notes:**
Cooldown of 16s.
The Pharaoh Predasite shakes, creating ~2m spore-spots that trail behind it for 2s. The trail lasts 10s.
If the Predasite doesn't move around, all spores may remain in one spot.
Toxin Damage bonus lasts for 15s and can be refreshed by touching the spores again.
Toxin damage bonus does not combine with other elements.
Toxin Damage bonus affects all players' weapons, archguns (when used as a heavy weapon), all players' companions and On-Call Railjack units exposed to the spores.
Affects Operator Amps and the Pharaoh Predasite itself.(No icon will appear by the Pharaoh to indicate its own buff)

## Anchored Glide

### Acquisition

- **Rank 0:** Disable Zephyr's reduced airborne gravity. Increase Ability Strength by 3%.
- **Rank 1:** Disable Zephyr's reduced airborne gravity. Increase Ability Strength by 5%.
- **Rank 2:** Disable Zephyr's reduced airborne gravity. Increase Ability Strength by 8%.
- **Rank 3:** Disable Zephyr's reduced airborne gravity. Increase Ability Strength by 10%.
- **Rank 4:** Disable Zephyr's reduced airborne gravity. Increase Ability Strength by 13%.
- **Rank 5:** Disable Zephyr's reduced airborne gravity. Increase Ability Strength by 15%.

### Drop Locations
- New Loka, Flawless — Common (100.00%)
- Red Veil, Exalted — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Anchored Glide is a Warframe Augment/Exilus Mod that sacrifices Zephyr's passive in exchange for increased Ability Strength.
**Notes:**
Only disables the reduced gravity aspect of the passive. The increased crit chance bonus while in midair is unaffected.

## Anemic Agility
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** naramon | **Drain:** 4 | **Compatible:** Pistol | **Max Rank:** 5

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +15% Fire Rate, -2.5% Damage
- **Rank 1:** +30% Fire Rate, -5% Damage
- **Rank 2:** +45% Fire Rate, -7.5% Damage
- **Rank 3:** +60% Fire Rate, -10% Damage
- **Rank 4:** +75% Fire Rate, -12.5% Damage
- **Rank 5:** +90% Fire Rate, -15% Damage

### Drop Locations
- Derelict Vault — Rare (4.17%)

### Wiki
Anemic Agility is a Corrupted Mod that increases a pistol's fire rate by 15% per rank but decreases its damage by 2.5% per rank, for a maximum of 90% fire rate and -15% damage at rank 5.
**Notes:**
Can be combined with Gunslinger, Lethal Torrent and Arcane Velocity for a total of +342% fire rate.
The -15% damage is additive with Hornet Strike and other mods that increase damage. Combining a max rank Anemic Agility with a max rank Hornet Strike (+220% damage) will lead to a total damage bonus of +205%.
The net damage loss from adding Anemic Agility to a weapon with a max-rank Hornet Strike is only around 4.7%.
Alongside a max-rank Hornet Strike, Anemic Agility represents a 5.3% DPS increase over Gunslinger, although for some weapons the increased ammo consumption and frequency of reloads will offset the modest gain in total DPS.
**Trivia:**
On release, Anemic Agility was able to drop from Grineer Ballistas, which made it the first Corrupted Mod to drop from an enemy unit. This was unintended however, and Anemic Agility was patched out of the Ballista's drop table in Update 15.7.2.
Sources

## Animal Instinct
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** naramon | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** COMPANION | **Max Rank:** 5

### Acquisition
Animal Instinct can be acquired as a random reward from Rotation C Nightmare Mode missions.
This mod is available as part of the 2100 and 4300 Platinum purchase packs.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +5m Loot Radar, +3m Enemy Radar
- **Rank 1:** +10m Loot Radar, +6m Enemy Radar
- **Rank 2:** +15m Loot Radar, +9m Enemy Radar
- **Rank 3:** +20m Loot Radar, +12m Enemy Radar
- **Rank 4:** +25m Loot Radar, +15m Enemy Radar
- **Rank 5:** +30m Loot Radar, +18m Enemy Radar

### Drop Locations
- Nightmare Mode Rewards, Rotation C — Uncommon (15.49%)

### Wiki
Animal Instinct is a Companion mod that increases the owner's Loot Radar and Enemy Radar radius.
**Notes:**
Animal Instinct will still function while the Companion is Incapacitated.
Venari can equip this mod, and if another Companion has it equipped both instances will stack their effects.
Does not work on Index points.
**Trivia:**
Despite not being a Corrupted Mod, Animal Instinct features a prime unit in its card image, in this case the Wyrm Prime.
Previously, the official website's Buy Platinum page incorrectly displayed the Enemy Sense as +20m at max rank. This has since been fixed.

## Anti-Flak Plating
**Rarity:** Uncommon | **Polarity:** vazarin | **Drain:** 4 | **Compatible:** WARFRAME | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod can acquired by reaching the rank of Whirlwind from the Conclave Syndicate, and spending 15,000 Standing to purchase.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +5 Blast Resistance, -2.5% Mobility
- **Rank 1:** +10 Blast Resistance, -5% Mobility
- **Rank 2:** +15 Blast Resistance, -7.5% Mobility
- **Rank 3:** +20 Blast Resistance, -10% Mobility

### Drop Locations
- Conclave, Whirlwind — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Anti-Flak Plating is Conclave-exclusive Warframe mod which grants resistance to Blast damage at the cost of reduced Mobility.
**Notes:**
Blast Damage Type Modifier bonus is additive to Health and Shield weaknesses/resistances.

## Anti-Grav Array
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** penjaga | **Drain:** 4 | **Compatible:** ROBOTIC | **Max Rank:** 3

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Increase height of owner's jumps by +10%.
- **Rank 1:** Increase height of owner's jumps by +20%.
- **Rank 2:** Increase height of owner's jumps by +30%.
- **Rank 3:** Increase height of owner's jumps by +40%.

### Drop Locations
- Amalgam Arca Heqet — Uncommon (1.00%)
- Amalgam Arca Kucumatz — Uncommon (1.00%)
- Amalgam Cinder Machinist — Uncommon (1.00%)
- Amalgam Phase Moa — Uncommon (1.00%)
- Amalgam Swarm Satyr — Uncommon (1.00%)

### Wiki
Anti-Grav Array is a Sentinel or MOA mod that increases their owner's jump heights.
**Notes:**
Only increases normal jump and double jump height. Bullet jump is unaffected (see Mobilize).
When using this mod and trying to jump lower, like into the entrance of a tube, hole in the wall, etc. crouch first, then aim at the hole, it will bullet jump out of the crouch and right into the hole.
Anti-Grav Array cannot be traded unless the player offering the item owns more than one copy (much like other Sentinel Precept mods).
**Tips:**
Can complement Ivara while under the effects of Infiltrate since she cannot bullet jump without breaking invisiblity; this can get her the height she needs in specific situations.
**Trivia:**
Anti-grav refers to Anti-gravity.

## Anti-Grav Grenade
**Rarity:** Common | **Polarity:** penjaga | **Drain:** 2 | **Compatible:** Moa | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
Anti-Grav Grenade is automatically acquired upon obtaining a MOA built with the Para Model.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** A grenade that levitates enemies in a 1.5m. After 6s afflicted enemies come crashing down, causing Impact Damage equal to 20% of the damage taken while floating.
- **Rank 1:** A grenade that levitates enemies in a 3m. After 6s afflicted enemies come crashing down, causing Impact Damage equal to 40% of the damage taken while floating.
- **Rank 2:** A grenade that levitates enemies in a 4.5m. After 6s afflicted enemies come crashing down, causing Impact Damage equal to 60% of the damage taken while floating.
- **Rank 3:** A grenade that levitates enemies in a 6m. After 6s afflicted enemies come crashing down, causing Impact Damage equal to 80% of the damage taken while floating.

### Wiki
Anti-Grav Grenade is a precept mod for MOAs to fire a grenade that reverses gravity in an area, inflicting Impact damage to targets and causing them to float upwards.
**Notes:**
Will only activate if the player is attacking enemies with a weapon and an enemy is nearby.
Cooldown of ~20s.
Grenade is thrown in an arc, which can stick to obstacles/terrain. The maximum throw distance is 45m.
24.1.2

## Anticipation
**Rarity:** Uncommon | **Polarity:** vazarin | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** WARFRAME | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod is acquired by attaining the rank of Mistral with Teshin, and spending 10,000 Standing to purchase.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Immune to Knockdown for an additional +1s after being knocked down., Immune to Stagger for an additional +1s after being Staggered.
- **Rank 1:** Immune to Knockdown for an additional +2s after being knocked down., Immune to Stagger for an additional +2s after being Staggered.
- **Rank 2:** Immune to Knockdown for an additional +3s after being knocked down., Immune to Stagger for an additional +3s after being Staggered.
- **Rank 3:** Immune to Knockdown for an additional +4s after being knocked down., Immune to Stagger for an additional +4s after being Staggered.

### Drop Locations
- Conclave, Mistral — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Anticipation is a Conclave-exclusive Exilus mod that grants an extended immunity to either Impairs or Knockdowns (Warframes have an innate 4 second immunity at base) depending on which was received.
**Trivia:**
At the time this mod only provided resistance to impairs after being impaired. In Update 18.2, Enguard's original effects were merged with this mod and Enguard was retooled into Armored Recovery.

## Antimatter Absorb
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Nova | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired by attaining the rank of General under Steel Meridian, or the rank of Genius under Cephalon Suda, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Antimatter Drop Augment: Absorbs enemy bullets within 2m, increasing the power of the explosion from Nova's particle of antimatter.
- **Rank 1:** Antimatter Drop Augment: Absorbs enemy bullets within 3m, increasing the power of the explosion from Nova's particle of antimatter.
- **Rank 2:** Antimatter Drop Augment: Absorbs enemy bullets within 4m, increasing the power of the explosion from Nova's particle of antimatter.
- **Rank 3:** Antimatter Drop Augment: Absorbs enemy bullets within 5m, increasing the power of the explosion from Nova's particle of antimatter.

### Drop Locations
- Cephalon Suda, Genius — Common (100.00%)
- Steel Meridian, General — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Antimatter Absorb is a Warframe Augment Mod for Nova's Antimatter Drop that allows it to absorb incoming enemy gunfire, adding to its total damage potential.
**Notes:**
Radius is affected by Ability Range.
Using the augment while aiming may result in the drop becoming stuck in Nova's hand and automatically detonating after a few seconds. This may be used as an emergency shield however, providing protection to gunfire from all directions.
4wN6tPwzp20

## Antimatter Mine
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Nova | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired by attaining the rank of Tempest in Conclave and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Antimatter Drop Augment: Creates a fully charged stationary orb that explodes after 12s or within enemy proximity.
- **Rank 1:** Antimatter Drop Augment: Creates a fully charged stationary orb that explodes after 10s or within enemy proximity.
- **Rank 2:** Antimatter Drop Augment: Creates a fully charged stationary orb that explodes after 8s or within enemy proximity.
- **Rank 3:** Antimatter Drop Augment: Creates a fully charged stationary orb that explodes after 6s or within enemy proximity.

### Drop Locations
- Conclave, Tempest — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Antimatter Mine is a Warframe Augment Mod for Nova's Antimatter Drop in Conclave which creates a fully charged stationary orb, that either explodes after 6 seconds or on enemy proximity.
**Tips:**
As ranking up this mod decreases the duration of the mine it can be beneficial to leave it unranked so it lasts longer. However it is unknown if ranking up the mod increases the enemy detection range which, if this were the case, would create a dilemma in choosing between higher duration or range.

## Apex Predator
On Hit:
**Rarity:** Uncommon | **Polarity:** naramon | **Drain:** 2 | **Compatible:** Rifle | **Max Rank:** 5

### Acquisition
This mod can acquired by reaching the rank of Mistral from the Conclave Syndicate, and spending 10,000 Standing to purchase.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Reveals target on Minimap for +1s.
- **Rank 1:** Reveals target on Minimap for +2s.
- **Rank 2:** Reveals target on Minimap for +3s.
- **Rank 3:** Reveals target on Minimap for +4s.
- **Rank 4:** Reveals target on Minimap for +5s.
- **Rank 5:** Reveals target on Minimap for +6s.

### Drop Locations
- Conclave, Mistral — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Apex Predator is a Conclave-exclusive rifle mod that causes targets that are hit to be temporarily visible on the minimap.
**Notes:**
Any enemy hit will also create a thin, red line that will trail behind the moving opponent.
**Trivia:**
Tracking Shot was likely a pre-release version of this mod. "Original" version granted the same effect, but with longer duration and smaller capacity.

## Arc Coil
**Rarity:** Common | **Polarity:** penjaga | **Drain:** 2 | **Compatible:** Diriga | **Max Rank:** 5

### Acquisition
Arc Coil is automatically acquired upon obtaining Diriga.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Sentinel will zap up to 2 enemies within 10m, dealing 100 Electricity Damage with a 10% Status Chance.
- **Rank 1:** Sentinel will zap up to 3 enemies within 10m, dealing 100 Electricity Damage with a 10% Status Chance.
- **Rank 2:** Sentinel will zap up to 4 enemies within 10m, dealing 100 Electricity Damage with a 10% Status Chance.
- **Rank 3:** Sentinel will zap up to 5 enemies within 10m, dealing 100 Electricity Damage with a 10% Status Chance.
- **Rank 4:** Sentinel will zap up to 6 enemies within 10m, dealing 100 Electricity Damage with a 10% Status Chance.
- **Rank 5:** Sentinel will zap up to 7 enemies within 10m, dealing 100 Electricity Damage with a 10% Status Chance.

### Wiki
Arc Coil is a precept mod for Diriga to zap multiple enemies with Electricity damage with a chance to stun.
**Notes:**
It is essentially a multiple target version of Electro Pulse, with a low chance to proc an electric stun instead of 100% of the time.
The damage is fairly low, and relies on the small chance of proccing to stun enemies.
Prevents Calculated Shot from firing when Arc Coil is in effect.
The cooldown is inconsistent, but approximately 1 second.
