## Bursting Mass
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** naramon | **Drain:** 4 | **Compatible:** Mutalist Quanta | **Max Rank:** 5

### Acquisition
Available for 20 from the Nightwave Cred Offerings store on a rotational basis.
Bursting mass was originally acquired by reaching Rank 18 in Nightwave during Series 1: The Wolf of Saturn Six or reaching Rank 20 during Intermission III.
Beginning with Nightwave Nora's Mix: Vol. 5, it was added to the Nightwave Cred Offerings.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** The infested mass accumulates 50% of the damage that goes through it and deals it in 15m radius when it expires. Secondary fire will manually detonate an existing mass.
- **Rank 1:** The infested mass accumulates 80% of the damage that goes through it and deals it in 15m radius when it expires. Secondary fire will manually detonate an existing mass.
- **Rank 2:** The infested mass accumulates 110% of the damage that goes through it and deals it in 15m radius when it expires. Secondary fire will manually detonate an existing mass.
- **Rank 3:** The infested mass accumulates 140% of the damage that goes through it and deals it in 15m radius when it expires. Secondary fire will manually detonate an existing mass.
- **Rank 4:** The infested mass accumulates 170% of the damage that goes through it and deals it in 15m radius when it expires. Secondary fire will manually detonate an existing mass.
- **Rank 5:** The infested mass accumulates 200% of the damage that goes through it and deals it in 15m radius when it expires. Secondary fire will manually detonate an existing mass.

### Wiki
Bursting Mass is a Weapon Augment Mod for Mutalist Quanta that accumulates the damage that passes through its infested mass, upon expiring it will release the accumulated damage in a radius. Using secondary fire will manually detonate the existing mass.
**Notes:**
Adds a separate damage instance to the orb, dealing the accumulated weapon damage in a 15m radius.
The explosion's damage composition matches all of the damage that passed through the orb, allowing for a rainbow of elements.
Damage is modified by the orb and varies depending on the Mutalist Quanta's multishot. Accumulated damage will be reduced to 66% of the total and Electricity will be added but this will never combine with other elements:
+100% Electricity at 1 Multishot
+366% Electricity at 2 Multishot
+500% Electricity at 3 Multishot
+366% Electricity at 4 Multishot (See bugs)
For example, a Kulstar with a max rank Hornet Strike and a max rank Pathogen Rounds firing through a double-stacked orb will contribute: 
200 x (1 + Hornet Strike) x (1 + Pathogen Rounds + Mutalist Quanta) x Mutalist Quanta Bursting Mass = 845Impact + 760Toxin + 3092Electricity = 4697 additional damage to the Bursting Mass explosion.
The explosion does not inflict status effects.
Faction damage bonuses cannot contribute damage, but faction bonuses on Mutalist Quanta itself will improve the explosion.
Critical bonus damage is not absorbed and the explosion cannot crit.
Damage falls off linearly from full 200% damage at the center of the explosion to 0% damage at the 15m edge.
The orb's standard Toxin explosion and its radius of 8m are unchanged.
Projectiles created from Warframe abilities can

## Buzz Kill
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** naramon | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Melee | **Max Rank:** 5

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired by successfully capturing or killing Eidolon Hydrolysts.
It is now offered as a periodic item for sale by Baro Ki'Teer, costing 375 and 150,000 to purchase. Note that Baro Ki'Teer's stock changes with each appearance, and this mod may not be available on a succeeding appearance.
This mod was previously acquired by scoring 10 Exposure Points in the Operation: Gate Crash Event, and again later as a rare drop during the Endurance portion of the Emergency Exit Tactical Alert.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +20% Slash
- **Rank 1:** +40% Slash
- **Rank 2:** +60% Slash
- **Rank 3:** +80% Slash
- **Rank 4:** +100% Slash
- **Rank 5:** +120% Slash

### Drop Locations
- Eidolon Hydrolyst — Rare (8.33%)

### Wiki
Buzz Kill is a mod that increases Slash damage of a melee weapon by 20% per rank for a maximum of 120% at rank 5.
**Notes:**
Slash bonus has no effect on the damage of the bleed status effect.
Can be combined with Carnis Mandible, Jagged Edge and Rending Strike to provide a 360% increase to Slash damage.
Compared to a base elemental mod (+90%) in terms of raw damage, Buzz Kill becomes more effective when a weapon's Slash damage exceeds 75% of total weapon damage. This does not take damage type multipliers into consideration.
**Trivia:**
Buzz Kill is the second in a three-member series of 120% melee physical damage mods in which the first letter of each mod forms the series "A-B-C".
Unlike its two cousin mods, which depict each of their modeling Warframes with their Immortal body skin, this mod depicts Valkyr with her base skin.

## Calculated Shot
**Rarity:** Common | **Polarity:** penjaga | **Drain:** 0 | **Compatible:** Diriga | **Max Rank:** 5

### Acquisition
Calculated Shot is automatically acquired upon obtaining Diriga.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Sentinel charges a powerful shot and fires at the first enemy within 40.8m.
- **Rank 1:** Sentinel charges a powerful shot and fires at the first enemy within 46.6m.
- **Rank 2:** Sentinel charges a powerful shot and fires at the first enemy within 52.5m.
- **Rank 3:** Sentinel charges a powerful shot and fires at the first enemy within 58.3m.
- **Rank 4:** Sentinel charges a powerful shot and fires at the first enemy within 64.1m.
- **Rank 5:** Sentinel charges a powerful shot and fires at the first enemy within 70m.

### Wiki
Calculated Shot is a precept mod for Diriga that allows it to engage enemies within sight with a long-range shot.
**Notes:**
Range indicates the maximum distance the Diriga attacks a target, however it will keep firing on the engaged target a few meters beyond maximum distance until it disengages.
Keep in mind that Diriga will use this precept regardless of the weapon equipped, which has interesting consequences with certain weapons;
If used in tandem with weapons whose projectiles have considerable travel time, the Diriga will not switch targets until after the burst has finished or the weapon has finished its cycle.
The Sweeper (Sweeper Prime)'s low accuracy and damage falloff are not taken into account, so the Diriga will barely damage its target, if at all. The bad scatter of the Deth Machine Rifle will make it have the same issue.
Using the Burst Laser (Prisma Burst Laser) will result in the weapon firing in heavily skewed angles or where moving enemies were when first engaging with them.
Even with the Diriga's signature Vulklok, given the projectile's travel time the player will likely need to have Terminal Velocity equipped in order to have a good chance at hitting moving targets beyond 40m.

Spread radius is affected by ability range.
Remaining duration on spread is not affected by mods.
Line of sight is not required to spread the effect.

- **Rank 1:** Rest & Rage Augment: Killing an affected enemy causes the effect to spread to enemies within 3m for 60% of the remaining duration.
- **Rank 2:** Rest & Rage Augment: Killing an affected enemy causes the effect to spread to enemies within 4m for 80% of the remaining duration.
- **Rank 3:** Rest & Rage Augment: Killing an affected enemy causes the effect to spread to enemies within 5m for 100% of the remaining duration.

### Wiki
Calm & Frenzy is a Warframe Augment Mod for Equinox that aids her in spreading the effects of Rest & Rage. Enemies slain while affected by Rest & Rage spreads it to others within close proximity, for the remaining duration.

## Capacitance
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Volt | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired by attaining the rank of Maxim under the Arbiters of Hexis, or the rank of Exalted under Red Veil, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Discharge Augment: Converts 1.5% of Damage dealt into Shields split between Volt and Squadmates.
- **Rank 1:** Discharge Augment: Converts 2% of Damage dealt into Shields split between Volt and Squadmates.
- **Rank 2:** Discharge Augment: Converts 2.5% of Damage dealt into Shields split between Volt and Squadmates.
- **Rank 3:** Discharge Augment: Converts 3% of Damage dealt into Shields split between Volt and Squadmates.

### Drop Locations
- Arbiters of Hexis, Maxim — Common (100.00%)
- Red Veil, Exalted — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Capacitance is a Warframe Augment Mod for Volt that converts a percentage of damage dealt by Discharge into shields, split between himself and allies.
**Notes:**
Can create Overshields.
Affected allies includes all Warframes, Companions, Eidolon Lures, summoned allied units such as Specters, allied Invasion units, Hostages, Kavor Defectors, Sortie and Arbitrations Defense Operatives, and Defense Objects.
On Defense Objects, their shield gain is capped at 250, and do not gain overshields.
Shield bonus is affected by Ability Strength, but the amount per second also seems to be an average instead of a static number, and can fluctuate for each tick dealt on enemies even if the damage remains the same.
This augment works best the more enemies there are in a group, due to how Discharge works, zapping each other and increasing the amount of bulk shield obtained within the first moments of the cast.
**Tips:**
Mods like Fast Deflection and Fortitude can be used to increase Volt's shield recharge rate, allowing overshields to add up sooner.
Enemies trapped in a vortex of Vauban's Bastille will all be hit at the same time, and will shock every enemy inside at once causing potentially massive bulks of shields each second as well as increased DPS of the ability. This also allows a low Power Range build to be viable.
Casting Electric Shield before casting can prevent some damage to Volt and his allies when casting, as the base ability prevents Volt from moving, and may only slowly regenerate shielding.

## Carnis Stinger
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** naramon | **Drain:** 4 | **Compatible:** Pistol | **Max Rank:** 5

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +15% Slash, +10% Status Chance
- **Rank 1:** +30% Slash, +20% Status Chance
- **Rank 2:** +45% Slash, +30% Status Chance
- **Rank 3:** +60% Slash, +40% Status Chance
- **Rank 4:** +75% Slash, +50% Status Chance
- **Rank 5:** +90% Slash, +60% Status Chance

### Drop Locations
- Deimos Carnis Rex — Rare (0.20%)
- Deimos Carnis — Rare (0.20%)
- Infested Oni — Rare (0.20%)
- Deimos/Cambion Drift (Level 25 - 30 Cambion Drift Bounty), Rotation B — Rare (1.70%)
- Deimos/Cambion Drift (Level 25 - 30 Cambion Drift Bounty), Rotation C — Rare (1.72%)
- Deimos/Cambion Drift (Level 25 - 30 Cambion Drift Bounty), Rotation A — Rare (1.75%)
- Deimos/Cambion Drift (Level 25 - 30 Cambion Drift Bounty), Rotation B — Rare (2.50%)
- Deimos/Cambion Drift (Level 25 - 30 Cambion Drift Bounty), Rotation C — Rare (2.50%)
- Deimos/Cambion Drift (Level 25 - 30 Cambion Drift Bounty), Rotation A — Rare (2.56%)

### Wiki
Carnis Stinger is a set mod that increases both the Slash Damage and Status Chance of Secondary Weapons.
**Notes:**
The Slash damage of a weapon must be at least 70% of the total for the raw damage gained from this mod to be comparable to an elemental damage counterpart like Scorch.
Pistols with high slash damage and status chance include;
Lato Prime
Vasto Prime (Akvasto Prime)
Akjagara (Akjagara Prime)
Kohmak (Twin Kohmak) is also a reasonable choice if your goal is to build for the set bonus, or if you specifically want the Slash status effect.
Can be combined with Maim and Razor Shot to provide a 300% increase to Slash damage.

## Carving Mantis
Strong slashes and quick stabs that keeps momentum forward.
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** naramon | **Drain:** -2 | **Compatible:** Dual Swords | **Max Rank:** 3

### Drop Locations
- Plains Commander — Legendary (0.50%)
- Mimic — Rare (2.14%)
- Earth/Cetus (Level 100 - 100 Cetus Bounty), Rotation A — Rare (5.41%)
- Earth/Cetus (Level 40 - 60 Cetus Bounty), Rotation A — Rare (5.41%)
- Earth/Cetus (Level 100 - 100 Cetus Bounty), Rotation A — Rare (8.16%)
- Earth/Cetus (Level 40 - 60 Cetus Bounty), Rotation A — Rare (8.16%)

### Wiki
Carving Mantis is a stance mod for Dual Swords type weapons. It combines strong slashes and quick stabs while keeping forward momentum.
**Notes:**
Many of the attacks do bonus Slash damage.
The last 5 attacks of Rapid Incisions do 25% bonus Slash damage.
Every attack of Ambush Predator except the first two hits of the second attack do 25% bonus Slash damage.
The first and third attack of Biting Mandibles do 25% bonus Slash damage.
Certain attacks have bonus status chance. Status chance bonus is multiplicative to modded status chance.
The second attack of Northern Coil has 25% bonus status chance.
**Trivia:**
Dire Courtship may be a reference to a female Praying Mantis' tendency to decapitate its mate.

## Cat's Eye
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** penjaga | **Drain:** 0 | **Compatible:** Adarza Kavat | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
Cat's Eye is automatically acquired upon obtaining a Adarza Kavat.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Adarza Kavat grants 15% increased critical chance for 4s to allies within 15m every 50s.
- **Rank 1:** Adarza Kavat grants 30% increased critical chance for 6s to allies within 20m every 40s.
- **Rank 2:** Adarza Kavat grants 45% increased critical chance for 8s to allies within 22m every 30s.
- **Rank 3:** Adarza Kavat grants 60% increased critical chance for 10s to allies within 25m every 20s.

### Drop Locations
- Kavat — Rare (0.83%)

### Wiki
Cat's Eye is a precept mod for the Adarza Kavat to temporarily increase the critical chance of nearby allies.
**Notes:**
Despite its description, Cat's Eye adds 60 percentage points to a weapon's critical chance after other bonuses, similar to Arcane Avenger. This type of buff is commonly referred to as an absolute bonus.
Cat's Eye stacks additively with other percentage point bonuses, such as Arcane Avenger. For example, an Atterax with a max rank True Steel, Blood Rush at a 4.0x combo multiplier, Cat's Eye, and Arcane Avenger will have a 25% (1 + 120% + 40%(4 - 1)) + 60% + 45% critical chance.
Highly effective with weapons with high critical damage multipliers.
Emplaced turrets (i.e. the Grineer Rampart) gain the ability to critically strike while under the effect of Cat's Eye.
While this also applies to weapons that cannot critically strike, these weapons often have a critical multiplier of 1.0x which yields no change in damage dealt.
Multiple instances of Cat's Eye do not stack.
The ability triggers when an enemy is within at least 25 meters.
The cooldown of 20 seconds starts when Cat's Eye's duration stops.

## Cataclysmic Continuum
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Limbo | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired by attaining the rank of Maxim under the Arbiters of Hexis, or the rank of Genius under Cephalon Suda, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Cataclysm Augment: Duration increased by 0.30s for each enemy killed.
- **Rank 1:** Cataclysm Augment: Duration increased by 0.5s for each enemy killed.
- **Rank 2:** Cataclysm Augment: Duration increased by 0.69s for each enemy killed.
- **Rank 3:** Cataclysm Augment: Duration increased by 1s for each enemy killed.

### Drop Locations
- Arbiters of Hexis, Maxim — Common (100.00%)
- Cephalon Suda, Genius — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Cataclysmic Continuum is a Warframe Augment Mod for Limbo that extends Cataclysm's duration for every enemy killed inside it.
**Notes:**
The Duration Bonus is not affected by Ability Duration.
This mod does not increase the duration beyond the maximum duration of Cataclysm, and the added seconds do not increase the Cataclysm's size neither do they stop it from shrinking. The increased duration does however decrease the shrinking speed.
The reduction of Cataclysm's radius with the augment equipped is summed up in the following formula:Reduction 2 Base Radius (1 + Range Mods) (3 Base Duration (1 + Duration Mods) + Augment Duration Bonus)
While the mod does reduce the shrinking rate of Cataclysm, it does not prevent it from reaching its minimum radius, though the augment does allow players to maintain the bubble at minimum radius indefinitely as long as they kill enemies in the bubble.
The effect only occurs when an enemy dies within the bubble. It still occurs when a Warframe outside the bubble kills an enemy within, but it does not occur when a Warframe within the bubble kills an enemy outside it.

## Cataclysmic Gate
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Wisp | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired by attaining the rank of Genius under Cephalon Suda, or the rank of Flawless under New Loka, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Sol Gate Augment : Wisp and 2 spectral images cast a single blast of Sol Gate, leaving an area with 500 Heat Damage / Second with guaranteed Heat and Radiation Status Effects for 5s.
- **Rank 1:** Sol Gate Augment : Wisp and 2 spectral images cast a single blast of Sol Gate, leaving an area with 500 Heat Damage / Second with guaranteed Heat and Radiation Status Effects for 7s.
- **Rank 2:** Sol Gate Augment : Wisp and 2 spectral images cast a single blast of Sol Gate, leaving an area with 500 Heat Damage / Second with guaranteed Heat and Radiation Status Effects for 8s.
- **Rank 3:** Sol Gate Augment : Wisp and 2 spectral images cast a single blast of Sol Gate, leaving an area with 500 Heat Damage / Second with guaranteed Heat and Radiation Status Effects for 10s.

### Wiki
Cataclysmic Gate is an Warframe Augment Mod for Wisp's Sol Gate that causes her and two spectral images of herself to launch a single Sol Gate that leaves behind a field that inflicts Heat and Radiation damage with guaranteed status effects over time.
**Notes:**
No longer a channeled ability with an energy drain over time.
Blasts three solar plasma beams from Wisp and her spectral images for up to 1 second, then explodes for 5000 radial Heat damage in a Ability Range10m, creating a spherical sun portal at the reticle location. Sun portal inflicts 500 damage per half second, evenly split between Impact, Puncture, and Slash, in a Ability Range10m area, with guaranteed status effects of Heat and Radiation per tick of damage.
Sol Gate's plasma beam phase can be skipped by pressing the ability button again (default 4), instantly deploying the sun portal.
Explosion and damage per half second damage and break destructible Objects in range.
Sun portals are enhanced by Reservoirs synergies.
The Haste Mote applies an additional guaranteed Corrosive status from every tick of sun portal damage.
The Vitality and Shock Motes each grant a 25% total damage bonus to the sun portal damage.

## Catalyzer Link
**Rarity:** Common | **Polarity:** madurai | **Drain:** 4 | **Compatible:** Rifle | **Max Rank:** 5

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** On Ability Cast: +10% Status Chance when Aiming for 2s
- **Rank 1:** On Ability Cast: +20% Status Chance when Aiming for 3s
- **Rank 2:** On Ability Cast: +30% Status Chance when Aiming for 5s
- **Rank 3:** On Ability Cast: +40% Status Chance when Aiming for 6s
- **Rank 4:** On Ability Cast: +50% Status Chance when Aiming for 8s
- **Rank 5:** On Ability Cast: +60% Status Chance when Aiming for 9s

### Drop Locations
- Deimos/Cambion Drift (Level 15 - 25 Cambion Drift Bounty), Rotation A — Uncommon (13.16%)
- Deimos/Cambion Drift (Level 15 - 25 Cambion Drift Bounty), Rotation A — Rare (5.68%)
- Deimos/Cambion Drift (Level 15 - 25 Cambion Drift Bounty), Rotation A — Rare (7.35%)
- Lua/Pavlov (Spy), Rotation B — Rare (7.49%)

### Wiki
Catalyzer Link is a common Rifle mod that increases status chance while aiming for a brief time after casting an ability.
**Notes:**
Catalyzer Link is additive with similar mods. For example, a Kuva Karak with a max rank Catalyzer Link and a max rank Galvanized Aptitude will have a 31% (1 + 60% + 80%) status chance.
While a secondary or melee weapon is equipped, Catalyzer Link's buff icon still triggers when an ability is casted, this appears to be a lingering effect to reap the benefits of the mod when you switch to your primary, as status chance does not seem to increase for secondary and melee weapons.

## Catalyzing Shields
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** vazarin | **Drain:** 10 | **Compatible:** WARFRAME | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** x0.80 Max Shield Capacity, 0.33s Full Shield Gate immunity duration
- **Rank 1:** x0.60 Max Shield Capacity, 0.67s Full Shield Gate immunity duration
- **Rank 2:** x0.40 Max Shield Capacity, 1.0s Full Shield Gate immunity duration
- **Rank 3:** x0.20 Max Shield Capacity, 1.33s Full Shield Gate immunity duration

### Drop Locations
- Derelict Vault — Rare (4.17%)

### Wiki
Catalyzing Shields is a Corrupted Mod for Warframes that sets the Shield Gating duration to a fixed value upon recovering any amount of Shields, and reduces maximum Shield capacity.
**Notes:**
This mod was intended to function how Shield Gating and Decaying Dragon Key worked prior to Update 34, where shield gating duration was based on simply fully recovering shields, thus causing lower shield values to be highly beneficial to reach the full duration. However this is not how this mod was implemented.
100% Shields at Shield Break: 1.33s of Shield Gating
75% Shields at Shield Break: 1.0s of Shield Gating
50% Shields at Shield Break: 0.67s of Shield Gating
25% Shields at Shield Break: 0.34s of Shield Gating
10% Shields at Shield Break: 0.33s of Shield Gating (minimum)
For unknown reasons, the duration scaling mechanic does not exist in the actual implementation of the mod, causing Catalyzing Shields to always set shield gates to last the full duration (i.e. 1.33 seconds at max rank) no matter the point your shield breaks.

## Catapult
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Grendel | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired by attaining the rank of General under Steel Meridian, or the rank of Exalted under Red Veil, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Pulverize Augment: to launch Grendel in your aim direction. Costs 15. Crouch to slow down.
- **Rank 1:** Pulverize Augment: to launch Grendel in your aim direction. Costs 10. Crouch to slow down.
- **Rank 2:** Pulverize Augment: to launch Grendel in your aim direction. Costs 7.5. Crouch to slow down.
- **Rank 3:** Pulverize Augment: to launch Grendel in your aim direction. Costs 5. Crouch to slow down.

### Drop Locations
- Red Veil, Exalted — Common (100.00%)
- Steel Meridian, General — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Catapult is a Warframe Augment Mod/Exilus Mod that lets Grendel launch himself in a given direction with Pulverize.
**Notes:**
Energy cost is affected by Ability Efficiency.
Once Catapult is activated, Grendel is launched toward the direction of the aiming reticle at his current speed plus 25 m/s, during which his field of vision widens considerably.
Colliding into obstacles including enemies and terrain will not decelerate Grendel while Catapult is still in effect.
Crouching during the Catapult will reduce Grendel's speed.

## Cathode Current
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Gyre | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired by attaining the rank of Maxim under Arbiters of Hexis, or the rank of Partner under The Perrin Sequence, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Cathode Grace Augment: Eliminating an enemy while Cathode Grace is active will release an additional discharge from Rotorswell with 50% Damage and extend its duration by the same amount.
- **Rank 1:** Cathode Grace Augment: Eliminating an enemy while Cathode Grace is active will release an additional discharge from Rotorswell with 100% Damage and extend its duration by the same amount.
- **Rank 2:** Cathode Grace Augment: Eliminating an enemy while Cathode Grace is active will release an additional discharge from Rotorswell with 150% Damage and extend its duration by the same amount.
- **Rank 3:** Cathode Grace Augment: Eliminating an enemy while Cathode Grace is active will release an additional discharge from Rotorswell with 200% Damage and extend its duration by the same amount.

### Drop Locations
- Arbiters of Hexis, Maxim — Common (100.00%)
- The Perrin Sequence, Partner — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Cathode Current is a Warframe Augment Mod for Gyre's Cathode Grace that causes enemies killed to release an additional discharge from Rotorswell and extend its duration.
**Notes:**
Bonuses are not affected by mods.
Rotorswell duration is increased by the same amount as Cathode Grace on kill. As such, its duration cannot be increased beyond the base duration plus 60 seconds.
Cathode Current will sometimes stop providing extra duration when Rotorswell is cancelled by a Nullifier bubble. Can be corrected by either entering another Nullifier bubble while Rotorswell is active, or by going down

## Celestial Stomp
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Wukong | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired by attaining the rank of Maxim under the Arbiters of Hexis, or the rank of Flawless under New Loka, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Celestial Twin Augment: Hold to command the twin to perform a slam attack suspending enemies in the air within 12m for 25 Energy.
- **Rank 1:** Celestial Twin Augment: Hold to command the twin to perform a slam attack suspending enemies in the air within 14m for 25 Energy.
- **Rank 2:** Celestial Twin Augment: Hold to command the twin to perform a slam attack suspending enemies in the air within 17m for 25 Energy.
- **Rank 3:** Celestial Twin Augment: Hold to command the twin to perform a slam attack suspending enemies in the air within 20m for 25 Energy.

### Drop Locations
- Arbiters of Hexis, Maxim — Common (100.00%)
- New Loka, Flawless — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Celestial Stomp is a Warframe Augment Mod for Wukong that commands his Celestial Twin to perform a slam attack for 25 Energy when the ability button (default 1) is held, suspending nearby enemies in the air.
**Notes:**
Affected enemies will be suspended in the air for 6 seconds, and need 2 additional seconds to stand up and recover.
Stun duration is not affected by Ability Duration.
Range is affected by Ability Range.
Does not require line of sight and can affect enemies behind walls and obstacles.
Deals 100 Blast damage with ~10% status chance to affected enemies.
Damage is not affected by Ability Strength.
Enemies cannot be damaged by subsequent slams until they have recovered from being suspended.
Wukong cannot unsummon/cancel Celestial Twin while Celestial Stomp is equipped.
Functions very similarly to Rhino Stomp, both in its slow strength on suspended enemies, and its CC effectiveness reduction against bosses and minibosses.

## Champion's Blessing
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Trinity | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
Can be acquired by attaining the rank of Partner under The Perrin Sequence, or the rank of Flawless under New Loka, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Blessing Augment: Gain Primary and Secondary Critical Chance for 12s for each percent you heal on allies up to 350%.
- **Rank 1:** Blessing Augment: Gain Primary and Secondary Critical Chance for 18s for each percent you heal on allies up to 350%.
- **Rank 2:** Blessing Augment: Gain Primary and Secondary Critical Chance for 24s for each percent you heal on allies up to 350%.
- **Rank 3:** Blessing Augment: Gain Primary and Secondary Critical Chance for 30s for each percent you heal on allies up to 350%.

### Drop Locations
- New Loka, Flawless — Common (100.00%)
- The Perrin Sequence, Partner — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Champion's Blessing is a Warframe Augment Mod for Trinity's Blessing that increases their own primary and secondary Critical Chance based on the total percentage health healed, up to a +350% bonus.
**Notes:**
The Champion's Blessing buff applies only to the ability caster.
Any ally healed with Blessing will trigger Champion's Blessing, including Warframes, Specters, and Companions, whether from other players, NPCs, or the self.
Combat Discipline can be used to deliberately inflict self-damage.
Note that Combat Discipline's self-damage is affected by damage-reducing abilities, such as Link and Blessing.
Only restored Health counts towards the bonus; restoring Shields has no effect.
Casting multiple Blessings will reset the buff timer.
Critical chance from multiple blessings will be added together up to the 350% maximum.
Critical chance bonus is additive with other sources of critical chance bonus.
The duration of Champion's Blessing is affected by Ability Duration.
Champion's Blessing has a longer duration than Blessing and will continue to function until its own duration expires.
Champion's Blessing can be stripped by nullification effects such as Stalker's Dispel or a Nullifier Crewman's shield.

## Chaos Sphere
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Nyx | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
Can be acquired by attaining the rank of Maxim under the Arbiters of Hexis, or the rank of Flawless under New Loka, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Chaos Augment: Enemies entering the Effect Range will be inflicted with Chaos. Effect Range lasts for 25% of the ability duration and shrinks over time.
- **Rank 1:** Chaos Augment: Enemies entering the Effect Range will be inflicted with Chaos. Effect Range lasts for 30% of the ability duration and shrinks over time.
- **Rank 2:** Chaos Augment: Enemies entering the Effect Range will be inflicted with Chaos. Effect Range lasts for 40% of the ability duration and shrinks over time.
- **Rank 3:** Chaos Augment: Enemies entering the Effect Range will be inflicted with Chaos. Effect Range lasts for 50% of the ability duration and shrinks over time.

### Drop Locations
- Arbiters of Hexis, Maxim — Common (100.00%)
- New Loka, Flawless — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Chaos Sphere is a Warframe Augment Mod for Nyx that allows Chaos to create a circular radius that affects enemies entering within its range for a fraction of its duration.
**Notes:**
Upon casting Chaos, a circular radius shown as a wall of energy is laid down on the location where the ability was cast. Any enemies entering through this radius will receive 10 Radiation status effects. This area-of-effect is stationary, and will not move along with Nyx.
The radius of this effect slowly shrinks within the duration of the Chaos Sphere's effect.
The shrinking radius of the effect is often faster than the speed of the advancing enemies, thus limiting the usefulness of this augment.
The Chaos ability timer will terminate if Chaos Sphere reaches the end of its duration and there are no more enemies affected by Chaos, whether from the initial cast or from the Chaos Sphere.

## Charged Bullets
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** madurai | **Drain:** 4 | **Compatible:** Archgun | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod is available as a potential end of mission reward during a Fomorian Sabotage encounter.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +15% Electricity, +15% Status Chance
- **Rank 1:** +30% Electricity, +30% Status Chance
- **Rank 2:** +45% Electricity, +45% Status Chance
- **Rank 3:** +60% Electricity, +60% Status Chance

### Drop Locations
- Profit-Taker — Rare (2.56%)
- Lektro Commander — Uncommon (3.00%)
- Fomorian Sabotage — Rare (5.00%)

### Wiki
Charged Bullets is a mod that increases Electricity damage and status chance of Archguns by 15% per rank for a maximum of 60% at rank 3.
**Notes:**
Charged Bullets is additive with similar mods. For example, an Imperator with a max rank Charged Bullets and Modified Munitions will have a 5% (1 + 60% + 60%) status chance.
Can be stacked with Electrified Barrel for a total of 180% Electricity damage.

## Charged Chamber
**Rarity:** Uncommon | **Polarity:** madurai | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Sniper | **Max Rank:** 5

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +10% Damage on first shot in Magazine
- **Rank 1:** +20% Damage on first shot in Magazine
- **Rank 2:** +30% Damage on first shot in Magazine
- **Rank 3:** +40% Damage on first shot in Magazine
- **Rank 4:** +50% Damage on first shot in Magazine
- **Rank 5:** +60% Damage on first shot in Magazine

### Drop Locations
- Axio Railgun Moa — Uncommon (0.34%)
- Orm Railgun Moa — Uncommon (0.34%)
- Railgun Moa — Uncommon (0.34%)
- Taro Railgun Moa — Uncommon (0.34%)
- Vapos Railgun Moa — Uncommon (0.34%)
- Vorac Railgun Moa — Uncommon (0.34%)
- Duviri/Endless: Tier 1 (Normal) — Legendary (0.67%)
- Duviri/Endless: Tier 3 (Normal) — Legendary (0.67%)
- Earth/Cetus (Level 10 - 30 Cetus Bounty), Rotation A — Uncommon (10.08%)
- Earth/Cetus (Level 10 - 30 Cetus Bounty), Rotation A — Uncommon (25.00%)
- Earth/Cetus (Level 10 - 30 Cetus Bounty), Rotation A — Rare (9.68%)

### Wiki
Charged Chamber is a sniper rifle mod that increases the damage from the first shot fired from a fresh magazine.
**Notes:**
Charged Chamber is multiplicative with other damage mods. For example, a Vectis installed with a max ranked Charged Chamber and Serration will deal 225 (1 + 165%) (1 + 40%) damage.
Stacks additively with Primed Chamber for up to 140% bonus damage.
Charged Chamber does not work with Sentinel weapons (you can fit it but there is no bonus damage).
If used with Ammo Efficiency bonuses, it will provide bonus damage to a shot as long as the magazine counter is at after a shot is fired.
For example, a Vulkar with Energized Munitions will have the 4th, 5th, 6th, and 7th shots buffed by Charged Chamber as the magazine counter is at 6 - 1 during those shots.
When used alongside 100% ammo efficiency, make sure one shot is missing from the magazine, since the buff doesn't apply on a completely full one.
**Tips:**
This mod goes particularly well with the Vectis as it has a one shot magazine, giving the bonus to all shots fired.
**Trivia:**
This is an Uncommon mod from a relatively rare enemy type, regularly drops on T3 Corpus Survivals. However, it is found occasionally in treasure rooms in Orokin Void missions.

## Charged Shell
**Rarity:** Uncommon | **Polarity:** naramon | **Drain:** 4 | **Compatible:** Shotgun | **Max Rank:** 3

## Charm
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** penjaga | **Drain:** 0 | **Compatible:** Smeeta Kavat | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
Charm is automatically acquired upon obtaining a Smeeta Kavat.
List of Possible Buffs
thumb|Charm buff icon
The duration of the buffs below are for a fully-ranked Charm. When the player receives a buff (apart from Rare Resource), a charm icon will appear to the top right corner of the HUD, it will also display for a brief time what kind of buff it is.
Average Time for a Buff Occurrence
The average time for a specific buff to occur is given by the formula
where mb is the average wait time and t is the cooldown indicated on the mod. So the mb for a Rank 3 Charm to give triple affinity is:
27/(0.4*0.1)s
This means for that one specific buff to occur with a max rank mod, the average time to wait is 11 minutes 15 seconds.
Alternatively, the average time for any buff to occur with a max rank mod is
27/(0.4*1)s
where 1 is equivalent to the sum of the chance of all buffs (100%).

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Smeeta Kavat has a 10% chance every 30s to bestow its owner with good fortune.
- **Rank 1:** Smeeta Kavat has a 20% chance every 29s to bestow its owner with good fortune.
- **Rank 2:** Smeeta Kavat has a 30% chance every 28s to bestow its owner with good fortune.
- **Rank 3:** Smeeta Kavat has a 40% chance every 27s to bestow its owner with good fortune.

### Drop Locations
- Hyekka — Rare (0.83%)
- Kuva Hyekka — Rare (0.83%)

### Wiki
Charm is a precept mod for the Smeeta Kavat to grant a random buff to its owner, indicated by a special icon next to its owner's health and shields.

- **Rank 10:** +330% Cold, +330% Status Chance, Converts all elemental damage from these claws to Cold damage

### Wiki
Chilling Claws is a Mod for Beast Claws that grants additional Cold Damage & Status Chance, while converting all elemental damage into Cold Damage.
**Notes:**
Cannot be equipped alongside Burning Claws, Sepsis Claws, and Shocking Claws.
Does not convert or mix elemental damage from Momentous Bond.
Only converts primary elemental damage; secondary elemental damage like Radiation from Radon Claws and Magnetic from Magnetic Claws will not be converted.

## Chilling Globe
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Frost | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired by attaining the rank of General under Steel Meridian, or the rank of Genius under Cephalon Suda, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.
This mod can periodically be acquired from The Circuit.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Snow Globe Augment: Enemies that enter have a 30% chance to become frozen solid for 10s.
- **Rank 1:** Snow Globe Augment: Enemies that enter have a 35% chance to become frozen solid for 10s.
- **Rank 2:** Snow Globe Augment: Enemies that enter have a 40% chance to become frozen solid for 10s.
- **Rank 3:** Snow Globe Augment: Enemies that enter have a 50% chance to become frozen solid for 10s.

### Drop Locations
- Cephalon Suda, Genius — Common (100.00%)
- Steel Meridian, General — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Chilling Globe is a Warframe Augment Mod for Frost that gives Snow Globe a chance to inflict 10 Cold status effects and freeze enemies solid that enter its range.
**Notes:**
Duration is affected by Ability Duration.
Enemies are frozen upon crossing the Snow Globe's perimeter.
Enemies that are not frozen cannot be frozen until they exit and reenter the globe.
**Trivia:**
This augment adds a small visual change to Snow Globe in the form of an aurora within the globe's circumference.

### Drop Locations
- Nightmare Mode Rewards, Rotation A — Uncommon (22.11%)

### Wiki
Chilling Reload is a mod that increases Cold damage and reload speed of Shotguns.
**Notes:**
Obtained from easy Nightmare Missions, which can be found on Mercury, Venus, Earth and Mars.
Can be combined with Chilling Grasp and Frigid Blast for a total of +210% additional Cold damage.
Can be combined with Tactical Pump and Seeking Fury for a maximum 85% reload speed bonus, granting an approximate ~46% reduction of reload time.

## Chromatic Blade
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Excalibur | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired by attaining the rank of General under Steel Meridian, or the rank of Maxim under the Arbiters of Hexis, and spending 25000 Standing to purchase.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Exalted Blade Augment: Exalted Blade's Damage Type changes depending on Excalibur's Emissive Color, and Status Chance is increased by 100%.
- **Rank 1:** Exalted Blade Augment: Exalted Blade's Damage Type changes depending on Excalibur's Emissive Color, and Status Chance is increased by 200%.
- **Rank 2:** Exalted Blade Augment: Exalted Blade's Damage Type changes depending on Excalibur's Emissive Color, and Status Chance is increased by 250%.
- **Rank 3:** Exalted Blade Augment: Exalted Blade's Damage Type changes depending on Excalibur's Emissive Color, and Status Chance is increased by 300%.

### Drop Locations
- Arbiters of Hexis, Maxim — Common (100.00%)
- Steel Meridian, General — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Chromatic Blade is a Warframe Augment Mod for Excalibur's Exalted Blade that increases Exalted Blade's status chance while changing its damage from physical damage to a primary elemental damage depending on Excalibur's chosen primary emission color.
**Notes:**
Status chance bonus is additive with other status chance mods and is affected by Ability Strength.
Total Status Chance Base status chance [1 + (Chromatic Blade Bonus (1 + Strength Mods)) + Status Mods]
For example, using a maxed Intensify, Drifting Contact, and Chromatic Blade results in:0.15 [1 + (Chromatic Blade Intensify) + Drifting Contact] status chance
Achieving 100% status chance without any status chance mods requires 189%Ability Strength.
Chromatic Blade's color selection is based on Excalibur's primary emission color:
White, grey, black, and some faded colors make Cold.
Blue and purple hues make Electricity.
Red, magenta, brown, orange and bright yellow hues make Heat.
Green, lime, teal and dark yellow hues make Toxin.
Purchasing color palettes is not required to unlock the all the elemental options.
While customizing Excalibur's emission color, Chromatic Blade's active element is indicated by an icon (Heat, Electricity, Toxin, Cold). This imgur album can serve as a reference for future color palette purchases.
Customizing the secondary emission and the energy colors will have no effect.
The Arthur Gemini Skin swaps between appearance configs, allowing the player to color for two separate elements and swap between them with the Gemini Emote.
Chromatic Blade's elemental damage replaces the innate physical elements and will combine other any elemental damage modded on Exalted Blade.
Physical damage mods will no longer affect Exalted Blade's damage with Chromatic Blade equipped.

### Wiki
Cleanse Corpus is a mod that increases the Faction Damage of shotguns against Corpus.
**Notes:**
Has no effect on Corrupted and Narmer Corpus (e.g. Corrupted Crewman, Narmer Crewman).
Despite being listed as a multiplier, it will stack additively with other sources of Damage to Corpus.
For example, if you had a Riven Mod with x0.5 Damage to Corpus and a max rank Cleanse Corpus with x1.3 Damage to Corpus, you would have a 1 - 0.5 + 0.3 = x0.8 multiplier for damage against Corpus with both mods equipped.

## Cleaving Whirlwind
Arcing cuts with spinning finish.
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** vazarin | **Drain:** -2 | **Compatible:** Heavy Blade | **Max Rank:** 3

### Drop Locations
- Ancient Disruptor — Rare (0.03%)
- Deimos Therid — Rare (0.03%)
- Tar Mutalist Moa — Rare (0.03%)
- Lua/Pavlov (Spy), Rotation C — Rare (2.01%)

### Wiki
Cleaving Whirlwind is a stance mod for Heavy Blade melee weapons. It is a momentum-based stance whose slow, sweeping attacks deal high damage in coned or radial areas around the wielder.
Cleaving Whirlwind
Cleaving Whirlwind
**Notes:**
Both Sundered Tusk and Drifting Stampede do 25% bonus Slash damage.
The Broken Bull Combo involves spinning the blade in a 360 arc around the Warframe while moving forward with each press of the melee key. This can be used to hit multiple enemies in a large area.
Can be used while sprinting.
Hits twice in each spin.
Attempting a 6th attack in this combo will stagger the player.
**Tips:**
Broken Bull punishes mashing forward melee by causing the Tenno to lose their balance and stagger if they try to continue spinning after the 5th spin. To avoid the stagger, either intersperse Broken Bull with attacks from other combos (e.g., the first attack of Crowd Fall) or briefly stop attacking to reset the combo.
Stagger recovery time is shortened dramatically with strong attack speed buffs such as Valkyr Warcry or Volt Speed.
Stagger recovery time is not affected by Pain Threshold.
With careful timing, one could achieve the third hit within the Broken Bull spin by turning 90 degrees clockwise after the second hit has landed.
**Trivia:**
Crowd Fall is the standard combo for most Heavy Blade melee weapons without a Stance mod on.
Cleaving Whirlwind is the only Stance Mod that has a stagger penalty.

## Clip Delegation
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** vazarin | **Drain:** 2 | **Compatible:** Sobek | **Max Rank:** 5

### Acquisition
Available for 20 from the Nightwave Cred Offerings store on a rotational basis.
Clip Delegation was originally acquired by reaching Rank 15 in Nightwave during Nora's Mix Volume 6.
Beginning with Nightwave Nora's Mix: Dreams of the Dead, it was added to the Nightwave Cred Offerings.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** On Reload: Next Magazine has Status Chance and Multishot increased by 5% per shot landed with current Magazine. Max 15 stacks.
- **Rank 1:** On Reload: Next Magazine has Status Chance and Multishot increased by 7% per shot landed with current Magazine. Max 15 stacks.
- **Rank 2:** On Reload: Next Magazine has Status Chance and Multishot increased by 9% per shot landed with current Magazine. Max 15 stacks.
- **Rank 3:** On Reload: Next Magazine has Status Chance and Multishot increased by 11% per shot landed with current Magazine. Max 15 stacks.
- **Rank 4:** On Reload: Next Magazine has Status Chance and Multishot increased by 13% per shot landed with current Magazine. Max 15 stacks.
- **Rank 5:** On Reload: Next Magazine has Status Chance and Multishot increased by 15% per shot landed with current Magazine. Max 15 stacks.

### Wiki
Clip Delegation is a Weapon Augment Mod exclusive to the Sobek and Kuva Sobek that, on reload, increases the next magazine's Status Chance and Multishot per shot landed with the current magazine, to a maximum of 15 stacks at all mod ranks.
**Notes:**
Stacks only considers per trigger pull. Individual pellets from Multishot or Punch Through hitting multiple targets do not grant additional stacks.
Reload from Holster effects only apply to the first reloaded tick. Subsequent ticks will then remove all stacks.
For Acid Shells builds, the increased Multishot and Status Chance will make the initial target easier to kill, but will have no effect on the area of effect explosions.
**Tips:**
Increasing Magazine Size or Ammo Efficiency allows more available shots before reloading.
Primary Crux would be a beneficial partner for this reason; it adds yet more Status Chance and Ammo Efficiency when an enemy's weakpoint is struck.

## Coaction Drift
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** naramon | **Drain:** 4 | **Compatible:** WARFRAME | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 5

### Acquisition
Coaction Drift is awarded upon completing the Collaboration Test in the Orokin Moon.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Squad benefits +2.5% more from Auras, You benefit an additional +2.5% from Auras
- **Rank 1:** Squad benefits +5% more from Auras, You benefit an additional +5% from Auras
- **Rank 2:** Squad benefits +7.5% more from Auras, You benefit an additional +7.5% from Auras
- **Rank 3:** Squad benefits +10% more from Auras, You benefit an additional +10% from Auras
- **Rank 4:** Squad benefits +12.5% more from Auras, You benefit an additional +12.5% from Auras
- **Rank 5:** Squad benefits +15% more from Auras, You benefit an additional +15% from Auras

### Wiki
Coaction Drift is a rare dual-stat Exilus mod that increases an Aura's strength and effectiveness.
**Notes:**
Aura Strength increases the effect of your aura on everyone. Aura Effectiveness increases the effect of everyone's auras on yourself.
When maxed, this will increase your aura's strength to 4 for yourself, and 1.15x for everyone else.
For example, when combined with Steel Charge, it will increase your melee damage by 60%, and increase your squadmates' melee damage by 1.15 60%.
Does not work with Power Donation, Combat Discipline or Summoner's Wrath.
This mod also takes effect in Archwing gameplay, as long as it is equipped on your Warframe.
For auras that do not affect you specifically, but as a group (such as Corrosive Projection), only Aura Strength applies.
The table on Coaction Drift/Aura Table lists the total combinations of Auras with up to four players.
**Trivia:**
For a brief period, Coaction Drift doubled the base Aura's effect before applying its bonus. This was fixed in Update 22.20.7.

## Coil Recharge
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Gyre | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired by attaining the rank of Maxim under Arbiters of Hexis, or the rank of Partner under The Perrin Sequence, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Coil Horizon Augment: Gyratory Sphere can be recalled. Enemies in its radius suffer 350 Electricity Damage and chain other enemies. Enemies pulled into detonation suffer additional discharges.
- **Rank 1:** Coil Horizon Augment: Gyratory Sphere can be recalled. Enemies in its radius suffer 400 Electricity Damage and chain other enemies. Enemies pulled into detonation suffer additional discharges.
- **Rank 2:** Coil Horizon Augment: Gyratory Sphere can be recalled. Enemies in its radius suffer 450 Electricity Damage and chain other enemies. Enemies pulled into detonation suffer additional discharges.
- **Rank 3:** Coil Horizon Augment: Gyratory Sphere can be recalled. Enemies in its radius suffer 500 Electricity Damage and chain other enemies. Enemies pulled into detonation suffer additional discharges.

### Wiki
Coil Recharge is a Warframe Augment Mod for Gyre's Coil Horizon that allows her Gyratory Sphere to be recalled on recast without detonating it, while discharging additional Electricity damage that chains to other enemies. Upon held cast, the sphere will detonate and enemies that are pulled in from the detonation will receive additional discharges.
**Notes:**
Has a duration of Ability Duration20 seconds, after which it will detonate.
Has a discharge range of 5 meters.
Discharges every 4 seconds. Every 15 meters traveled, the sphere will discharge to an additional enemy and reduce the discharge timer by 0.25 seconds down to a minimum of 0.25 seconds at 225 meters.
Critical hits from Coil Recharge's discharge will trigger Rotorswell's discharge.
On recall, the Gyratory Sphere will sling itself towards Gyre's current position, stopping in front of her, facilitating a melee strike to the sphere to fling it at the reticle's direction.

- **Rank 2:** Cosmic Crush Augment: The black hole saps the area of heat, freezing enemies in range. Enemies near the black hole are completely frozen for 4s.

### Wiki
Cold Snap is an Itzal Augment Mod for Cosmic Crush that inflicts a Cold proc to any enemy affected by the ability, with enemies close to the player being frozen outright.
**Notes:**
Duration is affected by Ability Duration.
**Tips:**
Cold Snap is a useful mod when deploying Itzal both in space and on Landscapes such as the Plains of Eidolon via an Archwing Launcher, both for increased survivability and crowd control. In the case of the latter, the ability can conveniently pull enemies into a frozen clump of helpless targets at the mercy of the Itzal flyer's weapons.

## Collision Force
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** naramon | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Melee | **Max Rank:** 5

### Acquisition
This mod can be obtained by killing the Eidolon Teralyst.
It is now offered as a periodic item for sale by Baro Ki'Teer, costing 400 and 250,000 to purchase. Note that Baro Ki'Teer's stock changes with each appearance, and this mod may not be available on a succeeding appearance.
This mod was previously acquired by amassing 1000 Points in the Operation: False Profit Event.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +20% Impact
- **Rank 1:** +40% Impact
- **Rank 2:** +60% Impact
- **Rank 3:** +80% Impact
- **Rank 4:** +100% Impact
- **Rank 5:** +120% Impact

### Drop Locations
- Eidolon Teralyst — Rare (8.33%)

### Wiki
The Collision Force mod increases Impact damage of melee weapons by 20% per rank for a maximum of 120% at rank 5.
**Notes:**
Can be combined with Primed Heavy Trauma to provide a 285% total increase to Impact damage.
Compared to a base elemental mod (+90%) in terms of raw damage, Collision Force becomes more effective when a weapon's Impact damage exceeds 75% of total weapon damage. This does not take damage type multipliers into consideration.
**Trivia:**
Collision Force is the last in a three-member series of 120% melee physical damage mods in which the first letter of each mod forms the series "A-B-C".
Similar to its cousins, this mod's artwork depicts its modeling Warframe, Rhino, with his Immortal body skin.

## Combat Discipline
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** vazarin | **Drain:** -2 | **Compatible:** AURA | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 5

### Acquisition
Awarded from Arbitrations missions.
Can be purchased from the Arbitrations vendor in the Arbiters of Hexis room in Relays for 20 Vitus Essence.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** You lose 1 Health on kill Squadmates gain 5 Health on kill
- **Rank 1:** You lose 2 Health on kill Squadmates gain 8 Health on kill
- **Rank 2:** You lose 4 Health on kill Squadmates gain 11 Health on kill
- **Rank 3:** You lose 6 Health on kill Squadmates gain 14 Health on kill
- **Rank 4:** You lose 8 Health on kill Squadmates gain 17 Health on kill
- **Rank 5:** You lose 10 Health on kill Squadmates gain 20 Health on kill

### Drop Locations
- Arbitrations, Rotation A — Rare (2.00%)
- Arbitrations, Rotation B — Rare (2.50%)
- Arbitrations, Rotation C — Rare (3.50%)

### Wiki
Combat Discipline is an aura mod that allows allies to heal on kills at the cost of the aura bearer losing health on kills.
* Aura mods increase the amount of Mod Capacity
**Notes:**
The aura bearer health loss and ally health gain are separately triggered, as follows:
When the aura bearer kills an enemy, only the aura bearer loses health.
When an ally kills an enemy, only that ally gains health.
Companion kills count as user kills.
Does not heal Companions.
Multiple instances of Combat Discipline in a squad stack in an unusual way: the formula for health gain for each squad member with the mod equipped is A+B+C-D, where A, B and C are the health gain values of ally instances of Combat Discipline, and D is the health loss value of the aura-bearer's own instance of the mod (each squad member has a different variable because this formula assumes that different players may choose to keep their Combat Discipline at different ranks).
Using this formula, if all 4 players in a squad equip max rank copies of Combat Discipline, each player will heal +50 health per kill (+20 from each ally and -10 for the player's own copy).
All the damage/heal happens on the same instance, making it not do any damage to the player doing kills.
The health loss is considered damage taken which bypasses shields, as such:
The aura bearer can be downed with this mod.
Losing 1 health from a rank 0 aura will kill the aura bearer on 2 health.
Damage Reduction affects the amount of health lost per kill.
Invulnerability fully prevents the health loss.
Allows Arcane Enhancements that trigger on damage (i.e.

## Combo Fury
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** naramon | **Drain:** 4 | **Compatible:** Thrown Melee | **Max Rank:** 5

### Acquisition
This mod can be purchased unranked from the Void Trader Baro Ki'Teer for 115,000 and 300. Remember however that Baro Ki'Teer's stock changes with each appearance, and may not have this item available at every time.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** On Melee Kill: +16.7% Reload Speed +16.7% Magazine Capacity for 12s on Secondary Weapon
- **Rank 1:** On Melee Kill: +33.3% Reload Speed +33.3% Magazine Capacity for 12s on Secondary Weapon
- **Rank 2:** On Melee Kill: +50% Reload Speed +50% Magazine Capacity for 12s on Secondary Weapon
- **Rank 3:** On Melee Kill: +66.6% Reload Speed +66.6% Magazine Capacity for 12s on Secondary Weapon
- **Rank 4:** On Melee Kill: +83.3% Reload Speed +83.3% Magazine Capacity for 12s on Secondary Weapon
- **Rank 5:** On Melee Kill: +100% Reload Speed +100% Magazine Capacity for 12s on Secondary Weapon

### Wiki
Combo Fury is a mod for Glaives that increases the reload speed and magazine capacity of secondary weapons for 12 seconds upon melee kills.
**Notes:**
Once the buff triggers, the secondary weapon must be reloaded to gain the double magazine bonus; it does not automatically double the current magazine.
Upon reloading, the extra magazine capacity is pulled from the player's secondary weapon ammo pool. If a player does not have enough reserve ammo to fully double the magazine, the magazine will use as much ammo as the player has.
When the ability ends, the surplus ammo in the magazine (above its normal capacity) is returned to the player's secondary ammo pool.
The timer for the buff is not reset if another melee kill is performed while it is active.
Although they are considered Thrown Melee weapons, this mod cannot be equipped on Deconstructor or Deconstructor Prime due to these weapons having no direct synergy with the player's secondary weapon.
**Tips:**
This mod works well with secondaries with an alt-fire that empties the magazine into a single torrent (such as the Kuva Kraken and Tenet Detron) to charge up Mag's short-tap Magnetize bubble, causing it to deal extensive damage against pulled targets and resulting in a high-damage explosion at the end of its duration.
This mod synergizes with the Cycron and its Tenet Cycron counterpart, as the increased magazine capacity allows it to sustain its beam longer and at the same time quickly recharge its ammo due to the increased reload speed. (Reload speed will not reduce the delay before recharge starts on Battery Weapons)
This mod also synergizes with the arcane, Secondary Dexterity, due to both needing melee kills to apply their buffs onto the secondary weapon.

## Combo Killer
**Rarity:** Uncommon | **Polarity:** naramon | **Drain:** 2 | **Compatible:** Thrown Melee | **Max Rank:** 5

Combo Killer is a Glaive mod that increases Combo duration and allows kills with secondary weapon to reset the combo timer.
**Notes:**
Keep in mind heavy attacks will still drain your combo multiplier, as this mod only affects the time it takes for you to entirely lose the combo multiplier you have.
**Trivia:**
For a short time after Update 22, this mod could have been acquired via Transmutation with [PH] (standing for placeholder) in the title.
Update 22.0.1 made this mod untradable.
Prior to Update 29.3.2, the conditions for resetting the combo timer required headshots.

### Wiki
Combustion Beam is a mod that causes enemies killed by primary Continuous Weapons to explode, dealing up to 600 Blast damage shortly after death.
**Notes:**
The Explosion does 600 Blast damage in an 18m radius at max rank.
Explosion has 0% Status Chance.
Explosion has 100% falloff.
Explosion requires Line of Sight.
Explosion ranges is not affected by (Primed Firestorm) Firestorm.
Explosion damage can only be increased by Faction Damage Bonus such as Bane of Infested.
Combustion Beam is only usable on primary Continuous Weapons. This includes Robotic Weapons like Artax, Cryotra, and Verglas.
A notable exception to this rule is the Tenet Flux Rifle, because it was derived from continuous weapon (Flux Rifle), even though its firing mechanics are similar to an automatic rifle.
Combustion beam essentially functions as a weaker Molecular Prime (chain reaction effect), making it effective at clearing weak low level enemies very rapidly.
Enemies who are vaporized when killed (e.g. Volatile Runner) will not activate the bonus damage effect.
Because the Panthera's secondary fire has a continuous and fixed range, Combustion Beam can also be equipped on it. Enemies killed by either the primary or secondary fire will trigger the explosion.

## Computer Cop
Stopping cyber-crime in its tracks
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** universal | **Drain:** 0 | **Compatible:** Parazon | **Max Rank:** 3

### Drop Locations

### Wiki
Concealed Explosives is a mod that gives certain thrown secondary weapons a chance of exploding on impact of a surface or an enemy.
It can be used on the following weapons:
Despair
Fusilai
Hikou (Hikou Prime)
Kunai (Mk1-Kunai)
Spira (Spira Prime)
**Notes:**
The explosion deals 250 Blast damage to the target, as well as to nearby enemies within 10 meters of the blast.
The radius can be increased with Fulmination (Primed Fulmination).
The explosion inflicts self-stagger.
The explosive damage from this mod cannot be increased with base damage, such as Hornet Strike, and elemental mods such as Heated Charge, but it will be increased by Expel mods, like Expel Infested.
Cannot be used on Castanas (Sancti Castanas), Pox, Staticor and Talons.
Can be installed on Aegrit and Cantare, but does not have any effect.
The explosions are silent.
The explosions will not damage enemies that the point of explosion does not have a line of sight to, whether it is obstructed by walls, objects, or even other enemies or their corpses.
The explosions themselves cannot proc Status Effects or crit.
The explosion damage is not affected by the Extinguished Dragon Key.
Damage cannot be increased with Mirage's Eclipse.

## Concentrated Arrow
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Ivara | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired by attaining the rank of Genius under Cephalon Suda, or the rank of Partner under the The Perrin Sequence, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Artemis Bow Augment: Fires a single arrow with +25% Base Critical Chance on full charge, additional +20% Chance and explodes in a 7m radius on Weak Points. Removes Punch Through.
- **Rank 1:** Artemis Bow Augment: Fires a single arrow with +25% Base Critical Chance on full charge, additional +30% Chance and explodes in a 7m radius on Weak Points. Removes Punch Through.
- **Rank 2:** Artemis Bow Augment: Fires a single arrow with +25% Base Critical Chance on full charge, additional +40% Chance and explodes in a 7m radius on Weak Points. Removes Punch Through.
- **Rank 3:** Artemis Bow Augment: Fires a single arrow with +25% Base Critical Chance on full charge, additional +50% Chance and explodes in a 7m radius on Weak Points. Removes Punch Through.

### Drop Locations
- Cephalon Suda, Genius — Common (100.00%)
- The Perrin Sequence, Partner — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Concentrated Arrow is a Warframe Augment Mod for Ivara's Artemis Bow that replaces Artemis Bow's multiple projectiles with a single arrow with increased Critical Chance. On weak points, the arrow has even higher critical chance and explodes. The single arrow is incapable of utilizing punch through.
**Notes:**
With the augment equipped, Artemis Bow fires only one arrow instead of the usual seven.
The single arrow has the same stats as an individual arrow without the augment, giving it 240 damage, 25% critical chance, 2.0x critical multiplier, and 20% status chance.
Charged shots have +25% base critical chance, increasing the weapon's base crit chance to 50%. This bonus is not affected by power strength.
Charging shots will not increase arrow damage, but will increase projectile speed.
Arrows are affected by Multishot but not by punch through.
Weakpoint hits (including headshots) will add a flat +50% critical chance to the weapon's final critical chance.

## Condition Overload
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** madurai | **Drain:** 10 | **Compatible:** Melee | **Max Rank:** 5

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +13.3% Melee Damage per Status Type affecting the target
- **Rank 1:** +26.7% Melee Damage per Status Type affecting the target
- **Rank 2:** +40% Melee Damage per Status Type affecting the target
- **Rank 3:** +53.3% Melee Damage per Status Type affecting the target
- **Rank 4:** +66.7% Melee Damage per Status Type affecting the target
- **Rank 5:** +80% Melee Damage per Status Type affecting the target

### Drop Locations
- Basal Diploid Rex — Legendary (0.02%)
- Basal Diploid — Legendary (0.02%)
- Deimos Carnis — Legendary (0.02%)
- Deimos Jugulus — Legendary (0.02%)
- Deimos Leaping Thrasher — Legendary (0.02%)
- Deimos Saxum — Legendary (0.02%)
- Drekar Butcher — Legendary (0.02%)
- Feral Diploid Rex — Legendary (0.02%)
- Feral Diploid — Legendary (0.02%)
- Kuva Bombard — Legendary (0.02%)
- Leaping Thrasher — Legendary (0.02%)
- Kuva Lich Agor Rok (Level 80 - 100) — Rare (0.05%)
- Duviri/Endless: Tier 6 (Normal) — Rare (1.03%)
- Duviri/Endless: Tier 4 (Normal) — Rare (1.49%)

### Wiki
Condition Overload is a melee mod that grants extra damage based on the number of unique status effects currently affecting the target.
**Notes:**
This damage does not apply to Slams and Heavy Slams.
There is no limit to the number of status effects the mod is affected by.
The bonus damage gained by this mod stacks additively with other damage mods like Pressure Point and Spoiled Strike to increase your melee damage (including proc damage like Slash). Total damage is calculated as such:
Total Damage Base Damage [1 + Damage Mods + (Condition Overload Multiplier n)] (1 + Elemental Mods) (with n being the number of unique status effects currently affecting the enemy)
The bonus damage of a new status effect is not applied to the hit which inflicted it (or its proc damage) - only subsequent strikes are amplified.
Gunblades' pellets hit consecutively and therefore can be amplified by other pellets of the same shot.
Status procs may originate from any source, including primary and secondary weapons, Warframe abilities, companions, other players, or the environment.
Impact, Puncture, Slash, Cold, Electricity, Heat, Toxin, Blast, Corrosive, Gas, Magnetic, Radiation, Viral, Void and Tau procs all count for the damage bonus. Additionally:
Lifted from Melee Heavy Attacks also counts.
Microwave from Nukor and Kuva Nukor is a unique invisible status. Microwave procs have infinite duration.
Knockdown while does not incur a visible status, still counts towards the damage bonus.
Note that conditions or abilities that prevent knockdowns (e.g. Bastille) will prevent it from increasing damage.
Since Lifted and knockdown will cancel out each other, the maximum number of status effects that can be applied to an enemy is 16.
Radial status effects (Blast, Electricity and Gas) still get the bonus damage from this mod as it is part of Modded Base Damage, and it is calculated based on the initial target, not individually per enemy hit.

## Conductive Sphere
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Gyre | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired by attaining the rank of Maxim under Arbiters of Hexis, or the rank of Partner under The Perrin Sequence, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Arcsphere Augment: Projectiles that pass through Arcsphere have guaranteed Electricity Status Effect and +15% Electricity Damage.
- **Rank 1:** Arcsphere Augment: Projectiles that pass through Arcsphere have guaranteed Electricity Status Effect and +35% Electricity Damage.
- **Rank 2:** Arcsphere Augment: Projectiles that pass through Arcsphere have guaranteed Electricity Status Effect and +55% Electricity Damage.
- **Rank 3:** Arcsphere Augment: Projectiles that pass through Arcsphere have guaranteed Electricity Status Effect and +75% Electricity Damage.

### Wiki
Conductive Sphere is a Warframe Augment Mod for Gyre's Arcsphere that causes projectiles traveled through it to inflict Electricity damage and guaranteed status effects.
**Notes:**
Grants Ability Strength75% electricity damage and Ability Strength75% electricity status damage.
Electricity damage bonus is additive with electricity damage sources, such as Stormbringer. However, unlike modded electricity damage, this bonus is not counted in the "electricity damage bonuses" portion of the status damage calculation (bug), and therefore does not add any damage to electricity statuses. See Electricity Damage for the status damage formula.
Electricity status damage bonus stacks additively with status damage sources, such as Rifle Elementalist. The bonus only applies to electricity procs, but works on both the guaranteed electric procs from this augment and natural electric procs from the weapon.
This behavior is after the U38.6 patch, which was supposed to fix the status damage calculation.
Shooting through multiple arcspheres will grant additional guaranteed electric procs and electricity damage, but will not grant additional electricity status damage.
Projectiles only need to pass through the Arcsphere's electric field in order to gain the bonus, not the arcsphere object itself.
Standing within the electric field will trigger the bonus on all shots fired.
Non-projectile (hitscan, beam, etc) weapons can trigger the effect as well, whether by shooting through the sphere or standing within it.
Visit Buffing Fields to learn more about the mechanic and targets (weapons, abilities, other) which can benefit from this mod.
Uniquely the added electricity damage is added to some explosive weapons (e.g. acceltra) but the guaranteed electric status is not.

## Conductor
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Octavia | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired by attaining the rank of Genius under the Cephalon Suda, or the rank of Flawless under the New Loka, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.
This mod can periodically be acquired from The Circuit.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Resonator Augment: Reactivate the ability to command Resonator to move to your aim point at 100% Speed.
- **Rank 1:** Resonator Augment: Reactivate the ability to command Resonator to move to your aim point at 115% Speed.
- **Rank 2:** Resonator Augment: Reactivate the ability to command Resonator to move to your aim point at 135% Speed.
- **Rank 3:** Resonator Augment: Reactivate the ability to command Resonator to move to your aim point at 150% Speed.

### Drop Locations
- Cephalon Suda, Genius — Common (100.00%)
- New Loka, Flawless — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Conductor is a Warframe Augment Mod for Octavia that allows her to command Resonator by reactivating it, ordering it to move to where her reticle is pointing at, with increased speed.
**Notes:**
Recasting costs no additional Energy, and places a Resonator-shaped marker on the ground.
Once the Ability is recast, the Resonator will stay at the marker for the remaining duration of the Ability.
Recasting the Ability a second time will move the Resonator to a new location, provided that marker is not within the radius of the previous one.
Holding down the Ability button for 1.5 seconds resets the marker, allowing Resonator to move freely once again.
Can be equipped in the Exilus slot.
The speed at which Resonator moves to the selected location is affected by Ability Strength. Thus, this means that with a negative Ability Strength, the Resonator can move slower than normal. This can be useful for "herding" groups of enemies to a specific location without moving too fast and getting Resonator out of attraction range.
**Trivia:**
Conductor is the fourth Exilus Augment mod to be released.

### Wiki
Constitution is a mod that increases Warframe Knockdown Recovery and Ability Duration of Warframe abilities.
**Notes:**
Can be stacked with Continuity (Primed Continuity) and/or Narrow Minded and/or Augur Message to further increase Ability Duration. Click here for more details.
Can be stacked with Handspring.
The Knockdown Recovery stat applies to self-stagger as well. (Does not affect the flinch animation that occurs at the edge of the explosion.)
At max rank it allows you to stand up while an Ancient or Scorpion is pulling you, allowing you to run away.

## Contagion Cloud
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Saryn | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired by attaining the rank of General under Steel Meridian, or the rank of Exalted under Red Veil, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Toxic Lash Augment: Create 5m toxic clouds, dealing 150 Toxin Damage/s for 8s with every kill. Damage is twice as strong for Melee kills.
- **Rank 1:** Toxic Lash Augment: Create 5m toxic clouds, dealing 175 Toxin Damage/s for 9s with every kill. Damage is twice as strong for Melee kills.
- **Rank 2:** Toxic Lash Augment: Create 5m toxic clouds, dealing 250 Toxin Damage/s for 10s with every kill. Damage is twice as strong for Melee kills.
- **Rank 3:** Toxic Lash Augment: Create 5m toxic clouds, dealing 300 Toxin Damage/s for 12s with every kill. Damage is twice as strong for Melee kills.

### Drop Locations
- Red Veil, Exalted — Common (100.00%)
- Steel Meridian, General — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Contagion Cloud is a Warframe Augment Mod for Saryn that causes kills made with Toxic Lash, and damage over time effects generated by it, to leave behind a poisonous cloud that deals Toxin damage over time to enemies within it. Melee kills double the damage dealt by the cloud.
**Notes:**
Cloud radius is affected by Ability Range.
Damage per second is affected by Ability Strength.
Duration is affected by Ability Duration.
Damage is unaffected by melee stealth multipliers.
**Tips:**
The Telos Boltace's suction slide attack works very well with this augment, pulling enemies into a group.

## Contagious Bond
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** madurai | **Drain:** 4 | **Compatible:** COMPANION | **Max Rank:** 5

### Acquisition
The mod can be bought from Son for 20,000 Standing after reaching Rank 3 - Associate with the Entrati.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** When your Companion kills an enemy afflicted with a Status Effect, 8% of the Status Effect spreads to other enemies within 1.5m. Max 100 stacks.
- **Rank 1:** When your Companion kills an enemy afflicted with a Status Effect, 17% of the Status Effect spreads to other enemies within 3m. Max 100 stacks.
- **Rank 2:** When your Companion kills an enemy afflicted with a Status Effect, 25% of the Status Effect spreads to other enemies within 4.5m. Max 100 stacks.
- **Rank 3:** When your Companion kills an enemy afflicted with a Status Effect, 33% of the Status Effect spreads to other enemies within 6m. Max 100 stacks.
- **Rank 4:** When your Companion kills an enemy afflicted with a Status Effect, 42% of the Status Effect spreads to other enemies within 7.5m. Max 100 stacks.
- **Rank 5:** When your Companion kills an enemy afflicted with a Status Effect, 50% of the Status Effect spreads to other enemies within 9m. Max 100 stacks.

### Drop Locations
- Entrati (Son), Associate — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Contagious Bond is a Companion mod that causes enemies killed by the companion to spread a fraction of their Status Effects to nearby enemies.
**Notes:**
At max rank, 50% of the number of status procs present on the target will be spread, rounded down.
Transferable Status Effects are capped at x100 Status Effects.
Requires 2 or more stacks of the proc for it to be spread
Status procs that are spread inherit 50% of the damage of the initial procs.

## Continuous Misery
**Rarity:** Common | **Polarity:** madurai | **Drain:** 4 | **Compatible:** Rifle | **Max Rank:** 3

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +25% Status Duration
- **Rank 1:** +50% Status Duration
- **Rank 2:** +75% Status Duration
- **Rank 3:** +100% Status Duration

### Drop Locations
- Sprag — Uncommon (0.76%)
- Ven'Kra Tel — Uncommon (0.76%)
- Deimos Undying Flyer — Uncommon (1.14%)
- Undying Flyer — Uncommon (1.14%)
- Eidolon Gantulyst — Uncommon (32.21%)
- Drekar Hellion — Uncommon (7.59%)

### Wiki
Continuous Misery is a mod that increases the status duration inflicted by rifles.
**Notes:**
The mod's effects are only visible on Status Effects with an associated duration, either increasing the duration of the associated debuff (Puncture, Cold, Magnetic, Radiation, and Viral), or the number of ticks associated with DoT effects (Slash, Toxin, Gas, and Heat).
This mod will not affect the duration of crowd control effects generated by Status Effects (staggers from Impact, the panic duration of Heat, and the stun animation caused by Electricity).
However, it does affect the duration of the freeze effect applied by Cold after reaching 10 stacks.
Increased Status Duration will slow down the Armor Strip effect of Heat. For example, with +100% status duration, the armor strip will occur every second instead of every half second. As a result, the full 50% armor strip will only be achieved after 4 seconds.
Status Effects from the radial blasts of Syndicate Weapon Augments and Syndicate Weapons are not affected by this mod.
**Tips:**
Most useful on rifles with high status chance like the Grinlok, Miter and the Amprex.
