## Coolant Leak
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** penjaga | **Drain:** 2 | **Compatible:** ROBOTIC | **Max Rank:** 3

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Unleashes a 4m radial blast of Cold when multiple enemies are nearby. Add 3 stacks. 10s cooldown.
- **Rank 1:** Unleashes a 6m radial blast of Cold when multiple enemies are nearby. Add 3 stacks. 10s cooldown.
- **Rank 2:** Unleashes a 8m radial blast of Cold when multiple enemies are nearby. Add 3 stacks. 10s cooldown.
- **Rank 3:** Unleashes a 10m radial blast of Cold when multiple enemies are nearby. Add 3 stacks. 10s cooldown.

### Drop Locations
- Orokin Drone — Legendary (0.02%)
- Orokin Spectator — Legendary (0.02%)

### Wiki
Coolant Leak is a mod for Robotic Companions to release freezing pulses, applying stacks of Cold to enemies in a radius.
**Notes:**
There must be at least 2 enemies within 10 meters for the mod to activate.
Unalerted enemies that fall under the effect of this mod will become alerted.
Cannot be used for Transmutation, although it will appear in the codex entry.
Coolant Leak cannot be traded unless the player offering the item owns more than one copy.
In Capture Missions, this mod's effect can be applied to the capture target, slowing their escape when they run away and making it easier to capture them.
**Trivia:**
Prior to Update 37, this was the first mod without rank, the second being the Legendary Core, the third being Shrapnel Rounds, the fourth being Precision Munition, and the fifth being Astral Autopsy.

## Cordon
**Rarity:** Common | **Polarity:** penjaga | **Drain:** 2 | **Compatible:** Nautilus | **Max Rank:** 5

### Acquisition
Cordon is automatically acquired upon obtaining Nautilus or Nautilus Prime.
Mechanics
When Cordon activates, it targets the closest enemy within 30m of you and creates a "pull destination" 5m away from you in the direction of the targeted enemy. All enemies within 30m of you will be pulled 15m towards that destination over the course of one second. For example, if the targeted enemy is directly north of you, then the pull destination will be 5m north of you, and all enemies within 30m will be pulled 15m towards that destination.
Does not require line of sight to trigger.
Does not alert enemies.
Can create a pull destination in midair if the target is airborne.
Enemies are continuously pulled towards the pull destination for one second, even after they've reached the destination. This means they won't continue sliding in the initial pull direction and overshoot the intended destination.
The 15m pull is affected by enemy movement speed modifiers. Slowing an enemy will reduce how far they are pulled, while speeding them up will increase it.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Nautilus forces enemies within 5m of the target into clusters for easier targeting. Cooldown: 15s.
- **Rank 1:** Nautilus forces enemies within 10m of the target into clusters for easier targeting. Cooldown: 15s.
- **Rank 2:** Nautilus forces enemies within 15m of the target into clusters for easier targeting. Cooldown: 15s.
- **Rank 3:** Nautilus forces enemies within 20m of the target into clusters for easier targeting. Cooldown: 15s.
- **Rank 4:** Nautilus forces enemies within 25m of the target into clusters for easier targeting. Cooldown: 15s.
- **Rank 5:** Nautilus forces enemies within 30m of the target into clusters for easier targeting. Cooldown: 15s.

### Wiki
Cordon is a precept mod for Nautilus and Nautilus Prime that forcibly gathers and pulls enemies into a concentrated spot. It has a 15-second cooldown for every use.

## Corrosive Projection
**Rarity:** Uncommon | **Polarity:** naramon | **Drain:** -2 | **Compatible:** AURA | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 5

### Acquisition
Available for 20 creds from the Nightwave Cred Offerings store on a rotational basis.
Armor Removal Interactions
General
This mod stacks additively with itself when multiple teammates are using it, reducing enemy armor up to 72% with a 4-player squad.
Armor reduction stacks additively with Warframe abilities that reduce armor, such as Fusion Strike and Terrify
For example, when Corrosive Projection is equipped, after a single cast of Terrify, an enemy will have 100% - 18% - 60% armor remaining.
The effectiveness of this mod is reduced under the "Augmented Enemy Armor" Sortie condition.
Currently, the only non-ability method to completely remove armor is to use Shattering Impact, Sharpened Claws or a dagger in tandem with Amalgam Argonak Metal Auger. It should be noted that using Corrosive Projection is multiplicative with these mods and thus will not cause them to strip armor faster.
Status Effects
When Corrosive Projection is combined with the status effect of Heat and Corrosive, the armor reduction is multiplicative. This can be expressed as the following:
Armor after reduction (1 - Heat status max armor reduction of 50% after 2 seconds.) [1 - (Innate Corrosive status armor reduction. + Corrosive status armor reduction of 6% per stack. Number of Corrosive stacks)] (1 - 18% armor reduction from one max-rank Corrosive Projection. Number Corrosive Projections)
For example, a full 4-player squad with max-rank Corrosive Projections, an active Heat proc, and max stacked Corrosive proc will result in an enemy's armor being reduced to:
(1 - 50%) [1 - (20% + 6% 10)] (1 - 18% 4).
If only one player has a max-rank Corrosive Projection with the above statuses, an enemy's armor would instead be reduced to:
(1 - 50%) [1 - (20% + 6% 10)] (1 - 18%).

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Enemies lose -3% Armor
- **Rank 1:** Enemies lose -6% Armor
- **Rank 2:** Enemies lose -9% Armor
- **Rank 3:** Enemies lose -12% Armor
- **Rank 4:** Enemies lose -15% Armor
- **Rank 5:** Enemies lose -18% Armor

### Drop Locations
- Duviri/Endless: Tier 1 (Normal) — Legendary (0.67%)
- Duviri/Endless: Tier 3 (Normal) — Legendary (0.67%)

### Wiki
Corrosive Projection is an aura mod that reduces armor for all enemies in a mission.
* Aura mods increase the amount of Mod Capacity

## Corrupt Charge
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** madurai | **Drain:** 4 | **Compatible:** Melee | **Max Rank:** 3

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +7.5 Initial Combo, -12.5% Combo Duration
- **Rank 1:** +15 Initial Combo, -25% Combo Duration
- **Rank 2:** +22.5 Initial Combo, -37.5% Combo Duration
- **Rank 3:** +30 Initial Combo, -50% Combo Duration

### Drop Locations
- Derelict Vault — Rare (4.17%)

### Wiki
Corrupt Charge is a Corrupted Mod that increases the initial Combo Counter of melee weapons while reducing the reset timer.
**Notes:**
Combo counter starts with 8/15/23/30 as base value, which at rank 2 is enough to start with a 2x combo multiplier.
Combines well with Killing Blow.
Decreases combo counter reset time by 12.5% / 25% / 37.5% / 50%.
Using a Heavy Attack on a melee weapon with this equipped will deplete the Combo Counter like usual, but it will then rapidly regenerate to full within 2 seconds.
The Combo Duration penalty is calculated from the melee weapon's base value. Meaning it always subtracts exactly 2.5 seconds and therefore is easily counteracted by Body Count and/or Drifting Contact.
It is possible to make a weapon's constant heavy attack speed faster than the regeneration rate, such as with a combination of Killing Blow, Amalgam Organ Shatter and one of Fury or Berserker Fury, or simply have a naturally very fast weapon.
Pairs extremely well with the Xoris, due to its innate infinite Combo Duration.

## Counter Pulse
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Mag | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
Can be acquired by attaining the rank of Partner under The Perrin Sequence, or the rank of Flawless under New Loka, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Polarize Augment: Enemy weapons are jammed and robotics are disabled for 2s when hit by Polarize.
- **Rank 1:** Polarize Augment: Enemy weapons are jammed and robotics are disabled for 2.5s when hit by Polarize.
- **Rank 2:** Polarize Augment: Enemy weapons are jammed and robotics are disabled for 3s when hit by Polarize.
- **Rank 3:** Polarize Augment: Enemy weapons are jammed and robotics are disabled for 4s when hit by Polarize.

### Drop Locations
- New Loka, Flawless — Common (100.00%)
- The Perrin Sequence, Partner — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Counter Pulse is a Warframe Augment Mod for Mag's Polarize that jams enemy weapons and disables Robotic enemies for a set duration.
**Notes:**
Duration is affected by Ability Duration.
Unlike Fracturing Crush which offers similar AoE enemy disruption, robotics and gun-wielding enemies will be unable to attack for the stun duration at the cost of having less total duration. Enemies affected by Fracturing Crush can resume using ranged attacks once they recover from the knockdown.
Affected robotic units are effectively put to sleep, making them vulnerable to stealth multipliers and standing finishers.
**Trivia:**
This mod replaced Shield Transference in Update 22.12.
Both mods, however, share the same picture on their mod cards.

**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** madurai | **Drain:** 10 | **Compatible:** Daggers | **Max Rank:** 3

### Stats by Rank

- **Rank 1:** +8 Initial Combo, +50% Finisher Damage

### Drop Locations

- Duviri/Endless: Tier 4 (Normal) — Rare (1.49%)
- Kuva Fortress/Pago (Spy), Rotation B — Uncommon (10.00%)
- Eris/Cyath (Spy), Rotation C — Rare (8.60%)
- Eris/Gnathos (Spy), Rotation C — Rare (8.60%)
- Neptune/Nereid (Spy), Rotation C — Rare (8.60%)

### Wiki
Covert Lethality is a mod that increases the base combo count of single daggers and deals double damage on finishers.
It can be used on the following weapons:
Ceramic Dagger
Dark Dagger
Heat Dagger
Innodem
Karyst (Karyst Prime)
Rakta Dark Dagger
Rumblejack
Sheev
Balla Dagger Zaw
Ash's Shadow Clones
**Notes:**
Stacks with Finishing Touch for +160% bonus finisher damage.
The "Initial Combo" bonus provided by this mod adds a flat number to the combo counter. When the combo counter is reset, either by using a heavy attack or through combo counter decay, it will reset to a minimum of this number.
Aside from Stealth attacks, the following Warframe abilities can also leave enemies vulnerable to melee finishers, regardless if the finisher is performed in front or behind the enemy;
Ash's Teleport.
Banshee's Savage Silence augment.
Savage Silence is required to make Silence open enemies to finishers, as the standard stun performed by the ability does not do so.
Baruuk's Lull
Equinox' Rest.
As any damage affected targets receive will make them fall to the ground, try to avoid attacking them if you plan to make use of Covert Lethality.
Excalibur's Radial Blind (power only, the slide attack Radial Blind from Exalted Blade does not open enemies to finishers).
Gara's passive may blind enemies when she casts an ability.
Inaros' Desiccation.
Do note that enemies are only open to melee finishers if they are facing Inaros when Desiccation is cast.
Ivara's Sleep Arrow.
Mirage's Prism.
Valkyr's Paralysis.
It is not advised to equip the Prolonged Paralysis augment, as it instead leaves enemies vulnerable to ground finishers.
Wukong's Cloud Walker.
**Trivia:**
Prior to Update 26, the mod possessed a unique mechanic called Lethal Damage, which refers to the ability to instantly kill an enemy upon performing a finisher attack regardless of their level or defenses.

## Crash Course
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** naramon | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Rifle | **Max Rank:** 5

### Acquisition
This mod is acquired by getting 1000 Points in the Operation False Profit Event.
It is now offered as a periodic item for sale by Baro Ki'Teer, costing 330 and 200,000 to purchase. Note that Baro Ki'Teer's stock changes with each appearance, and this modification may not be available on a succeeding appearance.
After the addition of the Plains of Eidolon, the mod may also obtainable by killing Eidolon Teralyst.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +20% Impact
- **Rank 1:** +40% Impact
- **Rank 2:** +60% Impact
- **Rank 3:** +80% Impact
- **Rank 4:** +100% Impact
- **Rank 5:** +120% Impact

### Drop Locations
- Eidolon Teralyst — Rare (8.33%)

### Wiki
The Crash Course mod increases Impact damage of rifles by 20% per rank for a maximum of 120% at rank 5.
**Notes:**
Can be combined with Rupture to provide a 210% increase to Impact damage.
When calculating total raw damage, a weapon will require an Impact damage percentage equal to 75% of its total base damage to equal the raw damage increase provided by a standard elemental mod (which increases corresponding elemental damages by 90%).
The higher the Impact damage percentage over 75%, the more viable this mod will be as opposed to an elemental mod, and vice versa for lower values.
These calculations only pertain to raw damage, and do not take into consideration enemy armor/flesh/shield damage multipliers.

Crash Shot is a Conclave exclusive shotgun mod that converts an amount of physical damage to Impact damage.

A weapon may only have one damage conversion mod equipped at a time.
**Trivia:**
The Excalibur, Nova, and Nyx in the picture are the same ones from their respective mod pictures (Ripper Rounds and Meteor Munitions).

## Creeping Bullseye
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** naramon | **Drain:** 4 | **Compatible:** Pistol | **Max Rank:** 5

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +33.3% Critical Chance, -3.3% Fire Rate
- **Rank 1:** +66.7% Critical Chance, -6.7% Fire Rate
- **Rank 2:** +100% Critical Chance, -10% Fire Rate
- **Rank 3:** +133.3% Critical Chance, -13.3% Fire Rate
- **Rank 4:** +166.7% Critical Chance, -16.7% Fire Rate
- **Rank 5:** +200% Critical Chance, -20% Fire Rate

### Drop Locations
- Derelict Vault — Rare (4.17%)

### Wiki
Creeping Bullseye is a Corrupted Mod for pistols that increases critical chance but reduces fire rate.
**Notes:**
Cannot be equipped simultaneously with Pistol Gambit or Primed Pistol Gambit.
**Tips:**
This fire rate penalty of this mod can be easily counteracted with Lethal Torrent, resulting in a 40% increase in fire rate. It can also be completely nullified by Semi-Pistol Cannonade on compatible weapons, resulting in a higher critical chance than Primed Pistol Gambit with functionally no downside.
The fire rate penalty can also be useful on weapons which already have a high fire rate and decent critical chance if the user does not want to fire in bursts to conserve ammunition, making the addition of Lethal Torrent on said weapon builds more viable.
The fire rate penalty supports Hemorrhage's low fire rate bonus.

## Creeping Terrify
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Nekros | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired by attaining the rank of Partner under The Perrin Sequence, or the rank of Exalted under Red Veil, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Terrify Augment: Affected enemies have 30% reduced Movement Speed.
- **Rank 1:** Terrify Augment: Affected enemies have 40% reduced Movement Speed.
- **Rank 2:** Terrify Augment: Affected enemies have 50% reduced Movement Speed.
- **Rank 3:** Terrify Augment: Affected enemies have 60% reduced Movement Speed.

### Drop Locations
- Red Veil, Exalted — Common (100.00%)
- The Perrin Sequence, Partner — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Creeping Terrify is a Warframe Augment Mod for Nekros' Terrify that slows down affected enemies.
**Notes:**
Speed reduction is affected by Ability Strength.
Cannot exceed 80%.
Maximum speed reduction is achievable with at least +34% Ability Strength at max rank.
The mod can be compared to Molecular Prime, as it slows enemies and compensates for its lack of a double damage modifier on affected targets with the ability's removal of enemy armor, making it more effective against armored units.
**Tips:**
This mod encourages players to build their Nekros for range and strength, which already fits well on players who have builds focused on Shadows of the Dead.

## Crescent Charge
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** penjaga | **Drain:** 0 | **Compatible:** Crescent Vulpaphyla | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
Crescent Charge is automatically acquired upon obtaining a Crescent Vulpaphyla.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Charges an enemy within 25m dealing Puncture Damage and lifting them. While lifted, enemy is flung at nearby foes dealing an additional 5% of the thrown enemy's health and adding 5 Puncture Status to enemies within 5m.
- **Rank 1:** Charges an enemy within 25m dealing Puncture Damage and lifting them. While lifted, enemy is flung at nearby foes dealing an additional 10% of the thrown enemy's health and adding 5 Puncture Status to enemies within 5m.
- **Rank 2:** Charges an enemy within 25m dealing Puncture Damage and lifting them. While lifted, enemy is flung at nearby foes dealing an additional 15% of the thrown enemy's health and adding 5 Puncture Status to enemies within 5m.
- **Rank 3:** Charges an enemy within 25m dealing Puncture Damage and lifting them. While lifted, enemy is flung at nearby foes dealing an additional 20% of the thrown enemy's health and adding 5 Puncture Status to enemies within 5m.

### Wiki
Crescent Charge is a precept mod for the Crescent Vulpaphyla to charge a single enemy within 25m, dealing Puncture damage and proccing Lifted. The lifted enemy is then thrown towards other enemies, dealing a percentage of the lifted enemy's health and applying Puncture stacks.
**Notes:**
Duration of the lift effect is generally less than 4 seconds.

## Crescent Devolution
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** penjaga | **Drain:** 0 | **Compatible:** Crescent Vulpaphyla | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
Crescent Devolution is automatically acquired upon obtaining a Crescent Vulpaphyla.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Devolves into its larval form when downed and charges at enemies, dealing 25 Puncture Damage. Regains true form after 30s.
- **Rank 1:** Devolves into its larval form when downed and charges at enemies, dealing 50 Puncture Damage. Regains true form after 30s.
- **Rank 2:** Devolves into its larval form when downed and charges at enemies, dealing 75 Puncture Damage. Regains true form after 30s.
- **Rank 3:** Devolves into its larval form when downed and charges at enemies, dealing 100 Puncture Damage. Regains true form after 30s.

### Wiki
Crescent Devolution is a precept mod for the Crescent Vulpaphyla to devolve into its larval form upon death and charge at enemies, dealing Puncture damage. The Vulpaphyla will respawn after 30 seconds.
**Notes:**
The larval form floats beside the owner, similar to a Sentinel.
Devolution prevents bleedout and thus the Vulpaphyla cannot be revived normally. Its transformation also cannot be sped up.
The larva will launch itself at enemies, then return to their owner's side after 5 seconds
Has a cooldown of ~8 seconds starting after it returns to the owner's side. The larva has been observed to use this ability a maximum of 3 times.
Guaranteed Impact status on the target. Sometimes applies Viral, exact conditions unknown. Source may an area around where the larval form hits the ground and explodes.
Damage is affected by Maul, but not elemental mods.

## Critical Delay
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** naramon | **Drain:** 4 | **Compatible:** Rifle | **Max Rank:** 5

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +33.3% Critical Chance, -3.3% Fire Rate (x2 for Bows)
- **Rank 1:** +66.7% Critical Chance, -6.7% Fire Rate (x2 for Bows)
- **Rank 2:** +100% Critical Chance, -10% Fire Rate (x2 for Bows)
- **Rank 3:** +133.3% Critical Chance, -13.3% Fire Rate (x2 for Bows)
- **Rank 4:** +166.7% Critical Chance, -16.6% Fire Rate (x2 for Bows)
- **Rank 5:** +200% Critical Chance, -20% Fire Rate (x2 for Bows)

### Drop Locations
- Derelict Vault — Rare (4.17%)

### Wiki
Critical Delay is a Corrupted Mod for rifles that increases critical chance but reduces fire rate.
**Notes:**
Cannot be equipped simultaneously with Point Strike.
**Tips:**
The choice between this mod and Point Strike depends greatly on the weapon.
If the fire rate penalty is offset by using fire rate increasing mods such as Speed Trigger, the resulting gain in fire rate (or net loss in fire rate, if Shred is used) may be enough to justify the opportunity cost of two mod slots because of the increased critical chance bonus, particularly so if no fire rate increasing mod would have been used in the absence of this mod.
The fire rate penalty is most severe on weapons with a charge trigger such as Lanka (both lengthening charge time and interval between shots), and bows (on which the negative property of the mod is doubled).
The fire rate penalty is fairly severe on automatic weapons, leading to less of a DPS increase than offered by Point Strike, even on those weapons which the most benefit from the critical chance bonus: Synapse, Amprex.
The fire rate penalty is least severe on non-charge trigger weapons with a magazine size of 1, where it is practically unnoticeable (e.g. Tonkor), and is experienced as being relatively mild on weapons which normally require adjusting aim between shots (e.g. Vectis, Vulkar, Dex Sybaris).
On such weapons, players may try using this mod alongside other critical chance mods to maximize critical chance, while practically ignoring the fire rate penalty.

## Critical Focus
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** madurai | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Archgun | **Max Rank:** 5

### Stats by Rank

- **Rank 1:** +20% Critical Chance and Damage when Aiming

Critical Focus is a mod that increases the critical chance and critical damage of an Archgun while aiming.
**Notes:**
Critical Focus is additive with similar mods. For example, an Imperator with a max rank Critical Focus and Parallax Scope will have a 24% (1 + 60% + 100%) critical chance.
Because you cannot Zoom while using Voidrig's Guard Mode, this mod has no effect when equipped on Arquebex.

## Critical Mutation
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** madurai | **Drain:** 2 | **Compatible:** Catabolyst | **Max Rank:** 5

### Acquisition
Available for 20 from the Nightwave Cred Offerings store on a rotational basis.
Critical Mutation was originally acquired by reaching Rank 13 in Nightwave during Nora's Mix Volume 5.
Beginning with Nightwave Nora's Mix: Vol. 9, it was added to the Nightwave Cred Offerings.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Each kill increases Critical Chance and Critical Damage by 5% up to 300%. Reduce by 5% when fewer than 3 enemies are struck by the grenade explosion.
- **Rank 1:** Each kill increases Critical Chance and Critical Damage by 10% up to 300%. Reduce by 10% when fewer than 3 enemies are struck by the grenade explosion.
- **Rank 2:** Each kill increases Critical Chance and Critical Damage by 15% up to 300%. Reduce by 15% when fewer than 3 enemies are struck by the grenade explosion.
- **Rank 3:** Each kill increases Critical Chance and Critical Damage by 20% up to 300%. Reduce by 20% when fewer than 3 enemies are struck by the grenade explosion.
- **Rank 4:** Each kill increases Critical Chance and Critical Damage by 25% up to 300%. Reduce by 25% when fewer than 3 enemies are struck by the grenade explosion.
- **Rank 5:** Each kill increases Critical Chance and Critical Damage by 30% up to 300%. Reduce by 30% when fewer than 3 enemies are struck by the grenade explosion.

### Wiki
Critical Mutation is a Weapon Augment Mod for the Catabolyst and Coda Catabolyst where each target eliminated increases Critical Chance and Critical Multiplier, to a maximum of 300%. The bonus is reduced by 30% everytime if the grenade hits less than 3 enemies.
**Notes:**
Critical Chance bonus is additive with mods such as Pistol Gambit
Critical Damage bonus is additive with mods such as Target Cracker.
Bonus Critical Chance and Critical Damage remain if the weapon is reloaded by holster mods such as Eject Magazine or the Synth Mods Set
Starting the reload animation and then interrupting it, such as by rolling, the grenade can still be thrown and it will not reload the weapon but it will still reduce the bonus by 30% if the grenade doesn't hit at least 3 enemies.
**Tips:**
Reducing the magazine capacity of the weapon with a Riven Mod can allow the weapon to reach its maximum strength grenade sooner with little downside.

## Critical Precision
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** madurai | **Drain:** 2 | **Compatible:** Tiberon | **Max Rank:** 5

### Acquisition
Available for 20 from the Nightwave Cred Offerings store on a rotational basis.
Critical Precision was originally acquired by reaching Rank 13 in Nightwave during Nora's Mix Volume 2.
Beginning with Nightwave Nora's Mix: Vol. 7, it was added to the Nightwave Cred Offerings.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Burst Fire Only: Headshots increase Critical Chance by 2.5% up to 500%. Missing with all shots in a burst removes up to 100% of this bonus Critical Chance.
- **Rank 1:** Burst Fire Only: Headshots increase Critical Chance by 4% up to 500%. Missing with all shots in a burst removes up to 100% of this bonus Critical Chance.
- **Rank 2:** Burst Fire Only: Headshots increase Critical Chance by 5.5% up to 500%. Missing with all shots in a burst removes up to 100% of this bonus Critical Chance.
- **Rank 3:** Burst Fire Only: Headshots increase Critical Chance by 7% up to 500%. Missing with all shots in a burst removes up to 100% of this bonus Critical Chance.
- **Rank 4:** Burst Fire Only: Headshots increase Critical Chance by 8.5% up to 500%. Missing with all shots in a burst removes up to 100% of this bonus Critical Chance.
- **Rank 5:** Burst Fire Only: Headshots increase Critical Chance by 10% up to 500%. Missing with all shots in a burst removes up to 100% of this bonus Critical Chance.

### Wiki
Critical Precision is a Weapon Augment Mod for Tiberon and Tiberon Prime that increases Critical Chance upon a headshot in burst-fire mode. Loses 100% of Critical Chance bonus when missing with all shots in a burst.
**Notes:**
Bonus is additive with similar buff such as Point Strike and Critical Delay.
Body shots do not deduct the critical bonus, and only one bullet from a single burst has to strike an enemy in order to keep the critical bonus. Only missing every shot will decrease the bonus by 100%.
For example, Missing a single burst while at a 500% bonus reduce it to 400%.
Bullets that hit enemy corpses are not considered missed shots, however shooting the heads of corpses will not increase the critical bonus.
The bonus is added individually for each projectile which strikes the target's head. Equipping multishot mods can therefore allow the bonus to climb significantly more quickly.
If using the Tiberon Prime, switching fire mode to either Semi or Automatic will immediately remove the bonus in its entirety.
The bonus is NOT lost when switching to or attacking with any other weapon or equipment, such as secondary or melee weapons, archguns, fishing spears, tranquilizer rifles, mining lasers or codex scanners.
**Tips:**
Critical Precision provides a significantly higher potential bonus to the Tiberon's Critical Chance than any other non-Riven mod. However, as this bonus is dependent upon consistent headshots, it is recommended to pair it with a Warframe whose abilities can make it easier to land headshots.

## Critical Surge
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Wisp | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired by attaining the rank of Genius under Cephalon Suda, or the rank of Flawless under New Loka, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Breach Surge Augment: Teleporting to a Reservoir costs 50% Energy and grants 2.5% Critical Chance to Primary Weapons per meter traveled for 9s. Maximum 250% Critical Chance.
- **Rank 1:** Breach Surge Augment: Teleporting to a Reservoir costs 50% Energy and grants 5% Critical Chance to Primary Weapons per meter traveled for 9s. Maximum 250% Critical Chance.
- **Rank 2:** Breach Surge Augment: Teleporting to a Reservoir costs 50% Energy and grants 7.5% Critical Chance to Primary Weapons per meter traveled for 9s. Maximum 250% Critical Chance.
- **Rank 3:** Breach Surge Augment: Teleporting to a Reservoir costs 50% Energy and grants 10% Critical Chance to Primary Weapons per meter traveled for 9s. Maximum 250% Critical Chance.

### Drop Locations
- Cephalon Suda, Genius — Common (100.00%)
- New Loka, Flawless — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Critical Surge is an Warframe Augment Mod for Wisp's Breach Surge that halves its energy cost and grants her additional primary weapon critical chance based on distance traveled, up to 250% critical chance upon teleporting to her Reservoirs.
**Notes:**
Critical chance per meter traveled is affected by Ability Strength.
Additively stacks with critical chance bonuses such as Point Strike.
Duration is affected by Ability Duration.
Max critical chance is not affected by mods.
Energy cost reduction is applied after efficiency mods, is not affected by Ability Strength, and is only applied when used on a Reservoir; if Breach Surge says it costs 20 energy, it'll cost 10.
Wisp cannot teleport to a pod while within 10 meters of it. Functionally, this means the minimum critical bonus is 100%.

Crushing Ruin is a stance mod for Hammer type weapons. Wielders of this Stance thrust in strong whirlwind attacks.
**Notes:**
Many of the combos do bonus Impact damage.
The first and last attacks of Raging Whirlwind do 50% bonus Impact damage.
The third attack of Shattered Village does 50% bonus Impact damage.

### Wiki
Cryo Coating is a mod that increases Cold damage and status chance on Archmelee weapons by 15% per rank for a maximum of 60% at rank 3.
**Notes:**
Cryo Coating is additive with similar mods. For example, a Kaszas with a max rank Cryo Coating and Sudden Impact will have a 15% (1 + 60% + 60%) status chance.
Can be stacked with Glacial Edge for a total of 180% Cold damage.

## Cunning Drift
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** madurai | **Drain:** 4 | **Compatible:** WARFRAME | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 5

### Acquisition
Cunning Drift is rewarded upon completing the Cunning Test in the Orokin Moon.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +2% Slide, -5% Friction, +2.5% Ability Range
- **Rank 1:** +4% Slide, -10% Friction, +5% Ability Range
- **Rank 2:** +6% Slide, -15% Friction, +7.5% Ability Range
- **Rank 3:** +8% Slide, -20% Friction, +10% Ability Range
- **Rank 4:** +10% Slide, -25% Friction, +12.5% Ability Range
- **Rank 5:** +12% Slide, -30% Friction, +15% Ability Range

### Wiki
Cunning Drift is a rare triple-stat Exilus mod that increases slide, reduces friction and increases Ability Range.
**Notes:**
Slide may possibly refer to the slide's speed, while Friction may refer to its deceleration over time.
Can be combined with Maglev and Streamlined Form for a total of 72% increase in slide speed and 90% decrease in friction.
The mod has a hidden stat where it provides an equal but negative slide effect when the player is in mid-air, cancelling out the slide bonus when players are sliding in mid-air (jump kicking).

## Cyclone Kraken
Brutal strikes with deft movement.
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** -2 | **Compatible:** Machetes | **Max Rank:** 3

### Drop Locations
- Kuva Fortress/Dakata (Caches), Rotation C — Uncommon (10.79%)

### Wiki
Cyclone Kraken is a stance mod for Machete-type weapons.
**Notes:**
Many of the attacks have physical damage (Impact, Puncture, and Slash damage) multipliers.
The first and last attack of Gale Triton have a 1.5x physical damage multiplier.
Every attack of Leviathan Rain has a 1.5x physical damage multiplier.
The second and third attack of Thunder Hydra have a 1.5x physical damage multiplier.
Certain attacks have status chance multipliers.
The first and second attacks of Gale Triton have 1.5x status chance multiplier.
The second and third attack of Thunder Hydra have 1.5x status chance multiplier.
**Trivia:**
The name of this stance and its signature combo names are based on mythical creatures of the sea.
Kraken is a gigantic sea monster that resembles a giant squid.
Triton is a Greek god of the sea, and also a generic term for a merman.
Leviathan is a sea serpent.
Siren is a sea creature that bewitches sailors.
Hydra is a multi-headed sea monster.

## Damage Decoy
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Loki | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired by attaining the rank of Maxim under Arbiters of Hexis, or the rank of Exalted under Red Veil, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Decoy Augment: Decoy can be cast on enemies. Enemies who attack that decoy receive 5 random Status Effects, and the reflected damage is increased by 200%.
- **Rank 1:** Decoy Augment: Decoy can be cast on enemies. Enemies who attack that decoy receive 5 random Status Effects, and the reflected damage is increased by 250%.
- **Rank 2:** Decoy Augment: Decoy can be cast on enemies. Enemies who attack that decoy receive 5 random Status Effects, and the reflected damage is increased by 300%.
- **Rank 3:** Decoy Augment: Decoy can be cast on enemies. Enemies who attack that decoy receive 5 random Status Effects, and the reflected damage is increased by 350%.

### Drop Locations
- Arbiters of Hexis, Maxim — Common (100.00%)
- Red Veil, Exalted — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Damage Decoy is a Warframe Augment Mod for Loki's Decoy ability that allows it to be cast on an enemy, turning that enemy hostile to everything and inciting former allies to attack them, while having their incoming damage reflected, amplified, and additionally succumbed to 5 random status effects.
**Notes:**
Has no effect if placing decoy normally, as this augment only affects the new enemy interaction.
Reflected damage is affected by Ability Strength.
The enemy will pulse with holographic energy identical to normal Decoy.
The enemy will still be hostile to the player, and can be injured or killed by players.
As such their damage output cannot be buffed by players in any way except for attack speed increases through Rest & Rage or speeding up enemies withMolecular Prime.
The enemy's health and shields will be increased by Ability Strength15% of all enemy shields and health within Ability Range15 meters of itself when cast, similar to a normal decoy.
If shielded enemies are in the area of effect, this will give shields to enemy types that typically do not have shields.
Because many status effects stun enemies (such as Impact or Heat) the damage reflected is limited and somewhat inconsistent since enemies that are stunned won't attack during their stun animation.
If an enemy randomly receives Radiation from this effect, it will draw aggro away from the Decoyed enemy, causing nearby enemies to attack the irradiated target instead and stopping them from receiving status or damage redirection from them.

## Damzav-Vati
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** madurai | **Drain:** 2 | **Compatible:** Akbronco Prime | **Max Rank:** 5

### Acquisition
Damzav-Vati can be obtained by reaching Rank 4 - Friend with the Entrati and purchasing it for 20,000 Standing from Father.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +40% Viral
- **Rank 1:** +80% Viral
- **Rank 2:** +120% Viral
- **Rank 3:** +160% Viral
- **Rank 4:** +200% Viral
- **Rank 5:** +240% Viral

### Drop Locations
- Entrati (Father), Friend — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Damzav-Vati is a Weapon Augment Mod for Akbronco Prime that adds Viral damage.
**Notes:**
The Viral damage from this mod is added after other modded elements, allowing combinations that otherwise are only possible via innate damage types.
For example, Pathogen Rounds will not combine with the augment and instead adds Toxin damage.
**Trivia:**
This is the first non-Riven mod that innately provides a secondary elemental damage type.
The name is likely of Sanskrit origin, with Damzav, possibly shortened from Damsavisa (दंशविष) meaning venom, and Vati (वाति) possibly meaning to blow, injure, or hurt someone.

## Dark Propagation
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Sevagoth | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired by attaining the rank of Genius under Cephalon Suda, or the rank of Partner under The Perrin Sequence, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.
The spread Sow debuff uses Sow's currently remaining duration, not a fresh timer.
When casting Death's Harvest via Sevagoth's Shadow, the damage over time effect and duration are determined by the Ability Strength and Ability Duration of the Exalted Shadow, while the spreading range scales solely by the Ability Range on Sevagoth.
The 15m radius scales with range mods.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Sow Augment: Enemies killed while inflicted with Sow spread its effect in a 9m radius. Shadow's Death Harvest also inflicts Sow.
- **Rank 1:** Sow Augment: Enemies killed while inflicted with Sow spread its effect in a 11m radius. Shadow's Death Harvest also inflicts Sow.
- **Rank 2:** Sow Augment: Enemies killed while inflicted with Sow spread its effect in a 13m radius. Shadow's Death Harvest also inflicts Sow.
- **Rank 3:** Sow Augment: Enemies killed while inflicted with Sow spread its effect in a 15m radius. Shadow's Death Harvest also inflicts Sow.

### Wiki
Dark Propagation is a Warframe Augment Mod for Sevagoth's Sow that spreads its effects when affected enemies die. Additionally, Death's Harvest gains the ability to inflict Sow.
**Tips:**
Due to the synergy between Sow and Reap, using Shadow Haze may be advised

## Dead Eye
**Rarity:** Uncommon | **Polarity:** madurai | **Drain:** -2 | **Compatible:** AURA | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 5

### Acquisition
Available for 20 creds from the Nightwave Cred Offerings store on a rotational basis.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Squad receives +8.8% Sniper Rifle Damage
- **Rank 1:** Squad receives +17.5% Sniper Rifle Damage
- **Rank 2:** Squad receives +26.2% Sniper Rifle Damage
- **Rank 3:** Squad receives +35% Sniper Rifle Damage
- **Rank 4:** Squad receives +43.8% Sniper Rifle Damage
- **Rank 5:** Squad receives +52.5% Sniper Rifle Damage

### Wiki
Dead Eye is an aura mod that increases the damage dealt with sniper rifles by 8.75% per rank, up to 52.5% at maximum.
* Aura mods increase the amount of Mod Capacity
**Notes:**
The damage provided by this mod stacks additively with other damage mods, e.g. Serration.
When first released by mistake, it gave 10% bonus damage per rank for a total of 60% damage at max rank. This was balanced to 5% per level for a total of 20% at max rank for its official release and changed to its current stats in Update 14.9.
The values displayed are rounded to one decimal place, making the increase per rank look inconsistent. A similar case is seen in all the normal punch through mods which increase by 0.35 per rank.
This mod only affects actual sniper rifles and no other weapon type. Even though bows and launchers draw from the sniper ammo pool, they are not affected by this mod.
**Trivia:**
For a short time after Update 10.6 this mod could have been acquired via Transmutation. This was due to a bug where all mods, including unreleased ones such as this one, became obtainable through transmutation. This has since been fixed, but players who obtained the mod this way were allowed to keep it.
Dead Eye mods obtained through transmutation before its release in Update 12 cannot be traded.

## Deadly Maneuvers
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** naramon | **Drain:** 4 | **Compatible:** Magnus | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
Available for 20 from the Nightwave Cred Offerings store on a rotational basis.
Deadly Maneuvers was originally acquired by reaching Rank 7 in Nightwave during Series 3.
Beginning with Nightwave Nora's Mix: Vol. 6, it was added to the Nightwave Cred Offerings.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** On Dodge, gain 100% Headshot Critical Chance for your next 2 shots.
- **Rank 1:** On Dodge, gain 200% Headshot Critical Chance for your next 2 shots.
- **Rank 2:** On Dodge, gain 300% Headshot Critical Chance for your next 2 shots.
- **Rank 3:** On Dodge, gain 400% Headshot Critical Chance for your next 2 shots.

### Wiki
Deadly Maneuvers is an weapon augment mod for Magnus and Magnus Prime that increases its critical chance on headshots for the next two shots after dodging.
**Notes:**
Dodging includes rolling, side dodging, and back flipping.
Merulina's dash is not included.
The critical chance is multiplicatively applied after mods on the Magnus (Magnus Prime). For example, with Primed Pistol Gambit installed the Magnus will have a total crit chance of 63.1%, and when under the effects of Deadly Maneuvers a headshot will have a critical chance of 315.5%.
Covenant's crit chance is added after Deadly Maneuvers is applied, unlike other Absolute Bonuses from Cat's Eye, Arcane Avenger and orbs from Mutalist Quanta and Simulor (Synoid Simulor).
Total Crit Chance Base Crit Chance ( (1 + Relative Bonus) + Absolute Bonus) (1 + Deadly Maneuvers) + Covenant

## Deceptive Bond
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Loki | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired by attaining the rank of Tempest in Conclave and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.
Beginning in Series 3, this augment can be purchased as a Nightwave Cred Offering for 20.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Decoy Augment: 20% of damage Loki takes is transferred to Decoy, and vice versa.
- **Rank 1:** Decoy Augment: 30% of damage Loki takes is transferred to Decoy, and vice versa.
- **Rank 2:** Decoy Augment: 45% of damage Loki takes is transferred to Decoy, and vice versa.
- **Rank 3:** Decoy Augment: 50% of damage Loki takes is transferred to Decoy, and vice versa.

### Drop Locations
- Conclave, Tempest — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Deceptive Bond is a Warframe Augment Mod for Loki's Decoy in PvE and Conclave that redirects damage dealt to Loki to the decoy, and vice versa.
**Notes:**
Damage transfer percentage is affected by Ability Strength. With 200% Strength or more, all damage will be redirected.
Damage transfer range has a range of 15 meters and is unaffected by Ability Range.
Whenever damage is being transferred, a tether similar to Link is formed between the Loki and Decoy.
Damage transferred from Decoy to Loki interacts normally with effects such as Adaptation, Rage or Quick Thinking.
However, with 200% Ability Strength, Decoy can be kept alive under enemy fire for as long as Loki can sustain their own health and shields.
Damage reduction abilities can be applied to the Decoy (i.e. Splinter Storm) however it will not double dip with the player's own DR, because damage redirection happens before damage reduction.
This mod is likely to be detrimental, given that Decoy is designed to attract enemy fire and can generate a health pool larger than the player's.

## Decisive Judgement
Fierce, double-handed strikes.

### Wiki
Decisive Judgement is a stance mod for Nikanas, which features two-handed slashes that can send the user airborne.

Decisive Judgement appears to be based off of the Kenjutsu style of swordplay.

## Defiled Snapdragon
Sudden lashes, lethal slashes.
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** madurai | **Drain:** -2 | **Compatible:** Blade And Whip | **Max Rank:** 3

### Drop Locations
- Basal Diploid Rex — Legendary (0.02%)
- Basal Diploid — Legendary (0.02%)
- Deimos Carnis — Legendary (0.02%)
- Deimos Jugulus — Legendary (0.02%)
- Deimos Leaping Thrasher — Legendary (0.02%)
- Deimos Saxum — Legendary (0.02%)
- Feral Diploid Rex — Legendary (0.02%)
- Feral Diploid — Legendary (0.02%)
- Leaping Thrasher — Legendary (0.02%)
- Kuva Scorpion — Rare (0.03%)
- Narmer Scorpion — Rare (0.03%)
- Scorpion — Rare (0.03%)
- Kuva Lich Agor Rok (Level 80 - 100) — Rare (0.05%)

### Wiki
Defiled Snapdragon is a stance mod for Blade and Whip type weapons.
Defiled Snapdragon
**Trivia:**
This is the only stance mod with a rhyming description.
The name of this stance, as well as its associated combos all reference dragons in some form;
Snapdragons are a genus of plants whose flowers are said to resemble the head of a dragon.
Drake is a synonym for dragon, and is derived from Draco, the Latin word for dragon.
Naga is a group of serpent deities associated with Hinduism and Buddhism.
Lindwurm is the German name for Lindworm, which is typically depicted as a bipedal, wingless dragon.
The Leviathan is a sea monster (commonly called a "sea serpent") referenced in Christianity and Judaism. The word has since become synonymous with any large sea creature such as whales.
Rolling Thunder was previously named Wings of the Wyvern. Wyverns are mythological creatures commonly mistaken for dragons, with the key difference being that wyverns have two legs, whereas dragons normally have four.
Soul of the Leviathan's ending combo looks similar to Swirling Tiger's Winding Claws

## Deft Tempo

- **Rank 3:** On Headshot: +10% Mobility for 4s

### Drop Locations
- Neptune/Cephalon Capture (Conclave), Rotation A — Rare (2.48%)
- Saturn/Annihilation (Conclave), Rotation A — Rare (2.48%)
- Saturn/Cephalon Capture (Conclave), Rotation A — Rare (2.48%)

### Wiki
Deft Tempo is Conclave-exclusive assault rifle mod which increases Mobility on headshots. It is no longer usable with launcher weapons since Update 18.5.
**Notes:**
Despite the description mentioning Mobility, it only affects Bullet Jump speed.
Headshot Kills are not necessary to activate this mod's effects; a simple tap on an opponents head will suffice.
Only Rifles can use this mod. Bows, Snipers, Shotguns, and Launchers do not use it.

## Depleted Reload
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** naramon | **Drain:** 2 | **Compatible:** Sniper | **Max Rank:** 5

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** -10% Magazine Capacity, +8% Reload Speed
- **Rank 1:** -20% Magazine Capacity, +16% Reload Speed
- **Rank 2:** -30% Magazine Capacity, +24% Reload Speed
- **Rank 3:** -40% Magazine Capacity, +32% Reload Speed
- **Rank 4:** -50% Magazine Capacity, +40% Reload Speed
- **Rank 5:** -60% Magazine Capacity, +48% Reload Speed

### Drop Locations
- Derelict Vault — Rare (4.17%)

### Wiki
Depleted Reload is a Corrupted sniper rifle mod that decreases magazine capacity and increases reload speed.
**Notes:**
For any non-integer magazine values the magazine size is simply rounded to the nearest whole number, and can not be reduce to 0. For example, a Vectis Prime modded with a max rank depleted Reload will have a calculated magazine size of 2 (1 + -60%), which will simply round to 1.
Using specifically with Vectis Prime allows better synergy with Charged Chamber or Primed Chamber.
The calculation for reload time using reload speed is:Base Reload Time (1 + Reload Speed Bonus)
This mod can be combined with Primed Fast Hands for a reload speed increase of 103%.
**Trivia:**
When this mod was first introduced, equipping it on the Vectis would reduce the magazine size to zero, causing the Vectis to draw directly from its ammo reserves and allowing it to fire very quickly. This was fixed in Update 17.0.3; now the Vectis is the only sniper rifle unaffected by the mod's penalty.
Was initially named "Reverse Tainted Mag" during the concept phase.
This is the only mod that reduces magazine size other than Riven Mods
DE:Erschöpftes Nachladen

## Desiccation's Curse
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Inaros | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired by attaining the rank of Maxim under Arbiters of Hexis, or the rank of Partner under The Perrin Sequence, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Desiccation Augment: Killing a blinded enemy with a Finisher has a 35% chance to summon a Swarm Kavat that will spread Scarab Swarm. Maximum Swarm Kavats +2.
- **Rank 1:** Desiccation Augment: Killing a blinded enemy with a Finisher has a 50% chance to summon a Swarm Kavat that will spread Scarab Swarm. Maximum Swarm Kavats +2.
- **Rank 2:** Desiccation Augment: Killing a blinded enemy with a Finisher has a 70% chance to summon a Swarm Kavat that will spread Scarab Swarm. Maximum Swarm Kavats +2.
- **Rank 3:** Desiccation Augment: Killing a blinded enemy with a Finisher has a 100% chance to summon a Swarm Kavat that will spread Scarab Swarm. Maximum Swarm Kavats +2.

### Drop Locations
- Arbiters of Hexis, Maxim — Common (100.00%)
- The Perrin Sequence, Partner — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Desiccation's Curse is a Warframe Augment Mod for Inaros's Desiccation ability that has a chance to summon a Scarab Swarm when killing a blinded enemy with a Finisher. The augment also increases the maximum limit of Swarm Kavats by 2.
**Notes:**
Maximum Swarm Kavat limit is increased to 5.
The augment functions normally when Desiccation is infused onto other Warframes, allowing them to summon up to 5 Swarm Kavats. However, any kills from the Swarm Kavats will not grant any affinity.
Swarm Kavats can only be spawned from enemies opened to finishers via Desiccation itself or when hit by a Swarm Kavat's Scarab Swarm.
Swarm Kavats are counted as summons and are affected by Summoner's Wrath, Arcane Camisado, and Theorem Infection.
**Trivia:**
Despite the change in this augment's function in Update 35.5, the image depicted still depicts a Sand Shadow, a sandy duplicate of a previously killed enemy, which Inaros can no longer create due to Swarm Kavats replacing them.

## Despoil
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Nekros | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
Can be acquired by attaining the rank of Partner under The Perrin Sequence, or the rank of Exalted under Red Veil, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Desecrate Augment: No longer consumes Energy, but consumes 16 Health per corpse instead.
- **Rank 1:** Desecrate Augment: No longer consumes Energy, but consumes 14 Health per corpse instead.
- **Rank 2:** Desecrate Augment: No longer consumes Energy, but consumes 12 Health per corpse instead.
- **Rank 3:** Desecrate Augment: No longer consumes Energy, but consumes 10 Health per corpse instead.

### Drop Locations
- Red Veil, Exalted — Common (100.00%)
- The Perrin Sequence, Partner — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Despoil is a Warframe Augment Mod for Nekros's Desecrate to consume health instead of energy.
**Notes:**
Desecrate will no longer consume energy, but instead consume health per corpse.
Health cost is affected by Ability Efficiency.
Desecrate will not work if Nekros has insufficient health to drain.
**Tips:**
Nekros' passive ability, Soul Siphon, causes him to regenerate 5 health points per death within a 10m radius, somewhat mitigating the damage from this augment.
This augment can be used with Equilibrium to recover both energy and health upon pickups of health orbs, which also happens to be a regular drop from Desecrate.
Does not interrupt Rejuvenation. As a result, its effect will begin immediately after taking damage.
Melee weapons equipped with Life Strike, Hirudo, Hema or a Furis equipped with Winds of Purity can be used to recover lost health.
Rage and Quick Thinking do not take effect from Despoil's health drain.
Health Conversion does not lose a stack from Despoil's health drain.
Does not activate Arcane Avenger.

## Detect Vulnerability
Upon completing research on an enemy, subsequent scans will reveal their weak points.
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** penjaga | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Helios | **Max Rank:** 5

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired from Cephalon Simaris for 75,000 Standing.

### Drop Locations
- Cephalon Simaris, Detect Vulnerability
 — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Sentinel Mods
Detect Vulnerability is a precept mod for Helios and Helios Prime that reveals weakspots on enemies that have had previously completed Codex scans. Hitting an enemy in the highlighted weakspot will apply a damage multiplier.
**Notes:**
Helios will randomly select a single enemy within 30 meters to mark with a weakspot for 10 seconds.
The mark has a 'fade-in' time of approximately 0.5 seconds and a duration of 9.5 seconds at max rank.
The mark will remain for the full duration even if the target moves further than 30 meters away after being marked.
Marked target will have a randomly selected body part highlighted in Helios's primary energy color, visible to all squad members. Attacks on the highlighted weakspot will deal 2.75x damage.
The highlighted area does not count as an actual weak point for the purposes of effects like Acuity Mods or charging Incarnon weapons.
The damage increase stacks multiplicatively with body part multipliers.
After the mark expires or the marked target dies, Helios will have a 2s cooldown before marking another target.
Enemies must have a completed codex entry prior to entering the mission to be eligible to be marked with a weakspot.
It is not necessary to have Investigator equipped for Detect Vulnerability to function, and marking an enemy with a weakspot does not consume a Codex Scanner charge.
Although the precept functions similarly to Banshee's Sonar ability, the highlighted weakspot is smaller and will not trigger Resonance.

### Wiki
Diamond Skin increases the Radiation damage resistance of a Warframe's shield and health.
**Notes:**
Currently, there are few non-player sources of Radiation. The most common source is laser traps in the Orokin Void.
Sortie missions may contain enemies modded with Radiation as well if specified.
Damage resistance works on both health and shields.
Increases effective health/shields against Radiation by 100% (100% - 45%)
Damage resistance bonus is multiplicative with other sources of damage resistance such as Adaptation and Aviator.
**Trivia:**
Prior to Damage 2.0, this mod increased resistance to Laser damage. Though Laser damage was removed from the game when Damage 2.0 rolled out, the mod card still claimed to increase laser resistance for quite some time.
This was later changed to Radiation damage in an unknown update.
Update 18.13.3 once again changed the function of this mod. The element resistance was changed from Radiation to Blast, however this was not mentioned. It was later changed to Radiation again also without being mentioned, meaning the change to Blast was most likely unintentional.
Before Update 9.5, it was uncommonly dropped in the Void and rarely dropped on other maps. As of Update 9.5, this mod can be found in normal maps.

## Dig
The kubrow sniffs out buried objects and digs them up.
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** penjaga | **Drain:** 0 | **Compatible:** Sahasa Kubrow | **Max Rank:** 5

### Acquisition
Dig is automatically acquired upon obtaining a Sahasa Kubrow.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +45% Success Chance
- **Rank 1:** +90% Success Chance
- **Rank 2:** +135% Success Chance
- **Rank 3:** +180% Success Chance
- **Rank 4:** +225% Success Chance
- **Rank 5:** +270% Success Chance

### Drop Locations
- Corrupted Drahk — Legendary (0.01%)
- Drahk — Legendary (0.01%)
- Kubrow — Legendary (0.01%)
- Kuva Drahk — Legendary (0.01%)
- Tamm — Legendary (0.01%)

### Wiki
Dig is a precept mod for the Sahasa Kubrow to dig up items upon the battlefield. They will be able to dig up random ammunition types, health orbs, and energy orbs if the player needs them. Successful digs also yield bonus resources.
**Notes:**
Has a cooldown of 14 seconds starting after the dig animation ends, effectively completing one Dig every ~20 seconds.
At max rank, the listed 270% success chance means that the Kubrow will always dig up two items, with a 70% chance of a third item.
When in use, the Kubrow will bark twice to notify the player, stop at a location and paw along the ground for 5 seconds, before barking twice to alert players of its find.
The mods that may occasionally be dug up are taken from the same mod list used by Void containers.
Kubrow prioritize items depending on its master's needs: if their master needs energy, Energy Orbs are retrieved, if they need ammunition, the relevant ammo type is retrieved, etc.
The threshold values for energy and health orb prioritization seem to be 80% (or lower) of total energy/health capacity.
Ammo is retrieved upon not having full reserves.
The type of resources dug up is determined at the very end of the digging animation.
As of Update 37, the sahasa kubrow can dig in the middle of combat as long as its health isn't critically low.
Kubrow can dig even in places on tilesets where normally finding such items in the floor/ground should not be possible. (i.e. Corpus Ship, Grineer Galleon, Orokin Tower, etc.)

- **Rank 1:** Secondary Fire mode has a 20% chance to disarm enemies., +0.5 'Purity'

### Wiki
Disarming Purity is a Weapon Augment Mod exclusive to the Panthera and Panthera Prime that gives is Alternate Fire a chance to disarm enemies and adds a Purity effect.
**Notes:**
Continuously firing at the same enemy will provide multiple chances to disarm them.
The area of effect from Blast inherits the disarm chance.
Toxic Lash procced from Combustion Beam or Blast procs will also inherit the disarm chance with each tick of Toxin.
Radial Disarm, a Loki ability that has a similar Disarming effect
Halikar, a thrown melee weapon that has a chance to disarm enemies when thrown.

## Discipline's Merit
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** naramon | **Drain:** 4 | **Compatible:** Melee | **Max Rank:** 3

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Enables Tennokai. Opportunities occur every 7 melee hits instead of at random.
- **Rank 1:** Enables Tennokai. Opportunities occur every 6 melee hits instead of at random.
- **Rank 2:** Enables Tennokai. Opportunities occur every 5 melee hits instead of at random.

### Wiki
Discipline's Merit is a Exilus Weapon Mod for melee weapons that enables Tennokai opportunities to occur after a set number of hits instead of at random.
**Notes:**
Makes Tennokai trigger after 4 melee inputs that hit an enemy.
Additional enemies hit beyond the first do not count as additional hits.
Gunblade shots are an exception to this.
Multi-strike attacks do not count as additional hits.
Gunblade shots are an exception to this; attacks that fire two shots in succession will count as two hits. Multishot does not count.
Heavy attacks do not count as a hit.
Performing a Heavy Slide is considered a hit.
Remaining hits to Tennokai trigger are shown on the buff UI.

## Disorienting Blast
**Rarity:** Common | **Polarity:** naramon | **Drain:** 4 | **Max Rank:** 3

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Void Blast has a 20% chance of confusing enemies for 4s, causing them to be unable to distinguish friend from foe.
- **Rank 1:** Void Blast has a 30% chance of confusing enemies for 8s, causing them to be unable to distinguish friend from foe.
- **Rank 2:** Void Blast has a 40% chance of confusing enemies for 12s, causing them to be unable to distinguish friend from foe.
- **Rank 3:** Void Blast has a 50% chance of confusing enemies for 16s, causing them to be unable to distinguish friend from foe.

## Dispatch Overdrive
**Rarity:** Uncommon | **Polarity:** naramon | **Drain:** 4 | **Compatible:** Melee | **Max Rank:** 5

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** On Heavy Attack Hit: +10% Movement Speed for 3s
- **Rank 1:** On Heavy Attack Hit: +20% Movement Speed for 5s
- **Rank 2:** On Heavy Attack Hit: +30% Movement Speed for 8s
- **Rank 3:** On Heavy Attack Hit: +40% Movement Speed for 10s
- **Rank 4:** On Heavy Attack Hit: +50% Movement Speed for 13s
- **Rank 5:** On Heavy Attack Hit: +60% Movement Speed for 15s

### Drop Locations
- Corrupted Butcher — Rare (0.17%)
- Duviri/Endless: Tier 1 (Normal) — Legendary (0.67%)
- Duviri/Endless: Tier 3 (Normal) — Legendary (0.67%)

### Wiki
Dispatch Overdrive is an Exilus Weapon Mod for melee that grants a movement speed buff on Heavy Attack.
**Notes:**
Applies to both sprint speed and base movement (walking) speed.
Can be triggered by heavy slams and projectiles launched by heavy attacks of some weapons, such as Arum Spinosa, Syam and other melee weapons.
Cannot be equipped on Exalted Weapons, Deconstructor or Deconstructor Prime.
Stacks with other movement speed buffs such as from Saryn's Molt.
Is triggered by the mid-air explosion of Glaives on heavy attack, but not by the thrown weapon itself or the explosions created from Volatile Quick Return.
**Tips:**
Can be useful while using Nyx with Assimilate.
Can also be useful while using Ivara with Prowl. The difference in movement speed is very noticeable with Prowl, and it can be easily triggered with a single heavy attack.
Pairs well with max Strength Volt if used with Speed.
Increases the speed of Wukong's Cloud Walker, as do other movement speed buffs, and can get additional speed for Cloud Walker from means such as infusing Saryn's Molt onto Wukong.
**Trivia:**
Prior to Update 26, this mod grants a movement speed buff on a Channeling kill.

## Distilled Contamination

### Stats by Rank

- **Rank 1:** Killing an enemy affected by Contamination Wave makes all affected enemies 30% more vulnerable, while also making the effect last 6s longer. Maximum 2 stacks.
- **Rank 2:** Killing an enemy affected by Contamination Wave makes all affected enemies 40% more vulnerable, while also making the effect last 8s longer. Maximum 2 stacks.

## Diversified Denial
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** penjaga | **Drain:** 0 | **Compatible:** Hound | **Max Rank:** 5

### Acquisition
Diversified Denial is automatically acquired upon obtaining a Hound built with the Urga Bracket.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Fabricate 3 Specters that fight for 5s. Each deals 35% of the Hound's damage and cannot use Abilities.
- **Rank 1:** Fabricate 3 Specters that fight for 10s. Each deals 45% of the Hound's damage and cannot use Abilities.
- **Rank 2:** Fabricate 3 Specters that fight for 15s. Each deals 55% of the Hound's damage and cannot use Abilities.
- **Rank 3:** Fabricate 3 Specters that fight for 20s. Each deals 65% of the Hound's damage and cannot use Abilities.
- **Rank 4:** Fabricate 3 Specters that fight for 25s. Each deals 75% of the Hound's damage and cannot use Abilities.
- **Rank 5:** Fabricate 3 Specters that fight for 30s. Each deals 85% of the Hound's damage and cannot use Abilities.

### Wiki
Diversified Denial is a precept mod for Hounds that summons three smaller specters of the Hound, each dealing a percentage of the Hound's damage.
**Notes:**
The Hound must be within ~40m of an enemy to activate.
Cooldown of 15s.
Other Precepts will not activate while specters are deployed.
If all three specters are killed, the Hound will reappear with 50% Health.
Specters cannot be healed with Pack Leader.
Synergizes with Shattering Impact and Batoten or Lacerten, as each specter's attack reduces enemy armor.
Cannot be equipped with Reflex Denial and Evasive Denial.

Dividing Blades is a Conclave-exclusive stance mod for dual swords, devised to immobilize and cut opponents with dicing motions.
Dividing Blades

## Divine Retribution
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Nezha | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired by attaining the rank of General under the Steel Meridian, or the rank of Genius under Cephalon Suda, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Divine Spears Augment: Status Effects spread to all speared enemies. Spear explosions scale by 0.75x of remaining Slash, Toxin and Heat Status Effects. Base Radius is 14m.
- **Rank 1:** Divine Spears Augment: Status Effects spread to all speared enemies. Spear explosions scale by 1x of remaining Slash, Toxin and Heat Status Effects. Base Radius is 14m.
- **Rank 2:** Divine Spears Augment: Status Effects spread to all speared enemies. Spear explosions scale by 1.25x of remaining Slash, Toxin and Heat Status Effects. Base Radius is 14m.
- **Rank 3:** Divine Spears Augment: Status Effects spread to all speared enemies. Spear explosions scale by 1.5x of remaining Slash, Toxin and Heat Status Effects. Base Radius is 14m.

### Drop Locations
- Cephalon Suda, Genius — Common (100.00%)
- Steel Meridian, General — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Divine Retribution is a Warframe Augment Mod for Nezha's Divine Spears that spreads status effects from a speared target onto all speared enemies. Spear retraction causes enemies to receive the full damage from all damage ticks of procs, increased by a damage multiplier. However, base Ability Range is reduced to 14 meters.
**Notes:**
Inflicting a status effect on a speared enemy will spread it to all speared enemies.
Triggers on status effects from any source.
Spread Heat, Electricity, Toxin, and Gas procs ignore elemental mods for their damage calculation.
Despite not being listed, Blast will also be redirected.
Spear retraction removes all Slash, Heat, and Toxin statuses then deals their remaining damage in a burst, multiplied by the status damage multiplier.
Effect triggers on both manual and automatic spear retraction.
Electricity and Gas statuses will not be removed or benefit from this effect.
Transferred Electricity status effects trigger Archon Stretch.
Transferred Toxin status effects do not trigger Archon Continuity.
Transferred Heat status effects do not trigger Archon Vitality.
Despite the description mentioning spear explosions, there is no area of effect.
Enemies who receive spread status effects will not have their status effect damage, duration, or "explosion" increased by Elementalist and Status Duration mods.
Targets directly hit will receive the corresponding bonuses for the specific status effects triggered by the corresponding hit.

## Dizzying Rounds
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** naramon | **Drain:** 4 | **Compatible:** Bronco | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
Available for 20 from the Nightwave Cred Offerings store on a rotational basis.
Dizzying Rounds was originally acquired by reaching Rank 20 in Nightwave during Series 3.
Beginning with Nightwave Nora's Mix: Vol. 6, it was added to the Nightwave Cred Offerings.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Shots from less than 2m stun enemies and open them to finishers. +50% Status Chance
- **Rank 1:** Shots from less than 4m stun enemies and open them to finishers. +100% Status Chance
- **Rank 2:** Shots from less than 6m stun enemies and open them to finishers. +150% Status Chance
- **Rank 3:** Shots from less than 8m stun enemies and open them to finishers. +200% Status Chance

### Wiki
Dizzying Rounds is a weapon augment mod for Bronco and Bronco Prime that increases its status chance while giving them the ability to stun enemies and open them up to finishers if shot within 8m of the player.
**Notes:**
Cannot be equipped on Akbronco or Akbronco Prime.
Projectile speed does not affect the distance at which the enemies are stunned.
Any damage dealt by the weapon using this mod will stun the target as long as they are within 8m when the damage is dealt. Including status effects.
The distance to the enemy when the status effects is applied does not matter. Only when the status effect deals damage.
Applies to the ricochet effect of the Bronco Incarnon Genesis transformation.

## Draining Bite
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** penjaga | **Drain:** 0 | **Compatible:** Vasca Kavat | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
Draining Bite is automatically acquired upon obtaining a Vasca Kavat.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Vasca Kavat inflicts 2 stacks of Slash at 37.5% of its melee damage and restores 2% Health to itself.
- **Rank 1:** Vasca Kavat inflicts 3 stacks of Slash at 75% of its melee damage and restores 5% Health to itself.
- **Rank 2:** Vasca Kavat inflicts 4 stacks of Slash at 112.5% of its melee damage and restores 7% Health to itself.
- **Rank 3:** Vasca Kavat inflicts 5 stacks of Slash at 150% of its melee damage and restores 10% Health to itself.

### Wiki
Draining Bite is a precept mod for the Vasca Kavat to deal extra damage, apply Slash stacks, and restore a percentage of their own Health.
**Notes:**
Has a cooldown of 20 seconds.
The attack uses the stats of Vasca Claws and is affected by mods.
Damage can be increased with damage mods such as Maul.
Critical chance can be increased with Bite and Hunter Synergy.
Critical damage can be increased with Bite and Radon Claws.
Additional status effects can be inflicted based on the claws' damage types and status chance, both affected by mods.
This includes converted elemental damage from Burning Claws, Chilling Claws, Sepsis Claws, and Shocking Claws.
Vasca Claws deal Slash damage innately, which can cause the attack to inflict more Slash stacks than expected.
Status duration for both the forced Slash status and other statuses can be increased with Magnetic Claws and Frenzied Posture.
Unaffected by Swipe.

## Dread Ward
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Garuda | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired by attaining the rank of General under Steel Meridian, or the rank of Exalted under Red Veil, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Dread Mirror Augment: Become unkillable for 4s when Dread Mirror kills a target by ripping its life force.
- **Rank 1:** Dread Mirror Augment: Become unkillable for 5s when Dread Mirror kills a target by ripping its life force.
- **Rank 2:** Dread Mirror Augment: Become unkillable for 6s when Dread Mirror kills a target by ripping its life force.
- **Rank 3:** Dread Mirror Augment: Become unkillable for 8s when Dread Mirror kills a target by ripping its life force.

### Drop Locations
- Red Veil, Exalted — Common (100.00%)
- Steel Meridian, General — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Dread Ward is a Warframe Augment Mod for Garuda that makes her unkillable when an enemy is instantly killed by Dread Mirror.
**Notes:**
This augment causes Garuda to become unkillable rather than invulnerable, meaning she will still take damage but her health will not drop to 0 while active.
Duration is refreshed, not stacked, if the augment is activated again during the effect.
The duration of the unkillable status is affected by Ability Duration.
The Dread Ward's effect takes priority over Quick Thinking.
The augment activates even if the target of Dread Mirror dies before the ability lands.

## Dreadful Killshot
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** madurai | **Drain:** 2 | **Compatible:** Basmu | **Max Rank:** 5

### Acquisition
Dreadful Killshot can be obtained by reaching Rank 10 with Nightwave during Nora's Mix: Dreams of the Dead.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Increased Damage and Status Chance by +10% for every 75 Warframe Health. Capped at 360%
- **Rank 1:** Increased Damage and Status Chance by +12% for every 75 Warframe Health. Capped at 360%
- **Rank 2:** Increased Damage and Status Chance by +14% for every 75 Warframe Health. Capped at 360%
- **Rank 3:** Increased Damage and Status Chance by +16% for every 75 Warframe Health. Capped at 360%
- **Rank 4:** Increased Damage and Status Chance by +18% for every 75 Warframe Health. Capped at 360%
- **Rank 5:** Increased Damage and Status Chance by +20% for every 75 Warframe Health. Capped at 360%

### Wiki
Dreadful Killshot is an Augment Mod exclusive to the Basmu that increases Damage and Status Chance for every 75 Current Warframe Health, up to 360% at all ranks.
**Notes:**
Buff is based on current health, including bonuses, not maximum health. Temporary buffs to health and damage to health can affect the current damage and status chance bonuses.
Both the Damage and Status chance bonuses are additive.
Buff is rounded down to the nearest multiple of 20%.
Accordingly, the equipped Warframe must have at least 675 current health for the damage bonus to outdo Serration.

## Dreamer's Bond
**Rarity:** Uncommon | **Polarity:** aura | **Drain:** -2 | **Compatible:** AURA | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 5

### Acquisition
Awarded from completing the Earth to Venus Junction.
Players who have already completed the Junction prior to Update 35.1 will receive the mod in their inbox messages.
Available for 20 creds from the Nightwave Cred Offerings store on a rotational basis.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Squad receives +0.05 Energy Regen/s and +0.25 Health Regen/s
- **Rank 1:** Squad receives +0.1 Energy Regen/s and +0.5 Health Regen/s
- **Rank 2:** Squad receives +0.15 Energy Regen/s and +0.75 Health Regen/s
- **Rank 3:** Squad receives +0.2 Energy Regen/s and +1 Health Regen/s
- **Rank 4:** Squad receives +0.25 Energy Regen/s and +1.25 Health Regen/s
- **Rank 5:** Squad receives +0.3 Energy Regen/s and +1.5 Health Regen/s

### Wiki
Dreamer's Bond is an aura mod that regenerates the health and energy pools of a given squad.
* Aura mods increase the amount of Mod Capacity
**Notes:**
Dreamer's Bond is a hybrid of two other existing aura mods, Rejuvenation and Energy Siphon, but with half the effectiveness of their effects.
Casting an ability will stop the energy generation for 1 second.
Channeled abilities that drain energy over time, such as Immolation, Sound Quake, and Renewal will completely prevent energy generation for as long as they are active. Abilities with variable energy drain such as Artemis Bow, Pacify & Provoke and Desecrate do not interrupt the energy generation.
**Trivia:**
Dreamer's Bond is the first aura mod with a Aura polarity.
When it was first introduced in Update 35.1 it temporarily had a Madurai polarity, matching the aura slots of starter Warframes Mag and Volt, until it was changed in Update 35.5.3.
Unfortunately, as Excalibur begins with no aura polarity, he only gains 7 bonus mod capacity instead of 14 compared to the other starter Warframes.

## Dreamer's Wrath
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** madurai | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Melee | **Max Rank:** 3

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Enables Tennokai. Increases opportunity chance by 12.5% and critical damage by 8% for Tennokai attacks.
- **Rank 1:** Enables Tennokai. Increases opportunity chance by 25% and critical damage by 16% for Tennokai attacks.
- **Rank 2:** Enables Tennokai. Increases opportunity chance by 37.5% and critical damage by 24% for Tennokai attacks.
- **Rank 3:** Enables Tennokai. Increases opportunity chance by 50% and critical damage by 32% for Tennokai attacks.

### Drop Locations
- Deimos/Cambire (Alchemy), Rotation C — Rare (6.00%)

### Wiki
Dreamer's Wrath is a Exilus Weapon Mod for melee weapons that enables Tennokai, increases Tennokai chance and Tennokai Heavy Attack Critical Damage.
**Notes:**
Tennokai opportunity chance is a multiplicative bonus, at max rank the chance to proc Tennokai is increased to 22.5%.
The critical damage is additive to mods such as Organ Shatter.
Affects only tennokai heavy attacks.

## Drifting Contact
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** vazarin | **Drain:** 2 | **Compatible:** Melee | **Max Rank:** 3

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +2.5s Combo Duration, +10% Status Chance
- **Rank 1:** +5s Combo Duration, +20% Status Chance
- **Rank 2:** +7.5s Combo Duration, +30% Status Chance
- **Rank 3:** +10s Combo Duration, +40% Status Chance

### Drop Locations
- Nightmare Mode Rewards, Rotation B — Uncommon (22.56%)

### Wiki
Drifting Contact is a rare Melee mod that extends the duration of the Melee Combo Counter and increases status chance.
**Notes:**
Drifting Contact is additive with similar mods. For example, a Galatine with a max rank Drifting Contact and Melee Prowess will have a 20% (1 + 40% + 90%) status chance.
Obtained from medium Nightmare Missions, which can be found on Phobos, Jupiter, Saturn, Void, Lua, Europa, Ceres and Kuva Fortress.
At max rank, Drifting Contact increases the total melee combo counter time to 15 seconds.
Combined with Body Count and Gladiator Rush, the counter time is increased to 33 seconds.
With "Power Spike" (Naramon Focus node) the Melee Combo Counter decreases by a certain amount (at Rank 1, 20) each time that the Counter would run out. So, with a maxed Drifting Contact, it decreases the Counter every 15 seconds. Without mods, the Counter gets decreased every 5 seconds.
Can be stacked with Melee Prowess, Volcanic Edge, Vicious Frost, Voltaic Strike and Virulent Scourge for a total of 295% increase in Status Chance. On weapons with at least 26% base Status Chance, this allows them to achieve a total Status Chance value of 100%, allowing every strike to deal Status Effect proc. On weapons with 27% base Status Chance Melee Prowess is no longer needed to achieve the 100% Status Chance value.
Weeping Wounds can further increase the Status Chance depending on the Melee Combo Counter.
Works well with other mods affecting Melee Combos like Weeping Wounds, Body Count, Gladiator Rush, Blood Rush, Rising Storm and Surging Dash.
