## Duality
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Equinox | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
Can be acquired by attaining the rank of Maxim under the Arbiters of Hexis, or the rank of Flawless under New Loka, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Metamorphosis Augment: Equinox's other half breaks free for 7s, dealing 100% Damage.
- **Rank 1:** Metamorphosis Augment: Equinox's other half breaks free for 8s, dealing 150% Damage.
- **Rank 2:** Metamorphosis Augment: Equinox's other half breaks free for 9s, dealing 200% Damage.
- **Rank 3:** Metamorphosis Augment: Equinox's other half breaks free for 10s, dealing 300% Damage.

### Drop Locations
- Arbiters of Hexis, Maxim — Common (100.00%)
- New Loka, Flawless — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Duality is a Warframe Augment Mod for Equinox that causes her opposite form to split from her when casting Metamorphosis, creating a Specter armed with the weapon Equinox was using during casting.
**Notes:**
The duration of the specter is affected by Ability Duration.
The damage output of the specter is not affected by Ability Strength.
The specter is an allied NPC that copies the weapon Equinox had equipped before casting Metamorphosis, and uses it to autonomously attack enemies.
Only one Duality specter from the player can exist at a time. If Metamorphosis is cast again before the specter's duration expires, the specter will die immediately before a new specter is spawned.
The specter is counted as a summon and is affected by Summoner's Wrath, Arcane Camisado, and Theorem Infection.
Summoner's Wrath acts as a faction damage bonus, stacking multiplicatively with the damage bonus from Duality and additively with faction damage mods on the specter's equipped weapon.
The specter will not use Warframe Abilities.
The specter will not swap between different weapons.
On weapons with an Alternate Fire mode, only the primary mode will be used.
Weapons with multiple modes, like Kuva Hind and Hystrix, will always be used in the mode that they start the mission in, regardless of which mode is being used before casting Metamorphosis.
The specter cannot use incarnon modes of weapons.

## Duplex Bond
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** madurai | **Drain:** 4 | **Compatible:** COMPANION | **Max Rank:** 5

### Acquisition
The mod can be bought from Son for 20,000 Standing after reaching Rank 3 - Associate with the Entrati.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Companion will clone itself each time you expend 100 energy, up to 3 clones. Clones live 5s and cannot use Abilities. Their kills have a 8% chance of dropping Energy Orbs.
- **Rank 1:** Companion will clone itself each time you expend 100 energy, up to 3 clones. Clones live 10s and cannot use Abilities. Their kills have a 16% chance of dropping Energy Orbs.
- **Rank 2:** Companion will clone itself each time you expend 100 energy, up to 3 clones. Clones live 15s and cannot use Abilities. Their kills have a 25% chance of dropping Energy Orbs.
- **Rank 3:** Companion will clone itself each time you expend 100 energy, up to 3 clones. Clones live 20s and cannot use Abilities. Their kills have a 33% chance of dropping Energy Orbs.
- **Rank 4:** Companion will clone itself each time you expend 100 energy, up to 3 clones. Clones live 25s and cannot use Abilities. Their kills have a 41% chance of dropping Energy Orbs.
- **Rank 5:** Companion will clone itself each time you expend 100 energy, up to 3 clones. Clones live 30s and cannot use Abilities. Their kills have a 50% chance of dropping Energy Orbs.

### Drop Locations
- Entrati (Son), Associate — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Duplex Bond is a Companion mod that creates a short-lived duplicate of the companion after 100 Energy is spent, up to 3 clones. Enemies killed by these clones have a chance at dropping an Energy Orb.
**Notes:**
Clones will attack enemies whether or not Assault Mode or Elusive Posture are equipped.
Clones are counted as companions, and will benefit from companion bonuses such as Summoner's Wrath and Theorem Infection.
Drain-over-time and channeled abilities like Wisp's Sol Gate and Ivara's Artemis Bow are capable of spawning clones.
Energy removed by the Energy Drain modifier in Nightmare missions counts towards the energy needed to spawn a companion copy.
Energy drained by Quick Thinking and Gladiator Finesse to stop lethal damage counts towards the energy needed to spawn a companion copy.
Since clone creation requires Energy spent, this mod cannot be triggered by Hildryn or Lavos. However, Hildryn can spawn clones by picking up Energy Orbs but not through ability usage.
Clones are completely invulnerable to any form of damage and can only die after their listed lifespan has elapsed since they were created.
Despite being invincible, enemies will still have their aggro drawn to them and attempt to fight them.
Cloned Sentinels will float toward enemies rather than sticking to the player, drawing some aggro away from the player as a result.
Invisibility applied to the player through Abilities or other methods is only shared with the main Companion; it is not applied to any summoned/spawned clones and they will remain visible to distract enemy fire.
Since the clones can only use basic attacks, they benefit greatly from Beast Claws Mods. They can use all the claw mods and deal full damage with them.

Eagle Eye is usable in both PvE and PvP.
**Notes:**

Companions

## Efficient Beams
Only consume ammo when dealing damage.

- **Rank 3:** +100% Status Chance
- **Rank 4:** +125% Status Chance
- **Rank 5:** +150% Status Chance

### Wiki
Efficient Beams is a Weapon Augment Mod for Convectrix that increases status chance and makes the weapon only consume ammo when dealing damage.
**Notes:**
Efficient Beams is additive with similar mods. For example, Convectrix with a max ranked Efficient Beams and Shotgun Savvy will have a 45% (1 + 150% + 90%) status chance.
The damage wind-up while the beams are converging and diverging will not lead to ammo loss.

This mod reloads a portion of your weapon's magazine every second. It will not replenish a weapon's total ammo pool, and will partially reload the magazine if there is less than a full magazine of ammo in the weapon's total pool.\
Bonus stacks additivly with the Synth mod set bonus.

## Electro Pulse
**Rarity:** Common | **Polarity:** penjaga | **Drain:** 2 | **Compatible:** Diriga | **Max Rank:** 5

### Acquisition
Electro Pulse is automatically acquired upon obtaining Diriga.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Every 10s, Sentinel will continually zap an enemy within 15m, trapping them in a stunned state.
- **Rank 1:** Every 9s, Sentinel will continually zap an enemy within 15m, trapping them in a stunned state.
- **Rank 2:** Every 8s, Sentinel will continually zap an enemy within 15m, trapping them in a stunned state.
- **Rank 3:** Every 7s, Sentinel will continually zap an enemy within 15m, trapping them in a stunned state.
- **Rank 4:** Every 6s, Sentinel will continually zap an enemy within 15m, trapping them in a stunned state.
- **Rank 5:** Every 5s, Sentinel will continually zap an enemy within 15m, trapping them in a stunned state.

### Wiki
Electro Pulse is a precept mod for Diriga to repeatedly stun a single enemy within range using Electricity status effect.
**Notes:**
The ability locks onto enemies within 10 meters and will track them until they die or are further than 15 meters away. After it loses a target there is a short cooldown time before it will activate this precept again.
Enemies will be able to fire a few shots off or use an ability between stun animations.
Can trigger Melee Influence.
Only activates while the enemies are attacking you.

## Electromagnetic Shielding
**Rarity:** Uncommon | **Polarity:** vazarin | **Drain:** 4 | **Compatible:** Ack & Brunt | **Max Rank:** 5

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** While blocking, redirect 25% of damage taken by allies within 2m to yourself.
- **Rank 1:** While blocking, redirect 30% of damage taken by allies within 4m to yourself.
- **Rank 2:** While blocking, redirect 35% of damage taken by allies within 6m to yourself.
- **Rank 3:** While blocking, redirect 40% of damage taken by allies within 8m to yourself.
- **Rank 4:** While blocking, redirect 45% of damage taken by allies within 10m to yourself.
- **Rank 5:** While blocking, redirect 50% of damage taken by allies within 12m to yourself.

### Drop Locations
- Kela De Thaym — Uncommon (11.11%)

### Wiki
Electromagnetic Shielding is an Exilus Weapon Mod exclusive to the Ack & Brunt that transfers a percentage of damage and all Status Effects that nearby allies receive to yourself, but only when you are blocking.
**Notes:**
When blocking, threads of energy similar to Trinity's Link will extend to all allies in range, using your warframe's energy color.
The mod's effect will only link to other players, and not Companions, objectives, or AI-controlled allies.
Damage absorbed with the use of Electromagnetic Shielding will be converted into health for Iron Skin and Warding Halo.
The mod's effective range cannot be modified by Reach or Primed Reach.
Many abilities may be used without interrupting the link, as long as the Block is held.
For reasons unknown, any Radiation damage or procs that allies receive will be reduced (and in the proc's case, nullified), but the damage or proc will not be transferred to the user.
If an ally is suffering a Radiation proc before a link is created, then the user will also suffer the proc once the link takes hold.
Damage redirected to you does activate abilities that reflect damage, such as Trinity's Link or Mesa's Shatter Shield.
Damage redirected to your Health will trigger Rage and/orHunter Adrenaline's energy conversion.

## Elemental Sandstorm
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Inaros | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired by attaining the rank of Maxim under the Arbiters of Hexis, or the rank of Partner under The Perrin Sequence, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Sandstorm Augment: Sandstorm gains 20% Ability Range and has a 25% chance of inflicting Status Effects based on the Damage Types on the equipped Melee Weapon.
- **Rank 1:** Sandstorm Augment: Sandstorm gains 30% Ability Range and has a 50% chance of inflicting Status Effects based on the Damage Types on the equipped Melee Weapon.
- **Rank 2:** Sandstorm Augment: Sandstorm gains 40% Ability Range and has a 75% chance of inflicting Status Effects based on the Damage Types on the equipped Melee Weapon.
- **Rank 3:** Sandstorm Augment: Sandstorm gains 50% Ability Range and has a 100% chance of inflicting Status Effects based on the Damage Types on the equipped Melee Weapon.

### Drop Locations
- Arbiters of Hexis, Maxim — Common (100.00%)
- The Perrin Sequence, Partner — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Elemental Sandstorm is a Warframe Augment Mod for Inaros that causes Sandstorm to inflict status procs based on equipped damage types and mods on melee weapons currently wielded. Also passively increases Sandstorm's Ability Range by 50%.
**Notes:**
Chance of inflicting status effects is affected by Ability Strength.
The augment does not work on any enemy which can't be lifted by Sandstorm.
Upon a Status Proc trigger, if the status chance is above 100%, the same status will be applied more than once. It will not combine other Status Procs in the same way as any typical Status Chance will.
When a Status Chance has been triggered. It picks only ONE of the Status Procs on the Melee weapon per second (instance), and multiplies its instance by x per 100% Status Chance. (Example: an unmodded Silva and Aegis with Inaros at 300% Power Strength will trigger a Fire Proc 3 times in that second.)

## Eleventh Storm
Rapid attacks using sword and shield.
**Rarity:** Uncommon | **Polarity:** madurai | **Drain:** -2 | **Compatible:** Sword And Shield | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
Eleventh Storm drops from Lephantis, specifically the first phase with the three separate heads. This gives three different opportunities for the mod to drop during the fight.
Eleventh Storm is included in the Mirage Bundle, which costs 600.

### Drop Locations
- Duviri/Endless: Tier 1 (Normal) — Legendary (0.67%)
- Duviri/Endless: Tier 3 (Normal) — Legendary (0.67%)
- Wolf Of Saturn Six — Uncommon (18.33%)
- Zealoid Prelate — Uncommon (18.33%)
- Ancient Infested — Rare (7.52%)
- Infested Corpus — Rare (7.52%)
- Infested Grineer — Rare (7.52%)

### Wiki
Eleventh Storm is a stance mod for Sword and Shield weapons that blends fast sword swings with shield bashes.
**Notes:**
Certain attacks have Impact or Slash damage multipliers.
The first attack of Striking Thunder has a 2x Impact damage multiplier.
The third attack of Striking Thunder has a 1.25x Slash damage multiplier.
**Trivia:**
When released, Eleventh Storm's polarity did not match Silva & Aegis. This was fixed in Update 14.0.1.
Because Lephantis's heads do not produce Codex entries when scanned, the Codex entry for Eleventh Storm cannot be revealed due to the lack of an enemy Codex entry to link to. This initially lead to confusion as to where the mod dropped, as Eleventh Storm's Codex entry did not reveal the enemy that dropped it.
The mod image shows Aegis (shield) closed while Silva (sword) is in the hand of the wielder, even though Aegis opens as soon as Silva is drawn.
During Prime Time 35, a Platinum contest was held, revealing that the names of the mod and its combos are inspired by Rock/Metal songs and awarding 100 Platinum each for whoever guessed the correct song for each corresponding name.

## Elusive Retribution

- **Rank 1:** Elude Augment: Every attack Baruuk Eludes increases his Melee Attack Speed and Melee Critical Damage by 5% up to 4 stacks. Stacks last 6s.
- **Rank 2:** Elude Augment: Every attack Baruuk Eludes increases his Melee Attack Speed and Melee Critical Damage by 5% up to 5 stacks. Stacks last 6s.

### Wiki
Elusive Retribution is a Warframe Augment Mod for Baruuk's Elude that temporarily increases Baruuk's Attack Speed and Melee Critical Damage for every attack dodged, stacking up to the listed limit.
**Notes:**
Buff stack cap is unaffected by mods.
Attack Speed and Critical Damage bonuses are affected by Ability Strength.
Buff duration is affected by Ability Duration.
When the buff times out, one stack is lost and the buff duration resets.

## Empowered Blades
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** naramon | **Drain:** -2 | **Compatible:** AURA | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 5

### Acquisition
Empowered Blades is dropped by the Feyarch Specter, available upon anointing a Silver Grove shrine with the Sunrise Apothic.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Squad receives +10% Status Chance and Status Damage on Heavy Attacks, but lose 15 Shields per hit.
- **Rank 1:** Squad receives +20% Status Chance and Status Damage on Heavy Attacks, but lose 30 Shields per hit.
- **Rank 2:** Squad receives +30% Status Chance and Status Damage on Heavy Attacks, but lose 45 Shields per hit.
- **Rank 3:** Squad receives +40% Status Chance and Status Damage on Heavy Attacks, but lose 60 Shields per hit.
- **Rank 4:** Squad receives +50% Status Chance and Status Damage on Heavy Attacks, but lose 75 Shields per hit.
- **Rank 5:** Squad receives +60% Status Chance and Status Damage on Heavy Attacks, but lose 90 Shields per hit.

### Drop Locations
- Feyarch Specter — Rare (6.06%)

### Wiki
Empowered Blades is an aura mod that increases status chance and status damage (where applicable) of Heavy Attacks, at the cost of draining a portion of shields per hit.
* Aura mods increase the amount of Mod Capacity
**Notes:**
Status damage bonus will only apply to Toxin, Gas, Heat, Electricity, Blast and Slash procs.
Bonus is additive to other sources of status damage such as Melee Elementalist or Ash's passive.
Status chance bonus is additive with other status chance mods such as Melee Prowess and Weeping Wounds.
A Skana equipped with Melee Prowess and Empowered Blades will have
16% * (1 + Melee Prowess + Empowered Blades) status chance on heavy attack.
Requires a shield stat of at least Shield and has to consume at least Shield in order to grant the bonuses.
Shield drain is per hit, making it costly against large groups of enemies or with multi-hit attacks.
Shield drain does not increase with multiple Empowered Blade auras present.
It is thus impossible to benefit from it on Nidus, Inaros or Kullervo
This Aura does not affect the Zenistar disc.
The buff can only affect one of your melee weapons at a time, decided at the start of the mission. Besides making testing in the Simulacrum inconvenient, this means that Exalted Weapons won't normally benefit. Entering the mission without a melee weapon equipped will solve the latter.
**Tips:**
Useful on Warframes with very high shields such as Hildryn, Frost Prime, Harrow and Mag Prime.
Can work well in conjunction with shield regeneration mods or items, such as Augur Reach to regenerate a small portion of shields on ability cast, or Squad Shield Restore.
A Hildryn or Mag Specter (Tenno) can be very useful for aiding in shield regeneration, thus allowing you to make use of this Aura more than once.

## Empowered Quiver
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Ivara | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired by attaining the rank of Genius under Cephalon Suda, or the rank of Partner under the The Perrin Sequence, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Quiver Augment: Dashwire grants allies 75% Critical Damage. Cloak arrow has a 50% chance to prevent Status Effects.
- **Rank 1:** Quiver Augment: Dashwire grants allies 80% Critical Damage. Cloak arrow has a 65% chance to prevent Status Effects.
- **Rank 2:** Quiver Augment: Dashwire grants allies 90% Critical Damage. Cloak arrow has a 80% chance to prevent Status Effects.
- **Rank 3:** Quiver Augment: Dashwire grants allies 100% Critical Damage. Cloak arrow has a 100% chance to prevent Status Effects.

### Drop Locations
- Cephalon Suda, Genius — Common (100.00%)
- The Perrin Sequence, Partner — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Empowered Quiver is a Warframe Augment Mod for Ivara that improves Quiver's Dashwire Arrow and Cloak Arrow. Dashwire Arrow grants Critical Multiplier bonus to allies standing on the wire, while Cloak Arrow grants a chance to resist Status Effects.
**Notes:**
Critical multiplier bonus and status effect resistance chance are affected by Ability Strength.
The critical multiplier bonus stacks additively with critical damage mods.
For example, combining Empowered Quiver with a maxed Vital Sense will increase a rifle's critical multiplier by 220%.
**Tips:**
Synergizes well with Prowl as Ivara gains additional damage on headshots, and crits will be multiplied further by this mod.
Similarly, the augment synergizes well with Piercing Navigator and/or Concentrated Arrow due the the critical chance bonuses granted by both augments.

## Endless Lullaby
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Baruuk | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired by attaining the rank of Flawless under New Loka, or the rank of Maxim under the Arbiters of Hexis, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Lull Augment: Performing a finisher on or killing a sleeping enemy will retrigger Lull for 25% of the remaining duration. Passive: +50% Lull Duration.
- **Rank 1:** Lull Augment: Performing a finisher on or killing a sleeping enemy will retrigger Lull for 50% of the remaining duration. Passive: +50% Lull Duration.
- **Rank 2:** Lull Augment: Performing a finisher on or killing a sleeping enemy will retrigger Lull for 75% of the remaining duration. Passive: +50% Lull Duration.
- **Rank 3:** Lull Augment: Performing a finisher on or killing a sleeping enemy will retrigger Lull for 100% of the remaining duration. Passive: +50% Lull Duration.

### Drop Locations
- Arbiters of Hexis, Maxim — Common (100.00%)
- New Loka, Flawless — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Endless Lullaby is a Warframe Augment Mod for Baruuk's Lull that re-triggers Lull for its remaining duration upon initiating a finisher on or killing a sleeping enemy. This mod also passively increases Lull's duration.
**Notes:**
There is a 3-second cooldown between Lull re-triggers, whether by initiating a finisher on or killing a sleeping enemy.
Lull fields are generated at the location of the player.
Re-triggered fields persist for the normal duration of the ability (e.g., 5 seconds at 100% power duration).
Multiple fields can exist simultaneously if one is triggered before older fields expire.
The Passive: +50% Lull Duration refers to the base duration of the ability and will therefore always extend the modded duration of the initial Lull by 10 seconds.
The duration increase refers only to the sleep duration of the ability, not the field duration.
The term remaining duration refers to the remaining time for which the triggering enemy would have slept.
Because there is a delay between when an enemy is first affected by a Lull field and when it falls asleep and the sleep timer starts decreasing, enemies slept by a re-triggered field will wake up later than enemies slept by the original field.
If the remaining sleep duration of a re-triggered field is short enough, and the field duration long enough, enemies can be 'chain slept', falling asleep and waking up repeatedly until the field expires.
27.1

## Endoparasitic Vector
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** penjaga | **Drain:** 0 | **Compatible:** Pharaoh Predasite | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
Endoparasitic Vector is automatically acquired upon obtaining a Pharaoh Predasite.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Transmit an endoparasite to an enemy within 7.5m. Tentacles spawn from it, clinging to enemies within 4m slowing them as they move further from the parasite, and dealing 5 Viral Damage/sec over 2s.
- **Rank 1:** Transmit an endoparasite to an enemy within 15m. Tentacles spawn from it, clinging to enemies within 6m slowing them as they move further from the parasite, and dealing 10 Viral Damage/sec over 3s.
- **Rank 2:** Transmit an endoparasite to an enemy within 22.5m. Tentacles spawn from it, clinging to enemies within 8m slowing them as they move further from the parasite, and dealing 15 Viral Damage/sec over 4s.
- **Rank 3:** Transmit an endoparasite to an enemy within 30m. Tentacles spawn from it, clinging to enemies within 10m slowing them as they move further from the parasite, and dealing 20 Viral Damage/sec over 5s.

### Wiki
Endoparasitic Vector is a precept mod for the Pharaoh Predasite to spit a mass of tentacles at a target, dealing Viral damage and slowing the target.
**Notes:**
Shoots an endoparasite projectile that stops when it encounters an enemy or surface.
When it hits a creature or surface, and at the end of its duration, the endoparasite causes Blast damage in a 5 meter radius.
If the projectile hits an enemy, it becomes attached to it for 5s.
The endoparasite causes Viral damage every second for 4 seconds in a 5 meter radius, each time applying a guaranteed Viral proc.
Damage and status effects caused by both the explosion and the damage over time effect are affected by elemental mods, such as Venom Teeth.
Status chance applies independently on the explosion and on the damage over time effect, as well as independently on the main target and other enemies within a 5 meter radius.
Explosion damage and the damage over time effect do not proc claws' innate Gas status effect.
Explosion damage and the damage over time effect is not affected by Maul or Bite.
Has a ~11 second cooldown.

## Enduring Bastille
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Vauban | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired by attaining the rank of Genius under Cephalon Suda, or the rank of Partner under The Perrin Sequence, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.
This mod can periodically be acquired from The Circuit.

### Stats by Rank

- **Rank 1:** Bastille Augment: Killing an enemy in Bastille extends the duration by 2s. Vortex's duration is increased by 55% of its Maximum Duration for each additional Vortex thrown into it.
- **Rank 2:** Bastille Augment: Killing an enemy in Bastille extends the duration by 2s. Vortex's duration is increased by 60% of its Maximum Duration for each additional Vortex thrown into it.
- **Rank 3:** Bastille Augment: Killing an enemy in Bastille extends the duration by 2s. Vortex's duration is increased by 70% of its Maximum Duration for each additional Vortex thrown into it.

### Wiki
Enduring Bastille is a Warframe Augment Mod for Vauban's Bastille that increases Bastille's duration when killing enemies inside it, and increases Vortex's duration for every additional Vortex thrown into it.
**Notes:**
Enemies killed inside Bastille extends the active duration of Bastille by Ability Duration2 seconds, up to a maximum of 2x the ability's duration after mods.
Throwing a Vortex into a Vortex extends the active duration of Vortex up by 70% of its maximum duration, up to a maximum of 10x the ability's duration after mods.

* Aura mods increase the amount of Mod Capacity
**Notes:**
Enemy Radar stacks with Enemy Sense, Vigilante Pursuit, Stealth Drift, Animal Instinct, Ivara's passive, and other Enemy Radars from squad members.

Stacking Enemy Radar effects allows players to see enemies in adjacent rooms. This is useful in several contested missions such as Survival.

## Energizing Shot
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** madurai | **Drain:** 0 | **Compatible:** Pistol (No Aoe) | **Max Rank:** 10

### Acquisition
Obtainable from the Arbitrations vendor in the Arbiters of Hexis room in Relays for 30 Vitus Essence.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Shoot Energy Orbs to obtain them with +10% extra effect.
- **Rank 1:** Shoot Energy Orbs to obtain them with +20% extra effect.
- **Rank 2:** Shoot Energy Orbs to obtain them with +30% extra effect.
- **Rank 3:** Shoot Energy Orbs to obtain them with +40% extra effect.
- **Rank 4:** Shoot Energy Orbs to obtain them with +50% extra effect.
- **Rank 5:** Shoot Energy Orbs to obtain them with +60% extra effect.
- **Rank 6:** Shoot Energy Orbs to obtain them with +70% extra effect.
- **Rank 7:** Shoot Energy Orbs to obtain them with +80% extra effect.
- **Rank 8:** Shoot Energy Orbs to obtain them with +90% extra effect.
- **Rank 9:** Shoot Energy Orbs to obtain them with +100% extra effect.
- **Rank 10:** Shoot Energy Orbs to obtain them with +110% extra effect.

### Wiki
Energizing Shot is an Exilus mod for non-area of effect secondary weapons that allows picking up Energy Orbs when shooting at them, also increasing the amount gained by doing so.
**Notes:**
This mod cannot be equipped on the following explosive area-of-effect secondaries (with the hidden AOE compatibility tag):
Aegrit
Akarius (Akarius Prime)
Angstrum (Prisma Angstrum)
Balefire Charger (Balefire Charger Prime)
Castanas (Sancti Castanas)
Embolist
Epitaph (Epitaph Prime)
Glory
Kompressa
Kulstar
Laetum
Noctua
Pox
Sepulcrum
Staticor
Stug
Talons
Tenet Plinx
Tenet Spirex
Tombfinger
Zakti (Zakti Prime)
Mutually exclusive with Concealed Explosives (cannot be equipped together).
Technically being an AoE weapon, the Sonicor can equip this mod. Both its wide projectile and explosion will pick up Energy orbs.
This mod will make projectiles stop when hitting energy orbs, potentially wasting ammo. This can vary with hitscan weapons.
Weapons with Multishot can pick up energy orbs with just a single pellet/projectile.
Energy orbs picked up this way will trigger on-Energy-pickup effects, like Arcane Energize, Energy Conversion, Equilibrium and Zenurik's Energy Pulse bonus regeneration.
Zenurik's Energy Pulse scales off of the increased energy gained, while Equilibrium (including Equinox's passive) does not.
Bonus does affect Equilibrium's conversion on Energy Orbs from dispensers in Arena gamemodes.
Energy orbs picked up this way during bleedout will revive the player if Equilibrium is equipped.
Increased Energy Orb Effectiveness is additive with the similar increase from Amber Archon Shard, but multiplicative with Dagath's Passive.
Works across the material and Rift planes.

## Energy Channel
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** madurai | **Drain:** 4 | **Compatible:** Melee | **Max Rank:** 10

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Converts 50% of Energy used to up to 50 Bonus Damage on next Melee Attack.
- **Rank 1:** Converts 100% of Energy used to up to 100 Bonus Damage on next Melee Attack.
- **Rank 2:** Converts 150% of Energy used to up to 150 Bonus Damage on next Melee Attack.
- **Rank 3:** Converts 200% of Energy used to up to 200 Bonus Damage on next Melee Attack.
- **Rank 4:** Converts 250% of Energy used to up to 250 Bonus Damage on next Melee Attack.
- **Rank 5:** Converts 300% of Energy used to up to 300 Bonus Damage on next Melee Attack.
- **Rank 6:** Converts 350% of Energy used to up to 350 Bonus Damage on next Melee Attack.
- **Rank 7:** Converts 400% of Energy used to up to 400 Bonus Damage on next Melee Attack.
- **Rank 8:** Converts 450% of Energy used to up to 450 Bonus Damage on next Melee Attack.
- **Rank 9:** Converts 500% of Energy used to up to 500 Bonus Damage on next Melee Attack.
- **Rank 10:** Converts 550% of Energy used to up to 550 Bonus Damage on next Melee Attack.

### Drop Locations
- Axio Moa — Rare (0.03%)
- Minima Moa — Rare (0.03%)
- Moa — Rare (0.03%)
- Orm Moa — Rare (0.03%)
- Ratel — Rare (0.03%)
- Taro Moa — Rare (0.03%)
- Vapos Moa — Rare (0.03%)
- Vorac Moa — Rare (0.03%)
- Artificer — Rare (1.01%)
- Councilor Vay Hek — Rare (1.01%)
- Duviri/Endless: Tier 6 (Normal) — Rare (1.03%)
- Duviri/Endless: Tier 4 (Normal) — Rare (1.49%)
- Hunhow — Rare (2.78%)
- Protector Stalker — Rare (2.78%)
- Shadow Stalker — Rare (2.78%)
- Stalker — Rare (2.78%)

### Wiki
Energy Channel is a mod that converts 50% of energy used to bonus damage on next melee attack and stores up to +50 bonus damage per rank, for a maximum of 200% and +200 at rank 3, respectively.
**Notes:**
Added in Update 8.
Upon striking an enemy, all of the stored energy will be discharged. The strike can be a normal strike, slide attack or a heavy attack.
The stored energy will not discharge when striking terrain or a cargo container.
Be careful not to hit a recently killed enemy, as it may discharge on swing contact.
If the attack hits multiple targets, the bonus damage is only applied to a single target. Additionally, if the attack hits the same target multiple times, the bonus damage is only applied to one hit.
Due to both the ratio and storage capacity scaling, the bonus damage maxes out after spending a total of 100 energy at any rank.
A weapon charged with energy via Energy Channel will glow the energy color of the particular weapon, with pulses of energy washing over it regularly.
Streamline affects energy stored by Energy Channel; it uses the adjusted ability cost.
The bonus damage is added after the critical hit multiplier.
The glowing effect will not apply to the Prova, nor will any other visual effects.
The damage done by Elemental Mods (i.e. Molten Impact) is amplified by this Mod.
The damage is not amplified by damage increasing Mods (i.e. Pressure Point, Killing Blow).
Before Update 13, this mod was called Melee Channel.
**Trivia:**
For Update 23.5, had a blank mod description. This bug was fixed in Update 23.6.

## Energy Conversion
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** madurai | **Drain:** 10 | **Compatible:** WARFRAME | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 5

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired from Cephalon Simaris for 100,000 Standing.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Energy Orbs grant 10% more Ability Strength to your next cast.
- **Rank 1:** Energy Orbs grant 18% more Ability Strength to your next cast.
- **Rank 2:** Energy Orbs grant 26% more Ability Strength to your next cast.
- **Rank 3:** Energy Orbs grant 34% more Ability Strength to your next cast.
- **Rank 4:** Energy Orbs grant 42% more Ability Strength to your next cast.
- **Rank 5:** Energy Orbs grant 50% more Ability Strength to your next cast.

### Drop Locations
- Cephalon Simaris, Energy Conversion
 — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
thumb|48x48px
Energy Conversion is a Warframe mod that increases Ability Strength for the next ability cast after picking up an Energy Orb.
**Notes:**
Does not stack, only one bonus at a time.
Additional Ability Strength is additive.
Ability Strength bonus affects Warframe Augment Mods.
Affects Channeled abilities, such as Exalted Blade and Razorwing, for the duration they are active.
Affects abilities that have a duration for the whole duration. For example, the shadows summoned by Shadows of the Dead keep the increased Ability Strength, even when the shadows are healed/teleported.
There is a very small delay between picking up an Energy Orb and Energy Conversion triggering, often related to host / client ping.
Using abilities while standing on an Energy Orb often will not trigger Energy Conversion.
Health Orbs picked up while using Equilibrium do not provide Ability Strength.
Despite appearing on the player's HUD as a buff (with the same visual and auditory cue as an Arcane Enhancement triggering), it will not be nullified by Nullifier Crewmen nor by passing through Void Portals in Orokin Sabotage.
Universal Orbs trigger this mod's effect upon collection.
**Tips:**
Most effective on Warframes that can reliably spawn Energy Orbs, such as:
Citrine's Fractured Blast
Ember's Exothermic
Follie's Passive
Gara's Spectrosiphon
Hildryn's Aegis Storm
Nova's Passive and Molecular Prime
Oraxia's Mercy's Kiss
Protea's Dispensary
Trinity's Pool of Life
Uriel's Passive
Voruna's Lycath's Hunt
Dethcube's Energy Generator
Broken Scepter interacting with robotic corpses
Glaxion's Photon Overcharge
Duplex Bond

- Reinforced Grineer Storage Container — Common (3.79%)
- Gyre Butcher — Common (3.99%)
- Gyre Elite Lancer — Common (3.99%)

### Wiki
Energy Inversion is a mod that increases the Shield Capacity of an Archwing.
**Notes:**
Consistent with Redirection, the increase is applied to the shield capacity of the Archwing after rank bonuses from levelling up the archwing.
For example: If a maxed Energy Inversion is applied to a level 30 Elytron, the Unranked shield capacity (745) is by the +200 bonus shields provided by Elytron being Rank 30 giving 945. This total is multiplied by 2 (+100% Shield bonus from Energy Inversion) for a grand total of 1890.

## Energy Siphon
**Rarity:** Uncommon | **Polarity:** naramon | **Drain:** -2 | **Compatible:** AURA | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 5

### Acquisition
Available for 20 creds from the Nightwave Cred Offerings store on a rotational basis.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Squad receives +0.1 Energy Regen/s
- **Rank 1:** Squad receives +0.2 Energy Regen/s
- **Rank 2:** Squad receives +0.3 Energy Regen/s
- **Rank 3:** Squad receives +0.4 Energy Regen/s
- **Rank 4:** Squad receives +0.5 Energy Regen/s
- **Rank 5:** Squad receives +0.6 Energy Regen/s

### Drop Locations
- Duviri/Endless: Tier 1 (Normal) — Legendary (0.67%)
- Duviri/Endless: Tier 3 (Normal) — Legendary (0.67%)

### Wiki
Energy Siphon is an aura mod that regenerates the energy pools of a given squad.
* Aura mods increase the amount of Mod Capacity
**Notes:**
Can be stacked with other instances of Energy Siphon for a total of 2.4 energy regeneration per second in regular missions.
It can also stack with Zenurik's Wellspring, reaching a total of 7.4 energy regeneration per second while in a party.
Casting an ability will stop the energy generation for 1 second.
Channeled abilities that drain energy over time, such as Immolation, Sound Quake, and Renewal will completely prevent energy generation for as long as they are active. Abilities with variable energy drain such as Artemis Bow, Pacify & Provoke and Desecrate do not interrupt energy siphon.
The Sortie "Energy Reduction" condition also reduces the rate of energy regeneration.
Being idle for 2 minutes will cause the regeneration to stop.
Using only certain abilities off of cooldown, with the right build and duration/efficiency, Energy Siphon can allow a player to regenerate the energy spent on the ability before it ends, thus letting them use that ability infinitely.

## Energy Transfer
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Equinox | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
Can be acquired by attaining the rank of Maxim under the Arbiters of Hexis, or the rank of Flawless under New Loka, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Mend & Maim Augment: 50% of charge is conserved when switching between forms.
- **Rank 1:** Mend & Maim Augment: 65% of charge is conserved when switching between forms.
- **Rank 2:** Mend & Maim Augment: 80% of charge is conserved when switching between forms.
- **Rank 3:** Mend & Maim Augment: 100% of charge is conserved when switching between forms.

### Drop Locations
- Arbiters of Hexis, Maxim — Common (100.00%)
- New Loka, Flawless — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Energy Transfer is a Warframe Augment Mod for Equinox that allows Mend & Maim to remain active when switching forms via Metamorphosis, transforming the stored damage OR health charge into the corresponding opposite.
**Tips:**
Players can use Metamorphosis to switch back and forth to reapply Maim's Slash damage, bleed proc, and stun on enemies that have already been hit before.
Like all channeled abilities, the Ability Strength will stay the same for the duration of the channeling even after using Metamorphosis.
It is possible to have Mend benefit from Provoke's Ability Strength increase.
22.17.3

## Enraged
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Valkyr | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
Can be acquired by attaining the rank of Partner under The Perrin Sequence, or the rank of Flawless under New Loka, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Hysteria Augment: Damage increased by 200%, Critical Chance increased by 200%. Hysteria lasts for 15s, and receives a matching cooldown.
- **Rank 1:** Hysteria Augment: Damage increased by 250%, Critical Chance increased by 250%. Hysteria lasts for 15s, and receives a matching cooldown.
- **Rank 2:** Hysteria Augment: Damage increased by 300%, Critical Chance increased by 300%. Hysteria lasts for 15s, and receives a matching cooldown.
- **Rank 3:** Hysteria Augment: Damage increased by 350%, Critical Chance increased by 350%. Hysteria lasts for 15s, and receives a matching cooldown.

### Drop Locations
- New Loka, Flawless — Common (100.00%)
- The Perrin Sequence, Partner — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Enraged is a Warframe Augment Mod for Valkyr's Hysteria that increases its damage and critical chance but fixes its duration to 15 seconds and adds a matching cooldown. The base Energy cost is also increased to 50, while the Energy drain over time is removed.
**Notes:**
Cooldown starts after the duration ends, and cannot start while Transferenced out.
Cooldown is equal to the length of time Hysteria was active. For example, if Hysteria was activated for 5 seconds, the cooldown will be 5 seconds.
Duration is affected by Ability Duration.
Bonus damage and critical chance are not affected by mods and stack additively with mods like Pressure Point and True Steel.
Cannot be equipped at the same time as Hysterical Assault.
Recurring bug, where critical bonus mods stack in the simulacrum but not in game.

## Entropy Burst
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** madurai | **Drain:** 4 | **Compatible:** Supra | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod is acquired by attaining the rank of Wise with Cephalon Suda, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +5 Final Status Chance, +0.3 'Entropy'
- **Rank 1:** +10 Final Status Chance, +0.5 'Entropy'
- **Rank 2:** +15 Final Status Chance, +0.8 'Entropy'
- **Rank 3:** +20 Final Status Chance, +1 'Entropy'

### Drop Locations
- Cephalon Suda, Wise — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Entropy Burst is a Weapon Augment Mod exclusive to the Supra and Supra Vandal that increases their status chance and adds an Entropy effect.
**Notes:**
Unlike other rifle mods that affect status chance, Entropy Burst is an additive effect rather than a multiplicative one. This gives the Supra/Supra Vandal, with its base status chance of 30%, a 50% Status Chance (30% + 20%), rather than 36% (30% (1 + 20%)).
Entropy Burst will not affect the values of other status-effect mods like Rifle Aptitude, as its bonus to the base status chance is added after all other multipliers.

### Wiki
Entropy Detonation is a Weapon Augment Mod exclusive to the Obex and Prisma Obex that causes enemies killed by lethal ground finishers to explode, dealing a flat amount of Blast plus a percentage of the enemy's maximum health as Blast damage in an area of effect. It also adds an Entropy effect.
**Notes:**
Only triggers off of ground finishers.
Normal attacks against prone enemies will not trigger this mod.
Explosion requires Line of Sight.

## Enveloping Cloud

### Wiki
Enveloping Cloud is a Warframe Augment Mod for Wukong's Cloud Walker that gives allies near the cloud temporary invisibility.
**Notes:**
Duration is affected by Ability Duration.
Range is affected by Ability Range.
Duration cannot be refreshed. However, allies within the cloud's radius will have their timers paused.
The following characters are affected by Enveloping Cloud:
Ally players
Atlas' Rumblers
Wukong's Companions
Celestial Twin
Warframe Specters
Rescue hostage
The following characters are not affected by Enveloping Cloud:
Wukong himself
Ally syndicate operatives, including Arbitration Defense operatives
Syndicate Spectres
Clem
Kavor Defectors

## Equilibrium
**Rarity:** Uncommon | **Polarity:** naramon | **Drain:** 4 | **Compatible:** WARFRAME | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 10

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Health pickups give +10% Energy. Energy pickups give +10% Health.
- **Rank 1:** Health pickups give +20% Energy. Energy pickups give +20% Health.
- **Rank 2:** Health pickups give +30% Energy. Energy pickups give +30% Health.
- **Rank 3:** Health pickups give +40% Energy. Energy pickups give +40% Health.
- **Rank 4:** Health pickups give +50% Energy. Energy pickups give +50% Health.
- **Rank 5:** Health pickups give +60% Energy. Energy pickups give +60% Health.
- **Rank 6:** Health pickups give +70% Energy. Energy pickups give +70% Health.
- **Rank 7:** Health pickups give +80% Energy. Energy pickups give +80% Health.
- **Rank 8:** Health pickups give +90% Energy. Energy pickups give +90% Health.
- **Rank 9:** Health pickups give +100% Energy. Energy pickups give +100% Health.
- **Rank 10:** Health pickups give +110% Energy. Energy pickups give +110% Health.

### Drop Locations
- Duviri/Endless: Tier 1 (Normal) — Legendary (0.67%)
- Duviri/Endless: Tier 3 (Normal) — Legendary (0.67%)
- Ancient Infested — Rare (7.52%)
- Infested Corpus — Rare (7.52%)
- Infested Grineer — Rare (7.52%)

### Wiki
Equilibrium is a mod that, upon looting a Health Orb or Energy Orb, converts a portion of the restored amount into additional Energy and Health respectively. Converted values are denoted by a Mutate symbol.
**Notes:**
Allows for the pickup of orbs while the associated resource pool is full, for the purpose of conversion. For example, if Health is full but Energy is not, a Health Orb will be picked up and converted to energy.
The final converted restoration is rounded down. For example, with a rank 10 Equilibrium, collecting a small Energy Orb worth 25 Energy will restore 27 Health, rather than 27.5 Health.
Health converted from Energy Orbs will not heal Companions.
The effects of Arcane Energize and Energy Pulse do not apply to energy from converted health orbs. Likewise, Arcane Pulse does not apply to health from converted energy orbs.
Increased Health and/or Energy Orb Effectiveness from Amber Archon Shard, Restorative Bond, or Dagath's Passive is not taken into account and will only use the orb's base values.
As of Update 13.9.3.1, Equilibrium can be acquired from Mod Packs and Transmutation.
Equilibrium stacks with Equinox's passive ability, which converts 10% of Health Orbs into Energy and vice versa, up to a total of 120% efficiency.
This effect is additive with the Violet Archon Shard bonus of Health pickups give +20% (+30%) Energy. Energy pickups give +20% (+30%) Health.
Equilibrium (and Violet Archon Shards, by extension) does work with Universal Orbs, adding a bonus replenishment of Health and Energy.

## Equilibrium Audit
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** penjaga | **Drain:** 0 | **Compatible:** Hound | **Max Rank:** 5

### Acquisition
Equilibrium Audit is automatically acquired upon obtaining a Hound built with the Hec Model.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Unleashes a series of shockwaves that knockdown enemies within 5m and deal 50 damage.
- **Rank 1:** Unleashes a series of shockwaves that knockdown enemies within 7m and deal 100 damage.
- **Rank 2:** Unleashes a series of shockwaves that knockdown enemies within 9m and deal 150 damage.
- **Rank 3:** Unleashes a series of shockwaves that knockdown enemies within 11m and deal 200 damage.
- **Rank 4:** Unleashes a series of shockwaves that knockdown enemies within 13m and deal 250 damage.
- **Rank 5:** Unleashes a series of shockwaves that knockdown enemies within 15m and deal 300 damage.

### Wiki
Equilibrium Audit is a precept mod for Hounds that releases numerous shockwaves to inflict Knockdown and damage.
**Notes:**
Cooldown of 30s.
Three shockwaves are released over several seconds.
Enemies that are already knocked down and are in their "getting up" animation will not be knocked down by subsequent shockwaves.
The Hound is locked into an animation for the duration.
Cannot be equipped with Null Audit and Repo Audit.

## Escape Velocity
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Nova | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

- **Rank 0:** Worm Hole Augment: Allies that travel through wormhole gain a 30% Speed bonus for 4s.
- **Rank 1:** Worm Hole Augment: Allies that travel through wormhole gain a 35% Speed bonus for 5s.

### Wiki
Escape Velocity is a Nova Warframe Augment/Exilus mod for Wormhole that grants Nova and her allies increased Movement Speed after teleporting.
**Notes:**
Movement speed boost is not affected by Ability Strength.
Stacks with Speed and affects archwing.
Duration is affected by Ability Duration.
Speed boost duration is refreshed when affected players teleport through an active Wormhole again.
Despite Escape Velocity's description that it affects allies, it will only apply its speed effect to Warframes and not to allies such as Specters, invasion allies, or allies created with abilities such as Loki's Decoy.
Unlike most other Augment mods, Escape Velocity is also classified as an Exilus mod, and thus can be inserted into a Warframe's Exilus slot.

## Eternal War
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Valkyr | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
Can be acquired by attaining the rank of Partner under The Perrin Sequence, or the rank of Flawless under New Loka, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.
This mod can periodically be acquired from The Circuit.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Warcry Augment: While active, Warcry's duration is increased by 1.25s for each Melee Kill.
- **Rank 1:** Warcry Augment: While active, Warcry's duration is increased by 1.5s for each Melee Kill.
- **Rank 2:** Warcry Augment: While active, Warcry's duration is increased by 1.75s for each Melee Kill.
- **Rank 3:** Warcry Augment: While active, Warcry's duration is increased by 2s for each Melee Kill.

### Drop Locations
- New Loka, Flawless — Common (100.00%)
- The Perrin Sequence, Partner — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Eternal War is a Warframe Augment Mod for Valkyr that extends Warcry's duration for each melee kill.
**Notes:**
The duration increase per enemy killed is affected by Ability Duration.
Kills extend the active duration of Warcry up to a maximum of double the ability's duration after mods.
Kills also extend Warcry's duration for allies.
Only melee kills made by the caster will extend the duration.
Kills with Hysterical Assault's leap do not count as melee kills.
Warcry will persist on allies as long as it persists on Valkyr. This means allies can enter Nullifier Crewman bubbles and fall off the map without losing the buff, but if Valkyr does either of those, everyone will lose the buff.
**Tips:**
One can cast Warcry on their squad at the beginning of a mission, which can then be maintained for the entire mission with Eternal War by rapidly melee-killing enemies, allowing the team to benefit from Warcry while keeping energy expenditure low. This is easiest to do in endless mode missions like Survival, Defense, and Interception. Care should be taken around Nullifier Crewmen and tilesets where one can easily fall off the map, as either will end Warcry prematurely.
**Trivia:**
After maximization, the highest attainable duration for Warcry is 53.55 seconds with 357% duration, meaning Eternal War can never raise its duration past 107.1 seconds, however with invigoration it can be further raised to 68.55 seconds or a max 137.1 seconds.

## Evasive Denial
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** penjaga | **Drain:** 0 | **Compatible:** Hound | **Max Rank:** 5

### Acquisition
Evasive Denial is automatically acquired upon obtaining a Hound built with the Zubb Bracket.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Teleports a safe distance away and engages a displacement field gaining 25% chance to dodge enemy fire for 3s.
- **Rank 1:** Teleports a safe distance away and engages a displacement field gaining 35% chance to dodge enemy fire for 4s.
- **Rank 2:** Teleports a safe distance away and engages a displacement field gaining 45% chance to dodge enemy fire for 5s.
- **Rank 3:** Teleports a safe distance away and engages a displacement field gaining 55% chance to dodge enemy fire for 6s.
- **Rank 4:** Teleports a safe distance away and engages a displacement field gaining 65% chance to dodge enemy fire for 7s.
- **Rank 5:** Teleports a safe distance away and engages a displacement field gaining 75% chance to dodge enemy fire for 8s.

### Wiki
Evasive Denial is a precept mod for Hounds that, upon taking enough damage, teleports it to a safe distance and increases its dodge chance.
**Notes:**
Activates when the Hound loses a significant portion of its EHP (~25%) within a short period.
If damage does not meet the damage/time threshold, Evasive Denial will not activate and the Hound may die.
Cooldown of 15s.
Cannot be equipped with Reflex Denial and Diversified Denial.

## Everlasting Ward
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Chroma | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired by attaining the rank of Genius under Cephalon Suda, or the rank of Partner under The Perrin Sequence, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.
This mod can periodically be acquired from The Circuit.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Elemental Ward Augment: Allies that leave the radius will retain the effect for 50% of the remaining duration.
- **Rank 1:** Elemental Ward Augment: Allies that leave the radius will retain the effect for 65% of the remaining duration.
- **Rank 2:** Elemental Ward Augment: Allies that leave the radius will retain the effect for 80% of the remaining duration.
- **Rank 3:** Elemental Ward Augment: Allies that leave the radius will retain the effect for 100% of the remaining duration.

### Drop Locations
- Cephalon Suda, Genius — Common (100.00%)
- The Perrin Sequence, Partner — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Everlasting Ward is a Warframe Augment Mod for Chroma that allows allies to retain Elemental Ward's effects upon moving out of its range, lasting for a percentage of Elemental Ward's remaining duration.
**Notes:**
There is a bug with Elemental Ward that makes this mod mandatory for anyone with a heat Chroma. The bug occurs when allies gain the buff, they leave the radius, then return to the radius during the same casting of Elemental Ward. Even if allies have taken no damage, upon returning they will keep their normal health and have a huge chunk of empty space where Medi-Ray and other healing sources will attempt to heal, which are wasted in the end.
For example: an ally with 740 health has their health increased to 1000 by a nearby Chroma. They leave, then come back into the radius and now have 740/1000 health, causing Medi-Ray and Curative Undertow to heal the player, wasting the ability or energy respectively.
This is not related to the nerf to Elemental Ward that used to allow players who entered the radius to become fully healed each time, this bug occurred after this nerf occurred. What is supposed to happen, is the ally will only heal once, but still gain the extra health each time they enter the radius.

**Notes:**
Damage bonus will not be applied to Exalted Weapons like Dex Pixia or Regulators.
Stacks additively with base damage bonuses like Hornet Strike.
Despite the description specifying headshots, the effect can be trigger on weak-point hits.
The effect can be refreshed with subsequent eximus headshot/weak-point hits.

## Expel Corpus
**Rarity:** Uncommon | **Polarity:** madurai | **Drain:** 2 | **Compatible:** Pistol | **Max Rank:** 3

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** x1.03 Damage to Corpus
- **Rank 1:** x1.05 Damage to Corpus
- **Rank 2:** x1.08 Damage to Corpus
- **Rank 3:** x1.1 Damage to Corpus

### Drop Locations
- Juno Malleus Machinist — Rare (0.22%)
- Duviri/Endless: Tier 1 (Normal) — Legendary (0.67%)
- Duviri/Endless: Tier 3 (Normal) — Legendary (0.67%)
- Demolisher Anti Moa — Uncommon (3.75%)
- Demolisher Bursa — Uncommon (3.75%)
- Demolisher Hyena — Uncommon (3.75%)
- Demolisher Machinist — Uncommon (3.75%)

### Wiki
Expel Corpus is a mod that increases the Faction Damage of pistols against Corpus.
**Notes:**
Has no effect on Corrupted and Narmer Corpus (e.g. Corrupted Crewman, Narmer Crewman).
Despite being listed as a multiplier, it will stack additively with other sources of Damage to Corpus.
For example, if you had a Riven Mod with x0.5 Damage to Corpus and a max rank Expel Corpus with x1.3 Damage to Corpus, you would have a 1 - 0.5 + 0.3 = x0.8 multiplier for damage against Corpus with both mods equipped.

## Explosive Demise
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** madurai | **Drain:** 4 | **Compatible:** Melee | **Max Rank:** 5

- **Rank 0:** Enemies killed explode, dealing 50 Damage shortly after death.
- **Rank 1:** Enemies killed explode, dealing 100 Damage shortly after death.
- **Rank 2:** Enemies killed explode, dealing 150 Damage shortly after death.
- **Rank 3:** Enemies killed explode, dealing 200 Damage shortly after death.
- **Rank 4:** Enemies killed explode, dealing 250 Damage shortly after death.
- **Rank 5:** Enemies killed explode, dealing 300 Damage shortly after death.

### Drop Locations
- Conclave, Whirlwind — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Explosive Demise is a melee mod in Conclave which causes killed enemies to explode shortly after death, dealing damage to surrounding enemies.
**Notes:**
Enemies explode approximately 5 seconds after death.
Other enemies killed by the explosion will spawn another 5 seconds later.
Explosions will not damage the user.
After the explosion, a small explosion FX will remain for roughly 5 seconds, which will cause Heat procs to occur on anyone who enters it, including the user.

## Explosive Legerdemain
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Mirage | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired by attaining the rank of Maxim under the Arbiters of Hexis, or the rank of Genius under Cephalon Suda, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Sleight of Hand Augment: Ammo and Orbs pickups are turned into proximity mines that deal 500 Damage with a 50% Status Chance.
- **Rank 1:** Sleight of Hand Augment: Ammo and Orbs pickups are turned into proximity mines that deal 600 Damage with a 65% Status Chance.
- **Rank 2:** Sleight of Hand Augment: Ammo and Orbs pickups are turned into proximity mines that deal 800 Damage with a 80% Status Chance.
- **Rank 3:** Sleight of Hand Augment: Ammo and Orbs pickups are turned into proximity mines that deal 1000 Damage with a 100% Status Chance.

### Drop Locations
- Arbiters of Hexis, Maxim — Common (100.00%)
- Cephalon Suda, Genius — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Duration values unranked
Explosive Legerdemain is a Warframe Augment Mod for Mirage that causes Sleight of Hand to turn all eligible Pickups into proximity mines ready to detonate.
**Notes:**
Damage is affected by Ability Strength, whereas status chance is not.
Pickup conversion range and explosion radius are affected by Ability Range.
Pickup conversion range is same as Sleight of Hand trap radius.
Duration of 60 seconds is not affected by Ability Duration.
Duration is paused when it attracts enemies.
Converts Ammo Pickups (Cold damage), Affinity Orbs (Toxin damage), Energy Orbs (Electricity damage), and Health Orbs (Heat damage) into proximity mines.
Universal Orbs will be destroyed without creating any type of proximity mines.
Affected pickups are destroyed in the process and cannot be picked up by Mirage. Ally players' client-side pickups are unaffected.
Proximity mines will detonate when an enemy steps on the mine or when the mine's duration expires.
**Tips:**
This augment mod synergizes extremely well with the Archon mod set, as each proximity trap can proc the four base elemental damage that will trigger four of the five mods' special effects.
This mod may be used for crowd control in missions with tight paths and corridors.
Works very well in Sanctuary Onslaught due to the large amount of enemies.
**Trivia:**
The word legerdemain refers to the skillful use of one's hands in performing sleight-of-hand magic tricks.

## Exposing Harpoon
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** naramon | **Drain:** 4 | **Compatible:** Harpak | **Max Rank:** 5

### Acquisition
Available for 20 from the Nightwave Cred Offerings store on a rotational basis.
Exposing Harpoon was originally acquired by reaching Rank 20 in Nightwave during Series 2: The Emissary or Rank 20 during Nora's Choice.
Beginning with Nightwave Nora's Mix: Vol. 5, it was added to the Nightwave Cred Offerings.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Pulling an enemy will give +100% Critical Chance for 15s.
- **Rank 1:** Pulling an enemy will give +130% Critical Chance for 15s.
- **Rank 2:** Pulling an enemy will give +160% Critical Chance for 15s.
- **Rank 3:** Pulling an enemy will give +200% Critical Chance for 15s.
- **Rank 4:** Pulling an enemy will give +250% Critical Chance for 15s.
- **Rank 5:** Pulling an enemy will give +300% Critical Chance for 15s.

### Wiki
Exposing Harpoon is a Weapon Augment Mod for Harpak that grants increased critical chance for 15 seconds upon pulling an enemy with Alternate Fire.
**Notes:**
Exposing Harpoon is additive with similar mods. For example, with a max rank Exposing Harpoon and Point Strike, Harpak's primary fire will have a 20% (1 + 300% + 150%) critical chance.
Duration is fixed at 15 seconds and can be refreshed.
Although the mod description specifies pulling an enemy, activation only requires hitting an enemy with the secondary fire. This works against enemies that cannot be pulled (such as bosses) and will trigger even if the target point is invulnerable at the time of the attack.
Does not activate when hitting the Condrix.

## Fanged Fusillade
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** naramon | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Rifle | **Max Rank:** 5

### Acquisition
This mod was originally acquired by getting 10 Exposure Points in the Operation: Gate Crash Event.
Was available as a random end-of-mission reward from accomplishing the Endurance challenge of the Emergency Exit Tactical Alert.
Fanged Fusillade is now offered as a periodic item for sale by Baro Ki'Teer costing 375 and 150,000 to purchase. Note that Baro Ki'Teer's stock changes with each appearance, and this mod may not be available on a succeeding appearance.
Fanged Fusillade also has an 8.33% chance to drop from the Eidolon Hydrolyst.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +20% Slash
- **Rank 1:** +40% Slash
- **Rank 2:** +60% Slash
- **Rank 3:** +80% Slash
- **Rank 4:** +100% Slash
- **Rank 5:** +120% Slash

### Drop Locations
- Eidolon Hydrolyst — Rare (8.33%)

### Wiki
Fanged Fusillade is a mod that increases Slash damage of a rifle by 20% per rank for a maximum of 120% at rank 5.
**Notes:**
Can be combined with Sawtooth Clip to provide a 210% increase to Slash damage.
When calculating total raw damage, a weapon will require a Slash damage percentage equal to 75% of its total base damage to equal the raw damage increase provided by a standard elemental mod (which increases corresponding elemental damages by 90%).
The higher the Slash damage percentage over 75%, the more viable this mod will be as opposed to an elemental mod, and vice versa for lower values.
These calculations only pertain to raw damage, and do not take into consideration enemy armor/flesh/shield damage multipliers.
Dread, Flux Rifle, Miter, and Nagantaka are the only weapons that can use this mod effectively, due to having above 75% Slash damage.
**Trivia:**
A fusillade describes a series of multiple shots fired simultaneously or in rapid succession.

Fass Canticle is a Exilus Weapon Mod for Tomes that grants Allies Shield Recharge Rate and Shield Recharge Delay on kill.
**Notes:**
Cannot be equipped alongside Jahu Canticle, Khra Canticle, and Lohk Canticle.
Tome Mods with on-kill conditions do not require the Grimoire to make the killing blow. The Grimoire has to damage the enemy at least once to mark it with Tome effects and be equipped as the held weapon when the marked enemy is killed by other sources, such as Warframe abilities.

### Wiki
Fast Deflection increases the Shield recharge rate and reduces the shield recharge delay of a Warframe.
**Notes:**
A max rank Fast Deflection will increase a Warframe's per-second shield recharge rate from 15 + 5% of max shields to 28.5 + 9.5% of max shields.
Shield regeneration delay reduction is capped at -80%.
Can be combined with Fortitude for +190% shield recharge and -45% shield recharge delay, Vigilante Vigor for +150% shield recharge and -75% shield recharge delay, or both for a total of +250% shield recharge and -75% shield recharge delay.
Fast Deflection is useful on Frames with a low health pool and thin armor rating, like Volt, Hildryn, Mag, Gyre or Caliban who rely heavily on shields to avoid damage.
Reduces the time for the natural shield recharge that brings back the full effectiveness of Shield Gating.
Gauss gains shield recharge and reduced shield recharge delay scaling with his Battery; adding Fast Deflection reduces the threshold at which -75% shield recharge delay is reached and makes his shield charge much more quickly, making him very hard to kill especially in tandem with Arcane Aegis.

### Wiki
Fast Hands is a rifle mod that increases reload speed.
**Notes:**
The calculation of reload time using reload speed is: Reload Time Base Reload Time (1 + Reload Speed Bonus)
At max rank, a +30% reload speed bonus reduces reload times by about 23%.
On Bow type weapons such as the Paris, Fast Hands increases the notching speed of the arrow.
**Trivia:**
Prior to Update 16.7, this mod was usable in PvP games until it was replaced by its own specific PvP mods along with a number of other mods: Quickdraw, Slip Magazine, Tactical Pump, Steady Hands, Stabilizer, Burdened Magazine, Magazine Warp and Terminal Velocity.
The weapon being held by Ember as shown in the mod is the Braton Vandal.

## Fatal Acceleration
**Rarity:** Uncommon | **Polarity:** madurai | **Drain:** 4 | **Compatible:** Shotgun | **Max Rank:** 3

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +10% Projectile Speed
- **Rank 1:** +20% Projectile Speed

### Drop Locations
- Deimos Leaper — Uncommon (0.33%)
- Leaper — Uncommon (0.33%)

- Duviri/Endless: Tier 3 (Normal) — Legendary (0.67%)
- Eidolon Teralyst — Uncommon (22.22%)

### Wiki
Fatal Acceleration is a mod that increases the Projectile Speed of non-hitscan Shotguns, and increases the effective range of shotguns before Damage Falloff takes effect.
**Tips:**
This mod can be used in conjunction with Fomorian Accelerant, effectively doubling the Drakgoon's projectile speed on top of giving each projectile additional ricochets.
**Trivia:**
Previously, the shotgun featured in the mod's picture was the Tigris, which at the time did not get any benefit from the mod's effects.
The picture also featured Volt with his Pulse Helmet, but the dangling ornaments on the back of the helmet were missing.

## Fatal Attraction
**Rarity:** Common | **Polarity:** penjaga | **Drain:** 2 | **Compatible:** Djinn | **Max Rank:** 5

### Acquisition
Fatal Attraction is automatically acquired upon obtaining Djinn.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Enemies within 10m are drawn to you. Once they are within 3.5m, they detonate for 100 Damage. Kill them before detonation to increase lure time and reduce cooldown.
- **Rank 1:** Enemies within 12m are drawn to you. Once they are within 4m, they detonate for 125 Damage. Kill them before detonation to increase lure time and reduce cooldown.
- **Rank 2:** Enemies within 14m are drawn to you. Once they are within 4.5m, they detonate for 150 Damage. Kill them before detonation to increase lure time and reduce cooldown.
- **Rank 3:** Enemies within 16m are drawn to you. Once they are within 5m, they detonate for 180 Damage. Kill them before detonation to increase lure time and reduce cooldown.
- **Rank 4:** Enemies within 18m are drawn to you. Once they are within 5.5m, they detonate for 200 Damage. Kill them before detonation to increase lure time and reduce cooldown.
- **Rank 5:** Enemies within 20m are drawn to you. Once they are within 6m, they detonate for 250 Damage. Kill them before detonation to increase lure time and reduce cooldown.

### Drop Locations
- Corrupted Moa — Uncommon (0.76%)

### Wiki
Fatal Attraction is a precept mod for Djinn to lure enemies into close range before dealing a radial blast of damage.
**Notes:**
It does not pull the enemies close like Mag's Pull, but rather causes them to harmlessly walk towards the sentinel, preventing them from using their weaponry.
Has a 5 second attraction period. If there are not enough enemies within kill range once the period's up, the ability will not activate and it will proceed to cooldown.
During this attraction period, all enemies are drawn to the Sentinel.
Guaranteed Corrosive proc upon activation. Deals damage and apply one proc every second for 5 seconds. The base damage is closer to 330 than 250 (as of Update 29.9.1), 20% of this damage is done every second.
Has a 30 second cooldown.
Gives the Sentinel no experience for activating this ability.
Since Sentinel can gain ally buffs. Fatal Attraction damage can be further enhanced with Rhino's Roar.
The mod will not activate unless nearby enemies are alerted and player is not invisible. If the mod activates and the player change the conditions such as distance to enemies or their visibility, Fatal Attraction will be cancelled.
Has synergies with the Gazal Machete;
Anytime Djinn casts Fatal Attraction, the Gazal Machete gains additional +??% Corrosive damage for 30 seconds.
Kills done by the Gazal Machete will empower Fatal Attraction, dealing additional Corrosive damage on the radial blast. Gazal kill counts does not seem to have a timer on them. Kill count either get spent when Fatal Attraction dealt damage or lost when Djinn dies.
There is no way to display Fatal Attraction damage or Gazal kill count on screen (player can only assume its damage by checking enemy health).
Max kill count seems to be 7 for a total damage of approximately 2800.

### Wiki
Fateful Truth is a Conclave-exclusive stance mod for Nikanas, designed to immobilize and knockdown opponents with swift speed.
Fateful Truth
**Notes:**
Leading Blade can be used as a traversing move, allowing users to dash from point A to point B.
The attack speed will effect its distance it travels to.

## Fear Sense
**Rarity:** Uncommon | **Polarity:** penjaga | **Drain:** 2 | **Compatible:** Kavat | **Max Rank:** 3

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** The Kavat senses the weaknesses of enemies within 25m. For 25s, its attacks deal +50% damage and stagger with a 30% chance of knockdown.
- **Rank 1:** The Kavat senses the weaknesses of enemies within 25m. For 25s, its attacks deal +100% damage and stagger with a 30% chance of knockdown.
- **Rank 2:** The Kavat senses the weaknesses of enemies within 25m. For 25s, its attacks deal +150% damage and stagger with a 30% chance of knockdown.
- **Rank 3:** The Kavat senses the weaknesses of enemies within 25m. For 25s, its attacks deal +200% damage and stagger with a 30% chance of knockdown.

### Drop Locations
- Kavat — Uncommon (6.11%)

### Wiki
Fear Sense is a Kavat-exclusive mod that allows the companion to emit a sonar wave that gives the Kavat increased damage on marked targets, and gives them a chance to stagger targets.
*Cooldown is 10 seconds at all ranks.
**Notes:**
The +200% damage bonus is additive with other sources of base damage, but is multiplicative to both melee damage bonuses (such as Maul) and faction damage bonuses (such as Roar and Summoner's Wrath)
Damage bonus stacks additively with itself if cast multiple times on the same enemy, giving an effective damage multiplier of 3x with 1 cast, 5x with 2 casts, 7x on 3 casts, etc.
Cooldown of 10 seconds.
Dead enemies may still glow for a short time or may begin to glow if the ability is cast as they die. This may cause confusion as the bright color incorrectly implies the enemy is alive even if they are not.
The pulse ignores walls, but the highlight is not visible through them.

## Ferocity
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** penjaga | **Drain:** 0 | **Compatible:** Sahasa Kubrow | **Max Rank:** 5

### Acquisition
Ferocity is automatically acquired upon obtaining a Sahasa Kubrow.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Kubrow attacks an enemy open to finishers with +50% crit chance. If the enemy dies during the attack, enemies within 1m have a 30% chance to be knocked down and opened to finishers.
- **Rank 1:** Kubrow attacks an enemy open to finishers with +100% crit chance. If the enemy dies during the attack, enemies within 2m have a 30% chance to be knocked down and opened to finishers.
- **Rank 2:** Kubrow attacks an enemy open to finishers with +150% crit chance. If the enemy dies during the attack, enemies within 3m have a 30% chance to be knocked down and opened to finishers.
- **Rank 3:** Kubrow attacks an enemy open to finishers with +200% crit chance. If the enemy dies during the attack, enemies within 4m have a 30% chance to be knocked down and opened to finishers.
- **Rank 4:** Kubrow attacks an enemy open to finishers with +250% crit chance. If the enemy dies during the attack, enemies within 5m have a 30% chance to be knocked down and opened to finishers.
- **Rank 5:** Kubrow attacks an enemy open to finishers with +300% crit chance. If the enemy dies during the attack, enemies within 6m have a 30% chance to be knocked down and opened to finishers.

### Drop Locations
- Corrupted Drahk — Legendary (0.01%)
- Drahk — Legendary (0.01%)
- Kubrow — Legendary (0.01%)
- Kuva Drahk — Legendary (0.01%)
- Tamm — Legendary (0.01%)

### Wiki
Ferocity is a precept mod for the Sahasa Kubrow to use finisher attacks with increased Critical Chance on enemies. If the enemy is slain by the finisher, nearby enemies have a chance to be knocked down, opening them to finishers.
**Notes:**
The Kubrow will be wrestled down onto the ground if it fails to kill its target, leaving it vulnerable for a few seconds.
**Trivia:**
Along with Savagery, this is the other Kubrow ability mod that is transmutable.

### Wiki
Fetch is a Companion mod that pulls in Pickups, Ammo, and Mods toward the Warframe from farther away.
**Notes:**
Fetch will still function while the Companion is Incapacitated.
If affected by Confusion from Radiation, Fetch will be disabled until the effect runs out.
Fetch will still pull the loot if you are in the Rift Plane, however, you will not be able to collect these items until you are no longer in the Rift Plane or you bring them in by entering Limbo's Cataclysm.
The following items cannot be attracted by Fetch, despite not requiring to be picked up manually, but can still be attracted by Itzal's Cosmic Crush:
Cetus Wisps
Sentient Cores
Eidolon Shards
Profit-Taker Orb's drops
Exploiter Orb's drops
Will allow Venari to pick up Index points in The Index, sucking them up like normal items when dropped. It also allows the pickup of energy orbs from dispensers around Index maps.
For Venari, Fetch will not have its effect stacked with either another Fetch or Vacuum on Khora's second Companion.
**Trivia:**
In the game files, the mod is called "BeastUniversalVacuum".
Prior to Update 37.0, Vacuum's range was lower by 2 meters than Fetch's.

### Wiki
The Finishing Touch mod increases damage dealt by Stealth Attacks, Counterattack Finishers, and Ground Finishers performed by melee weapons equipped with it.
**Tips:**
Glaive weapons like the Kestrel go well with this mod because of their knockdown ability when thrown. Works especially well versus MOAs if you can get to them fast enough.
Fist weapons such as the Tekko when equipped with the Seismic Palm stance will also synergize well due to many of the combos ending in knockdowns or opening the enemy to a Finisher
Finishing touch does not increase the 8x damage multiplier when performing standard attacks on unalerted enemies (attacks that are not finishing maneuvers such as slide attacks). The same thing applies to melee riven mods that increase or decrease finisher damage.
**Notes:**
The 60% damage increase applies to the total damage dealt by the relevant maneuvers.

## Fireball Frenzy
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Ember | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired by attaining the rank of General under Steel Meridian, or the rank of Exalted under the Red Veil, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Fireball Augment: Hold to cast will grant all allies within 15m an additional 50% Heat Damage to their attacks for 28s.
- **Rank 1:** Fireball Augment: Hold to cast will grant all allies within 15m an additional 65% Heat Damage to their attacks for 32s.
- **Rank 2:** Fireball Augment: Hold to cast will grant all allies within 15m an additional 80% Heat Damage to their attacks for 36s.
- **Rank 3:** Fireball Augment: Hold to cast will grant all allies within 15m an additional 100% Heat Damage to their attacks for 40s.

### Drop Locations
- Red Veil, Exalted — Common (100.00%)
- Steel Meridian, General — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Fireball Frenzy is a Warframe Augment Mod for Ember that allows Fireball to, instead of charging, be held on cast, creating a wave of energy traveling outward from the user that temporarily grants the caster and nearby allies additional Heat damage to all attacks.
**Notes:**
Damage bonus is affected by Ability Strength.
Duration is affected by Ability Duration.
Range is affected by Ability Range.
Adds Heat damage to weapons. This damage bonus does not combine with other elements, but does contribute to DoT.
Does not affect exalted weapons or warframe abilities.
Multiple Fireball Frenzy do not stack; the buff with the highest Ability Strength will take effect.
However if a weaker with more Ability Duration is cast, it will take effect after the stronger buff expires.

## Fired Up
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** madurai | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Sentinel | **Max Rank:** 5

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** 0.8% Heat Damage on weapon per hit. Stacks up to 16%. Resets after 5s with no hits.
- **Rank 1:** 1.7% Heat Damage on weapon per hit. Stacks up to 33%. Resets after 5s with no hits.
- **Rank 2:** 2.5% Heat Damage on weapon per hit. Stacks up to 50%. Resets after 5s with no hits.
- **Rank 3:** 3.4% Heat Damage on weapon per hit. Stacks up to 67%. Resets after 5s with no hits.
- **Rank 4:** 4.2% Heat Damage on weapon per hit. Stacks up to 84%. Resets after 5s with no hits.
- **Rank 5:** 5% Heat Damage on weapon per hit. Stacks up to 100%. Resets after 5s with no hits.

### Drop Locations
- Orokin Drone — Legendary (0.02%)
- Orokin Spectator — Legendary (0.02%)
- Corrupted Crewman — Legendary (0.03%)

### Wiki
Fired Up is a Sentinel mod that accumulates Heat damage to a Sentinel's weapon upon hitting an enemy, resetting after 5 seconds elapsed with no hits.
**Notes:**
Heat element applied by this mod counts for Manifold Bond, since it is applied to the weapon.
Despite adding heat damage only to weapon hits, heat procs from Manifold Bond benefit from the increased damage for the Heat Inherit mechanic.
Heat element applied by this mod will combine with elements already present in the weapon.
Clones created by Duplex Bond do not benefit from Fired Up and do not contribute to stacking it.

Since Firestorm increases blast radius, it increases total blast area with the square of the bonus and the blast volume with the cube of the bonus.
Due to the range increase, this mod lessens the damage falloff from the epicenter of the explosion.
This mod increases the range of self-stagger.
