## Fleeting Expertise
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** naramon | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** WARFRAME | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 5

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +10% Ability Efficiency, -10% Ability Duration
- **Rank 1:** +20% Ability Efficiency, -20% Ability Duration
- **Rank 2:** +30% Ability Efficiency, -30% Ability Duration
- **Rank 3:** +40% Ability Efficiency, -40% Ability Duration
- **Rank 4:** +50% Ability Efficiency, -50% Ability Duration
- **Rank 5:** +60% Ability Efficiency, -60% Ability Duration

### Drop Locations
- Derelict Vault — Rare (4.17%)

### Wiki
Fleeting Expertise is a Corrupted Mod that increases Ability Efficiency but reduces Ability Duration of Warframe abilities.
**Notes:**
The cost of abilities or their drains can not be less than 25% of their original value. Because of this, Ability Efficiency has an indirect cap of +75%.
This means that if combined with a maxed Streamline, any Fleeting Expertise rank above four lowers overall duration without increasing efficiency.
If your total Duration is below 100%, the excess efficiency above 175% will still count towards abilities that drain energy over time.
Efficiency is capped at +75%, however the cap is applied to the result; Therefore efficiency used to compute drain is not capped, although drain is capped.
Abilities that drain energy over time (such as Immolation or Peacemaker) will have varying benefits from this mod depending on the amount of Duration Mods you equip.
Duration mods on these abilities affect the actual length of one second of energy drain.
**Tips:**
Benefits abilities that are not affected by duration the most. Thus, it is recommended to equip this mod on Warframes or builds that are not heavily reliant on Ability Duration (such as Mag or a Desecrate-oriented build for Nekros).
A combination of Continuity and Constitution can almost completely negate the Ability Duration penalty of this mod.
Also, if using a fully ranked Primed Continuity, Ability Duration is only affected by a very small amount of 5%, versus a 2% affect of Continuity and Constitution, but with the advantage of only costing one mod slot and using less mod space.

- Eris/Hymeno (Survival), Rotation B — Rare (6.67%)
- Eris/Nimus (Survival), Rotation B — Rare (6.67%)
- Europa/Cholistan (Excavation), Rotation B — Rare (6.67%)
- Kuva Fortress/Taveuni (Survival), Rotation B — Rare (6.67%)
- Lua/Tycho (Survival), Rotation B — Rare (6.67%)
- Neptune/Despina (Excavation), Rotation B — Rare (6.67%)
- Neptune/Kelashin (Survival), Rotation B — Rare (6.67%)
- Pluto/Hieracon (Excavation), Rotation B — Rare (6.67%)
- Pluto/Palus (Survival), Rotation B — Rare (6.67%)
- Sedna/Amarna (Survival), Rotation B — Rare (6.67%)
- Sedna/Scylla (Survival), Rotation B — Rare (6.67%)
- Sedna/Selkie (Survival), Rotation B — Rare (6.67%)
- Sedna/Yemaja (Survival), Rotation B — Rare (6.67%)
- Uranus/Assur (Survival), Rotation B — Rare (6.67%)
- Uranus/Cupid (Survival), Rotation B — Rare (6.67%)
- Uranus/Ophelia (Survival), Rotation B — Rare (6.67%)

### Wiki
Flow increases the Maximum Energy reserve of a Warframe.
**Notes:**
The energy increase is applied to the base energy reserve of a Warframe after energy gains from Rank Bonuses are applied, but before flat bonuses such as Arcane Battery or Azure Archon Shards.
Example: A rank 0 Loki has an energy pool of 175. When equipped with a max rank Flow, the Warframe would have 175 (1 + Flow) = 350 maximum energy.
A rank 30 Loki has a base energy pool of 175, plus 50 from rank bonuses, for a total of 225 base energy. When equipped with a max rank Flow, the Warframe would have (175 + 50) (1 + Flow) = 450 maximum energy.
This mod is better than Streamline mod for some purposes.
Due to a higher energy pool, abilities can be cast more often. The additional energy allows for more uses than the reduced usage of Streamline.
The additional energy benefits certain mods (such as Quick Thinking and Rage).
The higher energy cap allows toggled duration abilities like Sound Quake and Absorb to last longer.
However, as the mod simply increases the amount of energy the player can store it isn't beneficial when energy is constantly being used. Players who frequently use abilities should consider Streamline or a combination.
For specific Warframe base and maximum values, see Warframes Comparison.

**Notes:**
The status chance bonus is composed of two parts:
A +150% status chance bonus that is always applied. This bonus stacks additively with other status mods equipped.
A status chance bonus that oscillates between 0% and +100.8% as the weapon fires. This bonus is multiplicative to other status chance bonuses.

## Focus Energy
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** madurai | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Melee | **Max Rank:** 3

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +10% Heavy Attack Efficiency, +15% Electricity
- **Rank 1:** +20% Heavy Attack Efficiency, +30% Electricity
- **Rank 2:** +30% Heavy Attack Efficiency, +45% Electricity
- **Rank 3:** +40% Heavy Attack Efficiency, +60% Electricity

### Drop Locations
- Nightmare Mode Rewards, Rotation C — Uncommon (15.49%)

### Wiki
Focus Energy is a mod that increases Heavy Attack Efficiency and Electricity damage on melee weapons by 10% and 15% per rank, at a maximum of 40% and 60% at rank 3, respectively.
**Notes:**
Obtained from Nightmare Mode mission rewards.
Stacks with Reflex Coil, capped at 90% Heavy Attack Efficiency.
The electricity damage stacks with Shocking Touch and Voltaic Strike, and will also combine to form Magnetic, Radiation or Corrosive if their respective elements (Cold, Heat, Toxin) are added.
**Trivia:**
Previously, the in-game description of this mod incorrectly stated:"Convert 10% of energy used to Bonus Damage on next Melee Attack. Store up to +0.2 Bonus Damage"
This was fixed in Update 9.3.
Prior to Update 26, this mod increased Channeling efficiency and Electricity damage on melee weapons.

## Focused Defense
**Rarity:** Common | **Polarity:** vazarin | **Drain:** 4 | **Compatible:** Melee | **Max Rank:** 3

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +5 Parry Angle
- **Rank 1:** +10 Parry Angle
- **Rank 2:** +15 Parry Angle
- **Rank 3:** +20 Parry Angle

### Drop Locations
- Deimos/Cambion Drift (Level 30 - 40 Cambion Drift Bounty), Rotation B — Uncommon (12.50%)
- Deimos/Cambion Drift (Level 30 - 40 Cambion Drift Bounty), Rotation B — Rare (5.38%)
- Deimos/Cambion Drift (Level 30 - 40 Cambion Drift Bounty), Rotation B — Rare (6.85%)
- Lua/Pavlov (Spy), Rotation B — Rare (7.49%)

### Wiki
Focused Defense is an Exilus Weapon Mod for melee that increases Blocking angle.
**Trivia:**
Prior to Update 26, this mod provided an armor bonus while Channeling at the cost of Channeling Efficiency.
Because Channeling while blocking will negate all incoming damage (rendering the Armor increase irrelevant), the effect is only present when Channeling but not blocking.
Even with the highest possible Armor before Abilities (a Valkyr with Steel Fiber and Armored Agility, for a damage resistance of 83.61%), a 20% increase to Armor only results in a 2.35% increase to damage resistance.
Blocking but not Channeling with any melee weapon will reduce damage by a significantly greater amount (the lowest damage block of all melee weapons is 35%).
As the use of abilities blocks the use of channeling this mod has no effect on Warframe abilities.

## Focused Prospectus
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** penjaga | **Drain:** 0 | **Compatible:** Hound | **Max Rank:** 5

### Acquisition
Focused Prospectus is automatically acquired upon obtaining a Hound built with the Frak Stabilizer.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Fires a beam of energy that deals 150 Heat Damage over 2s.
- **Rank 1:** Fires a beam of energy that deals 300 Heat Damage over 2.5s.
- **Rank 2:** Fires a beam of energy that deals 450 Heat Damage over 3s.
- **Rank 3:** Fires a beam of energy that deals 600 Heat Damage over 3.5s.
- **Rank 4:** Fires a beam of energy that deals 750 Heat Damage over 4s.
- **Rank 5:** Fires a beam of energy that deals 900 Heat Damage over 4.5s.

### Wiki
Focused Prospectus is a precept mod for Hounds that fires a continuous beam that deals Heat damage.
**Notes:**
Fires a beam at enemy for 4.5 seconds, pushing them back and dealing 180 Heat damage per tick with a tick rate of 5 and 100% Status Chance.
Targeted enemy is pushed back a significant distance.
Hound is locked into a "bracing" animation for the duration of Focused Prospectus.
Cooldown of 15s.
Cannot be equipped with Synergized Prospectus and Aerial Prospectus.

## Fortitude
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** naramon | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** WARFRAME | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +10% Chance to Resist Knockdown, +25% Shield Recharge
- **Rank 1:** +20% Chance to Resist Knockdown, +50% Shield Recharge
- **Rank 2:** +30% Chance to Resist Knockdown, +75% Shield Recharge
- **Rank 3:** +40% Chance to Resist Knockdown, +100% Shield Recharge

### Drop Locations
- Nightmare Mode Rewards, Rotation B — Uncommon (15.49%)
- Derelict Vault — Rare (4.17%)

### Wiki
Fortitude is a mod that increases chance to resist knockdown effects and increases the shield recharge rate of a Warframe.
Knockdown and stagger animations will not be played when a player successfully resists a knockdown or stagger.
**Notes:**
May be stacked with Sure Footed for complete immunity to knockdown
Kuva Guardians often ignore passive knockdown immunity granted to the player with these mods.
Can be stacked with Fast Deflection to provide a total of +190% shield recharge rate.
Shield recharge modifiers affect the rate at which you gain shields, but Fortitude does not shorten the delay between taking damage and initiating the recharge.
**Trivia:**
Fortitude was added in Update 9.
This mod used to share its icon with Vigor, another Nightmare Mode mod.
In Update 13.0 Vigor got its own icon.

## Four Riders
Fast strikes, powerful slams.
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** madurai | **Drain:** -2 | **Compatible:** Claws | **Max Rank:** 3

### Drop Locations
- Deimos Undying Flyer — Legendary (0.03%)
- Undying Flyer — Legendary (0.03%)
- Kuva Lich Agor Rok (Level 80 - 100) — Rare (0.05%)
- Grineer Manic — Legendary (0.34%)
- Sedna/Undine (Arena) — Legendary (0.40%)

### Wiki
Four Riders is a stance mod for Claw-type weapons, featuring fast strikes and powerful slams.
Can also be equipped to Garuda Talons and Garuda Prime Talons.
**Notes:**
Certain attacks have bonus status chance. Status chance bonus is multiplicative to modded status chance.
The first and second attacks of Three Lessons have 50% bonus status chance.
**Trivia:**
The name of the stance refers to the Four Horsemen of the Apocalypse. This is supported by how the first four combo names are named after each rider. Aggravated Swarm is Pestilence, Hungering Encroachment is Famine/Strife, Raging Conflict is War and Eternal Fall is Death.
Initially, the stance was thought to be bugged so that it can only be equipped on Fists and not claws. It was later found out that there were two separate versions of the stance — one for fists (which had a Vazarin polarity) and one for claws (which had a Madurai polarity). This was eventually confirmed to be a bug, and the fists variant was subsequently removed.
Most of the stance's attacks are sideways slashes clearly suited for the Ripkas, though they are equally effective with all claws.

## Fracturing Crush
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Mag | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
Can be acquired by attaining the rank of Partner under The Perrin Sequence, or the rank of Flawless under New Loka, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.
This mod can periodically be acquired from The Circuit.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Crush Augment: Crush gains +50% casting speed. The armor of surviving enemies decreases by 30% and they are unable to move for 4s.
- **Rank 1:** Crush Augment: Crush gains +50% casting speed. The armor of surviving enemies decreases by 45% and they are unable to move for 5s.
- **Rank 2:** Crush Augment: Crush gains +50% casting speed. The armor of surviving enemies decreases by 60% and they are unable to move for 6s.
- **Rank 3:** Crush Augment: Crush gains +50% casting speed. The armor of surviving enemies decreases by 75% and they are unable to move for 7s.

### Drop Locations
- New Loka, Flawless — Common (100.00%)
- The Perrin Sequence, Partner — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Fracturing Crush is a Warframe Augment Mod for Mag that increases the Casting Speed of Crush and allows to permanently reduce armor and temporarily immobilize survivors.
**Notes:**
Immobilize duration is affected by Ability Duration.
Armor reduction is affected by Ability Strength, fully removing armor at 134% Ability Strength. Using Corrosive Projection will allow a full strip at 110% Ability Strength.
Immobilized enemies are unable to move and forced to stand still, but are still able to look around and attack.
Recasting Crush refreshes the immobilize duration.

## Frail Momentum
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** madurai | **Drain:** 4 | **Compatible:** Shotgun | **Max Rank:** 5

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +15% Fire Rate, -2.5% Damage
- **Rank 1:** +30% Fire Rate, -5% Damage
- **Rank 2:** +45% Fire Rate, -7.5% Damage
- **Rank 3:** +60% Fire Rate, -10% Damage
- **Rank 4:** +75% Fire Rate, -12.5% Damage
- **Rank 5:** +90% Fire Rate, -15% Damage

### Drop Locations
- Derelict Vault — Rare (4.17%)

### Wiki
Frail Momentum is a Corrupted Mod that increases the fire rate of shotguns at the cost of damage by 15% and -2.5% per rank at a maximum of 90% and -15% at rank 5, respectively.
**Notes:**
Can be combined with Shotgun Barrage and Accelerated Blast to provide a 240% increase in fire rate.
Unlike Anemic Agility and Vile Acceleration, Frail Momentum gives the same amount of fire rate as the non-corrupt equivalent, Shotgun Barrage. As such, Frail Momentum should only be used in conjunction with Shotgun Barrage in builds aiming to maximize fire rate.
The negative damage boost is additive with other damage boosting mods such as Point Blank. Combining a maxed Point Blank (+90% damage) with Frail Momentum will result in a total damage bonus of +75%.
The actual net damage loss when combined with a maxed Point Blank is 7.9%.
The actual net damage loss when combined with a maxed Primed Point Blank is 5.7%.

## Freeze Force
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Frost | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired by attaining the rank of General under Steel Meridian, or the rank of Genius under Cephalon Suda, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Freeze Augment: Hold to cast will grant all allies within 15m an additional 50% Cold Damage to their attacks for 28s.
- **Rank 1:** Freeze Augment: Hold to cast will grant all allies within 15m an additional 65% Cold Damage to their attacks for 32s.
- **Rank 2:** Freeze Augment: Hold to cast will grant all allies within 15m an additional 80% Cold Damage to their attacks for 36s.
- **Rank 3:** Freeze Augment: Hold to cast will grant all allies within 15m an additional 100% Cold Damage to their attacks for 40s.

### Drop Locations
- Cephalon Suda, Genius — Common (100.00%)
- Steel Meridian, General — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Freeze Force is a Warframe Augment Mod for Frost that allows Freeze to be held on cast, creating a wave of energy traveling outward from the user that temporarily grants the caster and nearby allies additional Cold damage to all attacks.
**Notes:**
Damage bonus is affected by Ability Strength.
Duration is affected by Ability Duration.
Range is affected by Ability Range.
Add Cold damages to the weapon. This damage bonus does not combine with other elements.
Does not affect Exalted Weapons or Warframe Abilities.
Multiple Freeze Forces do not stack; the buff with the highest Ability Strength will take effect.
However, if a weaker buff with a longer Ability Duration is cast, it will take effect after the stronger buff expires.

## Frigid Blast
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** madurai | **Drain:** 4 | **Compatible:** Shotgun | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition

Drop Locations:
Pago
Originally this mod could be acquired from Operation Cryotic Front by scoring more than 1000 points in a single mission.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +15% Cold, +15% Status Chance
- **Rank 1:** +30% Cold, +30% Status Chance
- **Rank 2:** +45% Cold, +45% Status Chance
- **Rank 3:** +60% Cold, +60% Status Chance

### Drop Locations
- Kuva Fortress/Pago (Spy), Rotation B — Uncommon (10.00%)
- Eris/Cyath (Spy), Rotation C — Rare (8.60%)
- Eris/Gnathos (Spy), Rotation C — Rare (8.60%)

### Wiki
Frigid Blast is a mod that increases both the Cold damage and status chance of a shotgun by 15% per rank for a maximum of 60% at rank 3.
**Notes:**
Frigid Blast is additive with similar mods. For example, a Strun with a max rank Frigid Blast and Shotgun Savvy will have a 20% (1 + 60% + 90%) status chance.
This mod can be combined with Toxic Barrage, Shell Shock and Scattering Inferno to achieve a combined status chance bonus of 240%. With Shotgun Savvy, this bonus can be increased to 330%.
Can be combined with Chilling Reload and Chilling Grasp for a total of +210% additional Cold damage.

## Frost Jaw
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** madurai | **Drain:** 4 | **Compatible:** Claws | **Max Rank:** 3

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +15% Cold, +15% Status Chance
- **Rank 1:** +30% Cold, +30% Status Chance

### Wiki
Frostbite is a mod that increases both the Cold damage and status chance of a sidearm by 15% per rank for a maximum of 60% at rank 3.
**Notes:**
Frostbite is additive with similar mods. For example, a Sicarus with a max rank Frostbite and Sure Shot will have a 6% (1 + 60% + 90%) status chance.
This mod can be combined with Jolt, Pistol Pestilence and Scorch to achieve a combined status chance bonus of 240%. With Sure Shot and Stunning Speed, this bonus can be increased to 360%. With Saxum Spittle, Jugulus Spines, and Carnis Stinger, this bonus can be increased to 540%.

## Full Contact
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** naramon | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Shotgun | **Max Rank:** 5

### Acquisition
This mod is acquired by getting 1000 Points in the Operation False Profit Event.
It is now offered as a periodic item for sale by Baro Ki'Teer, costing 365 and 220,000 to purchase. Note that Baro Ki'Teer's stock changes with each appearance, and this mod may not be available on a succeeding appearance.
After the addition of the Plains of Eidolon, the mod may also obtainable by killing Eidolon Teralyst.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +20% Impact
- **Rank 1:** +40% Impact
- **Rank 2:** +60% Impact
- **Rank 3:** +80% Impact
- **Rank 4:** +100% Impact
- **Rank 5:** +120% Impact

### Drop Locations
- Eidolon Teralyst — Rare (8.33%)

### Wiki
The Full Contact mod increases Impact damage of shotguns by 20% per rank for a maximum of 120% at rank 5.
**Notes:**
Can be combined with Disruptor to provide a 210% increase to Impact damage.
When calculating total raw damage, a weapon will require an Impact damage percentage equal to 75% of its total base damage to equal the raw damage increase provided by a standard elemental mod (which increases corresponding elemental damages by 90%).
The higher the Impact damage percentage over 75%, the more viable this mod will be as opposed to an elemental mod, and vice versa for lower values.
These calculations only pertain to raw damage, and do not take into consideration enemy armor/flesh/shield damage multipliers.
The only weapons that are at or above the 75% Impact damage percentage are Sobek and Sweeper /Prime.

## Fulmination
Improves the Blast Radius of weapons with Radial Attacks.
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** madurai | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Pistol | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
Fulmination was first acquired from Ergo Glast in a Tenno relay for 75 Animo Beacons during the Operation: Ambulas Reborn event.
It returned as an offering sold by Nakak (as Operational Supply) in Cetus during the Operation: Plague Star event for 5,000 and 1,500 Standing Operational Supply Standing.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +6% Blast Range
- **Rank 1:** +12% Blast Range
- **Rank 2:** +18% Blast Range
- **Rank 3:** +24% Blast Range

### Drop Locations
- Duviri/Endless: Tier 6 (Normal) — Rare (1.03%)
- Duviri/Endless: Tier 4 (Normal) — Rare (1.49%)
- Operational Supply, Neutral — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Fulmination is a secondary weapon mod that increases the blast radius of weapons with radial attacks.
**Notes:**
While it can be installed on any secondary weapon, Fulmination only has noticeable effects on the following explosive weapons:Aegrit
Akarius (Akarius Prime)
Angstrum (Prisma Angstrum)
Incarnon mode of the Atomos
Balefire Charger
Castanas (Sancti Castanas)
Catabolyst (Coda Catabolyst)
Cyanex
Incarnon mode of the Despair
Epitaph (Epitaph Prime)
Kompressa (Kompressa Prime
Kulstar
Kuva Seer
Incarnon mode of the Laetum
Pox
Sepulcrum
Sonicor
Sporelacer
Staticor
Stug
Tenet Spirex
Thrown weapons equipped with Concealed Explosives
Tombfinger
Talons
Zakti (Zakti Prime)
Zymos
Grimoire Alternate fire
Glory
An increase of 24% in blast radius adds up to a 53.8% increase in the total blast area and a 90.6% increase in the total blast volume.
Due to the range increase, this mod lessens the damage falloff from the epicenter of the explosion.
This mod increases the range of self-stagger.

## Funnel Clouds
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Zephyr | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired by attaining the rank of Exalted under the Red Veil, or the rank of Flawless under New Loka, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Tornado Augment: Creates 2 additional tornadoes. All tornadoes are 50% their original size and won't pick up enemies.
- **Rank 1:** Tornado Augment: Creates 4 additional tornadoes. All tornadoes are 50% their original size and won't pick up enemies.
- **Rank 2:** Tornado Augment: Creates 6 additional tornadoes. All tornadoes are 50% their original size and won't pick up enemies.
- **Rank 3:** Tornado Augment: Creates 8 additional tornadoes. All tornadoes are 50% their original size and won't pick up enemies.

### Drop Locations
- New Loka, Flawless — Common (100.00%)
- Red Veil, Exalted — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Funnel Clouds is a Warframe Augment Mod for Zephyr that increases the number of active Tornado while decreasing their size and removes their ability to pick up enemies.
**Notes:**
Funnel Clouds adds additional tornadoes, yielding a total of 11 tornadoes per cast at max rank. All tornadoes summoned are only 50% of their regular size.
Wandering Tornadoes can still be guided by the player.
Tornadoes will move faster than their non-augmented counterparts.
All tornadoes are summoned regardless of the number of nearby enemies.
Tornadoes deal as much damage as full-sized tornadoes, just to fewer enemies due to their size.
Tornadoes will no longer innately lift enemies off the ground and trap them.
Enemies in a ragdolled state, such as those affected by Airburst, will then be picked up and trapped by tornadoes.
Tornadoes will patrol randomly in all directions and move toward enemies in range.
**Tips:**
Funnel Clouds is well-suited to a strength and duration based Zephyr build, as the tornadoes can dish out massive amounts of damage without help from players' weapons.
**Trivia:**
A funnel cloud is a funnel-shaped cloud of condensed water droplets, associated with twisting columns of wind and extending from the base of a cloud, but not touching the ground.

## Furious Javelin
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Excalibur | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired by attaining the rank of General under Steel Meridian, or the rank of Maxim under the Arbiters of Hexis, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.
This mod can periodically be acquired from The Circuit.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Radial Javelin Augment: Each enemy hit will increase Excalibur's Melee Damage by 8% for 9s.
- **Rank 1:** Radial Javelin Augment: Each enemy hit will increase Excalibur's Melee Damage by 10% for 11s.
- **Rank 2:** Radial Javelin Augment: Each enemy hit will increase Excalibur's Melee Damage by 12% for 13s.
- **Rank 3:** Radial Javelin Augment: Each enemy hit will increase Excalibur's Melee Damage by 15% for 16s.

### Drop Locations
- Arbiters of Hexis, Maxim — Common (100.00%)
- Steel Meridian, General — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Furious Javelin is a Warframe Augment Mod that temporarily increases Excalibur's melee damage for every enemy hit by Radial Javelin.
**Notes:**
A rippling aura will appear under Excalibur when the melee damage buff is active. This aura will become brighter with every cast of Radial Javelin.
Duration of melee damage buff is affected by Ability Duration.
The bonus melee damage is affected by Ability Strength.
Melee Damage bonus stacks multiplicatively with other sources of Melee Damage.
For example, with a maxed Pressure Point and Intensify equipped, 5 enemies hit, and a weapon that deals 100 damage, the modified damage will be:Base Damage (1 + Damage Mods) (1 + Furious Javelin Bonus (1 + Ability Strength) #Enemies Hit) 100 (1 + 1.2) (1 + 0.15 (1 + 0.3) 5)
Does not multiply with Condition Overload bonus.
Recasts refresh the duration, but override the previous bonus, regardless of whether it was higher or lower.
The damage bonus applies to Exalted Blade and its waves as well.

### Wiki
Fury is a mod that increases the attack speed of a melee weapon by 5% per rank for a maximum of 30% at rank 5.
**Notes:**
This mod is best used for the following setups:
For weapons with a combination of high damage potential and attack speed, such as the Orthos Prime and Dual Kamas.
For weapons with slow attack speed but high damage per hit to eliminate its weakness through slow attack speed, like the Jat Kittag.
For weapons with stun capabilities to increase its chance to stun enemies.
This mod affects Charge Attack release animation, but it does not affects charge time.
Compared to Quickening, this mod costs the same number of points while giving 10% less attack speed and no combo count bonus.
Cannot be equipped with Berserker Fury.
**Trivia:**
A Primed version was leaked in update Update 17.11 and was patched in Update 17.11.0.1.

## Fused Crucible
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Qorvex | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired by attaining the rank of Genius under Cephalon Suda, or the rank of General under Steel Meridian, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Crucible Blast Augment: Becomes a channeled ability consuming 35 Energy/s that ramps up. While active Chyrinka Pillars trigger chain reactions and their duration is frozen. Mobility is greatly reduced.
- **Rank 1:** Crucible Blast Augment: Becomes a channeled ability consuming 30 Energy/s that ramps up. While active Chyrinka Pillars trigger chain reactions and their duration is frozen. Mobility is greatly reduced.
- **Rank 2:** Crucible Blast Augment: Becomes a channeled ability consuming 25 Energy/s that ramps up. While active Chyrinka Pillars trigger chain reactions and their duration is frozen. Mobility is greatly reduced.
- **Rank 3:** Crucible Blast Augment: Becomes a channeled ability consuming 20 Energy/s that ramps up. While active Chyrinka Pillars trigger chain reactions and their duration is frozen. Mobility is greatly reduced.

### Wiki
Fused Crucible is a Warframe Augment Mod for Qorvex's Crucible Blast that changes it into channeled ability with a ramping energy drain. While active, Qorvex can move with reduced mobility and any active Chyrinka Pillars will have their durations paused and be forcibly empowered.
**Notes:**
Qorvex is completely Invulnerable while channeling Fused Crucible.
Qorvex can move at 50% Movement Speed and can only perform the Rolling maneuver at 40% reduced speed while channeling Fused Crucible.
Energy drain and energy ramp up drain are both affected by Ability Efficiency and Ability Duration.
Recasting the ability will reset the energy ramp-up.
While not reflected in associated tool tips, Ability Duration will impact how quickly it drains energy, in line with other channeled abilities.
Fused Crucible will end if Qorvex runs out of energy, or if deactivated by pressing the ability key again (default 4).
Qorvex cannot replenish energy using Energy Vampire, Rally Point, Rift Plane's innate energy regeneration, Squad Energy Restores, Energy Siphon, Energy Nexus, Dreamer's Bond, Wellspring, Rage, and/or Hunter Adrenaline while Fused Crucible is active.
Fused Crucible's energy drain is not converted into shields by Augur Mods.
Energy can be replenished by Energy Orbs, Orokin Void Death Orb's energy restores, Equilibrium, Spellbound Harvest, Arcane Energize, and Emergence Dissipate's Energy Motes while the ability is active.

## Fused Reservoir
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Wisp | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired by attaining the rank of Genius under Cephalon Suda, or the rank of Flawless under New Loka, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Reservoirs Augment: Adds a fourth reservoir that gives the effects of all three. Costs 300% more Energy.
- **Rank 1:** Reservoirs Augment: Adds a fourth reservoir that gives the effects of all three. Costs 270% more Energy.
- **Rank 2:** Reservoirs Augment: Adds a fourth reservoir that gives the effects of all three. Costs 250% more Energy.
- **Rank 3:** Reservoirs Augment: Adds a fourth reservoir that gives the effects of all three. Costs 200% more Energy.

### Drop Locations
- Cephalon Suda, Genius — Common (100.00%)
- New Loka, Flawless — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Fused Reservoir is a Warframe Augment Mod/Exilus Mod for Wisp that allows her to spawn a fourth Reservoirs with all three buffs at an increased energy cost. Fused Reservoir counts as 3 summoned Reservoirs out of the 6 total wisp may have at once.
**Notes:**
Energy cost is not affected by mods.
Fused Reservoir counts as 3 summoned Reservoirs and Wisp is still limited to 6 total Reservoirs at once, meaning only 2 of the combined versions can be used at any given time.
Doubles the energy cost of using the combined version, rather than tripling it, saving 25 base energy when using the combined version.
The combined version has all three motes, regardless of whether Wisp is of a high enough level to have them.
Missions will start with the Fused Reservoir automatically selected.
**Tips:**
Useful for Empowered builds, requiring only one instance of Empower to charge all three versions at once, saving energy.
Because it simply adds another type of Reservoir and does not remove the normal ones, the player can still mix and match to place more motes in more locations if necessary (such as Defection), keeping in mind the 6 max Reservoirs still applies.

## Gaia's Tragedy
Slow, powerful strikes.
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** vazarin | **Drain:** -2 | **Compatible:** Fists | **Max Rank:** 3

### Drop Locations

- Datalyst — Uncommon (1.00%)

- Fog Scrambus — Uncommon (1.00%)

- Juno Nul Comba — Uncommon (1.00%)
- Juno Nullifier Crewman — Uncommon (1.00%)
- Juno Sap Comba — Uncommon (1.00%)
- Juno Slo Comba — Uncommon (1.00%)
- Juno Sniper Crewman — Uncommon (1.00%)
- Nul Comba — Uncommon (1.00%)
- Nul Scrambus — Uncommon (1.00%)
- Orm Pilot — Uncommon (1.00%)
- Sap Comba — Uncommon (1.00%)
- Sap Scrambus — Uncommon (1.00%)
- Scrofa Research Chief — Uncommon (1.00%)
- Slo Comba — Uncommon (1.00%)
- Slo Scrambus — Uncommon (1.00%)
- Taro Pilot — Uncommon (1.00%)
- Vapos Bioengineer — Uncommon (1.00%)
- Vorac Pilot — Uncommon (1.00%)

### Wiki
Gaia's Tragedy is a stance mod for Fist type weapons.
**Notes:**
Many of the attacks do bonus Slash damage.
The third and fourth attacks of River's Grief do 100% bonus Slash damage
The first attack and third attacks of Ocean's Contempt do 100% bonus Slash damage.
Every attack of Mountain's Rage does 100% bonus Slash damage.
The second and third attacks of Forest's Remorse do 100% bonus Slash damage.
Certain Attacks have bonus Status Chance. Status chance bonus is multiplicative to modded status chance.
The last attack of Ocean's Contempt has a bonus 100% status chance.
The first and third attacks of Mountain's Rage have a bonus 100% status chance.
The radial slam damage on the first and second attacks of Mountain's Rage does not generate combo. Additionally, the 2.5 multiplier on the second hit is rounded down to 2 combo points per hit.
**Trivia:**
Gaia was the Ancient Greek personification of the earth and one of the primordial deities. She was considered the mother of all living things.
The name of this stance (and the fact that Atlas is depicted on the mod) is probably linked to the death of Antaeus (who was one of Gaia's sons) at the hands of Heracles.

## Gale Kick
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** madurai | **Drain:** 4 | **Compatible:** WARFRAME | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +25% of Melee Damage converted to Impact Damage on Jump Kick, knocking down nearby enemies on kill.
- **Rank 1:** +50% of Melee Damage converted to Impact Damage on Jump Kick, knocking down nearby enemies on kill.
- **Rank 2:** +75% of Melee Damage converted to Impact Damage on Jump Kick, knocking down nearby enemies on kill.
- **Rank 3:** +100% of Melee Damage converted to Impact Damage on Jump Kick, knocking down nearby enemies on kill.

### Drop Locations
- Amalgam Arca Heqet — Uncommon (1.00%)
- Amalgam Arca Kucumatz — Uncommon (1.00%)
- Amalgam Cinder Machinist — Uncommon (1.00%)
- Amalgam Phase Moa — Uncommon (1.00%)
- Amalgam Swarm Satyr — Uncommon (1.00%)
- Duviri/Endless: Tier 6 (Normal) — Rare (1.03%)
- Duviri/Endless: Tier 4 (Normal) — Rare (1.49%)

### Wiki
Gale Kick is an Exilus mod that makes Jump Kicks deal a percentage of the equipped melee weapon's damage as Impact damage. If the target dies from the kick, nearby enemies will be knocked down.
**Notes:**
Modifiers that do affect Gale Kick:
Base damage of the melee weapon.
Base damage mods (Pressure Point, Steel Charge, Justice Blades, Energy Channel, Scindo Manticore Skin etc.)
Energy Channel's bonus is permanent as long as the player does not swing their actual melee weapon to discharge the bonus damage.
Furious Javelin
Having an Exalted melee weapon active will make Gale Kick scale from it, including damage granted by Ability Strength.
Modifiers that do not affect Gale Kick:
Physical or elemental damage mods (Collision Force, Fever Strike, etc.)
Faction damage mods (Smite Corpus etc.)
Sliding or parkour velocity mods (Maglev, Endurance Drift etc.)
Conditional mods (Healing Return, Condition Overload etc.)
Shattering Impact
Void Strike
Riven Mods that have a base damage boost stat
Jump kicks normally deal impact damage equal to 20 + 100% melee base damage (including all of the above damage interactions), with Gale Kick increasing the total damage to 20 + 200%
**Trivia:**
Kills done in this way are not tallied as kills (melee or otherwise) in the stats screen at the end of a mission, allowing solo players to complete a Jump Kick-only Exterminate mission with zero kills.

## Galvanized Acceleration
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** madurai | **Drain:** 2 | **Compatible:** Shotgun | **Max Rank:** 10

### Acquisition
This mod can be bought from the Arbitrations vendor in the Arbiters of Hexis room in Relays for 20 Vitus Essence.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +2.7% Projectile Speed and +2.7% Beam Range, On Kill: +2.7% Projectile Speed and +2.7% Beam Range for 10s. Stacks up to 2x.
- **Rank 1:** +5.5% Projectile Speed and +5.5% Beam Range, On Kill: +5.5% Projectile Speed and +5.5% Beam Range for 10s. Stacks up to 2x.
- **Rank 2:** +8.2% Projectile Speed and +8.2% Beam Range, On Kill: +8.2% Projectile Speed and +8.2% Beam Range for 10s. Stacks up to 2x.
- **Rank 3:** +10.9% Projectile Speed and +10.9% Beam Range, On Kill: +10.9% Projectile Speed and +10.9% Beam Range for 10s. Stacks up to 2x.
- **Rank 4:** +13.6% Projectile Speed and +13.6% Beam Range, On Kill: +13.6% Projectile Speed and +13.6% Beam Range for 10s. Stacks up to 2x.
- **Rank 5:** +16.4% Projectile Speed and +16.4% Beam Range, On Kill: +16.4% Projectile Speed and +16.4% Beam Range for 10s. Stacks up to 2x.
- **Rank 6:** +19.1% Projectile Speed and +19.1% Beam Range, On Kill: +19.1% Projectile Speed and +19.1% Beam Range for 10s. Stacks up to 2x.
- **Rank 7:** +21.8% Projectile Speed and +21.8% Beam Range, On Kill: +21.8% Projectile Speed and +21.8% Beam Range for 10s. Stacks up to 2x.
- **Rank 8:** +24.5% Projectile Speed and +24.5% Beam Range, On Kill: +24.5% Projectile Speed and +24.5% Beam Range for 10s. Stacks up to 2x.
- **Rank 9:** +27.3% Projectile Speed and +27.3% Beam Range, On Kill: +27.3% Projectile Speed and +27.3% Beam Range for 10s. Stacks up to 2x.
- **Rank 10:** +30% Projectile Speed and +30% Beam Range, On Kill: +30% Projectile Speed and +30% Beam Range for 10s. Stacks up to 2x.

### Wiki
Galvanized Acceleration is the Galvanized variant of Fatal Acceleration, increasing Projectile Speed/Beam Length and increases the effective range of shotguns before Damage Falloff takes effect. Kills grant a buff that further increases projectile speed/beam length for 10 seconds and stacks up to 2 times.
**Notes:**
When the buff times out, one stack is lost and the buff duration resets.
Only kills from a weapon equipped with this mod will give stacks, and only the weapon equipped with this mod will benefit from the buff. If equipped on both a normal weapon and an Exalted Weapon, it will affect both weapons.
Kills from status procs from a weapon equipped with this mod will give stacks.
Sinister Reach will not be affected by this mod as it gives a flat beam length increase after percent bonuses.
Currently, there are no Galvanized counterparts of this mod for primary rifle/primary beam weapons, nor secondary/secondary beam weapons (there are no Galvanized variants of Terminal Velocity, Sinister Reach, Lethal Momentum and Ruinous Extension).
Similarly, while primary rifles and secondaries have access to Galvanized Scope and Galvanized Crosshairs, both of which are Galvanized variants of Argon Scope and Hydraulic Crosshairs, primary shotguns do not have a Galvanized variant of Laser Sight, which functions identically to the two mods mentioned (but grants a slightly lower critical chance bonus).

## Galvanized Aptitude
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** vazarin | **Drain:** 2 | **Compatible:** Rifle | **Max Rank:** 10

### Acquisition
This mod can be bought from the Arbitrations vendor in the Arbiters of Hexis room in Relays for 20 Vitus Essence.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +7.3% Status Chance, On Kill: +3.6% Direct Damage per Status Type affecting the target for 20s. Stacks up to 2x.
- **Rank 1:** +14.5% Status Chance, On Kill: +7.3% Direct Damage per Status Type affecting the target for 20s. Stacks up to 2x.
- **Rank 2:** +21.8% Status Chance, On Kill: +10.9% Direct Damage per Status Type affecting the target for 20s. Stacks up to 2x.
- **Rank 3:** +29.1% Status Chance, On Kill: +14.5% Direct Damage per Status Type affecting the target for 20s. Stacks up to 2x.
- **Rank 4:** +36.4% Status Chance, On Kill: +18.2% Direct Damage per Status Type affecting the target for 20s. Stacks up to 2x.
- **Rank 5:** +43.6% Status Chance, On Kill: +21.8% Direct Damage per Status Type affecting the target for 20s. Stacks up to 2x.
- **Rank 6:** +50.9% Status Chance, On Kill: +25.5% Direct Damage per Status Type affecting the target for 20s. Stacks up to 2x.
- **Rank 7:** +58.2% Status Chance, On Kill: +29.1% Direct Damage per Status Type affecting the target for 20s. Stacks up to 2x.
- **Rank 8:** +65.5% Status Chance, On Kill: +32.7% Direct Damage per Status Type affecting the target for 20s. Stacks up to 2x.
- **Rank 9:** +72.7% Status Chance, On Kill: +36.4% Direct Damage per Status Type affecting the target for 20s. Stacks up to 2x.
- **Rank 10:** +80% Status Chance, On Kill: +40% Direct Damage per Status Type affecting the target for 20s. Stacks up to 2x.

### Wiki
Galvanized Aptitude is the Galvanized variant of Rifle Aptitude, increasing status chance. Kills grant a buff that increases direct hit damage (not area-of-effect) for each status type affecting the target for 20 seconds and stacks up to 2 times.
**Notes:**
The damage increase is usually additive with other damage mods like Serration, with exceptions where it is multiplicative. See Condition Overload (Mechanic) for more details.
The damage increase is not applied on the hit that procs a new status effect, it is only applied on following hits.
For example: Galvanized Aptitude has 2 stacks, weapon has 100 base damage and Serration for a total of 100 * (1 + Serration) damage. First hit procs Impact and deals 265 damage. Then on the following hit the +80% damage from the 2 stacks is applied for a total of 100 * (1 + Serration + Galvanized Aptitude) damage.
However, Multishot instances are treated as sequential hits, allowing a weapon with multishot to benefit from the damage increase even on the first trigger pull.
For example: Galvanized Aptitude has 2 stacks, weapon has 100 base damage, Serration, and a multishot of 3.
The first multishot instance deals 100 * (1 + Serration) damage and procs Impact.
The second multishot instance will then apply the +80% damage from 2 stacks, for a total of 100 * (1 + Serration + Galvanized Aptitude) damage.
If the second instance procs Puncture, then the third instance will apply +160% damage (80% for 2 stacks, times 2 different status effects), for a total of 100 * (1 + Serration + Galvanized Aptitude) damage.
Thus, in one trigger pull the weapon will have dealt 1035 total damage, instead of the 795 that would have been dealt otherwise.

## Galvanized Chamber
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** madurai | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Rifle | **Max Rank:** 10

### Acquisition
This mod can be bought from the Arbitrations vendor in the Arbiters of Hexis room in Relays for 20 Vitus Essence.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +7.3% Multishot, On Kill: +2.7% Multishot for 20s. Stacks up to 5x.
- **Rank 1:** +14.5% Multishot, On Kill: +5.5% Multishot for 20s. Stacks up to 5x.
- **Rank 2:** +21.8% Multishot, On Kill: +8.2% Multishot for 20s. Stacks up to 5x.
- **Rank 3:** +29.1% Multishot, On Kill: +10.9% Multishot for 20s. Stacks up to 5x.
- **Rank 4:** +36.4% Multishot, On Kill: +13.6% Multishot for 20s. Stacks up to 5x.
- **Rank 5:** +43.6% Multishot, On Kill: +16.4% Multishot for 20s. Stacks up to 5x.
- **Rank 6:** +50.9% Multishot, On Kill: +19.1% Multishot for 20s. Stacks up to 5x.
- **Rank 7:** +58.2% Multishot, On Kill: +21.8% Multishot for 20s. Stacks up to 5x.

### Wiki
Galvanized Chamber is the Galvanized variant of Split Chamber, increasing multishot. Kills grant a buff that further increases multishot for 20 seconds and stacks up to 5 times.
**Notes:**
When the buff times out, only one stack is lost and the buff duration resets.
Only kills from a weapon equipped with this mod will give stacks, and only the weapon equipped with this mod will benefit from the buff. If equipped on both a normal weapon and an Exalted Weapon, this mod will affect both weapons.
Kills from status procs (including from Hunter Munitions) from a weapon equipped with this mod will give stacks.

### Wiki
Galvanized Crosshairs is the Galvanized variant of Hydraulic Crosshairs, where headshots provide a 12-second critical chance buff while aiming. Headshot kills will grant a 12-second buff that further increases critical chance while aiming and stacks up to 5 times.
**Notes:**
Unlike most other galvanized mods, Galvanized Crosshairs stacks each have their own duration.
Only kills from a weapon equipped with this mod will give stacks, and only the weapon equipped with this mod will benefit from the buff.
Most status procs target center-mass and cannot trigger the critical chance bonus or the on kill effect.
Electricity and Gas procs are able to trigger both effects when aimed at the head however, as they are able to land headshots themselves.

- **Rank 3:** +29.1% Status Chance, On Kill: +14.5% Direct Damage per Status Type affecting the target for 20s. Stacks up to 2x.

- **Rank 7:** +58.2% Status Chance, On Kill: +29.1% Direct Damage per Status Type affecting the target for 20s. Stacks up to 2x.
- **Rank 8:** +65.5% Status Chance, On Kill: +32.7% Direct Damage per Status Type affecting the target for 20s. Stacks up to 2x.
- **Rank 9:** +72.7% Status Chance, On Kill: +36.4% Direct Damage per Status Type affecting the target for 20s. Stacks up to 2x.
- **Rank 10:** +80% Status Chance, On Kill: +40% Direct Damage per Status Type affecting the target for 20s. Stacks up to 2x.

### Wiki
Galvanized Savvy is the Galvanized variant of Shotgun Savvy, increasing status chance. Kills grant a buff that increases direct hit damage (not area-of-effect) for each status type affecting the target for 20 seconds and stacks up to 2 times.
**Notes:**
The damage increase is usually additive with other damage mods like Point Blank, with exceptions where it is multiplicative. See Condition Overload (Mechanic) for more details.
The damage increase is not applied on the hit that procs a new status effect, it is only applied on following hits.
For example: Galvanized Aptitude has 2 stacks, weapon has 100 base damage and Primed Point Blank for a total of 100 * (1 + Primed Point Blank) damage. First hit procs Impact and deals 265 damage. Then on the following hit the +80% damage from the 2 stacks is applied for a total of 100 * (1 + Primed Point Blank + Galvanized Savvy) damage.
However, Multishot instances are treated as sequential hits, allowing a weapon with multishot to benefit from the damage increase even on the first trigger pull.
For example: Galvanized Aptitude has 2 stacks, weapon has 100 base damage, Primed Point Blank, and a multishot of 3.
The first multishot instance deals 100 * (1 + Primed Point Blank) damage and procs Impact.
The second multishot instance will then apply the +80% damage from 2 stacks, for a total of 100 * (1 + Primed Point Blank + Galvanized Savvy) damage.
If the second instance procs Puncture, then the third instance will apply +160% damage (80% for two stacks times 2 different status effects), for a total of 100 * (1 + Primed Point Blank + Galvanized Savvy) damage.

## Galvanized Scope
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** madurai | **Drain:** 2 | **Compatible:** Rifle | **Max Rank:** 10

### Acquisition
This mod can be bought from the Arbitrations vendor in the Arbiters of Hexis room in Relays for 20 Vitus Essence.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** On Headshot: +10.9% Critical Chance when Aiming for 12s On Headshot Kill: +3.6% Critical Chance when Aiming for 12s. Stacks up to 5x.
- **Rank 1:** On Headshot: +21.8% Critical Chance when Aiming for 12s On Headshot Kill: +7.3% Critical Chance when Aiming for 12s. Stacks up to 5x.
- **Rank 2:** On Headshot: +32.7% Critical Chance when Aiming for 12s On Headshot Kill: +10.9% Critical Chance when Aiming for 12s. Stacks up to 5x.
- **Rank 3:** On Headshot: +43.6% Critical Chance when Aiming for 12s On Headshot Kill: +14.5% Critical Chance when Aiming for 12s. Stacks up to 5x.
- **Rank 4:** On Headshot: +54.6% Critical Chance when Aiming for 12s On Headshot Kill: +18.2% Critical Chance when Aiming for 12s. Stacks up to 5x.
- **Rank 5:** On Headshot: +65.5% Critical Chance when Aiming for 12s On Headshot Kill: +21.8% Critical Chance when Aiming for 12s. Stacks up to 5x.
- **Rank 6:** On Headshot: +76.4% Critical Chance when Aiming for 12s On Headshot Kill: +25.5% Critical Chance when Aiming for 12s. Stacks up to 5x.
- **Rank 7:** On Headshot: +87.3% Critical Chance when Aiming for 12s On Headshot Kill: +29.1% Critical Chance when Aiming for 12s. Stacks up to 5x.
- **Rank 8:** On Headshot: +98.2% Critical Chance when Aiming for 12s On Headshot Kill: +32.7% Critical Chance when Aiming for 12s. Stacks up to 5x.
- **Rank 9:** On Headshot: +109.1% Critical Chance when Aiming for 12s On Headshot Kill: +36.4% Critical Chance when Aiming for 12s. Stacks up to 5x.
- **Rank 10:** On Headshot: +120% Critical Chance when Aiming for 12s On Headshot Kill: +40% Critical Chance when Aiming for 12s. Stacks up to 5x.

### Wiki
Galvanized Scope is the Galvanized variant of Argon Scope, where headshots provide a 12-second critical chance buff while aiming. Headshot kills will grant a 12-second buff that further increases critical chance while aiming and stacks up to 5 times.
**Notes:**
Unlike most other galvanized mods, Galvanized Scope stacks each have their own duration. The duration doesn't pause after you gain more stacks, so you will lose them before the visual timer on the HUD runs out.
Only kills from a weapon equipped with this mod will give stacks, and only the weapon equipped with this mod will benefit from the buff. If equipped on both a normal weapon and an Exalted Weapon, this mod will affect both weapons.
Can be activated by Pax Seeker's projectiles.
Most status procs target center-mass and cannot trigger the critical chance bonus or the on kill effect.
Electricity and Gas procs are able to trigger both effects when aimed at the head however, as they are able to land headshots themselves.
Given that Galvanized Scope is an upgraded version of Argon Scope, the caveats of the latter still apply, namely:
Argon Scope#Notes

## Galvanized Shot
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** vazarin | **Drain:** 2 | **Compatible:** Pistol | **Max Rank:** 10

### Acquisition
This mod can be bought from the Arbitrations vendor in the Arbiters of Hexis room in Relays for 20 Vitus Essence.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +7.3% Status Chance, On Kill: +3.6% Direct Damage per Status Type affecting the target for 14s. Stacks up to 3x.
- **Rank 1:** +14.5% Status Chance, On Kill: +7.3% Direct Damage per Status Type affecting the target for 14s. Stacks up to 3x.
- **Rank 2:** +21.8% Status Chance, On Kill: +10.9% Direct Damage per Status Type affecting the target for 14s. Stacks up to 3x.
- **Rank 3:** +29.1% Status Chance, On Kill: +14.5% Direct Damage per Status Type affecting the target for 14s. Stacks up to 3x.
- **Rank 4:** +36.4% Status Chance, On Kill: +18.2% Direct Damage per Status Type affecting the target for 14s. Stacks up to 3x.
- **Rank 5:** +43.6% Status Chance, On Kill: +21.8% Direct Damage per Status Type affecting the target for 14s. Stacks up to 3x.
- **Rank 6:** +50.9% Status Chance, On Kill: +25.5% Direct Damage per Status Type affecting the target for 14s. Stacks up to 3x.
- **Rank 7:** +58.2% Status Chance, On Kill: +29.1% Direct Damage per Status Type affecting the target for 14s. Stacks up to 3x.
- **Rank 8:** +65.5% Status Chance, On Kill: +32.7% Direct Damage per Status Type affecting the target for 14s. Stacks up to 3x.
- **Rank 9:** +72.7% Status Chance, On Kill: +36.4% Direct Damage per Status Type affecting the target for 14s. Stacks up to 3x.
- **Rank 10:** +80% Status Chance, On Kill: +40% Direct Damage per Status Type affecting the target for 14s. Stacks up to 3x.

### Wiki
Galvanized Shot is the Galvanized variant of Sure Shot, increasing status chance. Kills grant a buff that increases direct hit damage (not area-of-effect) for each status type affecting the target for 14 seconds and stacks up to 3 times.
**Notes:**
The damage increase is usually additive with other damage mods like Hornet Strike, with exceptions where it is multiplicative. See Condition Overload (Mechanic) for more details.
The damage increase is not applied on the hit that procs a new status effect, it is only applied on following hits.
For example: Galvanized Shot has 2 stacks, weapon has 100 base damage and Hornet Strike for a total of 100 * (1 + Hornet Strike) damage. First hit procs Impact and deals 320 damage. Then on the following hit the +80% damage from the 2 stacks is applied for a total of 100 * (1 + Hornet Strike + Galvanized Shot) damage.
However, Multishot instances are treated as sequential hits, allowing a weapon with multishot to benefit from the damage increase even on the first trigger pull.
For example: Galvanized Shot has 2 stacks, weapon has 100 base damage, Hornet Strike, and a multishot of 3.
The first multishot instance deals 100 * (1 + Hornet Strike) damage and procs Impact.
The second multishot instance will then apply the +80% damage from 2 stacks, for a total of 100 * (1 + Hornet Strike + Galvanized Shot) damage.
If the second instance procs Puncture, then the third instance will apply +160% damage (80% for 2 stacks, times 2 different status effects), for a total of 100 * (1 + Hornet Strike + Galvanized Shot) damage.
Thus, in one trigger pull the weapon will have dealt 1200 total damage, instead of the 960 that would have been dealt otherwise.

## Galvanized Steel
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** madurai | **Drain:** 2 | **Compatible:** Melee | **Max Rank:** 10

### Acquisition
This mod can be bought from the Arbitrations vendor in the Arbiters of Hexis room in Relays for 20 Vitus Essence.

- **Rank 1:** +20% Critical Chance (x2 for Heavy Attacks), On Melee Kill: +5.5% Critical Damage for 20s. Stacks up to 4x.
- **Rank 2:** +30% Critical Chance (x2 for Heavy Attacks), On Melee Kill: +8.2% Critical Damage for 20s. Stacks up to 4x.
- **Rank 3:** +40% Critical Chance (x2 for Heavy Attacks), On Melee Kill: +10.9% Critical Damage for 20s. Stacks up to 4x.
- **Rank 4:** +50% Critical Chance (x2 for Heavy Attacks), On Melee Kill: +13.6% Critical Damage for 20s. Stacks up to 4x.
- **Rank 5:** +60% Critical Chance (x2 for Heavy Attacks), On Melee Kill: +16.4% Critical Damage for 20s. Stacks up to 4x.
- **Rank 6:** +70% Critical Chance (x2 for Heavy Attacks), On Melee Kill: +19.1% Critical Damage for 20s. Stacks up to 4x.
- **Rank 7:** +80% Critical Chance (x2 for Heavy Attacks), On Melee Kill: +21.8% Critical Damage for 20s. Stacks up to 4x.
- **Rank 8:** +90% Critical Chance (x2 for Heavy Attacks), On Melee Kill: +24.5% Critical Damage for 20s. Stacks up to 4x.
- **Rank 9:** +100% Critical Chance (x2 for Heavy Attacks), On Melee Kill: +27.3% Critical Damage for 20s. Stacks up to 4x.
- **Rank 10:** +110% Critical Chance (x2 for Heavy Attacks), On Melee Kill: +30% Critical Damage for 20s. Stacks up to 4x.

### Wiki
Galvanized Steel is the Galvanized variant of True Steel, increasing Critical Chance while increasing Critical Damage on kill.
**Notes:**
When the buff times out, one stack is lost and the buff duration resets.
Only kills from a weapon equipped with this mod will give stacks, and only the weapon equipped with this mod will benefit from the buff. If equipped on both a normal weapon and an Exalted Weapon, it will affect both weapons.
Compared to Sacrificial Steel, Galvanized Steel provides less overall critical chance, but the additional Critical Damage, reduced mod capacity, and only requiring standard Forma for Polarization makes it more versatile.
Known Bugs
Killing enemies with Archmelees also increase the stack, but the bonus does not apply.

## Gastro
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Grendel | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired by attaining the rank of General under Steel Meridian, or the rank of Exalted under Red Veil, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Regurgitate Augment: Regurgitated enemies bounce up to 3 times, creating gas clouds that last 3s and inflict nearby enemies with Gas Status Effect.
- **Rank 1:** Regurgitate Augment: Regurgitated enemies bounce up to 3 times, creating gas clouds that last 5s and inflict nearby enemies with Gas Status Effect.
- **Rank 2:** Regurgitate Augment: Regurgitated enemies bounce up to 3 times, creating gas clouds that last 6s and inflict nearby enemies with Gas Status Effect.
- **Rank 3:** Regurgitate Augment: Regurgitated enemies bounce up to 3 times, creating gas clouds that last 8s and inflict nearby enemies with Gas Status Effect.

### Wiki
Gastro is a Warframe Augment Mod for Grendel's Regurgitate that causes spat out enemies to bounce and create Gas clouds.
**Notes:**
Regurgitated enemies bounce up to 3 times, exploding in a Ability Range6 meter AoE and leaving a Ability Range6 meter gas cloud on each bounce.
Explosions and gas clouds do not require Line of Sight to damage enemies.
The bounce explosions deal the same damage as Regurgitate normally does (Ability Strength2000 + 10% of Regurgitated enemy's current HP), but dealt as Gas damage instead of Toxin damage. Each explosion also procs 2 guaranteed Gas statuses, with the final explosion additionally proccing 1 Toxin status.
Each explosion deals 75% less damage than the last, dealing 100% of the expected damage on the first explosion, 25% on the second, and 6.25% on the last.
Explosion damage has no radial falloff, dealing full damage even at the edge of its range.
Proc damage scales with the damage of the explosion that caused them, dealing 50% of that damage per second for 6 seconds.
Like Regurgitate, each explosion permanently strips Ability Strength75% armor from any enemies hit.
Armor is stripped before damage is dealt.
Stacks with each bounce for a maximum of Ability Strength225% armor strip after 3 bounces, allowing for a full armor strip with as low as 45% ability strength.
Gas clouds remain for Ability Duration8 seconds and inflict Ability Strength250 Gas damage per second with a guaranteed Gas proc on every hit.
Gas clouds are not affected by the 75% damage falloff per bounce and will always deal the full Ability Strength250 damage per second.
Gas procs from the clouds have 50% reduced duration, only lasting for 3 seconds and dealing 3 ticks of Ability Strength125 damage before expiring.

## Gemini Cross

**Trivia:**
Gemini Cross is the first Stance mod that can be attained via mission rewards, in this case from Excavation missions; all other Stances drop exclusively from enemy units. It can otherwise be normally obtained from Corrupted MOAs.

## Ghost
**Rarity:** Common | **Polarity:** penjaga | **Drain:** 2 | **Compatible:** Shade | **Max Rank:** 5

### Acquisition
Ghost is automatically acquired upon obtaining Shade, Prisma Shade, or Shade Prime.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Cloaks owner when enemies are within 14m. The cloak is disrupted if owner attacks.
- **Rank 1:** Cloaks owner when enemies are within 16m. The cloak is disrupted if owner attacks.
- **Rank 2:** Cloaks owner when enemies are within 18m. The cloak is disrupted if owner attacks.
- **Rank 3:** Cloaks owner when enemies are within 20m. The cloak is disrupted if owner attacks.
- **Rank 4:** Cloaks owner when enemies are within 22m. The cloak is disrupted if owner attacks.
- **Rank 5:** Cloaks owner when enemies are within 24m. The cloak is disrupted if owner attacks.

### Drop Locations
- Deimos Runner — Uncommon (0.76%)
- Orphid Husks — Uncommon (0.76%)
- Volatile Runner — Uncommon (0.76%)
- The Quills, Architect — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Ghost is a precept mod for Shade, Prisma Shade, and Shade Prime that activates Invisibility for itself and its owner.
**Notes:**
When an enemy is within 24m of the player, Shade will cloak both itself and its master with Invisibility.
Activation does not require line of sight from player or Shade.
The activation range of Ghost is centered on Shade, not the player.
Shade does a twirl when activating Ghost.
Invisibility lasts indefinitely until one of the following happens:
The player attacks with a weapon.
Attacking with the Operator does not break Ghost.
Codex Scanner and Synthesis Scanner are considered weapons.
No enemies are within the detection range.
Ghost will remain active for 3 seconds when enemies are no longer within range. Moving near enemies again will sustain it.
Shade uses a higher ranked Precept.
Vacuum, Odomedic, and Anti-Grav Array do not break Ghost.
The player is inactive for 1 minute.
Has a 7 second cooldown after leaving any kind of invisibility, including itself.
Activating other forms of invisibility while Ghost is active will not break the effect of Ghost after the other effect ends, provided there is still an enemy in proximity and no weapon/ability that would break the effect was used.

## Gnashing Payara
Lunging punctures with impaling spirals.

### Wiki
Gnashing Payara is a stance mod for dual daggers that deals spinning attacks, stabs and slashes.
**Notes:**
The first attack of Flash Flurry has a 2x Slash damage multiplier.
**Trivia:**
A Payara is a carnivorous, freshwater fish with distinctive fangs protruding from its lower jaw.

## Gourmand
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Grendel | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired by attaining the rank of General under Steel Meridian, or the rank of Exalted under Red Veil, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Feast Augment: Instead of Energy, consumes 300 Health on cast. Glutton grants an additional 75 Armor per enemy.
- **Rank 1:** Feast Augment: Instead of Energy, consumes 260 Health on cast. Glutton grants an additional 100 Armor per enemy.
- **Rank 2:** Feast Augment: Instead of Energy, consumes 240 Health on cast. Glutton grants an additional 125 Armor per enemy.
- **Rank 3:** Feast Augment: Instead of Energy, consumes 200 Health on cast. Glutton grants an additional 150 Armor per enemy.

### Drop Locations
- Red Veil, Exalted — Common (100.00%)
- Steel Meridian, General — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Gourmand is a Warframe Augment Mod for Grendel's Feast to consume health instead of energy and increases armor for enemies in his gut.
**Notes:**
Health drain on cast is affected by Ability Efficiency.
Armor bonus is not affected by mods.
At max rank, the armor bonus increases Grendel's passive armor bonus to 400 per enemy and the total armor bonus to 2,000 at the 5 enemy cap.

## Greedy Pull
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Mag | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
Can be acquired by attaining the rank of Partner under The Perrin Sequence, or the rank of Flawless under New Loka, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Pull Augment: Adds 40% chance to pull pickups towards Mag.
- **Rank 1:** Pull Augment: Adds 60% chance to pull pickups towards Mag.
- **Rank 2:** Pull Augment: Adds 80% chance to pull pickups towards Mag.
- **Rank 3:** Pull Augment: Adds 100% chance to pull pickups towards Mag.

### Drop Locations
- New Loka, Flawless — Common (100.00%)
- The Perrin Sequence, Partner — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Greedy Pull is a Warframe Augment Mod that gives Mag's Pull the ability to attract pickups towards herself.
**Notes:**
Pulls all pickups within a radius approximately equal to Pull's range toward Mag.
Pickup pull radius is affected by Ability Range.
Pickup travel distance per cast is affected by Ability Strength.
Mission items such as Datamasses and Power Cells are unaffected.
Line of sight is required to pull pickups.
Pickups are only pulled for the player casting it. Pickups for other players in the squad will remain in their original positions.

## Grim Fury
Lightning fast hit chains and hard-hitting combos.
**Rarity:** Uncommon | **Polarity:** unairu | **Drain:** -2 | **Compatible:** Sparring | **Max Rank:** 3

### Drop Locations
- Kuva Powerclaw — Rare (0.17%)
- Narmer Powerfist — Rare (0.17%)
- Powerfist — Rare (0.17%)
- Earth/Cetus (Level 10 - 30 Cetus Bounty), Rotation C — Uncommon (10.08%)
- Earth/Cetus (Level 10 - 30 Cetus Bounty), Rotation C — Uncommon (25.00%)
- Earth/Cetus (Level 10 - 30 Cetus Bounty), Rotation C — Rare (9.68%)

### Wiki
Grim Fury is a stance mod for Sparring weapons like the Kogake, that deals in powerful kicking finishers.
**Notes:**
1st Attack of Rising from Ashes combo will lift enemies.
Sliding heavy attacks use the 2nd move in Rising from Ashes.
The last attack of Bright Blaze does 25% bonus Impact damage.
The first attack of Burning Desire does 200% bonus Impact damage.
**Trivia:**
Unusually, Grim Fury currently has incompatible Stance polarity with all available Sparring weapons, making it the only stance mod with such a discrepancy.

## Growing Power
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** madurai | **Drain:** -2 | **Compatible:** AURA | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 5

### Acquisition
Growing Power is dropped by the Knave Specter, available upon anointing a Silver Grove shrine with the Nightfall Apothic.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** On Status Effect with Weapon: Squad increases personal Ability Strength by 4% for 1s.
- **Rank 1:** On Status Effect with Weapon: Squad increases personal Ability Strength by 8% for 2s.
- **Rank 2:** On Status Effect with Weapon: Squad increases personal Ability Strength by 13% for 3s.
- **Rank 3:** On Status Effect with Weapon: Squad increases personal Ability Strength by 17% for 4s.
- **Rank 4:** On Status Effect with Weapon: Squad increases personal Ability Strength by 21% for 5s.
- **Rank 5:** On Status Effect with Weapon: Squad increases personal Ability Strength by 25% for 6s.

### Drop Locations
- Knave Specter — Rare (6.06%)

### Wiki
Growing Power is an aura mod that temporarily increases Ability Strength after inflicting a Status Effect with weapons on enemies.
* Aura mods increase the amount of Mod Capacity
**Notes:**
Companion weapons that inflict a Status Effect trigger Growing Power.
Does not trigger when other players inflict a Status Effect.
Does not trigger when dealing a Status Effect with Warframe abilities, only with equipped weapons.
Syndicate Radial Effects do not trigger Growing Power.
Slash procs from Hunter Munitions will trigger this mod.
Warframe abilities that add status effects to weapons will trigger this mod (e.g. Toxic Lash)
Channeled Abilities (e.g., Exalted Blade) which are activated while the Ability Strength buff is active will keep the bonus as long as they're kept active, even if the buff duration is finished.
Multiple instances of this Aura will increase the Ability Strength bonus only, not the buff duration.
Multiple instances of this Aura do increase the bonus additively, but currently do not show the increase on the HUD.
Ability Duration does not increase the duration of the buff.
The Ability Strength gained is additive with other Ability Strength mods. For example, a Warframe equipped with maxed Intensify and an active Growing Power buff would have +55% Ability Strength.
**Tips:**
Robotic Weapons or Beast Claws with high status chance can easily proc Growing Power, thus removing the need to use specific Primary, Secondary or Melee Weapons.
Coaction Drift will increase the Ability Strength bonus of this aura.

### Wiki
Guardian is a mod that grants a Sentinel, MOA, or Hound the ability to replenish its owner's shield when depleted.
**Notes:**
Has a 30 second cooldown.
Manifold Bond can be used to reduce the cooldown, allowing it to be ready to activate sooner.
Gives 25 affinity to the Companion for activating this ability.
Activates instantaneously when shields are depleted. However, activation can be delayed if a sentinel is occupied by other actions.
Should be put in the top-left mod slot. Not doing so may cause a sentinel to prioritize other actions before replenishing shields, creating a significant delay, which might prove lethal to squishy warframes.
The Companion will still recharge its master's shields while under the effects of an Ancient Disruptor's energy drain which results in wasting the recharge.
Does not provide any shields in the Nightmare Mode with "No Shields" modifier, although the Companion still receives the 25 affinity for activating the ability.
**Trivia:**
Prior to Update 12, this mod shared its image with Sanctuary.

## Guardian Armor
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Chroma | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired by attaining the rank of Genius under Cephalon Suda or the rank of Partner under The Perrin Sequence, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Vex Armor Augment: Chroma reduces the damage his squadmates within Affinity Range take by 75%. Squad kills restore 2% of his Health over 3s and increases Vex Armor duration by 1s.
- **Rank 1:** Vex Armor Augment: Chroma reduces the damage his squadmates within Affinity Range take by 75%. Squad kills restore 3% of his Health over 3s and increases Vex Armor duration by 1s.
- **Rank 2:** Vex Armor Augment: Chroma reduces the damage his squadmates within Affinity Range take by 75%. Squad kills restore 4% of his Health over 3s and increases Vex Armor duration by 1s.
- **Rank 3:** Vex Armor Augment: Chroma reduces the damage his squadmates within Affinity Range take by 75%. Squad kills restore 5% of his Health over 3s and increases Vex Armor duration by 1s.

### Drop Locations
- Cephalon Suda, Genius — Common (100.00%)
- The Perrin Sequence, Partner — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Guardian Armor is a Warframe Augment Mod for Chroma's Vex Armor that provides Damage Reduction to allies within Affinity Range while causing their kills to heal Chroma.
**Notes:**
Duration is capped at current modded Vex Armor duration.
Kills made by Chroma will trigger the duration increase and healing.
However, kills made by Chroma's companions will not.
Additional kills while the heal over time is active will refresh it; additional kills do not stack the heal over time.
Damage reduction does not apply to Chroma; only squadmates benefit from it.
Despite specifying squadmates, it does apply to defense objectives as well.
Guardian armor can activate Archon Intensify, provided that Health is actually restored, which demands that the player first take damage.
Restoring Health of ally Warframes or Companions will also grant the bonus.

### Wiki
Guardian Derision is an Exilus Weapon Mod for melee that allows the player to taunt enemies into attacking them instead of allies when blocking.
**Notes:**
While blocking, enemies within 15m become taunted which will shift their focus to the user.
Duration is permanent.
Effective range is not affected by Reach.
The taunt does not affect enemies across the Rift Plane.
The taunt is high priority and will steal aggro from other aggro-increasing abilities.
The taunt overrides other targeting modifiers such as Radiation status and Nyx's Chaos. Friendly fire is not disabled, so confused enemies can still incidentally damage, and be damaged by, their former allies.
**Tips:**
Taunting enemies ensures they will fire (if able) towards the Warframe and into damage-absorbing effects, such as:
Arcanes that trigger "On Damaged"
Warframe abilities that absorb damage
Chroma's Vex Armor
Frost's Snow Globe
Garuda's Dread Mirror
Nezha's Warding Halo
Nyx's Absorb (renders Nyx immobile)
Nyx's Assimilate augment allows her to block (among other actions) during Absorb and refresh Guardian Derision's taunt.
Rhino's Iron Skin
Weapon effects that trigger on block
Silva & Aegis (Prime)
Avenging Truth
Vaykor Sydon
Loki can taunt targets away from their own Decoy, including the augmented Savior Decoy.

## Guided Effigy
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Chroma | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired by attaining the rank of Genius under Cephalon Suda, or the rank of Partner under The Perrin Sequence, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Effigy Augment: Cast and hold to make Effigy move to your aim point. Deals 1000 Damage/s and restores 2 Energy for each enemy in its path. Roars on arrival, stunning nearby enemies.
- **Rank 1:** Effigy Augment: Cast and hold to make Effigy move to your aim point. Deals 2000 Damage/s and restores 3 Energy for each enemy in its path. Roars on arrival, stunning nearby enemies.
- **Rank 2:** Effigy Augment: Cast and hold to make Effigy move to your aim point. Deals 3000 Damage/s and restores 4 Energy for each enemy in its path. Roars on arrival, stunning nearby enemies.
- **Rank 3:** Effigy Augment: Cast and hold to make Effigy move to your aim point. Deals 4000 Damage/s and restores 5 Energy for each enemy in its path. Roars on arrival, stunning nearby enemies.

### Drop Locations
- Cephalon Suda, Genius — Common (100.00%)
- The Perrin Sequence, Partner — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Guided Effigy is a Warframe Augment Mod for Chroma's Effigy that allows him to control the Effigy, dealing rapid damage to enemies in its path while recovering energy and stunning enemies at the destination.
**Notes:**
While Effigy is active, hold 4 while aiming at a valid location to mark Effigy's destination with a beacon for no energy cost.
If a beacon is already placed, holding 4 again will move the beacon to the new location.
Once a beacon is created, Effigy will take flight at high speed toward the beacon, occasionally leaping great heights and distances if there is terrain elevation difference or teleporting to the beacon if unable to reach by normal means.
Damage is affected by Ability Strength.
The flight's damage is not affected by Summoner's Wrath, Theorem Infection, and Vex Armor.
The flight's damage does not contribute to Arcane Camisado. However, its movement speed is affected.
Energy refund per enemy is inversely affected by Ability Efficiency.
Upon arrival, Effigy's roar cooldown is reset, allowing it roar and stun enemies if they are in range or bash away enemies in melee range.

## Guided Ordnance
**Rarity:** Uncommon | **Polarity:** madurai | **Drain:** 2 | **Compatible:** Assault Rifle | **Max Rank:** 5

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** On Hit: +5% Accuracy when Aiming for 2s
- **Rank 1:** On Hit: +10% Accuracy when Aiming for 3s
- **Rank 2:** On Hit: +15% Accuracy when Aiming for 5s
- **Rank 3:** On Hit: +20% Accuracy when Aiming for 6s
- **Rank 4:** On Hit: +25% Accuracy when Aiming for 8s
- **Rank 5:** On Hit: +30% Accuracy when Aiming for 9s

### Drop Locations
- Duviri/Endless: Tier 1 (Normal) — Legendary (0.67%)
- Duviri/Endless: Tier 3 (Normal) — Legendary (0.67%)
- Deimos/Cambion Drift (Level 30 - 40 Cambion Drift Bounty), Rotation B — Uncommon (13.21%)
- Deimos/Cambion Drift (Level 30 - 40 Cambion Drift Bounty), Rotation B — Rare (7.53%)
- Deimos/Cambion Drift (Level 30 - 40 Cambion Drift Bounty), Rotation B — Rare (9.59%)

### Wiki
Guided Ordnance is an assault rifle mod that temporarily increases accuracy while aiming upon hitting an enemy.
**Notes:**
This mod is highly ideal for use in low-accuracy weapons with a high fire rate, such as the Gorgon and Supra, as their high fire rate gives them a high chance to hit, allowing them to refresh the duration of the accuracy bonus continuously.
This mod will not activate on hitting a Nullifier's bubble.
Status Effects from damage types such as Toxin or Slash will continue to refresh the mod's effects so long as they are causing damage.
Stacks additively with other sources of weapon spread modifier.
**Trivia:**
One of only five mods that can increase accuracy in Warframe.
Prior to Update Specters of the Rail 6, this mod was erroneously named "Guided Ordinance".
Surprisingly, while the mod is labeled to work on assault rifles, the image depicts the Kohm, which is a shotgun.

## Gun Glide
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** madurai | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Assault Rifle | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired by attaining the rank of Whirlwind under the Conclave Syndicate, and spending 15,000 Standing to purchase.
This mod can be obtained as a drop from Battalyst enemy.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** -5% Weapon Recoil, and +5% Accuracy when Sliding
- **Rank 1:** -10% Weapon Recoil, and +10% Accuracy when Sliding
- **Rank 2:** -15% Weapon Recoil, and +15% Accuracy when Sliding
- **Rank 3:** -20% Weapon Recoil, and +20% Accuracy when Sliding

### Drop Locations
- Brachiolyst Disperser — Rare (0.08%)
- Brachiolyst — Rare (0.08%)
- Battalyst — Rare (0.42%)
- Decaying Battalyst — Rare (0.42%)
- Erra — Rare (0.42%)
- Symbilyst — Rare (0.42%)
- Tyro Conculyst — Rare (0.42%)
- Conclave, Whirlwind — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Gun Glide is a rifle mod usable in both PvE and Conclave that reduces recoil and increases accuracy when firing while sliding.
**Notes:**
Stacks with Stabilizer and Vile Precision for a total of -170% reduced recoil, at the cost of -36% fire rate. The excess -70% reduction will not be utilized in the process.
In PvP situations, stacks with Hydraulic Gauge for a total of -80% reduced recoil.
Stacks additively with other sources of weapon spread modifier.
