## Hallowed Eruption
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Oberon | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired by attaining the rank of General under Steel Meridian, or the rank of Flawless under New Loka, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Hallowed Ground Augment: Reactivate to deal all Remaining Damage and Radiation Status. Passive: +50% Hallowed Ground Duration.
- **Rank 1:** Hallowed Ground Augment: Reactivate to deal all Remaining Damage and Radiation Status. Passive: +100% Hallowed Ground Duration.
- **Rank 2:** Hallowed Ground Augment: Reactivate to deal all Remaining Damage and Radiation Status. Passive: +150% Hallowed Ground Duration.
- **Rank 3:** Hallowed Ground Augment: Reactivate to deal all Remaining Damage and Radiation Status. Passive: +200% Hallowed Ground Duration.

### Drop Locations
- New Loka, Flawless — Common (100.00%)
- Steel Meridian, General — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Hallowed Eruption is a Warframe Augment Mod for Oberon's Hallowed Ground that causes recasts to detonate the ground for all its remaining damage with a chance to inflict a Radiation status. It also passively increases the duration of Hallowed Ground.
**Notes:**
Hallowed Eruption's damage is given by Hallowed Ground's damage multiplied by its remaining duration, so it is affected by both Ability Strength and Ability Duration. For example:
Without further modding, its maximum damage is.
With Intensify, its maximum damage becomes.
With Continuity, its maximum damage becomes.
The detonation area is a sphere centered on the point where Hallowed Ground was created, inheriting the Hallowed Ground's radius. It is able to hit enemies that are not affected by Hallowed Ground, as long as they are in Hallowed Eruption's area.
The detonation is not affected by line of sight.
Detonating Hallowed Ground has no cast animation or Energy cost.
With this Augment, only one patch of Hallowed Ground can be active at a time.
Hallowed Reckoning's fields are not considered to be Hallowed Ground, so they are not affected by Hallowed Eruption.

## Hallowed Reckoning
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Oberon | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired by attaining the rank of General under Steel Meridian, or the rank of Flawless under New Loka, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Reckoning Augment: Reckoning gains +40% range. A 1.5m radius around each affected enemy grants bonus armor to allies and deals 150 damage per second to enemies.
- **Rank 1:** Reckoning Augment: Reckoning gains +40% range. A 2m radius around each affected enemy grants bonus armor to allies and deals 200 damage per second to enemies.
- **Rank 2:** Reckoning Augment: Reckoning gains +40% range. A 2.5m radius around each affected enemy grants bonus armor to allies and deals 250 damage per second to enemies.
- **Rank 3:** Reckoning Augment: Reckoning gains +40% range. A 3m radius around each affected enemy grants bonus armor to allies and deals 300 damage per second to enemies.

### Drop Locations
- New Loka, Flawless — Common (100.00%)
- Steel Meridian, General — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Confirm armor bonuses b/c mod description doesn't explicitly tell how much
Hallowed Reckoning is a Warframe Augment Mod for Oberon's Reckoning that makes enemies hit drop patches on the ground, dealing Radiation damage over time to enemies and providing armor to allies standing on them with a brief grace period upon stepping off. It also passively increases Reckoning's range.
**Notes:**
Damage is dealt in ticks, one immediately upon creation of the fields and one every second thereafter.
This mod does not interact with Hallowed Eruption:
The small Hallowed Ground patches created by Hallowed Reckoning cannot be detonated.
The small patches do not count toward Hallowed Eruption's one-patch limit; they neither remove nor are removed by the main Hallowed Ground patch.
The small patches do not receive Hallowed Eruption's duration bonus.

## Hammer Shot
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** vazarin | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Rifle | **Max Rank:** 3

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +15% Critical Damage, +20% Status Chance
- **Rank 1:** +30% Critical Damage, +40% Status Chance
- **Rank 2:** +45% Critical Damage, +60% Status Chance
- **Rank 3:** +60% Critical Damage, +80% Status Chance

### Drop Locations
- Nightmare Mode Rewards, Rotation A — Rare (1.01%)

### Wiki
Hammer Shot is a mod that increases the critical damage and the status chance of rifles.
**Notes:**
Hammer Shot is additive with similar mods.
For example, a Snipetron with a max rank Hammer Shot and Rifle Aptitude will have a 12% (1 + 80% + 90%) status chance.
Can be combined with Vital Sense to increase critical damage by 180%.
This mod is useful with weapons such as Amprex or Zenith with both high critical and status chances.

### Wiki
Handspring is an Exilus mod that speeds up a Warframe's knockdown recovery rate.
**Notes:**
Works with anything that would knock a player down, and additionally shortens the stagger animation from self-stagger weapons (does not affect the flinch animation that occurs at the edge of the explosion.)
This is very useful when you are swarmed and a Shockwave MOA, Bursa, Shield Lancer or Ancient Healer/Ancient Disruptor knocks you down. It's also useful against bosses that have a stomp attack, such as Jackal, Hyena Pack, and Ambulas.
This mod is particularly valuable when attempting to solo a mission as knockdowns and the long period of helplessness they cause are one of the most common causes of death when you have no teammates to back you up.
Maxed (and other ranks) Handspring speeds up the recovery animation by changing it to a flip-up animation.
The flip-up animation is similar, if not identical, to that of MOA's kick-up.
Unranked Handspring speeds up the recovery animation without changing the animation (like Constitution).
If stacked with ranked Constitution, the recovery animation may change to the flip-up animation if the recovery time is short enough.
The animation depicted in the mod's picture is actually Back Handspring's animation.
Can be combined with Constitution for a total of +200% knockdown recovery (further testing is needed)

## Hard Engage
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** penjaga | **Drain:** 2 | **Compatible:** Moa | **Max Rank:** 5

### Acquisition
Hard Engage is automatically acquired upon obtaining a MOA built with the Nychus Model.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Engages enemies within 10m with melee attacks, dealing 15 Impact Damage. Melee attacks are enhanced by Mods equipped in the Moa's weapon.
- **Rank 1:** Engages enemies within 10m with melee attacks, dealing 30 Impact Damage. Melee attacks are enhanced by Mods equipped in the Moa's weapon.
- **Rank 2:** Engages enemies within 10m with melee attacks, dealing 45 Impact Damage. Melee attacks are enhanced by Mods equipped in the Moa's weapon.
- **Rank 3:** Engages enemies within 10m with melee attacks, dealing 60 Impact Damage. Melee attacks are enhanced by Mods equipped in the Moa's weapon.
- **Rank 4:** Engages enemies within 10m with melee attacks, dealing 75 Impact Damage. Melee attacks are enhanced by Mods equipped in the Moa's weapon.
- **Rank 5:** Engages enemies within 10m with melee attacks, dealing 90 Impact Damage. Melee attacks are enhanced by Mods equipped in the Moa's weapon.

### Wiki
Hard Engage is a precept mod for MOAs to target enemies within 10 meters with melee attacks, kicking them to deal Impact damage.
**Notes:**
Melee attacks have 0% base critical chance, 2x critical damage, and 20% status chance.
Hard Engage is not affected by the MOA's equipped weapon stats, including any innate elements. Hard Engage is affected by most of the weapon's equipped mods.
The weapon's type (such as Shotgun, Rifle, or Pistol) will determine which mods are available.
Multishot, Fire Rate, Reload, and Ammo mods have no effect.
Shivering Contagion does not apply.
Mods that increase Impact damage, such as Full Contact, will increase the damage dealt.
At close range, the MOA will always prefer melee attacks over shooting the equipped weapon.
Most melee attacks hit twice.
Melee attacks trigger Seismic Bond and Vicious Bond, unlike Blast Shield.
Clones spawned via Duplex Bond will also use melee attacks.
**Trivia:**
Currently the only rare/non-common mod exclusive to MOAs.
In addition, as one of the mods with a max rank of 5, it ends up costing significantly more to max, compared to other MOA mods.

## Harkonar Scope
**Rarity:** Uncommon | **Polarity:** naramon | **Drain:** 4 | **Compatible:** Sniper | **Max Rank:** 5

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +2s Combo Duration
- **Rank 1:** +4s Combo Duration
- **Rank 2:** +6s Combo Duration
- **Rank 3:** +8s Combo Duration
- **Rank 4:** +10s Combo Duration
- **Rank 5:** +12s Combo Duration

### Drop Locations
- Duviri/Endless: Tier 1 (Normal) — Legendary (0.67%)
- Duviri/Endless: Tier 3 (Normal) — Legendary (0.67%)
- Kela De Thaym — Uncommon (11.11%)

### Wiki
Harkonar Scope is a Sniper Mod that increases a sniper rifle's combo duration while aiming.
**Notes:**
All sniper rifles have a 2 second combo duration, with the exception of the Lanka, which has a 6 second combo duration. Harkonar Scope at max rank extends this to 14 seconds, and to 18 seconds for Lanka.
The extended combo duration only applies to a stack if the player is already scoped when the previous stack expires. As such, the most efficient combo conservation can either be achieved by always being scoped in, or only every 14 (18) seconds, when the counter decrements.
**Trivia:**
This mod, along with the other Executioner mods, are the second set mods to feature non-generic enemies on the mod card, the first being Vengeful Revenant.

## Hata-Satya
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** madurai | **Drain:** 2 | **Compatible:** Soma Prime | **Max Rank:** 5

### Acquisition
Hata-Satya can be obtained by reaching Rank 4 - Friend with the Entrati and purchased for 20,000 Standing from Father.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Each hit increases Critical Chance by 0.2%. Resets upon reloading or holstering.
- **Rank 1:** Each hit increases Critical Chance by 0.4%. Resets upon reloading or holstering.
- **Rank 2:** Each hit increases Critical Chance by 0.6%. Resets upon reloading or holstering.
- **Rank 3:** Each hit increases Critical Chance by 0.8%. Resets upon reloading or holstering.
- **Rank 4:** Each hit increases Critical Chance by 1%. Resets upon reloading or holstering.
- **Rank 5:** Each hit increases Critical Chance by 1.2%. Resets upon reloading or holstering.

### Drop Locations
- Entrati (Father), Friend — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Hata-Satya is a Weapon Augment Mod for Soma Prime that increases Critical Chance on every hit, until Soma Prime is reloaded or holstered. The critical chance bonus is capped at 500% at all mod ranks.
**Notes:**
Hata-Satya is additive with similar mods. For example, a max rank, max bonus Hata-Satya and Point Strike will have a 30% (1 + 500% + 150%) critical chance.
Additional hits from Multishot and Punch Through also count towards the bonus.
Bonus damage instances from Toxic Lash and Xata's Whisper count as one extra hit.
Swapping to Incarnon Form counts as reloading the Soma Prime and will therefore end the bonus.
Using melee, Parazon mercy finishers, or activating an Exalted Weapon ability are counted as holstering the Soma Prime and will therefore end the bonus.
Bonus ends as soon as the reload animation begins or when using Blood Forge.
Hits against invulnerable enemies, including Blood Altar and The Lost, do count toward the bonus.
Hits against Object-class health, barrier-like entities (Arctic Eximus snow globes and Nullifier bubbles), and enemies "converted" into allies (The Lost, Mind Control, Enthrall, and Parasitic Link), do not count toward the bonus.
**Trivia:**
The name of this mod might come from Sanskrit. Hata means killed, Satya means truth.

**Notes:**

Increasing the zoom will marginally increase the accuracy.
It will NOT shrink the spread cone of shotgun type sidearms (Bronco, Akbronco, Bronco Prime, Akbronco Prime, Brakk, Detron).
Works well with Seer, as the weapon itself has high zoom.

## Healing Flame
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Ember | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired by attaining the rank of General under Steel Meridian, or the rank of Exalted under the Red Veil, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Fire Blast Augment: Each enemy hit heals by 10 to 20 depending on current Immolation level. With Ember, over healing grants you Overguard.
- **Rank 1:** Fire Blast Augment: Each enemy hit heals by 15 to 30 depending on current Immolation level. With Ember, over healing grants you Overguard.
- **Rank 2:** Fire Blast Augment: Each enemy hit heals by 20 to 40 depending on current Immolation level. With Ember, over healing grants you Overguard.
- **Rank 3:** Fire Blast Augment: Each enemy hit heals by 25 to 50 depending on current Immolation level. With Ember, over healing grants you Overguard.

### Drop Locations
- Red Veil, Exalted — Common (100.00%)
- Steel Meridian, General — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Healing Flame is a Warframe Augment Mod for Ember that heals the player for each enemy hit by Fire Blast. This healing will be converted to Overguard if received while at maximum health.
**Notes:**
Heal amount is affected by Ability Strength.
Overguard granted by Healing Flame has a cap of 10,000, affected by Ability Strength.
When using with subsumed Fire Blast, Healing Flame will heal equal to 50% of Ember's heat level: 37 health. Overguard will also not be generated.
Immolation does not have to be active for the ability to grant overguard, however it will always be at the minimum amount.

## Healing Return
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** vazarin | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Melee | **Max Rank:** 10

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Restores 1 Health per Status Type affecting the target
- **Rank 1:** Restores 2 Health per Status Type affecting the target
- **Rank 2:** Restores 3 Health per Status Type affecting the target
- **Rank 3:** Restores 4 Health per Status Type affecting the target
- **Rank 4:** Restores 5 Health per Status Type affecting the target
- **Rank 5:** Restores 6 Health per Status Type affecting the target
- **Rank 6:** Restores 7 Health per Status Type affecting the target
- **Rank 7:** Restores 8 Health per Status Type affecting the target
- **Rank 8:** Restores 9 Health per Status Type affecting the target
- **Rank 9:** Restores 10 Health per Status Type affecting the target
- **Rank 10:** Restores 11 Health per Status Type affecting the target

### Drop Locations
- Crawler — Rare (0.03%)
- Deimos Undying Flyer — Legendary (0.03%)
- Undying Flyer — Legendary (0.03%)
- Duviri/Endless: Tier 6 (Normal) — Rare (1.03%)
- Duviri/Endless: Tier 4 (Normal) — Rare (1.49%)

### Wiki
Healing Return is a mod for melee weapons that heals the player when hitting a target currently affected by a Status Effect.
**Notes:**
Heals the user the stated amount for each status effect currently afflicting the target. For example, with a Rank 10 Healing Return (+11), attacking an enemy afflicted with Slash, Puncture, and Toxin statuses will heal 113 health, a total of 33 health.
Healing Return can be used to much avail on certain low status weapons as well:
High Impact damage weapons like Heliocor or Shaku will trigger Healing return with every attack, regardless of an Impact proc actually occurring.
Certain weapons (such as dual daggers equipped with Gnashing Payara, or Gram even without a stance) trigger a "flinch" effect on enemies with every hit, which triggers Healing Return.
Note that many Stances have at least one combo that has a force proc.
Multiple instances of the same status effect (e.g. 4 individual Slash DoTs) will not increase the healing given.
Both the Redeemer and Sarpa's charge attacks will heal the user. Each individual pellet triggers an instance of healing (e.g., if 5 pellets hit an enemy afflicted with a status effect, the healing amount will be 511 = 55HP).

## Health Conversion
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** vazarin | **Drain:** 10 | **Compatible:** WARFRAME | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 5

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired from Cephalon Simaris for 100,000 Standing.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Health Orbs grant 75 Armor, stacking up to 3x. Taking damage will consume a stack after 3s.
- **Rank 1:** Health Orbs grant 150 Armor, stacking up to 3x. Taking damage will consume a stack after 3s.
- **Rank 2:** Health Orbs grant 225 Armor, stacking up to 3x. Taking damage will consume a stack after 3s.
- **Rank 3:** Health Orbs grant 300 Armor, stacking up to 3x. Taking damage will consume a stack after 3s.
- **Rank 4:** Health Orbs grant 375 Armor, stacking up to 3x. Taking damage will consume a stack after 3s.
- **Rank 5:** Health Orbs grant 450 Armor, stacking up to 3x. Taking damage will consume a stack after 3s.

### Drop Locations
- Cephalon Simaris, Health Conversion
 — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Health Conversion is a Warframe mod that grants additional Armor for every Health Orb picked up, up to a maximum of 3 stacks of bonus armor. Taking health damage when a stack is present will consume 1 stack after a 3 second delay.
**Notes:**
Picking up Health Orbs will grant 450 armor, stacking up to 3 times, for a maximum Armor bonus of 1350.
The Armor bonus is added to the Warframe's total armor and does not increase the Warframe's base armor.
Mods like Steel Fiber that multiply a Warframe's base armor do not increase the effects of Health Conversion, while those that multiply total armor, like Ironclad Charge, do.
The stacks are shown as glowing orange blocks orbiting the player's Warframe.
Universal Orbs will also add an armor stack upon collection.
Energy Orbs picked up while using Equilibrium do not provide armor stacks.
This mod allows Health Orbs to be picked up while at maximum Health and less than 3 stacks are present.
Taking Health damage will consume a stack after 3 seconds.
Only damage dealt to health will consume a stack.
The 3 second stack consume timer is not affected by Ability Duration.
Only one stack can be consumed at a time. If additional health damage is taken within the 3-second consume time, no additional stacks will be consumed.
Health damage from using Garuda's Bloodletting, Hema's reload mechanism, or Nekros' Desecrate with Despoil augment will not consume a stack.
Health damage from Combat Discipline, however, will consume a stack.

## Heartseeker
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** madurai | **Drain:** 10 | **Compatible:** Daggers | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod can acquired by reaching the rank of Whirlwind from the Conclave Syndicate, and spending 15,000 Standing to purchase.

- **Rank 1:** Lock onto targets within 0.5m, but reduces blocking effectiveness., -25% Damage Block
- **Rank 2:** Lock onto targets within 0.75m, but reduces blocking effectiveness., -37.5% Damage Block

### Drop Locations

### Wiki
Heartseeker is a Conclave only Mod for single Daggers that allows the user to lock on to melee targets within range, but with the caveat of decreased damage blocking effectiveness.
**Notes:**
This mod can only be used inside Conclave missions and can only be equipped on Conclave loadouts.

## Hearty Nourishment
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Grendel | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired by attaining the rank of General under Steel Meridian, or the rank of Exalted under Red Veil, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Nourish Augment: Clear Status Effects and gain 2s of Status Immunity for each victim in Grendel's stomach.
- **Rank 1:** Nourish Augment: Clear Status Effects and gain 3s of Status Immunity for each victim in Grendel's stomach.
- **Rank 2:** Nourish Augment: Clear Status Effects and gain 4s of Status Immunity for each victim in Grendel's stomach.
- **Rank 3:** Nourish Augment: Clear Status Effects and gain 5s of Status Immunity for each victim in Grendel's stomach.

### Drop Locations
- Red Veil, Exalted — Common (100.00%)
- Steel Meridian, General — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Hearty Nourishment is a Warframe Augment Mod for Grendel that allows Nourish to remove Status Effects and give him immunity based on the number of enemies in his stomach.
**Notes:**
Duration per enemy is affected by Ability Duration.
As Warframes other than Grendel are unable to Feast to have enemies in their stomachs, neither the removal of Status Effects nor the status immunity are applied, rendering this augment useless on Warframes infused with Nourish.
Prevents blocking while active.

### Wiki
Heated Charge is a mod that increases Heat damage of a secondary weapon by 15% per rank for a maximum of 90% at rank 5.
**Tips:**
Heat damage is exceptionally effective against Infested.
Due to the nature of Infested's strategy, namely charging in numbers, it is recommended to apply this mod on Shotgun-type pistols like the Bronco and the Detron as they spread multiple pellets widely, effectively applying damage on multiple enemies.
It is rewarded frequently on defense missions.

## Heavy Caliber
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** madurai | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Rifle | **Max Rank:** 10

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +15% Damage, -5% Accuracy
- **Rank 1:** +30% Damage, -10% Accuracy
- **Rank 2:** +45% Damage, -15% Accuracy
- **Rank 3:** +60% Damage, -20% Accuracy
- **Rank 4:** +75% Damage, -25% Accuracy
- **Rank 5:** +90% Damage, -30% Accuracy
- **Rank 6:** +105% Damage, -35% Accuracy
- **Rank 7:** +120% Damage, -40% Accuracy
- **Rank 8:** +135% Damage, -45% Accuracy
- **Rank 9:** +150% Damage, -50% Accuracy
- **Rank 10:** +165% Damage, -55% Accuracy

### Drop Locations
- Derelict Vault — Rare (4.17%)

### Wiki
Heavy Caliber is a Corrupted Mod that increases damage of Primary Weapons (excluding Shotguns) at the cost of increased spread (reducing accuracy). Some weapons will have additional spread added, in addition to the base amount.
**Notes:**
Instead of reducing the accuracy by 5% per rank, each rank increases spread by a specific value per rank depending on the weapon.
Damage bonus stacks additively with similar mods, such as Serration.
Since the two stack additively and Heavy Caliber costs 2 more mod capacity for the same bonus, combining the two is slightly less efficient in terms of damage per capacity cost. Maxed Serration grants +11.79% bonus damage per 1 mod capacity, while maxed Heavy Caliber grants +10.31% bonus damage per mod capacity. Combining the two, Serration and Heavy Caliber grant +11% bonus damage per 1 mod capacity, which is +0.79% less bonus damage per capacity than Serration alone.
Added spread is not affected by bonuses that increase accuracy, such as Twitch or Guided Ordnance.
For example, a Braton with Heavy Caliber and Twitch will have a max deviation of Braton's Max Deviation (1 - Twitch) + Heavy Caliber degrees.
Causes beam of all continuous weapons to noticeably wobble.
**Trivia:**
When it was first released, Heavy Caliber's original penalty was an increase in recoil as well as damage. However the recoil penalty did not affect recoil-less weapons (Flux Rifle, Ignis, Synapse, bows and Lanka among others), effectively acting as another instance of Serration. This was confirmed to be unintentional in Warframe Prime Time - Episode 2 (9:40). The mod's penalty was changed in Update 10.4.

## Heavy Impact
**Rarity:** Uncommon | **Polarity:** naramon | **Drain:** 4 | **Compatible:** WARFRAME | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 5

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Create 1m seismic shockwaves from heavy landings, dealing 50 Damage and knocking foes off their feet.
- **Rank 1:** Create 2m seismic shockwaves from heavy landings, dealing 100 Damage and knocking foes off their feet.
- **Rank 2:** Create 3m seismic shockwaves from heavy landings, dealing 150 Damage and knocking foes off their feet.
- **Rank 3:** Create 4m seismic shockwaves from heavy landings, dealing 200 Damage and knocking foes off their feet.
- **Rank 4:** Create 5m seismic shockwaves from heavy landings, dealing 250 Damage and knocking foes off their feet.
- **Rank 5:** Create 6m seismic shockwaves from heavy landings, dealing 300 Damage and knocking foes off their feet.

### Drop Locations

- Kuva Trokarian — Rare (0.22%)

- Stalker — Uncommon (30.56%)
- Ceres/Hapke (Spy), Rotation C — Rare (8.60%)
- Earth/Cambria (Spy), Rotation C — Rare (8.60%)
- Mars/Arval (Spy), Rotation C — Rare (8.60%)
- Mercury/Suisei (Spy), Rotation C — Rare (8.60%)
- Phobos/Grildrig (Spy), Rotation C — Rare (8.60%)
- Phobos/Shklovsky (Spy), Rotation C — Rare (8.60%)
- Venus/Unda (Spy), Rotation C — Rare (8.60%)
- Venus/Vesper (Spy), Rotation C — Rare (8.60%)

### Wiki
Heavy Impact is a Exilus mod that allows a Warframe to create shockwave from Hard Landings that knocks foes off their feet.
**Notes:**
The shockwave is activated by the speed of landing rounded down to the speed threshold:
16 m/s is the required threshold for damage; 32 m/s earns 2.0x damage, 48 m/s earns 3.0x, etc.
Impact radius is scaled without rounding: 1.0x at 16 m/s, 1.5x at 24 m/s, 2.0x at 32 m/s, etc.
Despite the mod describing "heavy landings," the shockwave will trigger even if the hard landing animation is avoided, such as by sliding during landing.
However, immunity to hard landings from Kavat's Grace or Valkyr's passive makes it impossible to activate Heavy Impact.
Some melee weapons will hit the ground first when using a jump attack, meaning Heavy Impact will not activate.
The blast color is affected by your Warframe's energy color.
Directional melee and wall running can activate Heavy Impact independent of speed of landing.
Rhino's passive ability matches Heavy Impact at max rank. Equipping Heavy Impact on Rhino will further increase the effectiveness.

### Wiki
Heavy Trauma is a mod that increases the Impact damage of melee weapons by 15% per rank for a maximum of 90% at rank 5.
**Notes:**
This mod will only increase Impact, and no other damage types.
Works well with weapons with high base Impact damage such as the Magistar and the Jat Kittag.
Can be combined with Collision Force to provide a 210% increase to Impact damage.
**Trivia:**
"Trauma" describes tissue damage, in this case, resulting from heavy blows from an impact oriented melee weapon.
This mod formerly had a description that said it increases Puncture Damage when it was first released. Its Slash counterpart (Jagged Edge) also had the same error.

### Wiki
The Hellfire mod increases Heat damage by 15% per rank for a maximum of 90% at rank 5.
**Notes:**
Though dropped infrequently, it can be obtained as a reward from Dark Sector defense missions.
There is a relatively easy way to farm this on the Kuva Fortress tile set. In many end rooms, there are Latcher Turrets that will deploy 2 waves of 8 Latchers when your warframe is detected by the scanners. An AoE weapon such as Ignis or a weapon that chains like Atomos can make quick work of the Latchers and you can repeat this infinitely. The turret will retreat into the ground after deploying its second wave, you can spawn it again by being scanned another time.

## Helminth Ferocity
Allows the Kubrow to use Finisher attacks on an enemy.
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** penjaga | **Drain:** 0 | **Compatible:** Helminth Charger | **Max Rank:** 5

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +20% Finisher Damage
- **Rank 1:** +40% Finisher Damage
- **Rank 2:** +60% Finisher Damage
- **Rank 3:** +80% Finisher Damage
- **Rank 4:** +100% Finisher Damage
- **Rank 5:** +120% Finisher Damage

### Wiki
The mod allowed the Helminth Charger to use finisher attacks on enemies.
This mod was unique to the Helminth Charger and was granted to players upon incubating of a Helminth Charger.
**Notes:**
Shared the same function as the Savagery and Ferocity mod.
The Charger would be wrestled down onto the ground if it failed to kill its target, leaving it vulnerable for a few seconds.
Finishers performed by the companion would have triggered Arcane Ultimatum for the Warframe.
Although this mod has been removed from previous owners inventories and is not present in the codex, it was still possible to acquire via transmutation until Update 21.3.1. Existing copies of the mod acquired this way remained in their owners inventory.

## Hemorrhage
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** madurai | **Drain:** 10 | **Compatible:** Pistol | **Max Rank:** 5

- **Rank 3:** Impact Status Effects have 25% chance to apply a Slash Status Effect (x2 when Fire Rate is below 2.5)
- **Rank 4:** Impact Status Effects have 30% chance to apply a Slash Status Effect (x2 when Fire Rate is below 2.5)
- **Rank 5:** Impact Status Effects have 35% chance to apply a Slash Status Effect (x2 when Fire Rate is below 2.5)

### Wiki
Hemorrhage is a pistol mod that gives a chance of every Impact status effect to produce an instance of a Slash proc. This chance is doubled when the weapon's fire rate is below 2.5.
**Notes:**
Cannot produce multiple procs in a single instance of damage alongside any other Slash sources such as a weapon's innate Slash or the debuff from Seeking Talons.
Proccing Impact more than once in a single instance of damage will not allow this mod to proc more than once, nor will it increase the chance of the mod proccing.
Hard Engage works with this mod.
Fire Rate buffs such as Gauss' Redline, Wisp's Haste Reservoirs, and Arcane Velocity can negatively impact this mod's performance since it may increase the weapon's fire rate past 2.5.
If the weapon's fire rate is exactly 2.5, it will not receive the 2x bonus.
**Tips:**
Secondary weapons with very high Impact Damage & Status Chance include:
Akarius (Akarius Prime)
Akstiletto (Akstiletto Prime)
Atomos (Incarnon Form only)
Catabolyst (on Reload effect direct hit)
Cyanex
Marelok (Vaykor Marelok)
Secondary weapons with a guaranteed Impact Status Effect include:
Catchmoon (Secondary) (within 10 meters of the enemy)
Epitaph (Epitaph Prime) (on direct hit)
Lex (Lex Prime) (Incarnon Form only)
Sonicor
Tenet Plinx (Alt-fire direct hit)
Tenet Spirex
Tombfinger (Secondary)
Zakti (Zakti Prime) (on direct hit)
Zymos
While having a low status chance, Mesa's Regulators' extreme fire rate guarantees that the Slash status will proc.
Creeping Bullseye has potential synergy with this mod, due to its fire rate penalty reducing fire rate below 2.5 on some weapons.

High Noon is a stance mod for gunblades, blending swift slashes with gunshots.

The second, third, and fourth attacks of Final Showdown does 25% bonus Impact, Puncture, and Slash damage.
Every attack of Tomahawk Double-Tap does 25% bonus Impact, Puncture, and Slash damage.

## High Voltage
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** madurai | **Drain:** 4 | **Compatible:** Rifle | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
High Voltage is offered as a periodic item for sale by Baro Ki'Teer, costing 300 and 150,000 to purchase.
This mod can be obtained as a potential cache reward in Hive Sabotage missions in 22-23 expected tries and 150±50 tries to nearly guarantee.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +15% Electricity, +15% Status Chance
- **Rank 1:** +30% Electricity, +30% Status Chance
- **Rank 2:** +45% Electricity, +45% Status Chance
- **Rank 3:** +60% Electricity, +60% Status Chance

### Drop Locations
- Eris/Candiru (Caches), Rotation C — Rare (4.51%)
- Eris/Lepis (Caches), Rotation C — Rare (4.51%)
- Eris/Naeglar (Caches), Rotation C — Rare (4.51%)
- Eris/Psoro (Caches), Rotation C — Rare (4.51%)
- Eris/Viver (Caches), Rotation C — Rare (4.51%)

### Wiki
High Voltage is a mod that increases both the Electricity damage and status chance of a rifle by 15% per rank for a maximum of 60% at rank 3.
**Notes:**
High Voltage is additive with similar mods.
For example, a Snipetron with a max rank High Voltage and Rifle Aptitude will have a 12% (1 + 60% + 90%) status chance.
Can be combined with Rime Rounds, Thermite Rounds and Malignant Force to achieve a combined status chance bonus of 240%. With Rifle Aptitude and Hammer Shot, this bonus can be increased to 410%, more than quintupling the weapon's status chance.
With the four damage + status chance mods you can reach 100% status chance with any primaries that have a base status chance of 30% or higher.
Historical acquisition methods:
This mod was originally acquired by scoring over 250 points in the Tethra's Doom event.
This mod and the other Tethra's Doom mods were made available again during Operation: Mutalist Incursions as a drop in the unlockable Survival mission.

## Hok-Kaal
**Rarity:** Common | **Polarity:** universal | **Drain:** 0 | **Compatible:** ANY | **Max Rank:** 5

### Acquisition
Dropped by certain enemies in The Perita Rebellion
Sold by Marie as part of her rotating shop.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** After using Void Mode, the next Amp attack deals x0.5 bonus damage (cooldown 5s)
- **Rank 1:** After using Void Mode, the next Amp attack deals x1.0 bonus damage (cooldown 5s)
- **Rank 2:** After using Void Mode, the next Amp attack deals x1.5 bonus damage (cooldown 5s)
- **Rank 3:** After using Void Mode, the next Amp attack deals x2.0 bonus damage (cooldown 5s)
- **Rank 4:** After using Void Mode, the next Amp attack deals x2.5 bonus damage (cooldown 5s)
- **Rank 5:** After using Void Mode, the next Amp attack deals x3.0 bonus damage (cooldown 5s)

### Drop Locations
- Anarch Capsarii — Common (0.17%)
- Anarch Grineer Trapper — Common (0.17%)
- Anarch Tenebra — Common (0.17%)

### Wiki
Hok-Kaal is a Naramon-school Antique Mod for Tektolyst Artifacts that increases Amp Damage after using Void Mode.
**Notes:**
Bonus damage is multiplicative to sources of additive damage like Eternal Eradicate.
Because of its multiplicative nature unranked mod will halve total damage of a shot.
Bonus damage is only applied to the first individual shot. For example, if a single button press triggers a burst of Cantic Prism, only the first shot of this burst will be amplified. However, multishot from Hayan-Dabor or consecutive hits of a single glaive from Phahd Scaffold will have bonus damage on each hit.
The buff stays even when returning to your warframe.
Has a 5 second cooldown that starts after gaining the buff
This means you can use it in quick succession if you wait 5 seconds after gaining the buff before using it.

## Hollow Point
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** naramon | **Drain:** 4 | **Compatible:** Pistol | **Max Rank:** 5

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +10% Critical Damage, -2.5% Damage
- **Rank 1:** +20% Critical Damage, -5% Damage
- **Rank 2:** +30% Critical Damage, -7.5% Damage
- **Rank 3:** +40% Critical Damage, -10% Damage
- **Rank 4:** +50% Critical Damage, -12.5% Damage
- **Rank 5:** +60% Critical Damage, -15% Damage

### Drop Locations
- Derelict Vault — Rare (4.17%)

### Wiki
Hollow Point is a Corrupted Mod that increases critical multiplier while reducing the damage of Pistols.
**Notes:**
Target Cracker provides the same critical damage bonus without any penalty (although it does have a Madurai instead of a Naramon, which works with different builds).
In most cases, this mod will lower average damage. As such, it is not recommended for any gun other than those with a high critical hit rate (such as the Magnus) and only with particular mod setups already in place.
It is worth noting that modifications to the same stat are additive and as a result Hollow Point's damage penalty is less substantial when placed on a gun already modified with a high-ranking Hornet Strike. In fact, a gun with a maxed rank copy of Hornet Strike will drop from 320% damage per shot to 305% damage per shot, which is slightly less than an effective damage drop of 5%.
**Trivia:**
In firearm ballistics, hollow-point bullets are bullets made with a bore-hole, or a similar design in their tips, and therefore have less mass and potential energy. However, upon impact they rapidly deform and expand, causing a significantly wider wound channel and depending on the caliber and target, may even come to a stop when compared to other round types like a full metal jacket (solid bullet), which can easily over-penetrate and waste potential kinetic energy.

## Holster Amp
**Rarity:** Uncommon | **Polarity:** naramon | **Drain:** -2 | **Compatible:** AURA | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 5

### Acquisition
Available for 20 creds from the Nightwave Cred Offerings store on a rotational basis.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** On Equip: Squad gains 10% Weapon Damage for 3s
- **Rank 1:** On Equip: Squad gains 20% Weapon Damage for 3s
- **Rank 2:** On Equip: Squad gains 30% Weapon Damage for 3s
- **Rank 3:** On Equip: Squad gains 40% Weapon Damage for 3s
- **Rank 4:** On Equip: Squad gains 50% Weapon Damage for 3s
- **Rank 5:** On Equip: Squad gains 60% Weapon Damage for 3s

### Drop Locations
- Duviri/Endless: Tier 1 (Normal) — Legendary (0.67%)
- Duviri/Endless: Tier 3 (Normal) — Legendary (0.67%)

### Wiki
Holster Amp is an aura mod that increases weapon damage for 3 seconds upon holstering a weapon.
* Aura mods increase the amount of Mod Capacity
**Notes:**
Stacks additively with mods like Serration and Hornet Strike, as well as Vigorous Swap and Arcane Arachne
If a weapon is swapped while the buff is active, duration will be refreshed.
Triggers on manual and automatic weapon swaps.
Manual
Switching between guns or melee (default F)
Quick swap by:
holding a gun, then using a light melee attack (default E)
holding a melee, then firing a gun (default LMB) or aiming (default RMB)
Automatic
By enabling the "Auto Swap Weapon On Empty" toggle in the Keyboard/Mouse tab.
Also triggered after a Mercy kill.
The buff is applied at the end of the swapping animation.
Archwing weapons can trigger the mod, and are affected by its buff.
Special Interactions
Arcane Arachne#Special Interactions
Peacemaker cannot trigger Holster Amp, but can benefit from its buff.
After swapping to melee to trigger Holster Amp, Titania can refresh it while it's active by activating Razorwing.
Swapping between Dex Pixia and Diwata in Razorwing does not trigger Holster Amp.

## Homing Fang
Fast strikes, many hits.
**Rarity:** Uncommon | **Polarity:** naramon | **Drain:** -2 | **Compatible:** Daggers | **Max Rank:** 3

### Drop Locations
- Nauseous Crawler — Uncommon (0.33%)
- Nauseous Void Shade — Uncommon (0.33%)
- Void Shade — Uncommon (0.33%)

### Wiki
Homing Fang is a stance mod for daggers that features highly mobile spinning attacks and powerful finishers.
**Notes:**
Certain attacks have either a Puncture or Slash damage multiplier.
The second attack of Cutting Arches has a 2x Slash damage multiplier.
The first attack of Life Eater has a 2x Puncture damage multiplier.
The first hit of Cutting Arches' first attack only generates 1 combo instead of 2.
First attack of Butterfly Slash will open enemies up for finishers
**Trivia:**
The final attack of Cutting Arches has identical animation to Crimson Dervish's Coiling Impale combo.
Homing Fang is thus far, the only stance mod with a Polarity that uniquely matches a Syndicate-exclusive Weapon...in this case, it is the Rakta Dark Dagger.

### Wiki
Hornet Strike is a mod that increases the base damage of secondary weapons by 20% per rank, at a maximum of 220% at rank 10.
**Notes:**
Note that Hornet Strike damage is applied before every other damage type. Therefore, every other damage-increasing mod also benefits from Hornet Strike. A weapon with a base damage of 18 equipped with a rank 9 Hornet Strike mod (200%) would have a new base damage of 54: 18 (1 + 2.0) 54.
This new base damage will be what is used to calculate elemental damages.
Due to how it affects the base damage, this mod is necessary for all secondary weapons that aren't using damage increasing arcanes or GunCO effects.

## Howl
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** penjaga | **Drain:** 0 | **Compatible:** Raksa Kubrow | **Max Rank:** 5

### Acquisition
Howl is automatically acquired upon obtaining a Raksa Kubrow.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** The kubrow cries out with a blood-curdling howl that strikes fear into 5 enemies within 4m for 6s.
- **Rank 1:** The kubrow cries out with a blood-curdling howl that strikes fear into 7 enemies within 8m for 8s.
- **Rank 2:** The kubrow cries out with a blood-curdling howl that strikes fear into 9 enemies within 12m for 10s.
- **Rank 3:** The kubrow cries out with a blood-curdling howl that strikes fear into 11 enemies within 16m for 12s.
- **Rank 4:** The kubrow cries out with a blood-curdling howl that strikes fear into 13 enemies within 20m for 14s.
- **Rank 5:** The kubrow cries out with a blood-curdling howl that strikes fear into 15 enemies within 24m for 16s.

### Drop Locations
- Corrupted Drahk — Legendary (0.01%)
- Drahk — Legendary (0.01%)
- Kubrow — Legendary (0.01%)
- Kuva Drahk — Legendary (0.01%)
- Tamm — Legendary (0.01%)

### Wiki
Howl is a precept mod for the Raksa Kubrow that releases a frightening cry that inflicts Slow.
**Notes:**
Cooldown of 40s.
Affected enemies are slowed by ~40%.

## Hunter Command
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** madurai | **Drain:** 4 | **Compatible:** BEAST | **Max Rank:** 5

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Applying a Slash Status to an enemy causes the Companion to attack them for 1s.
- **Rank 1:** Applying a Slash Status to an enemy causes the Companion to attack them for 2s.
- **Rank 2:** Applying a Slash Status to an enemy causes the Companion to attack them for 3s.
- **Rank 3:** Applying a Slash Status to an enemy causes the Companion to attack them for 4s.
- **Rank 4:** Applying a Slash Status to an enemy causes the Companion to attack them for 5s.
- **Rank 5:** Applying a Slash Status to an enemy causes the Companion to attack them for 6s.

### Drop Locations
- Ghoul Auger Alpha — Rare (0.51%)
- Hemocyte — Uncommon (10.53%)
- Earth/Cetus (Level 40 - 50 Ghoul Bounty), Rotation A — Rare (7.14%)
- Earth/Cetus (Level 40 - 50 Ghoul Bounty), Rotation A — Rare (9.43%)

### Wiki
Hunter Command is a set mod dropped from the Hemocyte during Operation: Plague Star and Ghoul Purge bounties that causes your Beast companion to attack the nearest enemy affected by a Slash status effect.
**Notes:**
This is a behavioral mod that is designed to make your Companion attack a nearby enemy when that enemy receives a Slash status proc on them, when the companion would otherwise be passive.
If the companion is already attacking an enemy when a nearby enemy gains a Slash status proc, this mod will not make the companion change targets.
The companion will not teleport to the new target and will not have any enhanced movement speed as it approaches the target.
This mod can be used on Khora's pet Venari to make it periodically attack enemies even while it is in Heal or Protect mode.

## Hunter Munitions
**Rarity:** Uncommon | **Polarity:** madurai | **Drain:** 4 | **Compatible:** PRIMARY | **Max Rank:** 5

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +5% chance to apply on Critical
- **Rank 1:** +10% chance to apply on Critical
- **Rank 2:** +15% chance to apply on Critical
- **Rank 3:** +20% chance to apply on Critical
- **Rank 4:** +25% chance to apply on Critical
- **Rank 5:** +30% chance to apply on Critical

### Drop Locations
- Hemocyte — Uncommon (10.53%)
- Ghoul Rictus Alpha — Uncommon (11.05%)
- Earth/Cetus (Level 15 - 25 Ghoul Bounty), Rotation A — Rare (7.14%)
- Earth/Cetus (Level 15 - 25 Ghoul Bounty), Rotation A — Rare (9.43%)
- Earth/Cetus (Level 15 - 25 Ghoul Bounty), Rotation A — Rare (9.71%)

### Wiki
Hunter Munitions is a set mod dropped from the Hemocyte during Operation: Plague Star, and Ghoul Purge bounties, that grants Primary Weapons a chance to force a Slash status effect upon a Critical Hit, alongside any other Status Effect(s), that would have occurred from Status Chance, or a weapon trait.
**Notes:**
Ideal on weapons with high Critical Chance and low Status Chance such as the Lenz, in order to help it scale better against armored targets.
Pure elemental weapons such as Synapse can trigger Slash procs using this mod.
On direct hit, AoE weapons can potentially trigger Hunter Hunter Munitions twice, once on direct impact and an another on the AoE component.
Works with Exalted Weapons such as Artemis Bow.
Slash proc damage scales with damage multipliers. As such, Headshots, orange and red Critical Hits will greatly increase the damage dealt.
As with all Slash procs, their damage only depends on the weapons modded base Damage and is therefore not affected by elemental and physical damage mods.
Slash is affected by status duration mods. Using Hunter Track in tandem with Hunter Munitions will increase the base 6-second duration to 11.4 seconds, allowing for 11 ticks of damage rather than the normal 6 ticks.
The 30% trigger chance is independent to itself and is not affected by the weapon's Status Chance, or damage type distribution, besides being indirectly affected by its Critical Chance.
Cannot produce multiple procs in a single instance of damage alongside forced Slash from sources such as Internal Bleeding or the debuff from Seeking Talons, but can stack with Slash statuses applied using a weapon's innate status chance.
Procs of Hunter Munitions and Internal Bleeding are drawn independently, and if both proc at the same time, only 1 slash proc is applied.

## Hunter Recovery
**Rarity:** Common | **Polarity:** madurai | **Drain:** 4 | **Compatible:** BEAST | **Max Rank:** 5

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Warframe healed for +5% Companion Damage dealt
- **Rank 1:** Warframe healed for +10% Companion Damage dealt
- **Rank 2:** Warframe healed for +15% Companion Damage dealt
- **Rank 3:** Warframe healed for +20% Companion Damage dealt
- **Rank 4:** Warframe healed for +25% Companion Damage dealt
- **Rank 5:** Warframe healed for +30% Companion Damage dealt

### Drop Locations
- Earth/Cetus (Level 40 - 50 Ghoul Bounty), Rotation A — Uncommon (12.14%)
- Hemocyte — Uncommon (28.95%)
- Earth/Cetus (Level 40 - 50 Ghoul Bounty), Rotation A — Common (36.76%)
- Ghoul Auger Alpha — Common (37.94%)
- Earth/Cetus (Level 40 - 50 Ghoul Bounty), Rotation A — Rare (8.93%)

### Wiki
Hunter Recovery is a set mod dropped from the Hemocyte during Operation: Plague Star and Ghoul Purge bounties that allows a Kubrow or Kavat Companion to heal the player's Warframe for a percentage of the damage it deals.
**Notes:**
Does work with Panzer Vulpaphyla; in combination with Viral Quills, it can restore a good deal of health if there are a large number of enemies present.
Clones summoned from the Duplex Bond mod cannot and will not heal the Warframe.

## Hunter Synergy
**Rarity:** Uncommon | **Polarity:** madurai | **Drain:** 4 | **Compatible:** Claws | **Max Rank:** 5

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +5% Primary Weapon Critical Chance added to Companion
- **Rank 1:** +10% Primary Weapon Critical Chance added to Companion
- **Rank 2:** +15% Primary Weapon Critical Chance added to Companion
- **Rank 3:** +20% Primary Weapon Critical Chance added to Companion
- **Rank 4:** +25% Primary Weapon Critical Chance added to Companion
- **Rank 5:** +30% Primary Weapon Critical Chance added to Companion

### Drop Locations
- Hemocyte — Uncommon (10.53%)
- Ghoul Auger Alpha — Uncommon (11.05%)
- Earth/Cetus (Level 40 - 50 Ghoul Bounty), Rotation A — Rare (7.14%)
- Earth/Cetus (Level 40 - 50 Ghoul Bounty), Rotation A — Rare (9.43%)
- Earth/Cetus (Level 40 - 50 Ghoul Bounty), Rotation A — Rare (9.71%)

### Wiki
Hunter Synergy is a set mod for Beast Claws that grants the Companion a Critical Chance bonus based on the total Critical Chance of the player's Primary Weapon.
**Notes:**
The mod additively increases the Kubrow's or Kavat's critical chance by a percentage of the total Critical Chance on the player's primary weapon.
For example, equipping a Soma with 75% Critical Chance will add 22.5% Critical Chance to the companion.
This amount is affected by Point Strike and other critical chance-enhancing mods for primary weapons, including temporary buffs like Galvanized Scope.
This bonus stacks after other mods have been applied.
If you have a Primary Weapon with 100% Critical Chance, a Kubrow with 10% base critical chance with both Bite and Hunter Synergy equipped will have: 10% (Base) + 10% x 330% (Bite) + 30% (Hunter Synergy) = 73%.
For weapons with Incarnon forms, switching to Incarnon form will increase the Critical Chance bonus for the purpose of Tenacious Bond, but does not actually increase the Critical Chance of the Claws.
For example, Strun Prime without an Evolution IV bonus and modded with Critical Deceleration has a Critical Chance of 72% outside of Incarnon form and 144% in it. A Kubrow with 10% base Critical Chance will have 31.6% Critical Chance with the Strun Prime equipped, but switching to Incarnon form will activate Tenacious Bond's bonus while the Kubrow's Critical Chance remains 31.6%.

## Hunter Track
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** madurai | **Drain:** 4 | **Compatible:** PRIMARY | **Max Rank:** 5

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +15% Status Duration
- **Rank 1:** +30% Status Duration
- **Rank 2:** +45% Status Duration
- **Rank 3:** +60% Status Duration
- **Rank 4:** +75% Status Duration
- **Rank 5:** +90% Status Duration

### Drop Locations
- Ghoul Rictus Alpha — Rare (0.51%)
- Hemocyte — Uncommon (10.53%)
- Earth/Cetus (Level 15 - 25 Ghoul Bounty), Rotation A — Rare (7.14%)
- Earth/Cetus (Level 15 - 25 Ghoul Bounty), Rotation A — Rare (9.43%)

### Wiki
Hunter Track is a set mod dropped from the Hemocyte during Operation: Plague Star and Ghoul Purge bounties that increases the Status Duration of Status Effects inflicted by Primary Weapons.
**Notes:**
Can be equipped on any Primary Weapon.
Stacks with Lingering Torment on Shotguns for +180% Status Duration.
Stacks with Continuous Misery on Rifles for +190% status duration.
The base mod's effects are only visible on Status Effects with an associated duration, either increasing the duration of the associated debuff (Puncture, Cold, Magnetic, Radiation, and Viral), or the number of ticks associated with DoT effects (Slash, Toxin, Gas, and Heat).
This mod will not affect the duration of crowd control effects generated by Status Effects (staggers from Impact, the panic duration of Heat, and the stun animation caused by Electricity).
Increased Status Duration will slow down the Armor Strip effect of Heat. For example, with +100% status duration, the armor strip will occur every second instead of every half second. As a result, the full 50% armor strip will only be achieved after 4 seconds.
Status Effects from Syndicate Radial Effects are not affected by this mod.
**Trivia:**
Along with Vigilante Armaments and Hunter Munitions, this is one of the only primary weapon mods that can be used on any primary weapon.

## Hunter's Bonesaw

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Increase Finisher Damage by 20% against prone enemies.
- **Rank 1:** Increase Finisher Damage by 40% against prone enemies.
- **Rank 2:** Increase Finisher Damage by 60% against prone enemies.
- **Rank 3:** Increase Finisher Damage by 80% against prone enemies.
- **Rank 4:** Increase Finisher Damage by 100% against prone enemies.
- **Rank 5:** Increase Finisher Damage by 120% against prone enemies.

### Drop Locations
- Kela De Thaym — Uncommon (11.11%)

### Wiki
Hunter's Bonesaw is a Ripkas-exclusive mod that increases damage and Finisher damage against enemies that are knocked down.
**Tips:**
This works well with Divine Spears, as it easily leaves enemies vulnerable to ground finishers.
**Trivia:**
This mod, along with the other Executioner mods, are the second set mods to feature non-generic enemies on the mod card, the first being Vengeful Revenant.

## Huntersetmod

## Hush
**Rarity:** Common | **Polarity:** naramon | **Drain:** 2 | **Compatible:** Rifle | **Max Rank:** 3

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Reduces the chance an enemy will hear gunfire by 25%.
- **Rank 1:** Reduces the chance an enemy will hear gunfire by 50%.
- **Rank 2:** Reduces the chance an enemy will hear gunfire by 75%.
- **Rank 3:** Reduces the chance an enemy will hear gunfire by 100%.

### Drop Locations
- Orokin Drone — Uncommon (0.76%)
- Orokin Spectator — Uncommon (0.76%)

### Wiki
Hush is a Mod that reduces Noise Level on Rifle/Bow Weapons. At max rank, it renders any Rifles/Bows completely silent and undetectable while firing.
**Notes:**
Added in Update 10.
This Mod reduces or eliminates firing noise. Enemies that are not killed quick enough will still be alerted.
Shooting a silent Weapon within 6 meters of an enemy or at the terrain near an enemy will cause them to stop and look around, but will not change their actual alert state.
Applying this Mod on a high damage Weapon (such as the Latron Prime) will make it suitable for stealth combat, making it a decent alternative to a bow.
At max rank, Hush will allow Ivara to stay invisible while Prowl is active.

## Hushed Invisibility
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Loki | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired by attaining the rank of Maxim under the Arbiters of Hexis, or the rank of Exalted under the Red Veil, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.
This mod can periodically be acquired from The Circuit.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Invisibility Augment: Weapon noise is reduced by 50% while invisible.
- **Rank 1:** Invisibility Augment: Weapon noise is reduced by 65% while invisible.
- **Rank 2:** Invisibility Augment: Weapon noise is reduced by 80% while invisible.
- **Rank 3:** Invisibility Augment: Weapon noise is reduced by 100% while invisible.

### Drop Locations
- Arbiters of Hexis, Maxim — Common (100.00%)
- Red Veil, Exalted — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Hushed Invisibility is a Warframe Augment Mod for Loki's Invisibility that allows it to reduce the noise level of weapons fired.
**Notes:**
When doing a Stealth mission, enemies still need to be killed in a short time period, otherwise they will become alerted and alert all nearby enemies.
Surviving enemies will also blindly fire at the point of origin of the attack, posing risk for Loki.
This augment will not be active if Loki becomes invisible by other means, e.g. Untraceable, Arcane Trickery, or etc.

## Hydraulic Crosshairs
**Rarity:** Common | **Polarity:** madurai | **Drain:** 2 | **Compatible:** Pistol | **Max Rank:** 5

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** On Headshot: +22.5% Critical Chance when Aiming for 2s
- **Rank 1:** On Headshot: +45% Critical Chance when Aiming for 3s
- **Rank 2:** On Headshot: +67.5% Critical Chance when Aiming for 5s
- **Rank 3:** On Headshot: +90% Critical Chance when Aiming for 6s
- **Rank 4:** On Headshot: +112.5% Critical Chance when Aiming for 8s
- **Rank 5:** On Headshot: +135% Critical Chance when Aiming for 9s

### Drop Locations
- Lua/Pavlov (Spy), Rotation A — Uncommon (12.18%)
- Deimos/Cambion Drift (Level 30 - 40 Cambion Drift Bounty), Rotation A — Uncommon (12.50%)
- Deimos/Cambion Drift (Level 30 - 40 Cambion Drift Bounty), Rotation A — Rare (5.38%)
- Deimos/Cambion Drift (Level 30 - 40 Cambion Drift Bounty), Rotation A — Rare (6.85%)

### Wiki
Hydraulic Crosshairs is a common Pistol mod that increases critical chance while aiming for a brief time upon a headshot.
**Notes:**
Hydraulic Crosshairs is additive with similar mods. For example, a Sicarus with a max rank Hydraulic Crosshairs and Pistol Gambit will have a 16% (1 + 135% + 120%) critical chance.
Hydraulic Crosshairs will trigger upon headshots, i.e. any weapon hit to an enemy's head, whether the target survives or not.
Headshots will refresh Hydraulic Crosshairs' timer.
If activated, Hydraulic Crosshairs will display an icon timer beside the player's health UI, and the Warframe will be briefly surrounded by an energy ribbon.
Hydraulic Crosshairs' buff does not seem to multiply prior flat-crit% buffs such as Arcane Avenger.
**Tips:**
This mod is best used on full-auto pistols with high critical stats, such as the Aksomati, as their high rate of fire ensures that they have a better chance of landing headshots against enemies.
Can be combined with Primed Pistol Gambit for a total increase of +322% after getting a shot to an enemies' head. This allows the Akmagnus, Lato Vandal, Lex Prime, Spira, Spira Prime, Pandero, Plinx and Twin Grakatas to gain a critical chance of over 100% after performing a headshot, allowing orange crits.

## Hysterical Assault
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Valkyr | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
Can be acquired by attaining the rank of Partner under The Perrin Sequence, or the rank of Flawless under New Loka, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Hysteria Augment: Attack with Valkyr's Talons to leap onto targeted enemies up to 50m away. Gain +20% Parkour Velocity and +20% Sprint Speed for 5s.
- **Rank 1:** Hysteria Augment: Attack with Valkyr's Talons to leap onto targeted enemies up to 50m away. Gain +30% Parkour Velocity and +30% Sprint Speed for 5s.
- **Rank 2:** Hysteria Augment: Attack with Valkyr's Talons to leap onto targeted enemies up to 50m away. Gain +40% Parkour Velocity and +40% Sprint Speed for 5s.
- **Rank 3:** Hysteria Augment: Attack with Valkyr's Talons to leap onto targeted enemies up to 50m away. Gain +50% Parkour Velocity and +50% Sprint Speed for 5s.

### Drop Locations
- New Loka, Flawless — Common (100.00%)
- The Perrin Sequence, Partner — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Hysterical Assault is a Warframe Augment/Exilus mod for Valkyr that allows her to pounce onto enemies while in Hysteria by meleeing while aiming, dealing damage on contact, then provides a boost to Parkour Velocity and Sprint Speed. It also provides brief Invulnerability when switching off her Valkyr Talons while in Hysteria.
**Notes:**
Leap distance is affected by Ability Range.
Leaping requires the reticle to be aimed near an enemy.
Cannot be equipped at the same time as Enraged.
The added invulnerability can allow Valkyr to briefly fire another weapon and switch back to melee without ever losing invulnerability.
Can be cast during any melee attack to cancel it, or slam attack to slightly redirect the impact.
Can be cast during abilities like Rip-Line, and potentially other non full body animations.
The contact damage can not crit, but otherwise acts as a single attack from Valkyr Talons.
25.3

### Wiki
Hysterical Fixation is a Conclave-exclusive Warframe Augment Mod for Valkyr's Hysteria that replenishes a percentage of your shields for every kill while in Hysteria.

## Iatric Mycelium
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** penjaga | **Drain:** 0 | **Compatible:** Vizier Predasite | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
Iatric Mycelium is automatically acquired upon obtaining a Vizier Predasite.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Release a trail of spores every 20s that heal the companion and its allies for 75 health over 5s.
- **Rank 1:** Release a trail of spores every 16s that heal the companion and its allies for 150 health over 5s.
- **Rank 2:** Release a trail of spores every 12s that heal the companion and its allies for 225 health over 5s.
- **Rank 3:** Release a trail of spores every 8s that heal the companion and its allies for 300 health over 5s.

### Wiki
Iatric Mycelium is a precept mod for the Vizier Predasite to leave a trail of spores that apply Health regeneration to allies when touched.
**Notes:**
Spore trails remain active for 5 seconds.
The regeneration effect can be refreshed by walking into the spores again.
Can heal both the Operator and allied Necramech that come in contact.
Can heal Nekros' Shadows of the Dead
Spore trail follows the Predasite, and as such all spores may remain in one spot.
Seems to cast every ~23 seconds, instead of the listed 8 seconds at max rank.
Despite the fact other companion precepts can trigger Archon Mod effects like Archon Stretch, this precept cannot trigger Archon Intensify.

## Ice Spring
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** vazarin | **Drain:** 2 | **Compatible:** WARFRAME | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 10

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +2.2% to Parkour Velocity, +2.2% Aim Glide/Wall Latch Duration, +25% Cold on Bullet Jump

- **Rank 3:** +8.8% to Parkour Velocity, +8.8% Aim Glide/Wall Latch Duration, +100% Cold on Bullet Jump
- **Rank 4:** +11% to Parkour Velocity, +11% Aim Glide/Wall Latch Duration, +125% Cold on Bullet Jump
- **Rank 5:** +13.2% to Parkour Velocity, +13.2% Aim Glide/Wall Latch Duration, +150% Cold on Bullet Jump
- **Rank 6:** +15.4% to Parkour Velocity, +15.4% Aim Glide/Wall Latch Duration, +175% Cold on Bullet Jump
- **Rank 7:** +17.6% to Parkour Velocity, +17.6% Aim Glide/Wall Latch Duration, +200% Cold on Bullet Jump
- **Rank 8:** +19.8% to Parkour Velocity, +19.8% Aim Glide/Wall Latch Duration, +225% Cold on Bullet Jump
- **Rank 9:** +22% to Parkour Velocity, +22% Aim Glide/Wall Latch Duration, +250% Cold on Bullet Jump
- **Rank 10:** +24.2% to Parkour Velocity, +24.2% Aim Glide/Wall Latch Duration, +275% Cold on Bullet Jump

### Drop Locations
- Drekar Butcher — Legendary (0.02%)
- Kuva Jester — Uncommon (0.50%)
- Duviri/Endless: Tier 6 (Normal) — Rare (1.03%)
- Duviri/Endless: Tier 4 (Normal) — Rare (1.49%)
- Uranus/Desdemona (Caches), Rotation C — Rare (4.51%)
- Uranus/Prospero (Caches), Rotation C — Rare (4.51%)
- Uranus/Setebos (Caches), Rotation C — Rare (4.51%)

### Wiki
Ice Spring is an Exilus mod that increases the speed of Bullet Jump, lengthens the duration of Aim Glide and Wall Latch, and adds Cold damage to Bullet Jump.
**Notes:**
Also increases the distance/speed of rolls.
At max rank, Ice Spring adds a freezing effect that deals 375 Cold damage with a 200% status chance within a 3-meter radius as well as a guaranteed knockdown within a 0.75-meter radius from where a player initiates a Bullet Jump.
Visual effects added during Bullet Jumps will always be the same color, regardless of Warframe Energy or Emission colors.
Only one of Firewalker, Ice Spring, Lightning Dash, Toxic Flight can be equipped at one time.
Last updated: Update 22.19.1

## Ice Storm
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** madurai | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Pistol | **Max Rank:** 3

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +10% Magazine Capacity, +10% Cold
- **Rank 1:** +20% Magazine Capacity, +20% Cold
- **Rank 2:** +30% Magazine Capacity, +30% Cold
- **Rank 3:** +40% Magazine Capacity, +40% Cold

### Drop Locations
- Nightmare Mode Rewards, Rotation A — Uncommon (18.97%)
- Sister Of Parvos (Ascension Hard Mode) — Uncommon (20.00%)
- Sister Of Parvos (Ascension Mode) — Uncommon (6.60%)

### Wiki
Ice Storm is a mod that increases the magazine capacity and Cold damage of Pistols.
**Notes:**
For any non-integer magazine values the magazine size is simply rounded to the nearest whole number. For example, a Angstrum modded with a max rank Ice Storm will have a calculated magazine size of 3 (1 + 40%), which will simply round to 3 (1 + 40%).
Ice Storm gives a higher magazine capacity increase than its counterpart Slip Magazine with an additional 10% bonus to magazine size as well as Cold damage for the same cost, the only difference being that Slip Magazine has a Naramon polarity instead of Ice Storm's Madurai polarity.

## Ice Wave Impedance
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Frost | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired by attaining the rank of General under Steel Meridian, the rank of Genius under Cephalon Suda, or the rank of Tempest in Conclave, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Ice Wave Augment: Create a frozen trail for 6 seconds. Enemies that touch it are inflicted with Cold Status every second.
- **Rank 1:** Ice Wave Augment: Create a frozen trail for 8 seconds. Enemies that touch it are inflicted with Cold Status every second.
- **Rank 2:** Ice Wave Augment: Create a frozen trail for 10 seconds. Enemies that touch it are inflicted with Cold Status every second.
- **Rank 3:** Ice Wave Augment: Create a frozen trail for 12 seconds. Enemies that touch it are inflicted with Cold Status every second.

### Drop Locations
- Cephalon Suda, Genius — Common (100.00%)
- Conclave, Tempest — Common (100.00%)
- Steel Meridian, General — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Ice Wave Impedance is a Warframe Augment Mod usable in both PvE and Conclave for Frost's Ice Wave that makes it leave a trail of frozen ground after casting, applying Cold status effect per second.
**Notes:**
Cold Status per second is not affected by mods.
Trail duration is affected by Ability Duration.
Multiple frozen trails can be active at once, limited only by the duration of each trail.
Allied players are unaffected by the trail.
Nullifier Crewman will destroy any cold area that their bubbles touch.
The trail will not apply any Cold status effects to the enemies with overguard.
The trail will apply maximum of 6Cold status effects to the enemies on the trail (combining to 10 with the initial cast of the ability)
The trail does not count as part of the Ice Wave ability and will not freeze enemies for the enhanced duration of Ice Wave freeze. Instead on reaching 10Cold status effects the standard Frozen duration of 3 seconds will be applied.
**Tips:**
Works extremely well against melee-focused enemies such as most Infested, slowing entire mobs of enemies and making it easier to kill them.
Infested can be even further slowed by the player and squad members equipping Infested Impedance.
This augment synergizes very well with Loki's Radial Disarm, forcing enemy units to use melee attacks and thus become more susceptible to the augment's effects.
**Trivia:**
Once cast, the effects of the frozen trail created by this Augment mod operates in a similar fashion to the Cryo Floors in Orokin Void missions.
Along with Afterburn, Iron Shrapnel, and Mesa's Waltz, this mod is one of the few Augments usable in Conclave.

## Icy Avalanche
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Frost | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired by attaining the rank of General under Steel Meridian, or the rank of Genius under Cephalon Suda, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Avalanche Augment: Allies within Affinity Range are coated in ice that grants 40 Overguard per enemy hit. Overguard increases by 5% of your Armor once per enemy.
- **Rank 1:** Avalanche Augment: Allies within Affinity Range are coated in ice that grants 45 Overguard per enemy hit. Overguard increases by 10% of your Armor once per enemy.
- **Rank 2:** Avalanche Augment: Allies within Affinity Range are coated in ice that grants 50 Overguard per enemy hit. Overguard increases by 15% of your Armor once per enemy.
- **Rank 3:** Avalanche Augment: Allies within Affinity Range are coated in ice that grants 60 Overguard per enemy hit. Overguard increases by 20% of your Armor once per enemy.

### Drop Locations
- Cephalon Suda, Genius — Common (100.00%)
- Steel Meridian, General — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Icy Avalanche is a Warframe Augment Mod for Frost's Avalanche that grants allies within the Affinity Range Overguard for each enemy hit. A percentage of Frost's armor is also added to the Overguard once per enemy.
**Notes:**
Overguard granted by Icy Avalanche has a cap of Ability Strength15,000.
Enemies still frozen by a previous casting of Avalanche will still grant Overguard.
**Tips:**
Ivara's Quiver, Vauban's Bastille vortex and similar abilities that attract enemies to a single location synergize well with Icy Avalanche, as they can be used to group enemies closer together allowing for a larger number of enemies to be hit by Avalanche.

## Immolated Radiance
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Ember | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired by attaining the rank of General under Steel Meridian, or the rank of Exalted under the Red Veil, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Immolation Augment: Allies within Affinity range will receive 20% of Immolation's Damage Reduction.
- **Rank 1:** Immolation Augment: Allies within Affinity range will receive 30% of Immolation's Damage Reduction.
- **Rank 2:** Immolation Augment: Allies within Affinity range will receive 40% of Immolation's Damage Reduction.
- **Rank 3:** Immolation Augment: Allies within Affinity range will receive 50% of Immolation's Damage Reduction.

### Drop Locations
- Red Veil, Exalted — Common (100.00%)
- Steel Meridian, General — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Immolated Radiance is a Warframe Augment Mod for Ember that allows Immolation to affect friendly players within affinity range, reducing incoming damage.
**Notes:**
As Immolation's Damage Reduction has a maximum value of 90%, the Damage Reduction shared via this augment is capped at 45%.
This mod only affects other Warframes.

### Wiki
Impaler Munitions is a Conclave exclusive pistol mod that converts an amount of physical damage to Puncture damage.
**Notes:**
As this mod only converts physical damage, weapons that deal elemental damage will be unaffected.
A weapon may only have one damage conversion mod equipped at a time.
**Trivia:**
Despite it being a Puncture mod, the Vasto deals Slash damage.

## Infectious Bite
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** penjaga | **Drain:** 0 | **Compatible:** Medjay Predasite | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
Infectious Bite is automatically acquired upon obtaining a Medjay Predasite.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** An attack that deals +50% Finisher Damage and infects the target with a Virus that grows 1 volatile pustules that react to damage and explode.
- **Rank 1:** An attack that deals +100% Finisher Damage and infects the target with a Virus that grows 2 volatile pustules that react to damage and explode.
- **Rank 2:** An attack that deals +150% Finisher Damage and infects the target with a Virus that grows 3 volatile pustules that react to damage and explode.
- **Rank 3:** An attack that deals +200% Finisher Damage and infects the target with a Virus that grows 4 volatile pustules that react to damage and explode.

### Wiki
Infectious Bite is a precept mod for the Medjay Predasite to perform a Finisher attack on enemies and infect them with a volatile virus.
**Notes:**
If the target survives the finisher, pustules will grow across their body, afflicting them with a Viral proc. The pustules will absorb damage and burst after either 3 seconds or the pustules have absorb enough damage, dealing all accumulated damage to enemies within 3 meters.

## Infectious Injection
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** madurai | **Drain:** 4 | **Compatible:** Archmelee | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod is available as a potential end of mission reward during a Fomorian Sabotage encounter.

- **Rank 2:** +45% Toxin, +45% Status Chance
- **Rank 3:** +60% Toxin, +60% Status Chance

- Blite Captain — Uncommon (3.00%)
- Fomorian Sabotage — Rare (5.00%)

### Wiki
Infectious Injection is a mod that increases Toxin damage and status chance on Archmelee weapons by 15% per rank for a maximum of 60% at rank 3.
**Notes:**
Infectious Injection is additive with similar mods. For example, an Kaszas with a max rank Infectious Injection and Sudden Impact will have a 15% (1 + 60% + 60%) status chance.
Can be stacked with Poisonous Sting for a total of 180% Toxin damage.

### Wiki
Infested Impedance is an aura mod that reduces the speed of Infested enemies within a 16 meters radius around the user.
* Aura mods increase the amount of Mod Capacity
**Notes:**
Only applies to enemies within 16 meters.
Multiple copies stack additively.
Does not affect attack speed or special movement abilities such Leaper's leap attack or dodge.
**Trivia:**
It was obtainable during the Artifact Defense Event, and is now in Nightwave Offerings.
Update 11.5.6 lowered speed reduction from 5% to 3% per rank.
Update 13.8 changed the Aura to a radial effect focused on the user, as opposed to a map-wide effect.

## Infiltrate
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Ivara | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired by attaining the rank of Genius under Cephalon Suda, or the rank of Partner under The Perrin Sequence, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.
This mod can periodically be acquired from The Circuit.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Prowl Augment: Ivara is able to bypass laser barriers and gains 10% Movement Speed.
- **Rank 1:** Prowl Augment: Ivara is able to bypass laser barriers and gains 15% Movement Speed.
- **Rank 2:** Prowl Augment: Ivara is able to bypass laser barriers and gains 20% Movement Speed.
- **Rank 3:** Prowl Augment: Ivara is able to bypass laser barriers and gains 25% Movement Speed.

### Drop Locations
- Cephalon Suda, Genius — Common (100.00%)
- The Perrin Sequence, Partner — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Infiltrate is a Warframe Augment Mod for Ivara that allows her to pass through Laser Barriers unharmed, and increases her Movement Speed while Prowl is active.
**Notes:**
When Prowl is active with Infiltrate equipped, Ivara will not be affected by Laser Barriers, Sensor Bars, Laser Plates, or Cutting Lasers. Bypassing them will not set off any connected Spy, Rescue, or Reactor Sabotage security alarms.
The effects of Infiltrate do not apply during the casting animation for Prowl, but only after the animation is completed.
In the Earth Grineer Forest Vault with the downward tunnel and elevator ramp over water, the Motion Detector will still trigger the alarms if Ivara touches it, however it will not damage her. It cannot be destroyed and thus it is recommended to take an alternate route.
This is most likely a bug.
Lasers in Infested Ship Rescue will still trigger alarms on contact.
Speed bonus is affected by Ability Strength.
While on ziplines, including Quiver, Prowl's movement speed penalty doesn't apply but Infiltrate's speed bonus does, causing Ivara to run much faster on ziplines.

## Insatiable
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Nidus | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired by attaining the rank of General under the Steel Meridian, or the rank of Partner under the The Perrin Sequence, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.
This mod can periodically be acquired from The Circuit.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Ravenous Augment: Nidus has a chance for additional Mutation stacks while in the infestation. The base 35% chance increases with power strength, and multiple stacks are possible above 100%.
- **Rank 1:** Ravenous Augment: Nidus has a chance for additional Mutation stacks while in the infestation. The base 40% chance increases with power strength, and multiple stacks are possible above 100%.
- **Rank 2:** Ravenous Augment: Nidus has a chance for additional Mutation stacks while in the infestation. The base 50% chance increases with power strength, and multiple stacks are possible above 100%.
- **Rank 3:** Ravenous Augment: Nidus has a chance for additional Mutation stacks while in the infestation. The base 60% chance increases with power strength, and multiple stacks are possible above 100%.

### Drop Locations
- Steel Meridian, General — Common (100.00%)
- The Perrin Sequence, Partner — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Insatiable is a Warframe Augment Mod for Nidus's Ravenous that grants him a chance to generate an additional Mutation stack when gaining one while standing on the spawning grounds.
**Notes:**
Bonus Mutation stack chance is affected by Ability Strength.
Stack chance can increase over 100%, giving Nidus additional chances to receive multiple stacks. For example, 150% chance guarantees 1 additional stack and a 50% chance to generate a second stack.
Stacks gained from enemies outside of Ravenous' radius can generate bonus Mutation stacks as long as Nidus stands on the spawning grounds.
Mutation stacks gained from Maggot explosions at the end of Ravenous' duration can generate bonus Mutation stacks.
**Tips:**
The augment enables players to collect Mutation stacks at a much higher rate.
Using a high range Larva lets players deal with faraway enemies easily while staying on the spawning grounds.
Casting Parasitic Link on an ally can significantly boost the stack gain.

## Insulation
**Rarity:** Common | **Polarity:** vazarin | **Drain:** 4 | **Compatible:** WARFRAME | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 5

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +10% Cold Resistance
- **Rank 1:** +20% Cold Resistance
- **Rank 2:** +30% Cold Resistance
- **Rank 3:** +40% Cold Resistance
- **Rank 4:** +50% Cold Resistance
- **Rank 5:** +60% Cold Resistance

### Drop Locations
- Kuva Napalm — Uncommon (0.76%)
- Napalm — Uncommon (0.76%)
- Narmer Napalm — Uncommon (0.76%)
- Tusk Napalm — Uncommon (0.76%)

### Wiki
Insulation increases the Cold damage resistance of a Warframe's health and shields.
**Notes:**
Damage resistance works on both health and shields.
Increases effective health/shields by +150% against Cold damage.
100% (100% - 60%) 2.5x more effective health
Damage resistance bonus is multiplicative with other sources of damage resistance such as Adaptation and Aviator.
This is different from the Warm Coat mod. It does not reduce the effect of the Ice environmental hazard, which is a reduction to max shields, but instead reduces Cold damage.

## Internal Bleeding
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** madurai | **Drain:** 10 | **Compatible:** Rifle | **Max Rank:** 5

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Impact Status Effects have 10% chance to apply a Slash Status Effect (x2 when Fire Rate is below 2.5)

- **Rank 2:** Impact Status Effects have 20% chance to apply a Slash Status Effect (x2 when Fire Rate is below 2.5)
- **Rank 3:** Impact Status Effects have 25% chance to apply a Slash Status Effect (x2 when Fire Rate is below 2.5)
- **Rank 4:** Impact Status Effects have 30% chance to apply a Slash Status Effect (x2 when Fire Rate is below 2.5)
- **Rank 5:** Impact Status Effects have 35% chance to apply a Slash Status Effect (x2 when Fire Rate is below 2.5)

### Drop Locations

- Neptune/Brom Cluster (Caches), Rotation B — Rare (9.09%)
- Neptune/Enkidu Ice Drifts (Caches), Rotation B — Rare (9.09%)

- Pluto/Obol Crossing (Caches), Rotation B — Rare (9.09%)
- Pluto/Peregrine Axis (Caches), Rotation B — Rare (9.09%)
- Pluto/Profit Margin (Caches), Rotation B — Rare (9.09%)
- Pluto/Seven Sirens (Caches), Rotation B — Rare (9.09%)
- Veil Proxima/Arc Silver (Caches), Rotation B — Rare (9.09%)
- Veil Proxima/Calabash (Caches), Rotation B — Rare (9.09%)
- Veil Proxima/Erato (Caches), Rotation B — Rare (9.09%)
