- Veil Proxima/Numina (Caches), Rotation B — Rare (9.09%)
- Veil Proxima/Sabmir Cloud (Caches), Rotation B — Rare (9.09%)
- Venus/Beacon Shield Ring (Caches), Rotation B — Rare (9.68%)
- Venus/Bifrost Echo (Caches), Rotation B — Rare (9.68%)
- Venus/Falling Glory (Caches), Rotation B — Rare (9.68%)
- Venus/Luckless Expanse (Caches), Rotation B — Rare (9.68%)
- Venus/Orvin-Haarc (Caches), Rotation B — Rare (9.68%)
- Venus/Vesper Strait (Caches), Rotation B — Rare (9.68%)

### Wiki
Internal Bleeding is a rifle mod that gives every Impact status effect a chance to produce an instance of a Slash proc. This chance is doubled when the weapon's fire rate is below 2.5.
**Notes:**
Cannot produce multiple procs in a single instance of damage alongside any other Slash sources, such as a weapon's innate Slash, Hunter Munitions, or the debuff from Seeking Talons.
Procs of Hunter Munitions and Internal Bleeding are drawn independently, and if both proc at the same time, only 1 slash proc is applied.
With both mods, if a shot both crits and applies impact status, the probability of Slash proc is 0.35*0.3 + (1-0.35)*0.3 + 0.35*(1-0.3) Probability of both + Probability of only Hunter Munition + Probability of only Internal Bleeding (with attack speed >= 2.5) or 0.70*0.3 + (1-0.70)*0.3 + 0.70*(1-0.3) Probability of both + Probability of only Hunter Munition + Probability of only Internal Bleeding (with attack speed < 2.5).
Proccing impact more than once in a single instance of damage will not allow this mod to proc more than once, nor will it increase the chance of the mod proccing.
Hard Engage works with this mod.
Fire Rate buffs such as Gauss' Redline, Wisp' Haste Reservoirs and Arcane Acceleration can negatively impact this mod's performance since it may increase the weapon's fire rate past 2.5.
If the weapon's fire rate is exactly 2.5 it won't count towards the 70% chance.
For bows, the threshold is still depending of the Fire Rate, even if it is an hidden stat. Most bows have an unmodded Fire Rate of 1, except : Cinta with 1.5 Fire Rate, and Kuva Bramma and Nataruk with 0.667 Fire Rate.

## Intrepid Stand
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Styanax | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired by attaining the rank of Flawless under New Loka, or the rank of Maxim under Arbiters of Hexis, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Final Stand Augment: Each javelin that damages an enemy grants 30 Overguard to Styanax and 15 Overguard to his allies within Affinity Range.
- **Rank 1:** Final Stand Augment: Each javelin that damages an enemy grants 40 Overguard to Styanax and 20 Overguard to his allies within Affinity Range.
- **Rank 2:** Final Stand Augment: Each javelin that damages an enemy grants 50 Overguard to Styanax and 25 Overguard to his allies within Affinity Range.
- **Rank 3:** Final Stand Augment: Each javelin that damages an enemy grants 60 Overguard to Styanax and 30 Overguard to his allies within Affinity Range.

### Drop Locations
- Arbiters of Hexis, Maxim — Common (100.00%)
- New Loka, Flawless — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Intrepid Stand is a Warframe Augment Mod for Styanax that grants Overguard for himself and allies in Affinity Range upon striking enemies with Final Stand.
**Notes:**
Overguard amount is affected by Ability Strength.
Overguard granted by Intrepid Stand has a cap of 15,000, affected by Ability Strength.
Only ally Warframes and Companions can receive Overguard. It does not benefit Specters, Defense Objects, or other allied NPCs.
Hits are counted by either direct hits or the area of effect damage of the Javelins, regardless if they deal damage to the enemies.
As Overguard has a 0.5 second "gating" before shields are damaged, this gives Styanax synergy with his Rally Point, allowing him to regain shields for a full shield gate the next time they are depleted.
Functionally, this allows Styanax (and nearby allies with shields) to potentially have 1.8 seconds of protection before health is damaged.

Despite being a Parazon mod, Flawed Intruder can be ranked up and shows a capacity cost.

## Investigator
**Rarity:** Common | **Polarity:** penjaga | **Drain:** 4 | **Compatible:** Helios | **Max Rank:** 5

### Acquisition
Investigator is automatically acquired upon obtaining Helios or Helios Prime.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Helios Sentinel will scan objects and enemies within 20m over 5.0s. This consumes Codex Scanner charges.
- **Rank 1:** Helios Sentinel will scan objects and enemies within 26m over 4.4s. This consumes Codex Scanner charges.
- **Rank 2:** Helios Sentinel will scan objects and enemies within 32m over 3.8s. This consumes Codex Scanner charges.
- **Rank 3:** Helios Sentinel will scan objects and enemies within 38m over 3.2s. This consumes Codex Scanner charges.
- **Rank 4:** Helios Sentinel will scan objects and enemies within 44m over 2.6s. This consumes Codex Scanner charges.
- **Rank 5:** Helios Sentinel will scan objects and enemies within 50m over 2.0s. This consumes Codex Scanner charges.

### Wiki
Investigator is a precept mod for Helios and Helios Prime that scan objects and enemies it encounters, consuming Codex Scanner or Synthesis Scanner charges.
**Notes:**
Requires a Codex Scanner or Synthesis Scanner equipped in the gear slot for Helios to use scan charges from. If there are no charges, Helios will not scan anything.
The order of scanner usage is Codex Scanners then Synthesis Scanners. Once all Codex Scanners have been used, Helios will begin to consume Synthesis Scanners.
If one has the Sol-Battery Widget, scans will not drain charges from the Synthesis Scanners, allowing for free scanning. Scans will also prioritize using Synthesis Scanners over Codex Scanners.
It will not scan targets with complete codex entries.
Helios' scans will not give the player Cephalon Simaris standing, even if Synthesis Scanner charges were used. Scans only award affinity, like a normal Codex Scanner use would.

## Ion Infusion
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** madurai | **Drain:** 4 | **Compatible:** Archmelee | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod is available as a potential end of mission reward during a Fomorian Sabotage encounter.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +15% Electricity, +15% Status Chance
- **Rank 1:** +30% Electricity, +30% Status Chance
- **Rank 2:** +45% Electricity, +45% Status Chance
- **Rank 3:** +60% Electricity, +60% Status Chance

### Drop Locations
- Lektro Commander — Uncommon (3.00%)
- Fomorian Sabotage — Rare (5.00%)

### Wiki
Ion Infusion is a mod that increases Electricity damage and status chance on Archmelee weapons by 15% per rank for a maximum of 60% at rank 3.
**Notes:**
Ion Infusion is additive with similar mods. For example, an Kaszas with a max rank Ion Infusion and Sudden Impact will have a 20% (1 + 60% + 60%) status chance.
Can be stacked with Conductive Blade for a total of 180% Electricity damage.

### Wiki
Iron Phoenix is a stance mod for longswords, that provide very mobile combos that allows users to move semi-freely.
Iron Phoenix
**Notes:**
[*] The second attack of Taking Flight is considered a slam attack, inducing slam effects or guaranteed Status Effects when used with specific weapons.
Using the Heat Sword results in a fire explosion that can inflict Heat status proc.
Using the Mire results in Toxin status proc.
Using the Plasma Sword results in Electricity status proc.
Using the Dark Sword results in Toxin status proc.
Using the Ether Sword results in Radiation status proc.
Using the Pangolin Prime results in Slash status proc.
Certain attacks have Impact and Slash damage multipliers.
The first hit of Wings and Beak has a 0.1x Impact and Slash damage multiplier.
Certain attacks have a status chance multiplier.
The first attack of Wings and Beak has a 2x status chance multiplier.
**Trivia:**
The weapon pictured in the card image is the Skana, although it doesn't share a matching polarity.

## Iron Shrapnel
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Rhino | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired by attaining the rank of General under Steel Meridian, the rank of Partner under The Perrin Sequence, or the rank of Tempest in Conclave, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Iron Skin Augment: Recasting Iron Skin will cause it to detonate, dealing 40% of its remaining Health as Puncture Damage, and knocking down enemies.
- **Rank 1:** Iron Skin Augment: Recasting Iron Skin will cause it to detonate, dealing 60% of its remaining Health as Puncture Damage, and knocking down enemies.
- **Rank 2:** Iron Skin Augment: Recasting Iron Skin will cause it to detonate, dealing 80% of its remaining Health as Puncture Damage, and knocking down enemies.
- **Rank 3:** Iron Skin Augment: Recasting Iron Skin will cause it to detonate, dealing 100% of its remaining Health as Puncture Damage, and knocking down enemies.

### Drop Locations
- Conclave, Tempest — Common (100.00%)
- Steel Meridian, General — Common (100.00%)
- The Perrin Sequence, Partner — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Iron Shrapnel is a Warframe Augment Mod usable in both PvE and Conclave for Rhino's Iron Skin that when active allows it to be detonated by pressing the ability key (default 2), unleashing a radial AoE attack that deals a portion of its remainingOverguard as Puncture damage.
**Notes:**
Detonation range is affected by Ability Range.
Affects enemies behind walls and cover.
Deals a percentage of remaining Iron Skin Overguard as Puncture damage.
Damage decreases with range down to 1 damage.
Radial explosion inflicts Knockdown against enemies, flinging them away and making them vulnerable to Ground Finishers.
Roar affects Iron Shrapnel's damage.
The detonation does not occur if Iron Skin depletes naturally due to enemy fire.
Cannot be cast during Iron Skin's invulnerability period.
Detonating Iron Skin does not cost energy.
**Trivia:**
In generic usage, shrapnel is used to refer to the lethal fragments created by an explosion. It is named after the Shrapnel Shell, an anti-personnel artillery round that fired a salvo of high-speed pellets, itself named after Henry Shrapnel, inventor of said shell.
Along with Afterburn, Ice Wave Impedance, and Mesa's Waltz, this mod is one of the few Augments usable in Conclave.

## Ironclad Charge
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Rhino | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired by attaining the rank of General under Steel Meridian, or the rank of Partner under The Perrin Sequence, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.
This mod can periodically be acquired from The Circuit.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Rhino Charge Augment: Each enemy hit increases Rhino's Armor Rating by 12% for 4s.
- **Rank 1:** Rhino Charge Augment: Each enemy hit increases Rhino's Armor Rating by 25% for 6s.
- **Rank 2:** Rhino Charge Augment: Each enemy hit increases Rhino's Armor Rating by 37% for 8s.
- **Rank 3:** Rhino Charge Augment: Each enemy hit increases Rhino's Armor Rating by 50% for 10s.

### Drop Locations
- Steel Meridian, General — Common (100.00%)
- The Perrin Sequence, Partner — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Ironclad Charge is a Warframe Augment Mod that temporarily increases Rhino's armor for each enemy hit by Rhino Charge.
**Notes:**
Each enemy and Shadows of the Dead hit by Rhino Charge increases Rhino's total armor value.
Armor bonus is affected by Ability Strength.
Armor bonus duration is affected by Ability Duration.
When an armor buff is already applied, charging a second time and receiving another bonus will cancel the first bonus.
**Tips:**
The mentioned "Armor Rating" is a final multiplier to your total armor value after mods, arcanes, and abilities such as Parasitic Armor.
Example: A Steel Fiber (+100% armor) on Rhino Prime will net you 580 total armor. Hitting 2 enemies with Rhino Charge and the Ironclad Charge augment equipped will result in +100% Armor Rating for a total of 1160 armor.
Due to the fact that Iron Skin scales with armor, this can be used to great effect by charging at several enemies, then applying Iron Skin to take advantage of the armor buff. This can result in a much stronger Iron Skin.
Charging with a large bonus is not suggested, due to the likelihood that the bonus will be replaced by a smaller one.

## Ironclad Flight
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 4 | **Compatible:** Titania | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired by attaining the rank of Flawless in New Loka or the rank of Exalted under Red Veil and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Disable vacuum in Razorwing. Reduced Damage by 10% while Airborne.
- **Rank 1:** Disable vacuum in Razorwing. Reduced Damage by 20% while Airborne.
- **Rank 2:** Disable vacuum in Razorwing. Reduced Damage by 30% while Airborne.
- **Rank 3:** Disable vacuum in Razorwing. Reduced Damage by 40% while Airborne.

### Drop Locations
- New Loka, Flawless — Common (100.00%)
- Red Veil, Exalted — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Ironclad Flight is a Warframe Augment/Exilus Mod that sacrifices Titania's Razorwing's Vacuum-like passive in exchange for reducing damage taken while airborne.
**Notes:**
This mod does not disable Titania's innate passive of healing herself and allies after casting an ability.
Practically identical to Aviator, but with a different polarity and added "bonus". Cannot be equipped at the same time as that mod.
Can be used with Agility Drift to grant a 47.2% damage reduction while airborne. In addition, can be used with Agility Drift and Aerodynamic to grant 59.872% damage reduction while airborne.
Can be used in conjunction with rolling in the air for an even greater damage reduction.

*Aura mods increase the amount of mod capacity

## Irradiating Disarm
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Loki | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired by attaining the rank of Maxim under the Arbiters of Hexis, or the rank of Exalted under the Red Veil, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Radial Disarm Augment: Enemies will be affected by Radiation Status for 4s
- **Rank 1:** Radial Disarm Augment: Enemies will be affected by Radiation Status for 5s
- **Rank 2:** Radial Disarm Augment: Enemies will be affected by Radiation Status for 7s
- **Rank 3:** Radial Disarm Augment: Enemies will be affected by Radiation Status for 9s

### Drop Locations
- Arbiters of Hexis, Maxim — Common (100.00%)
- Red Veil, Exalted — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Irradiating Disarm is a Loki Warframe Augment Mod for Radial Disarm that allows it to inflict the Confusion Status Effect on enemies.
**Notes:**
Enemies hit by Radial Disarm are affected by Confusion status, affected by Ability Duration.
Although Radial Disarm can be re-cast at any time to disarm and apply Confusion to additional enemies, the duration cannot be extended or renewed on enemies who already have the effect on them. The effect must run out completely before they can be confused again.
Enemies with Overguard currently active will not be disarmed and will not be affected by the Confusion status.
Enemies that are already running to an objective (i.e. a cryopod or terminal, etc.) will sometimes continue to run to the location before turning and attacking each-other.
Bug
Using Irradiating Disarm in the proximity of Infested Hives will cause the Hives to glitch and remain invincible even after all tumors are destroyed.
Contrary to what the mod card says, affected enemies are not inflicted with Radiation status, so the Radiation symbol won't appear on enemy status bars. Nor, for example, will it count for Arcane Universal Fallout or as an additional status effect for Condition Overload.

## Jade's Judgment
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Jade | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired by attaining the rank of Maxim under Arbiters of Hexis, or the rank of Exalted under Red Veil, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Light's Judgment Augment: Enemies damaged by the well have a 2% chance per hit to spawn a Jade Light above them, damaging them for 1.5s.
- **Rank 1:** Light's Judgment Augment: Enemies damaged by the well have a 3% chance per hit to spawn a Jade Light above them, damaging them for 2s.
- **Rank 2:** Light's Judgment Augment: Enemies damaged by the well have a 4% chance per hit to spawn a Jade Light above them, damaging them for 2.5s.
- **Rank 3:** Light's Judgment Augment: Enemies damaged by the well have a 5% chance per hit to spawn a Jade Light above them, damaging them for 3s.

### Wiki
Jade's Judgment is a Warframe Augment Mod for Jade's Light's Judgment that has a chance to spawn a Jade Light above enemies damaged by its wells.
**Notes:**
Every half second tick of damage from each Light's Judgment Well has a Ability Strength5% chance to spawn a Jade Light beam, which lasts for Ability Duration3 seconds.
Overlapping wells can each damage the same target, increasing the likelihood of a beam spawning.
A maximum of 8 beams can be active at one time (not scaling with strength).
Spawn chance is affected by Ability Strength.
Beam duration is affected by Ability Duration.
The arsenal only displays the duration rounded to the nearest full second, despite the unrounded number being used for the ability.
An extra 16.67% duration is needed for each additional tick of damage from the Jade Light beam.
The first tick of damage is dealt immediately upon the beam being spawned. Additional ticks are dealt at intervals of 0.5 seconds, but delayed by one frame.
Because of this slight delay, reaching an exact multiple of 16.67% duration will not yield an additional tick of damage; a minuscule amount of extra duration is required beyond that. For example, 100% ability duration will give a beam duration of 3s, but a tick of damage will not be dealt at the 3 second mark. With 101% duration however, an additional tick will occur at the 3 second mark, for a total of 7 ticks instead of 6.
The beam deals 100 Heat damage every half second.
Damage is not affected by Ability Strength.
Base damage is effectively 150 due to Light's Judgment's inherent 50% damage vulnerability.
Total beam damage doubles after every hit, causing the damage to exponentially increase over time.
Every tick of damage has a 100% heat status chance (not scaling with strength).

### Wiki
Jagged Edge is a mod that increases the Slash damage of a melee weapon by 15% per rank for a maximum of 90% at rank 5.
**Notes:**
The increased Slash damage by this mod is based on the slash damage the weapon originally has.
In other words, the effectiveness of this mod would be very limited or even have no effect at all if modded on a weapon that does not deal mostly Slash damage, such as Fang Prime.
Slash bonus has no effect on the damage of the bleed status effect.
Mods that increase overall base damage (Pressure Point and Spoiled Strike) increases the effectiveness of this mod.
**Tips:**
Greatly increases the damage of weapons that deal mostly Slash damage, such as the Nikanas.
**Trivia:**
This mod formerly had a description that said it increases Puncture Damage when it was first released. Its Impact counterpart, Heavy Trauma, also had the same error.

## Jahu Canticle
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** madurai | **Drain:** 4 | **Compatible:** Tome | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
Available as part of the Essential Tome Mod Bundle for 140 on the Market.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Killing enemies reduces the Armor and Shields of other enemies within Affinity Range by 2%.
- **Rank 1:** Killing enemies reduces the Armor and Shields of other enemies within Affinity Range by 3%.
- **Rank 2:** Killing enemies reduces the Armor and Shields of other enemies within Affinity Range by 4%.
- **Rank 3:** Killing enemies reduces the Armor and Shields of other enemies within Affinity Range by 5%.

### Drop Locations
- Deimos/Munio (Defense), Rotation C — Rare (5.00%)

### Wiki
Jahu Canticle is a Exilus Weapon Mod for Tomes that reduces Enemy Armor and Shield on kill.
**Notes:**
Can fully armor strip an enemy.
Cannot be equipped alongside Fass Canticle, Khra Canticle, and Lohk Canticle.
Tome Mods with on-kill conditions do not require the Tome weapon to make the killing blow. Instead it has to damage an enemy at least once to mark it with the Tome effect and then be the held weapon when marked enemies are killed by an other source, such as Warframe abilities or companions.

## Jet Stream
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Zephyr | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired by attaining the rank of Exalted under the Red Veil, or the rank of Flawless under New Loka, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Turbulence Augment: Turbulence increases Movement Speed by 20% and Projectile Speed by 50% for Zephyr and her allies.
- **Rank 1:** Turbulence Augment: Turbulence increases Movement Speed by 25% and Projectile Speed by 65% for Zephyr and her allies.
- **Rank 2:** Turbulence Augment: Turbulence increases Movement Speed by 30% and Projectile Speed by 80% for Zephyr and her allies.
- **Rank 3:** Turbulence Augment: Turbulence increases Movement Speed by 40% and Projectile Speed by 100% for Zephyr and her allies.

### Drop Locations
- New Loka, Flawless — Common (100.00%)
- Red Veil, Exalted — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Jet Stream is a Warframe Augment Mod for Zephyr that grants Turbulence the ability to provide additional Movement Speed and Projectile Speed for Zephyr and nearby allies.
**Notes:**
Both movement speed and weapon Projectile Speed are affected by Ability Strength.
Buff to allies is affected by Ability Range.
Players retain the Jet Stream buff for an additional 3 seconds after leaving Turbulence's range.
The charged attacks of thrown melee weapons like the Glaive benefit from the Projectile Speed increase.
The Projectile Speed increase affects the damage falloff distance of weapons that have it, such as shotguns, similarly to Projectile Speed mods.
**Trivia:**
A jet stream is a fast-flowing atmospheric air current commonly used by aircraft to reduce the time of flights, explaining the augment's effects.

## Jolt
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** madurai | **Drain:** 4 | **Compatible:** Pistol | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
Jolt is offered as a periodic item for sale by Baro Ki'Teer, costing 300 and 150,000 to purchase.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +15% Electricity, +15% Status Chance
- **Rank 1:** +30% Electricity, +30% Status Chance
- **Rank 2:** +45% Electricity, +45% Status Chance
- **Rank 3:** +60% Electricity, +60% Status Chance

### Wiki
Jolt is a mod that increases both the Electricity damage and status chance of a pistol by 15% per rank for a maximum of 60% at rank 3.
**Notes:**
Jolt is additive with similar mods. For example, a Sicarus with a max rank Jolt and Sure Shot will have a 6% (1 + 60% + 90%) status chance.
This mod can be combined with Frostbite, Pistol Pestilence and Scorch to achieve a combined status chance bonus of 240%. With Sure Shot and Stunning Speed, this bonus can be increased to 360%. With Saxum Spittle, Jugulus Spines, and Carnis Stinger, this bonus can be increased to 540%.
**Trivia:**
Jolt was the first non-Primed mod sold by Baro Ki'Teer, and the first Event Mod that could be bought with credits.
Jolt could previously be obtained from:
Scoring over 250 points in the Tethra's Doom event.
During Operation: Mutalist Incursions as a drop in the unlockable Survival mission.

Jugulus Barbs is a set mod that increases both the Puncture Damage and Status Chance of a Melee Weapon.
**Notes:**
The puncture damage of a weapon must be at least 70% of the total for the raw damage gained from this mod to be comparable to an elemental damage counterpart like Volcanic Edge.
Melee weapons with high puncture damage and status chance include;
Destreza Prime
Endura
Ceramic Dagger
Fang Prime
Sydon (Vaykor Sydon)
Halikar
Boltace (Telos Boltace)
Cannot be equipped on Melee Robotic Weapons (Deconstructor (Deconstructor Prime), Akaten, Batoten, and Lacerten).

- Deimos/Cambion Drift (Level 25 - 30 Cambion Drift Bounty), Rotation C — Rare (2.50%)
- Deimos/Cambion Drift (Level 25 - 30 Cambion Drift Bounty), Rotation A — Rare (2.56%)

### Wiki
Jugulus Spines is a set mod that increases both the Puncture Damage and Status Chance of Secondary Weapons.
**Notes:**
The Puncture damage of a weapon must be at least 70% of the total for the raw damage gained from this mod to be comparable to an elemental damage counterpart like Scorch.
Pistols with high puncture damage and status chance include;
Cestra
Dual Cestra (Secura Dual Cestra)
Lex Prime (Aklex Prime)
Dex Furis
Telos Akbolto
Hikou Prime
Dual Toxocyst.
Onos (non incarnon mode).
Can be combined with Bore and No Return to provide a 300% increase to Puncture damage.

## Kavat's Grace
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** naramon | **Drain:** 4 | **Compatible:** WARFRAME | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Falling is 25% less likely to result in a hard landing.
- **Rank 1:** Falling is 50% less likely to result in a hard landing.
- **Rank 2:** Falling is 75% less likely to result in a hard landing.
- **Rank 3:** Falling is 100% less likely to result in a hard landing.

### Drop Locations
- Amalgam Arca Heqet — Uncommon (1.00%)
- Amalgam Arca Kucumatz — Uncommon (1.00%)
- Amalgam Cinder Machinist — Uncommon (1.00%)
- Amalgam Phase Moa — Uncommon (1.00%)
- Amalgam Swarm Satyr — Uncommon (1.00%)
- Duviri/Endless: Tier 6 (Normal) — Rare (1.03%)
- Duviri/Endless: Tier 4 (Normal) — Rare (1.49%)

### Wiki
Kavat's Grace is an Exilus mod that allows a Warframe to negate Hard Landings from higher fall velocities.
**Notes:**
By default, hard landings occur when landing with at least 20m/s velocity.
This mod also works in Player Hubs such as Relays and Clan Dojos.
Equipping this mod will also mitigate the effects of Heavy Impact and Rhino's Heavy Landing Passive.
Conversely, this mod is redundant on Valkyr as she already resists hard landings via her Nimble Passive.
**Trivia:**
This mod is based on cat righting reflexes, and is named after Kavats due to their feline appearance.

Tome Mods with on-kill conditions do not require the Grimoire to make the killing blow. The Grimoire has to damage the enemy at least once to mark it with Tome effects and be equipped as the held weapon when the marked enemy is killed by other sources, such as Warframe abilities.

## Killing Blow
**Rarity:** Uncommon | **Polarity:** madurai | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Melee | **Max Rank:** 5

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +20% Melee Damage On Heavy Attack, +10% Heavy Attack Wind Up Speed
- **Rank 1:** +40% Melee Damage On Heavy Attack, +20% Heavy Attack Wind Up Speed
- **Rank 2:** +60% Melee Damage On Heavy Attack, +30% Heavy Attack Wind Up Speed
- **Rank 3:** +80% Melee Damage On Heavy Attack, +40% Heavy Attack Wind Up Speed
- **Rank 4:** +100% Melee Damage On Heavy Attack, +50% Heavy Attack Wind Up Speed
- **Rank 5:** +120% Melee Damage On Heavy Attack, +60% Heavy Attack Wind Up Speed

### Drop Locations
- Arid Seeker — Rare (0.22%)
- Drekar Seeker — Rare (0.22%)
- Frontier Seeker — Rare (0.22%)
- Grineer Manic — Rare (0.22%)
- Kuva Seeker — Rare (0.22%)
- Narmer Seeker — Rare (0.22%)
- Seeker — Rare (0.22%)
- Tusk Seeker — Rare (0.22%)
- Duviri/Endless: Tier 1 (Normal) — Legendary (0.67%)
- Duviri/Endless: Tier 3 (Normal) — Legendary (0.67%)
- Lt Lech Kril — Uncommon (22.92%)
- Artificer — Rare (4.42%)
- Councilor Vay Hek — Rare (4.42%)
- Vay Hek Terra Frame — Rare (4.42%)
- Phorid — Rare (5.64%)

### Wiki
Killing Blow is a mod for melee weapons that increases Heavy Attack damage and wind up speed.
**Notes:**
Damage bonus is additive to mods such as Pressure Point.
This mod will greatly speed up the throwing animation of Glaives and increase the damage of their mid-air explosion, but will not affect the damage of the thrown weapon itself.
The damage of explosions caused by either Volatile Rebound or Volatile Quick Return is not affected by Killing Blow.
**Tips:**
Combines well with Corrupt Charge.
While the mod does not increase a thrown melee weapon's damage upon impact, the greatly increased speed of the throwing animation grants such weapons a far higher overall DPS. The mod is therefore largely essential in maximizing the potential of thrown melee weapons.
Alternatively, Amalgam Organ Shatter can be equipped to give an equivalent throwing animation speed increase, but boosts critical damage.
**Trivia:**
Unlike most mods, the picture shows an Excalibur wearing the Pendragon helmet, his Immortal Skin, as well as a different color scheme. This is a rare instance of a mod depicting a non-default character loadout. The other examples are Life Strike, Second Wind, True Punishment, Enduring Strike, Warrior's Grip, Quickening and Corrupt Charge, all of which were channeling mods, now converted into Heavy Attack mods.
Prior to Update 26, this mod increased the Channeling damage of a melee weapon.
Previous Behavior
Channeling increased, at most, weapon's melee damage by +50%, Killing Blow increased Channeling's damage based on this value.

## Kinetic Ricochet
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** naramon | **Drain:** 2 | **Compatible:** Tetra | **Max Rank:** 5

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Shots now bounce up to 1x and travel 5% further.
- **Rank 1:** Shots now bounce up to 2x and travel 10% further.
- **Rank 2:** Shots now bounce up to 3x and travel 15% further.
- **Rank 3:** Shots now bounce up to 4x and travel 20% further.
- **Rank 4:** Shots now bounce up to 5x and travel 25% further.
- **Rank 5:** Shots now bounce up to 6x and travel 30% further.

### Drop Locations
- 002-Er — Uncommon (1.25%)
- Armis Ulta — Uncommon (1.25%)
- Auditor — Uncommon (1.25%)
- Azoth — Uncommon (1.25%)
- Derim Zahn — Uncommon (1.25%)
- Dru Pesfor — Uncommon (1.25%)
- Jad Teran — Uncommon (1.25%)
- Jen Dro — Uncommon (1.25%)
- Lockjaw & Sol — Uncommon (1.25%)
- M-W.A.M. — Uncommon (1.25%)
- Nako Xol — Uncommon (1.25%)
- Pelna Cade — Uncommon (1.25%)
- Rana Del — Uncommon (1.25%)
- Raptor Rx — Uncommon (1.25%)
- Tia Mayn — Uncommon (1.25%)
- Ved Xol — Uncommon (1.25%)

### Wiki
Kinetic Ricochet is a mod that allows shots from the Tetra, Prisma Tetra, and Tenet Tetra to bounce and travel further. Shots will bounce off of enemies and terrain.
**Notes:**
Can be used on the Prisma Tetra and Tenet Tetra variants as well.
For the Tenet Tetra, this mod will not affect the grenade Alternate Fire.
Projectiles interact strangely with punch through effects - they will not bounce off of enemies, and will often stop after hitting terrain.
It seems that when the projectile hits a surface it decides if it can penetrate the object or not, if it can't, it bounces off the surface. This process goes on as long as the projectile has not penetrated anything yet. The projectile will only punch through what it can penetrate, and after the first penetration, it works just like normal projectile with the bounce effect.
Due to the bullet penetrating thin obstacles at first hit, it is recommended not to use punch through if you plan to shoot the floor or the walls to make full use the bounce effect as it will often penetrate the walls or floor and not stay in the space.
In general, it is recommended not to use any punch through mods.
The mod will reduce the range of the projectiles from infinite to 100 meters. Every time the projectile bounces, that range seems to be further decreased. After just two bounces, the projectile will fizzle out after traveling less than 32 meters from the initial firing position.
**Tips:**
Shots will not bounce if they are in flight for too long. It is easier to get maximum bounces using Terminal Velocity and other mods that increase flight speed.
Shots can hit the same enemy multiple times. For best results, fire into narrow hallways and crowded areas.
Works very well with Magnetize, allowing each projectile to hit the target all 6 times.

## Larva Burst
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Nidus | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired by attaining the rank of General under the Steel Meridian, or the rank of Partner under the The Perrin Sequence, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Larva Augment: Reactivate Larva to detonate and deal 300 Toxin Damage in a 2m radius. This damage stacks for every enemy grabbed by the Larva.
- **Rank 1:** Larva Augment: Reactivate Larva to detonate and deal 400 Toxin Damage in a 4m radius. This damage stacks for every enemy grabbed by the Larva.
- **Rank 2:** Larva Augment: Reactivate Larva to detonate and deal 500 Toxin Damage in a 6m radius. This damage stacks for every enemy grabbed by the Larva.
- **Rank 3:** Larva Augment: Reactivate Larva to detonate and deal 600 Toxin Damage in a 8m radius. This damage stacks for every enemy grabbed by the Larva.

### Drop Locations
- Steel Meridian, General — Common (100.00%)
- The Perrin Sequence, Partner — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Larva Burst is a Warframe Augment Mod for Nidus's Larva that allows the player to detonate Larva by recasting the ability, dealing Toxin damage proportional to the enemies trapped in Larva beforehand in a small area of effect.
**Notes:**
Explosion triggers on reactivating while Larva is active. Reactivation destroys Larva and does not cost energy.
Toxin damage is affected by Ability Strength and diminishes with distance from Larva.
Explosion has a 100% chance to proc Toxin Status Effect.
Explosion and Toxin DoT damage are not affected by Mutation stacks, instead stacking based on the number of enemies grabbed by Larva.
Damage is calculated based on the number of enemies grabbed during the initial cast, meaning killing enemies caught in Larva will not reduce explosion and Toxin DoT damage.
Explosion damage is calculated using the following formula:
Modified Damage 600 (1 + Strength Mods) Number of Caught Enemies
Explosion radius is affected by Ability Range.
Grabbed enemies are briefly launched up in the air on Larva explosion.
**Tips:**
The augment can be used as a quick way to spam Larva.
Allows a duration build Nidus to be viable as the ability to spam Larva is no longer hindered by duration.
Provides a quick way to deal with large amounts of enemies as the damage stacks with the amount of enemies grabbed by larva.
Also allows players to conserve energy as recasting doesn't cost energy.
On the other hand, players are unable to gain energy or Mutation stacks by killing enemies through recasting.
Most effective versus the Corpus as Toxin ignores shields and does bonus to humanoid enemies. Fairly effective versus the Infested, although Ancient Disruptors will reduce the damage dealt, and Toxic Ancients will eliminate toxin damage from nearby Infested.

## Laser Sight
**Rarity:** Uncommon | **Polarity:** madurai | **Drain:** 4 | **Compatible:** Shotgun | **Max Rank:** 5

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** On Headshot: +20% Critical Chance when Aiming for 2s
- **Rank 1:** On Headshot: +40% Critical Chance when Aiming for 3s
- **Rank 2:** On Headshot: +60% Critical Chance when Aiming for 5s
- **Rank 3:** On Headshot: +80% Critical Chance when Aiming for 6s
- **Rank 4:** On Headshot: +100% Critical Chance when Aiming for 8s
- **Rank 5:** On Headshot: +120% Critical Chance when Aiming for 9s

### Drop Locations
- Duviri/Endless: Tier 1 (Normal) — Legendary (0.67%)
- Duviri/Endless: Tier 3 (Normal) — Legendary (0.67%)
- Deimos/Cambion Drift (Level 30 - 40 Cambion Drift Bounty), Rotation A — Uncommon (13.21%)
- Deimos/Cambion Drift (Level 30 - 40 Cambion Drift Bounty), Rotation A — Rare (7.53%)
- Deimos/Cambion Drift (Level 30 - 40 Cambion Drift Bounty), Rotation A — Rare (9.59%)

### Wiki
Laser Sight is an uncommon Shotgun mod that increases critical chance while aiming for a brief time upon a headshot.
**Notes:**
Laser Sight is additive with similar mods. For example, a Strun with a max rank Laser Sight and Blunderbuss will have a 7.5% (1 + 120% + 90%) critical chance.
Laser Sight will trigger upon headshots, i.e. any weapon hit to an enemy's head, whether the target survives or not.
Headshots will refresh Laser Sight's timer.
If activated, Laser Sight will display an icon timer beside the player's health UI, and the Warframe will be briefly surrounded by an energy ribbon.

## Lasting Covenant
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Harrow | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired by attaining the rank of Maxim under the Arbiters of Hexis, or the rank of Exalted under the Red Veil, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.
This mod can periodically be acquired from The Circuit.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Covenant Augment: Headshot kills increase Critical Chance bonus duration by 1s.
- **Rank 1:** Covenant Augment: Headshot kills increase Critical Chance bonus duration by 1.5s.
- **Rank 2:** Covenant Augment: Headshot kills increase Critical Chance bonus duration by 2s.
- **Rank 3:** Covenant Augment: Headshot kills increase Critical Chance bonus duration by 3s.

### Drop Locations
- Arbiters of Hexis, Maxim — Common (100.00%)
- Red Veil, Exalted — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Lasting Covenant is a Warframe Augment Mod for Harrow that increases Covenant's critical chance buff duration each time an enemy is killed with a headshot.
**Notes:**
Bonus duration awarded from headshots is affected by Ability Duration.
The limit of bonus duration is three times of duration of critical chance buff.
For Ability Duration of 130%, Covenant would have 15.6s of critical chance buff initially, and bonus time would cap at 46.8s.
This can be continually replenished up to the cap with more headshot kills, allowing for unlimited duration as long as the user continues to gain headshot kills frequently enough.
**Tips:**
Synergizes well with Harrow's first ability, Condemn, as it puts enemies in a position that allows for easier headshots.

## Lasting Purity
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** naramon | **Drain:** 4 | **Compatible:** Vulkar | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod is acquired by attaining the rank of Pure with New Loka, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +15% Damage when Aiming, +0.3 'Purity'
- **Rank 1:** +30% Damage when Aiming, +0.5 'Purity'
- **Rank 2:** +45% Damage when Aiming, +0.8 'Purity'
- **Rank 3:** +60% Damage when Aiming, +1 'Purity'

### Drop Locations
- New Loka, Pure — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Lasting Purity is a Weapon Augment Mod exclusive to the Vulkar and Vulkar Wraith that adds Dead Aim (damage while zoomed in) and a Purity effect.
**Notes:**
Increases damage regardless of zoom level, so long as the player is aiming.
The damage increase stacks multiplicatively with base damage mods, elemental mods and headshot damage bonus.
The Purity effect will induce a screen shake when activated, which can hamper the weapon's crosshair whilst scoped.

## Lasting Sting
**Rarity:** Common | **Polarity:** madurai | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Melee | **Max Rank:** 10

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +10% Status Duration
- **Rank 1:** +20% Status Duration
- **Rank 2:** +30% Status Duration
- **Rank 3:** +40% Status Duration
- **Rank 4:** +50% Status Duration
- **Rank 5:** +60% Status Duration
- **Rank 6:** +70% Status Duration
- **Rank 7:** +80% Status Duration
- **Rank 8:** +90% Status Duration
- **Rank 9:** +100% Status Duration
- **Rank 10:** +110% Status Duration

### Drop Locations
- Ancient Disruptor — Uncommon (0.76%)
- Brood Mother — Uncommon (0.76%)
- Deimos Brood Mother — Uncommon (0.76%)
- Deimos Therid — Uncommon (0.76%)
- Tar Mutalist Moa — Uncommon (0.76%)
- Deimos Carnis — Uncommon (1.14%)
- Deimos Jugulus — Uncommon (1.14%)
- Deimos Leaping Thrasher — Uncommon (1.14%)
- Deimos Saxum — Uncommon (1.14%)
- Leaping Thrasher — Uncommon (1.14%)

### Wiki
Lasting Sting is a mod that increases the status duration inflicted by melee weapons.
**Notes:**
The mod's effects are only visible on Status Effects with an associated duration, either increasing the duration of the associated debuff (Corrosive, Puncture, Cold, Magnetic, Radiation, and Viral), or the number of ticks associated with DoT effects (Slash, Toxin, Gas, and Heat).
This mod will not affect the duration of crowd control effects generated by Status Effects (staggers from Impact, the panic duration of Heat, and the stun animation caused by Electricity).
However, it does affect the duration of the freeze effect applied by Cold after reaching 10 stacks.
Increased Status Duration will slow down the Armor Strip effect of Heat. For example, with +100% status duration, the armor strip will occur every second instead of every half second. As a result, the full 50% armor strip will only be achieved after 4 seconds.
Status Effects from the radial blasts of Syndicate Weapon Augments and Syndicate Weapons are not affected by this mod.
Due to status duration always rounding down, there is no difference in performance between a 9th rank and 10th rank version of this mod without other sources of status duration (such as Saryn or Lavos).
**Tips:**
Lasting Sting is most beneficial on melee weapons that deal guaranteed procs on slam attacks like the Prova and the Dark Sword, and on weapons with high status chance like the Ninkondi, Serro, Heat Sword and Nami Solo.
Stances that have guaranteed procs like Tranquil Cleave and Iron Phoenix can also benefit from this mod in extending the duration of their status effects.
Works in conjunction with Relentless Combination and high-slash damage melee or a stance that guarantees bleed procs (like Vulpine Mask), as Lasting Sting increases the number of ticks bleed procs will deal, hence increasing the combo counter even more.

### Wiki
Leaded Gas is an Weapon Augment Mod for Vesper 77 that grants Gas damage and Status Chance on weak point hits.
**Notes:**
The Gas damage bonus will increase the damage of gas status effects from the Vesper 77 while the buff is active.
This applies retroactively to gas clouds created before the buff was activated, and will no longer apply to lingering gas clouds after it expires.

## Lethal Torrent

This mod is available as a Nightmare Mode reward at the end of the mission.

- **Rank 0:** +10% Fire Rate, +10% Multishot
- **Rank 1:** +20% Fire Rate, +20% Multishot
- **Rank 2:** +30% Fire Rate, +30% Multishot

### Wiki
Lethal Torrent is a mod that increases both fire rate and multishot percentage. These additional rounds do not consume extra ammunition.
**Notes:**
Equipping this mod and Barrel Diffusion simultaneously will provide +180% multishot, giving the sidearm a 100% chance to fire 1 additional round (2 rounds/shot) and an 80% chance to fire 2 additional rounds (3 rounds/shot).
Likewise, equipping this mod, Gunslinger and Anemic Agility together will result in a 222% increase in fire rate.

### Wiki
Life Strike is a melee mod provides Life Steal on Heavy Attacks, converting weapon damage to health.
**Notes:**
This mod can be installed on Exalted Melee Weapons and works on any exalted weapons.
Any damage over time effects from Heavy Attacks will not benefit from Life Strike.
Life Strike works on Gunblade's heavy attack bullets, allowing lifesteal over a distance.
Life Strike works on Glaive weapons, but only on manually-detonated explosions and heavy attack ground slams.
Explosions from Volatile Quick Return or Volatile Rebound do not work.
While Life Strike can be installed on the Deconstructor (Deconstructor Prime), Helios (Prime) cannot manually detonate its weapon let alone perform any heavy ground slams and thus cannot gain any benefit.
Does not trigger Archon Intensify when used on exalted weapons such as Excalibur's Exalted Blade.
**Trivia:**
Unlike most mods, the picture shows an Excalibur wearing the Pendragon helmet, his Immortal Skin, as well as a different color scheme.
This is a rare instance of a mod depicting a non-default character loadout.
The other examples are Second Wind, True Punishment, Enduring Strike, Warrior's Grip, Killing Blow, Quickening, Corrupt Charge, Counter Pulse, and Flow (Primed Flow), all but two of which are channeling mods.
Coincidentally, all of said channeling mods depict Excalibur wielding a Nikana.
The only difference in each appearance (besides positioning) is the energy color used.
Prior to Update 26, this mod provided life steal on Channeling while reducing Channeling Efficiency.

## Lightning Dash
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 4 | **Max Rank:** 3

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Manifests ball lightning after a Void Sling that travels slowly and zaps enemies within 4m for 200 Damage. Costs 10 Energy.
- **Rank 1:** Manifests ball lightning after a Void Sling that travels slowly and zaps enemies within 8m for 400 Damage. Costs 10 Energy.
- **Rank 2:** Manifests ball lightning after a Void Sling that travels slowly and zaps enemies within 12m for 600 Damage. Costs 10 Energy.
- **Rank 3:** Manifests ball lightning after a Void Sling that travels slowly and zaps enemies within 16m for 800 Damage. Costs 10 Energy.

### Drop Locations
- Kuva Jester — Uncommon (0.50%)
- Duviri/Endless: Tier 6 (Normal) — Rare (1.03%)
- Duviri/Endless: Tier 4 (Normal) — Rare (1.49%)
- Uranus/Desdemona (Caches), Rotation C — Rare (4.51%)
- Uranus/Prospero (Caches), Rotation C — Rare (4.51%)
- Uranus/Setebos (Caches), Rotation C — Rare (4.51%)

### Wiki
Lightning Dash is a Exilus mod that increases the speed of Bullet Jump, lengthens the duration of Aim Glide and Wall Latch, and adds Electricity damage to Bullet Jump.
**Notes:**
Also increases the distance/speed of rolls.
At max rank, Lightning Dash adds an electrical effect that deals 375 Electricity damage with a 200% status chance within a 3-meter radius as well as a guaranteed knockdown within a 0.75-meter radius from where a player initiates a Bullet Jump.
Visual effects added during Bullet Jumps will always be the same color, regardless of Warframe Energy or Emission colors.
Only one of Firewalker, Ice Spring, Lightning Dash, Toxic Flight can be equipped at one time.
**Trivia:**
This mod was first teased on Steve Sinclair's twitter page on July 29, 2015. Interestingly, at that time the mod had a maximum drain of 16 and a Naramon polarity, as opposed to a maximum drain of 12 and a Madurai polarity.
It is the first Exilus mod revealed to the public, barring any preexisting mods that were converted to Exilus mods.

### Wiki
Lingering Torment is a mod that increases the status duration inflicted by Shotguns.
**Notes:**
The mod's effects are only visible on Status Effects with an associated duration, either increasing the duration of the associated debuff (Puncture, Cold, Corrosive, Magnetic, Radiation, and Viral), or the number of ticks associated with DoT effects (Slash, Electricity, Toxin, Gas, and Heat).
This mod will not affect the duration of crowd control effects generated by Status Effects (staggers from Impact and the panic duration of Heat).
However, it does affect the duration of the freeze effect applied by Cold after reaching 10 stacks.
Increased Status Duration will slow down the Armor Strip effect of Heat. For example, with +100% status duration, the armor strip will occur every second instead of every half second. As a result, the full 50% armor strip will only be achieved after 4 seconds.
Status Effects from the radial blasts of Syndicate Weapon Augments and Syndicate Weapons are not affected by this mod.
**Tips:**
This mod is ideal on shotguns with high status chance, such as the Boar Prime, Hek and Tigris if building for status effects.

## Lingering Transmutation
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Lavos | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
Can be acquired by attaining the rank of Exalted under Red Veil, or the rank of Flawless under New Loka, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Transmutation Probe Augment: Probe returns to cast position after reaching max range, and remains nearby for 6s. Recall Probe by recasting. Recast again to end.
- **Rank 1:** Transmutation Probe Augment: Probe returns to cast position after reaching max range, and remains nearby for 9s. Recall Probe by recasting. Recast again to end.
- **Rank 2:** Transmutation Probe Augment: Probe returns to cast position after reaching max range, and remains nearby for 12s. Recall Probe by recasting. Recast again to end.
- **Rank 3:** Transmutation Probe Augment: Probe returns to cast position after reaching max range, and remains nearby for 15s. Recall Probe by recasting. Recast again to end.

### Wiki
Lingering Transmutation is a Warframe Augment Mod for Lavos's Transmutation Probe that returns upon reaching maximum distance or by recasting the ability, then remains in-place for an extended duration. Recasting after the Probe has been recalled will prematurely end the ability.
**Notes:**
The lingering probe will reduce the cooldowns of all other Lavos' abilities every time it deals damage, as opposed to the standard probe which can only reduce cooldowns once per enemy hit.

## Link Fiber
**Rarity:** Uncommon | **Polarity:** vazarin | **Drain:** 4 | **Compatible:** COMPANION | **Max Rank:** 10

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Increase Max Armor by +11% of Warframe's Armor
- **Rank 1:** Increase Max Armor by +23% of Warframe's Armor
- **Rank 2:** Increase Max Armor by +34% of Warframe's Armor
- **Rank 3:** Increase Max Armor by +45% of Warframe's Armor
- **Rank 4:** Increase Max Armor by +57% of Warframe's Armor
- **Rank 5:** Increase Max Armor by +68% of Warframe's Armor
- **Rank 6:** Increase Max Armor by +80% of Warframe's Armor
- **Rank 7:** Increase Max Armor by +91% of Warframe's Armor
- **Rank 8:** Increase Max Armor by +102% of Warframe's Armor
- **Rank 9:** Increase Max Armor by +114% of Warframe's Armor
- **Rank 10:** Increase Max Armor by +125% of Warframe's Armor

### Drop Locations
- Corrupted Drahk — Rare (0.11%)
- Drahk — Rare (0.11%)
- Kubrow — Rare (0.11%)
- Kuva Drahk — Rare (0.11%)
- Tamm — Rare (0.11%)
- Earth/Cervantes (Caches), Rotation C — Rare (3.67%)

### Wiki
Link Fiber is a Companion mod that increases its Armor based on a percentage of the player's armor. The player's own armor is unaffected by this mod.
**Notes:**
Link Fiber can be equipped on Kubrows, Kavats, MOAs, and Hounds, but not on Sentinels.
Link Fiber cannot be equipped simultaneously with Metal Fiber.
Link Fiber uses the player's maximum Armor as the base, which means using Mods like Umbral Fiber (with full set bonus), Armored Agility, Gladiator Aegis, Saxum Carapace, Jugulus Carapace, Carnis Carapace, Stand United (with Coaction Drift) and Arcanes like Arcane Ultimatum, Arcane Guardian and Arcane Tanker can further increase a companion's maximum armor.
With Link Fiber on any Beast companion, a maximum armor value of (((1+1.1+0.45+0.45+0.9+0.9+0.9+(0.255*1.3))*700)*1.1)+50 (a 4,694.255/(4,694.255+300) damage reduction) can be achieved using the above armor mods with Valkyr Prime.
A companion can attain an even higher maximum armor value of over 5000 if the player chooses to use Chroma with a high power strength and the usage of Vex Armor. Exact numbers depend on accurate power strength, armor raising mods and Arcanes. With exactly 5000 armor, this would be a 94.34% damage reduction.
**Trivia:**
Prior to Update 34, this mod was named Link Armor.

## Link Redirection
**Rarity:** Uncommon | **Polarity:** vazarin | **Drain:** 4 | **Compatible:** COMPANION | **Max Rank:** 10

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Increase Shield by +11% of Warframe's Max Shield
- **Rank 1:** Increase Shield by +23% of Warframe's Max Shield
- **Rank 2:** Increase Shield by +34% of Warframe's Max Shield
- **Rank 3:** Increase Shield by +45% of Warframe's Max Shield
- **Rank 4:** Increase Shield by +57% of Warframe's Max Shield
- **Rank 5:** Increase Shield by +68% of Warframe's Max Shield
- **Rank 6:** Increase Shield by +80% of Warframe's Max Shield
- **Rank 7:** Increase Shield by +91% of Warframe's Max Shield
- **Rank 8:** Increase Shield by +102% of Warframe's Max Shield
- **Rank 9:** Increase Shield by +114% of Warframe's Max Shield
- **Rank 10:** Increase Shield by +125% of Warframe's Max Shield

### Drop Locations
- Corrupted Drahk — Rare (0.11%)
- Drahk — Rare (0.11%)
- Kubrow — Rare (0.11%)
- Kuva Drahk — Rare (0.11%)
- Tamm — Rare (0.11%)
- Earth/Cervantes (Caches), Rotation C — Rare (3.67%)

### Wiki
Link Redirection is a Companion mod that increases its Shields based on a percentage of the player's shields. The player's own shields are unaffected by this mod.
**Notes:**
Link Redirection can be equipped on Kubrows, Kavats, MOAs, and Hounds, but not on Sentinels.
Link Redirection cannot be equipped simultaneously with Calculated Redirection.
Redirection, Primed Vigor, and Augur Accord can help drastically improve a Beast's maximum shields, as this mod uses the player's maximum shields as the base.
With Link Redirection on a Huras Kubrow, a maximum shield value of ((1+4.4+2.2+1.8)*450+1125)*1.1+375 can be achieved using the above shield mods with a Hildryn.
Link Redirection will allow most direct forms of shield recovery to apply to the pet, including Harrow's Condemn, Hildryn's Pillage, the Rakta Dark Dagger's passive, Volt's Capacitance, and Protea's Grenade Fan.
Link Redirection does not create overshields.
Link Redirection does not share passive shield regeneration effects such as Arcane Aegis.
Link Redirection does not apply shield generation or overshields from Augur mods or Brief Respite
With a fully ranked copy of Link Redirection, the equipped Warframe must have 960 or greater modded shields in order to activate Reinforced Bond's fire rate bonus.
**Trivia:**
Prior to Update 34, this mod was named Link Shields.

Link Vitality is a Companion mod that increases its Health based on a percentage of the player's health. The player's own health is unaffected by this mod.
**Notes:**
Link Vitality can be equipped on Kubrows, Kavats, MOAs, and Hounds, but not on Sentinels.
Link Vitality cannot be equipped simultaneously with Enhanced Vitality.
Link Vitality uses the player's maximum health as the base, which means using Umbral Vitality with the full set bonus, Primed Vigor, Gladiator Resolve, Saxum Carapace, Jugulus Carapace, Carnis Carapace, and Physique (with Coaction Drift) can further increase a companion's maximum health.
Health buffs from other sources, including Chroma's Elemental Ward, Wisp's Reservoirs, or Arcane Blessing also apply.
With Link Vitality on a Sahasa Kubrow or Chesa Kubrow, a maximum health value of ((1+7.7+2.2+1.8+0.9+0.9+0.9+(0.18*1.3))*550+1725)*1.65+375*2 can be achieved using the above health mods with an Inaros Prime.
**Trivia:**
Prior to Update 34, this mod was named Link Health.

## Loot Detector
Minimap shows loot crates.
**Rarity:** Uncommon | **Polarity:** naramon | **Drain:** -2 | **Compatible:** AURA | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 5

### Acquisition
Available for 20 creds from the Nightwave Cred Offerings store on a rotational basis.
Tradeable from other players.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Squad receives +5m Loot Radar
- **Rank 1:** Squad receives +10m Loot Radar
- **Rank 2:** Squad receives +15m Loot Radar
- **Rank 3:** Squad receives +20m Loot Radar
- **Rank 4:** Squad receives +25m Loot Radar
- **Rank 5:** Squad receives +30m Loot Radar

### Drop Locations
- Duviri/Endless: Tier 1 (Normal) — Legendary (0.67%)
- Duviri/Endless: Tier 3 (Normal) — Legendary (0.67%)

### Wiki
Loot Detector is an aura mod that will increase the player's Loot Radar statistic, which will display resources, lootable canisters, chests, crates, and lootable corpses (which only appear during the self-destruction environmental hazard) on the mini-map for all players on a mission, as well as locate Medallions in Syndicate Alert Missions missions and Caches in Sabotage missions. It will not display lockers or dropped blueprints.
Loot Detector will also highlight mod objects as bright yellow/bright gold on the floor. The distance at which mods are highlighted is limited by the rank of the Loot Detector mod.
* Aura mods increase the amount of Mod Capacity
**Notes:**
When in a mission with other players, resources picked up by a player will not disappear from the mini-map until every player in that mission has picked it up.
Due to the random generation of every map, and also the Containers within them, you will be able to see Containers that you are unable to get to, especially at Rank 4 and above, due to them being in a location that may appear near but is only accessible from a different area of the map.
Will stack with a fully ranked Thief's Wit and Animal Instinct for a total detection range of 102 meters. Can further stack by applying Animal Instinct to Venari while playing Khora for a total range of 132 meters. Using a fully ranked Primed Animal Instinct on both a companion and Venari allows for a range of 182 meters.
Does not work on Index points.
**Trivia:**
Before it was officially released, for a short time after Update 10.6, this mod could have been acquired via Transmutation. This was due to a bug where all mods, including unreleased ones, became obtainable through transmutation. This has since been fixed, but players who obtained the mod this way were allowed to keep them.

## Looter
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** penjaga | **Drain:** 2 | **Compatible:** Carrier | **Max Rank:** 5

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired from Cephalon Simaris for 75,000 Standing.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Emits a pulse wave to break open Loot Crates within 12m.
- **Rank 1:** Emits a pulse wave to break open Loot Crates within 14m.
- **Rank 2:** Emits a pulse wave to break open Loot Crates within 16m.
- **Rank 3:** Emits a pulse wave to break open Loot Crates within 18m.
- **Rank 4:** Emits a pulse wave to break open Loot Crates within 20m.
- **Rank 5:** Emits a pulse wave to break open Loot Crates within 22m.

### Drop Locations
- Cephalon Simaris, Looter
 — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Looter is a precept mod for Carrier and Carrier Prime that periodically releases wave of energy that destroys all nearby breakable Storage Containers.
**Notes:**
Has a cooldown of 1 second.
Looter does not require a sentinel weapon or the Striker precept equipped.
Looter will trigger for Resource Deposits.
Use Looter alongside Vacuum to ensure the pickups be gathered once the containers are destroyed.
Looter will not attempt to trigger for Rare or Reinforced Storage Containers, possibly due to the container requiring more damage to open, or to prevent the contents from being overlooked.
Looter effects are silent and will not alert nearby enemies.
Looter will not detonate nearby Explosive Barrels or LN2 barrels, even if caught in the blast wave.
The wave of energy will break containers through walls. However, Carrier still needs a line of sight to a container to trigger Looter.
Looter will not trigger if the companion is busy doing something else such as shooting (with the Assault Mode precept) or boosting shields (with Shield Charger)

## Lorak Tauron Strike
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 4 | **Max Rank:** 3

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Summon forth Lorak from the Void and conjure its ruinous spells, calling forth a beam of energy from within. Enemies pierced by the beam have a 25% chance to drop Energy Orbs if killed within 10s.
- **Rank 1:** Summon forth Lorak from the Void and conjure its ruinous spells, calling forth a beam of energy from within. Enemies pierced by the beam have a 50% chance to drop Energy Orbs if killed within 10s.
- **Rank 2:** Summon forth Lorak from the Void and conjure its ruinous spells, calling forth a beam of energy from within. Enemies pierced by the beam have a 75% chance to drop Energy Orbs if killed within 10s.
- **Rank 3:** Summon forth Lorak from the Void and conjure its ruinous spells, calling forth a beam of energy from within. Enemies pierced by the beam have a 100% chance to drop Energy Orbs if killed within 10s.

## Loyal Merulina
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Yareli | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired by attaining the rank of Genius under Cephalon Suda, or the rank of Flawless under the New Loka, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Merulina Augment: Yareli no longer rides Merulina. Instead, Merulina follows her and casts Sea Snare on nearby enemies. 5s cooldown.
- **Rank 1:** Merulina Augment: Yareli no longer rides Merulina. Instead, Merulina follows her and casts Sea Snare on nearby enemies. 4s cooldown.
- **Rank 2:** Merulina Augment: Yareli no longer rides Merulina. Instead, Merulina follows her and casts Sea Snare on nearby enemies. 3s cooldown.
- **Rank 3:** Merulina Augment: Yareli no longer rides Merulina. Instead, Merulina follows her and casts Sea Snare on nearby enemies. 2s cooldown.

### Drop Locations
- Cephalon Suda, Genius — Common (100.00%)
- New Loka, Flawless — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Loyal Merulina is a Warframe Augment Mod for Yareli that disables riding Merulina in exchange for it shooting Sea Snares at enemies as it follows her.
**Notes:**
Cannot be equipped alongside Merulina Guardian.
Yareli still receives the Damage Redirection as if she was still riding Merulina.
Merulina's armor no longer affects the redirected damage it takes. This includes armor gained from Azure Archon Shard.
Yareli will lose her immunity to stagger status effects from enemy targets (such as Knockdown); however she will still be immune from self-inflicted stagger by weapons she uses. It's unclear if this is intentional or not, as riding Merulina normally provides complete status immunity, including knockdown immunity from enemies.
Loyal Merulina's Sea Snares inherits the duration, range, damage per second, damage increase per second, and damage vulnerability stats of Yareli's own Sea Snares. This is still the case even if Yareli's Sea Snares ability is overridden by another Helminth ability.
Sea Snare cooldown is affected by Ability Efficiency, reducing the cooldown to a minimum of 0.5 seconds at 175% efficiency.
Duration of Sea Snares cast by Merulina are shown on Merulina's ability icon.
Unlike Yareli's version of the ability, Loyal Merulina's Sea Snares are cast one at a time rather than in groups of five.
Damage vulnerability from the Sea Snares from Loyal Merulina stack with regular Sea Snares from her 1 and is multiplicative like all forms of damage vulnerability.
Loyal Merulina's Sea Snares will grant stacks of Arcane Camisado.

## Loyal Retriever
**Rarity:** Uncommon | **Polarity:** naramon | **Drain:** 2 | **Compatible:** BEAST | **Max Rank:** 5

### Acquisition
Players who owned a Smeeta Kavat prior to Update 37 will receive Loyal Retriever for free on login.
Loyal Retriever can be purchased from Master Teasonai in Cetus for:
1 Plains Kuaka Tag
1 Common Condroc Tag
1 Ostia Vasca Kavat Tag
1 Coastal Mergoo Tag

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** 3% chance to double Credit and Resource pickups
- **Rank 1:** 5% chance to double Credit and Resource pickups
- **Rank 2:** 7% chance to double Credit and Resource pickups
- **Rank 3:** 9% chance to double Credit and Resource pickups
- **Rank 4:** 11% chance to double Credit and Resource pickups
- **Rank 5:** 13% chance to double Credit and Resource pickups

### Wiki
Loyal Retriever is a Beast mod that grants a chance at doubled Credit and Resource pickups.
**Notes:**
Doubles Void Trace pick-ups.
Continues to function even while companion is downed.
Khora's Venari can not equip Loyal Retriever.
Cannot be equipped alongside Prosperous Retriever or Resourceful Retriever.
Has the potential to individually affect each ore type in a vein when hitting the special narrow brackets. This will be indicated with the "2x" icon near the name of the affected ore after mining the vein. The same applies to fishing and capturing wildlife, potentially giving you 2x fish or 2x/4x/6x tags on bad/good/perfect wildlife captures. Also may yield double the plants from harvesting.
Effect doubles Cryotic excavator earnings.
Certain resources, such as Orokin Cell, Riven Sliver, Kuva, etc., which appear on the bottom left of the screen when picked up, do not show a "2x" icon but are still doubled.

## Madurai Transmute Core
Ensures transmuted mod is of Madurai polarity and eliminates credit cost.
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** madurai | **Drain:** 0 | **Compatible:** MOD | **Max Rank:** 0

### Drop Locations
- Cephalon Simaris, Madurai Transmute Core
 — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Madurai Transmute Core is a special Core that can be used as an ingredient in Transmutation. When transmuted alongside other Mods, the core ensures that the resulting Mod will be of Madurai Madurai Polarity. Using a Transmutation Core also removes the credit cost for that transmutation, allowing for cheaper attempts at transmuting Rare-quality Mods. Transmutation Cores do not guarantee the rarity of the resulting mod, only their polarity.
Transmutation Cores can be bought from Cephalon Simaris for 5,000 Standing each.

## Magazine Extension
**Rarity:** Common | **Polarity:** naramon | **Drain:** 4 | **Compatible:** Archgun | **Max Rank:** 5

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +10% Magazine Capacity
- **Rank 1:** +20% Magazine Capacity
- **Rank 2:** +30% Magazine Capacity
- **Rank 3:** +40% Magazine Capacity
- **Rank 4:** +50% Magazine Capacity
- **Rank 5:** +60% Magazine Capacity

### Drop Locations
- Attack Drone — Uncommon (0.76%)
- Dargyn — Uncommon (0.76%)
- Tusk Dargyn — Uncommon (0.76%)
- Tusk Shield Dargyn — Uncommon (0.76%)
- Zeplen — Uncommon (0.77%)
- Gyre Eviscerator — Uncommon (1.26%)
- Gyre Supressor — Uncommon (1.26%)
- Uranus/Caelus (Interception), Rotation A — Rare (2.51%)
- Exo Butcher — Common (3.82%)
- Exo Elite Lancer — Common (3.82%)
- Exo Gokstad Officer — Common (3.82%)
- Exo Gokstad Pilot — Common (3.82%)
- Exo Roller Sentry — Common (3.82%)
- Gyre Gokstad Officer — Common (3.99%)
- Gyre Gokstad Pilot — Common (3.99%)
- Gyre Roller Sentry — Common (3.99%)

### Wiki
Magazine Extension is a mod that increases the magazine capacity of Archguns by 10% per rank for a maximum of 60% at rank 5.
**Notes:**
For any non-integer magazine values the magazine size is simply rounded to the nearest whole number. For example, a Dual Decurion modded with a max rank Magazine Extension will have a calculated magazine size of 32 (1 + 60%), which will simply round to 1.
**Notes:**
All Archguns use their entire ammunition reserve as the magazine that regenerates over time, thus acting as both an increase in magazine capacity as well as maximum ammunition capacity.
Does not increase reload rate. Due to this, unlike magazine capacity mods for non-Archwing weapons, Magazine Extension does not increase sustained DPS.

### Wiki
Magazine Warp is a mod that increases the magazine capacity of rifles.
**Notes:**
For any non-integer magazine values the magazine size is simply rounded to the nearest whole number. For example, a Braton modded with a max rank Magazine Warp will have a calculated magazine size of 45 (1 + 30%), which will simply round to 59.
Does not affect Incarnon mode.
**Trivia:**
Prior to Update 16.7, this mod was usable in PvP games until it was replaced by its own specific PvP mods along with a number of other mods: Quickdraw, Fast Hands, Slip Magazine, Tactical Pump, Steady Hands, Stabilizer, Burdened Magazine and Terminal Velocity.
Based on both Loki's leg pads and the direction the Gorgon is pointing at, the image appears to be mirrored.

## Maglev
**Rarity:** Uncommon | **Polarity:** naramon | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** WARFRAME | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 5

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +5% Slide, -5% Friction
- **Rank 1:** +10% Slide, -10% Friction
- **Rank 2:** +15% Slide, -15% Friction
- **Rank 3:** +20% Slide, -20% Friction
- **Rank 4:** +25% Slide, -25% Friction
- **Rank 5:** +30% Slide, -30% Friction

### Drop Locations
- Corrupted Ancient — Rare (0.17%)
- Duviri/Endless: Tier 1 (Normal) — Legendary (0.67%)
- Duviri/Endless: Tier 3 (Normal) — Legendary (0.67%)

### Wiki
Maglev is an Exilus mod that increases slide speed, and decreases Friction.
**Notes:**
Added in Update 10.
Slide may possibly refer to the slide's speed, while Friction may refer to its deceleration over time.
Can be combined with Cunning Drift and Streamlined Form for a total of 72% increase in slide speed and 90% decrease in friction.
**Trivia:**
Maglev is abbreviated from Magnetic Levitation, a method used to propel vehicles with magnets rather than with wheels, axles and bearings.
The mod has a hidden stat where it provides an equal but negative slide effect when the player is in mid-air, cancelling out the slide bonus when players are sliding in mid-air (jump kicking).

## Magma Chamber
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** madurai | **Drain:** 4 | **Compatible:** Archgun | **Max Rank:** 3

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +15% Heat, +15% Status Chance
- **Rank 1:** +30% Heat, +30% Status Chance
- **Rank 2:** +45% Heat, +45% Status Chance
- **Rank 3:** +60% Heat, +60% Status Chance

### Drop Locations
- Razorback — Rare (1.01%)
- Profit-Taker — Rare (2.56%)
- Pyr Captain — Uncommon (3.00%)

### Wiki
Magma Chamber is a mod that increases Heat damage and status chance of Archguns by 15% per rank for a maximum of 60% at rank 3.
It is available as a potential end of mission reward during a Razorback Armada encounter.
**Notes:**
Magma Chamber is additive with similar mods. For example, an Imperator with a max rank Magma Chamber and Modified Munitions will have a 5% (1 + 60% + 60%) status chance.
Can be stacked with Combustion Rounds for a total of 180% Heat damage.
**Trivia:**
Magma Chamber could originally be acquired by attaining 10 Battle Points (PC) or 5 Battle Points (PS4/XB1) in the Operation: Eyes of Blight event.

## Magnetic Welt
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** madurai | **Drain:** 10 | **Compatible:** Shotgun | **Max Rank:** 5

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Impact Status Effects have 10% chance to apply a Magnetic Status Effect (x2 when Fire Rate is below 2.5)
- **Rank 1:** Impact Status Effects have 15% chance to apply a Magnetic Status Effect (x2 when Fire Rate is below 2.5)
- **Rank 2:** Impact Status Effects have 20% chance to apply a Magnetic Status Effect (x2 when Fire Rate is below 2.5)
- **Rank 3:** Impact Status Effects have 25% chance to apply a Magnetic Status Effect (x2 when Fire Rate is below 2.5)
- **Rank 4:** Impact Status Effects have 30% chance to apply a Magnetic Status Effect (x2 when Fire Rate is below 2.5)
- **Rank 5:** Impact Status Effects have 35% chance to apply a Magnetic Status Effect (x2 when Fire Rate is below 2.5)

### Drop Locations
- Techrot Obsolyte — Rare (0.11%)
- Techrot Skuzzi — Rare (0.13%)

### Wiki
Magnetic Welt is a shotgun mod that gives a chance of every Impact status effect to produce an instance of a Magnetic proc. This chance is doubled when the weapon's fire rate is below 2.5.
**Notes:**
Cannot produce multiple procs in a single instance of damage alongside any other Magnetic sources such as a weapon's innate Magnetic.
Proccing Impact more than once in a single instance of damage will not allow this mod to proc more than once, nor will it increase the chance of the mod proccing.
Hard Engage works with this mod.
Fire Rate buffs such as Gauss' Redline, Wisp's Haste Reservoirs, and Arcane Tempo can negatively impact this mod's performance since it may increase the weapon's fire rate past 2.5.
If the weapon's fire rate is exactly 2.5, it will not receive the 2x bonus.
**Tips:**
Shotguns with very high Impact Damage & Status Chance include:
Boar (Boar Prime)
Sobek (Kuva Sobek)
Strun (Mk1-Strun, Strun Wraith, Strun Prime)
Shotguns with a guaranteed Impact Status Effect include:
Astilla (Astilla Prime) (on direct hit)
Arca Plasmor (Tenet Arca Plasmor) (within 14 meters of the enemy)
Catchmoon (Primary) (within 10 meters of the enemy)
Corinth (Corinth Prime) (Alt-fire direct hit)
Phantasma (Phantasma Prime) (Alt-fire direct hit, projectiles)
Critical Deceleration and Tainted Shell have potential synergy with this mod, due to their fire rate penalty reducing fire rate below 2.5 on some weapons.
**Trivia:**
A welt is a type of bruise, often as a result of impact from an elongated object without any sharp edges.
