## Magnetized Discharge
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Mag | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
Can be acquired by attaining the rank of Partner under The Perrin Sequence, or the rank of Flawless under New Loka, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Magnetize Augment: Recast on the target to detonate. Enemies hit have a 20% chance to be disarmed. Passive: +20% Magnetize Range.
- **Rank 1:** Magnetize Augment: Recast on the target to detonate. Enemies hit have a 30% chance to be disarmed. Passive: +25% Magnetize Range.
- **Rank 2:** Magnetize Augment: Recast on the target to detonate. Enemies hit have a 40% chance to be disarmed. Passive: +35% Magnetize Range.
- **Rank 3:** Magnetize Augment: Recast on the target to detonate. Enemies hit have a 50% chance to be disarmed. Passive: +45% Magnetize Range.

### Drop Locations
- New Loka, Flawless — Common (100.00%)
- The Perrin Sequence, Partner — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Magnetized Discharge is a Warframe Augment Mod for Mag's Magnetize that allows manual detonation by recasting the ability on an active Magnetize bubble. Enemies hit by the detonation have a chance to be disarmed. Additionally increases Magnetize's range.
**Notes:**
Press 2 while aiming at a Magnetize bubble to detonate it for no energy cost. Enemies caught in the explosion have a 12.5% / 25% / 37.5% / 50% chance to be disarmed by the blast.
Disarm chance is affected by Ability Strength. 100% disarm chance is achievable with at least 200% Ability Strength.
Both manual detonation and Magnetize bubble duration expiring can trigger the disarm chance.
Manual detonation will only detonate the targeted Magnetize bubble. If overlapping sections of 2 or more Magnetize bubbles are targeted, the closest bubble will be targeted and explode.
If you are standing inside a bubble it will still make it explode even if you aim on a target outside the bubble.
Passive range increase is additive with other Ability Range increases.
**Tips:**
This augment is useful whenever hostile Nullifier Crews are expected to appear, to allow Mag to detonate the bubble early instead of letting it be cancelled out by a Nullifier bubble and waste the accumulated damage.

## Magnum Force
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** madurai | **Drain:** 4 | **Compatible:** Pistol | **Max Rank:** 10

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +15% Damage, -5% Accuracy
- **Rank 1:** +30% Damage, -10% Accuracy
- **Rank 2:** +45% Damage, -15% Accuracy
- **Rank 3:** +60% Damage, -20% Accuracy
- **Rank 4:** +75% Damage, -25% Accuracy
- **Rank 5:** +90% Damage, -30% Accuracy
- **Rank 6:** +105% Damage, -35% Accuracy
- **Rank 7:** +120% Damage, -40% Accuracy
- **Rank 8:** +135% Damage, -45% Accuracy
- **Rank 9:** +150% Damage, -50% Accuracy
- **Rank 10:** +165% Damage, -55% Accuracy

### Drop Locations
- Derelict Vault — Rare (4.17%)

### Wiki
Magnum Force is a Corrupted Mod that increases damage of Secondary Weapons at the cost of increased spread (reducing accuracy) by +15% and +0.5 per rank, at a maximum of +165% and +5.5 at rank 10, respectively. Some weapons will have additional spread added, in addition to the base amount.
**Notes:**
Instead of reducing the accuracy by 5% per rank, each rank increases spread by a specific value per rank depending on the weapon.
Damage increase stacks additively with that of Hornet Strike and Augur Pact.
Added spread is not affected by bonuses that increase accuracy, such as Reflex Draw or Targeting Subsystem.
For example, a Lato with Magnum Force and Reflex Draw will have a max deviation of Lato's Max Deviation (1 - Reflex Draw) + Magnum Force degrees.
Causes beam of all continuous weapons to noticeably wobble.
**Tips:**
If a pistol already has a max rank Hornet Strike, adding a max rank Magnum Force represents only a 2.65 / (1 + 2.2 + 1.65) increase in damage per shot, compared to a 90% increase for a max rank uncommon elemental mod such as Heated Charge.
However, the Angstrum can benefit from this, as it allows its charged shots to cover a larger area at the cost of limiting long-range capabilities. Alongside it are other secondaries such as Zakti, Talons, Castanas, Staticor, Stug, Sonicor, Pox, and Kulstar when equipped with Multishot mods.
This mod also fixes the inaccuracy bug that occurs when using multishot on the Castanas and Sancti Castanas at low level, but it's still advised to max the mod anyway for extra damage and higher spread on the Castanas, leading to more crowd control.
This should only be used on weapons that already have Hornet Strike installed, as the bonuses gained from this mod are greater than Magnum Force and comes with no accuracy penalty. Also take Augur Pact into consideration as it costs less mod capacity without the accuracy penalty.

## Maim
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** naramon | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Pistol | **Max Rank:** 5

### Acquisition
This mod was once acquired by getting 10 Exposure Points in the Operation: Gate Crash Event.
Can also be acquired as an end-of-mission reward from accomplishing the Endurance challenge of the Emergency Exit Tactical Alert.
It is offered as a periodic item for sale by Baro Ki'Teer, costing 375 and 150,000 to purchase. Note that Baro Ki'Teer's stock changes with each appearance, and this mod may not be available on a succeeding appearance.
It is now available as a possible drop for capturing the Eidolon Hydrolyst.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +20% Slash
- **Rank 1:** +40% Slash
- **Rank 2:** +60% Slash
- **Rank 3:** +80% Slash
- **Rank 4:** +100% Slash
- **Rank 5:** +120% Slash

### Drop Locations
- Eidolon Hydrolyst — Rare (8.33%)

### Wiki
Not to be confused with Equinox's Day Form ability, Maim.
Maim is a mod that increases Slash damage of a pistol by 20% per rank for a maximum of 120% at rank 5.
**Notes:**
Can be combined with Carnis Stinger and Razor Shot to provide a 300% increase to Slash damage.
When calculating total raw damage, a weapon will require a Slash damage percentage equal to 75% of its total base damage to equal the raw damage increase provided by a standard elemental mod (which increases corresponding elemental damages by 90%).
The higher the Slash damage percentage over 75%, the more viable this mod will be as opposed to an elemental mod, and vice versa for lower values.
These calculations only pertain to raw damage, and do not take into consideration enemy armor/flesh/shield damage multipliers.
Due to this comparison, the only secondaries to effectively use Maim as a damage increase mod are Pyrana, Akjagara Prime and Euphona Prime's secondary shot, as all other weapons have 70% ratio or less.
Titania's Dex Pixia are also able to effectively use this mod, having a ratio of 80% slash.
That being said, this mod can still be effectively used to increase the chance of slash procs in status builds, given how the type of damage amounts affects the calculations for procs.

## Maiming Strike
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** madurai | **Drain:** 2 | **Compatible:** Melee | **Max Rank:** 5

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +25% Critical Chance for Slide Attack
- **Rank 1:** +50% Critical Chance for Slide Attack
- **Rank 2:** +75% Critical Chance for Slide Attack
- **Rank 3:** +100% Critical Chance for Slide Attack
- **Rank 4:** +125% Critical Chance for Slide Attack
- **Rank 5:** +150% Critical Chance for Slide Attack

### Drop Locations
- Duviri/Endless: Tier 6 (Normal) — Rare (1.03%)
- Duviri/Endless: Tier 4 (Normal) — Rare (1.49%)
- Deimos/Cambion Drift (Level 15 - 25 Cambion Drift Bounty), Rotation B — Uncommon (22.73%)
- Deimos/Cambion Drift (Level 15 - 25 Cambion Drift Bounty), Rotation B — Common (40.00%)

### Wiki
Maiming Strike is an Acolyte Melee mod that increases the chance of a critical hit on slide attacks.
**Notes:**
Maiming Strike stacks additively with similar mods. For example, an Atterax with a max rank True Steel, Maiming Strike, Blood Rush at a 2.0x combo multiplier, and an Arcane Avenger will have a 25% (1 + 120% + 150% + (2 - 1) 60%) + 45% critical chance on slide attacks.
**Trivia:**
Prior to Update 26, Maiming Strike provided a flat +90% critical chance slide attacks that is calculated after critical chance mods are applied.

## Malicious Code

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** 50% chance on Mercy Kill for enemies within 15m to cower in fear for 8s

### Drop Locations
- Neptune/Brom Cluster (Skirmish), Rotation A — Rare (6.67%)
- Pluto/Peregrine Axis (Skirmish), Rotation A — Rare (6.67%)
- Veil Proxima/Sabmir Cloud (Skirmish), Rotation A — Rare (6.67%)

### Wiki
Malicious Code is a Parazon mod that has a chance to make nearby enemies cower in fear after performing a Mercy kill.
**Trivia:**
Despite there being an actual cowering animation present in Kuva Lich's with quirks that make them fearful, enemies will actually roll over on the ground in a pain animation rather than cowering.

## Malicious Raptor
Puncturing strikes and quick slashes.
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** madurai | **Drain:** -2 | **Compatible:** Claws | **Max Rank:** 3

### Drop Locations
- Kuva Powerclaw — Rare (0.03%)
- Narmer Powerfist — Rare (0.03%)

### Wiki
Malicious Raptor is a stance mod for Claw-type weapons.
Can also be equipped to Garuda Talons and Garuda Prime Talons.
**Notes:**
The first attack of Jagged Gash does 100% bonus Puncture Damage.
Certain attacks have bonus status chance. Status chance bonus is multiplicative to modded status chance.
The first and second attacks of Three Lessons have 50% bonus status chance.
**Trivia:**
Update 13.9.1 temporarily changed this mod's rarity to Uncommon. This was reverted in Update 13.9.1.1.
The basic combo and three unique combos have rhyming names (Gash, Slash, Thrash, and Clash). They are all also related to verbs of violence or battle.

## Malignant Force
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** madurai | **Drain:** 4 | **Compatible:** Rifle | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
Originally acquired as a limited-edition reward from participating in The Cicero Crisis (50 points)
Can be acquired as a possible drop by killing Corrupted Vor in Orokin Void missions level >=40 such as Mithra and Mot
25% drop chance; it has 100% drop, but shares this with its counterparts - Pistol Pestilence, Virulent Scourge, Toxic Barrage
It was also acquired as a drop from Razorback Armada via killing the Razorback; however, this is no longer possible.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +15% Toxin, +15% Status Chance
- **Rank 1:** +30% Toxin, +30% Status Chance
- **Rank 2:** +45% Toxin, +45% Status Chance
- **Rank 3:** +60% Toxin, +60% Status Chance

### Drop Locations
- Corrupted Vor — Uncommon (25.00%)

### Wiki
Malignant Force is a mod that increases both the Toxin damage and status chance of a rifle by 15% per rank for a maximum of 60% at rank 3.
**Notes:**
Malignant Force is additive with similar mods. For example, a Snipetron with a max rank Malignant Force and Rifle Aptitude will have a 20% (1 + 60% + 90%) status chance.
This mod can be combined with High Voltage, Thermite Rounds and Rime Rounds to achieve a combined status chance bonus of 240%. With Rifle Aptitude and Hammer Shot, this bonus can be increased to 410%, more than quintupling the weapon's status chance.

## Manifold Bond
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** vazarin | **Drain:** 4 | **Compatible:** ROBOTIC | **Max Rank:** 5

### Acquisition
The mod can be bought from Master Teasonai for 20,000 Standing after reaching Rank 3 - Trusted with the Ostrons.
Compatible Mods

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Companion Precept Mods apply Status Effects from Companion weapons. Killing enemies with 6 or more unique Status Effects reduces Companion Ability cooldowns by 0.5s.
- **Rank 1:** Companion Precept Mods apply Status Effects from Companion weapons. Killing enemies with 5 or more unique Status Effects reduces Companion Ability cooldowns by 1s.
- **Rank 2:** Companion Precept Mods apply Status Effects from Companion weapons. Killing enemies with 5 or more unique Status Effects reduces Companion Ability cooldowns by 1.5s.
- **Rank 3:** Companion Precept Mods apply Status Effects from Companion weapons. Killing enemies with 4 or more unique Status Effects reduces Companion Ability cooldowns by 2s.
- **Rank 4:** Companion Precept Mods apply Status Effects from Companion weapons. Killing enemies with 4 or more unique Status Effects reduces Companion Ability cooldowns by 2.5s.
- **Rank 5:** Companion Precept Mods apply Status Effects from Companion weapons. Killing enemies with 3 or more unique Status Effects reduces Companion Ability cooldowns by 3s.

### Drop Locations
- Ostron (Master Teasonai), Trusted — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Manifold Bond is a Robotic Companion mod that allows the companion's abilities to inflict Status Effects from their equipped weapon. In addition, killing enemies afflicted by a certain number of status effects will reduce the cooldown of the companion's abilities.
**Notes:**
Companion Ability cooldown reduction can be triggered by the player or companion killing enemies affected by 3 or more unique Status Effects.
When a Companion uses a Precept that hits an enemy, all Damage Types, modded or innate, are inflicted as Status Effects on the enemy at one stack.
For example, a MOA is equipped with Shockwave Actuators and a Deth Machine Rifle modded for Gas and Cold. When it uses Shockwave Actuators, all enemies hit by the shockwave are inflicted with one stack of Gas, Cold, Impact, Puncture, and Slash.
Only whether an element is on the weapon is taken into account, not weighting or status duration. Mods such as Lasting Sting have no effect.
Melee attacks from Hard Engage count as using a Precept.

- **Rank 4:** On 6 Melee Kills within 6s: +100% Status and Critical Chance for Secondary Weapon for 20s
- **Rank 5:** On 6 Melee Kills within 6s: +120% Status and Critical Chance for Secondary Weapon for 24s

Marked Target is additive with similar mods. For example, an Imperator with a max rank Marked Target and Modified Munitions will have a 12% (1 + 120% + 60%) status chance.
Because you cannot Zoom while using Voidrig's Guard Mode, this mod has no effect when equipped on Arquebex.

**Trivia:**
Before Update 17.8, this mod was called Martial Magnetism, which allowed lock-on melee attacks at the cost of blocking effectiveness.
Shares the same appearance as Impenetrable Offense.
Attack speed bonus is applied on both quick melee and equipped melee.
The energy rate reduction will apply regardless of whether or not the melee weapon is equipped.

## Martyr Symbiosis
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** penjaga | **Drain:** 0 | **Compatible:** VULPAPHYLA | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
The mod can be bought from Son for 10,000 Standing after reaching Friend with the Entrati.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Drains and stores 5 health from corpses within 10m up to 10% of your health. If your health falls below 10%, Companion downs itself to heal you.
- **Rank 1:** Drains and stores 10 health from corpses within 15m up to 20% of your health. If your health falls below 10%, Companion downs itself to heal you.
- **Rank 2:** Drains and stores 15 health from corpses within 20m up to 30% of your health. If your health falls below 10%, Companion downs itself to heal you.
- **Rank 3:** Drains and stores 20 health from corpses within 25m up to 40% of your health. If your health falls below 10%, Companion downs itself to heal you.

### Drop Locations
- Entrati (Son), Friend — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Martyr Symbiosis is a mod for Vulpaphylas that allows them to store health from nearby corpses. When the owner falls below 10% of their maximum health, the Vulpaphyla will sacrifice itself to heal them.
**Notes:**
When this mod is equipped, magenta tethers will briefly link the Vulpaphyla and any nearby corpses as health is stored. When the player's health falls below the threshold, they gain 1 second of invulnerability while the stored health is transferred to them.
This invulnerability phase does not purge negative status effects on the Warframe.
In the event the player gains increased maximum health, such as a Heat-based Elemental Ward or Health Reservoirs, the Vulpaphyla will harvest more health accordingly. The extra health stored will persist even after the buffs have expired.

- Mars/Spear (Defense), Rotation C — Rare (7.69%)
- Mercury/Lares (Defense), Rotation C — Rare (7.69%)
- Phobos/Drunlo (Defense), Rotation C — Rare (7.69%)
- Phobos/Gulliver (Defense), Rotation C — Rare (7.69%)
- Venus/Tessera (Defense), Rotation C — Rare (7.69%)

### Wiki
Master Thief is a Exilus mod that grants a chance of unlocking locked lockers.
**Notes:**
Upon approaching a locked locker (with a red light) the mod automatically tries to make it unlockable. If successful, the locker will shimmer with a blue light, accompanied by a brief hissing sound, after which the locker's lights will turn to green or white (depending on the faction), signifying that it can now be opened.
If an unsuccessful attempt at unlocking a locker happens, the locker's lights will be turned off, meaning players can't unlock it anymore.
Players can also use this mod to open MOA Cabinet Spawners, but if it fails there will be no visual changes to the cabinet.
The effects of this mod do not stack when multiple players in a party have it equipped. If a locker fails to unlock when Master Thief is applied to it, it will not unlock if other players with the mod equipped also try on the same locker.
It also seems it doesn't stack with Scavenge either, as lockers that don't unlock from the mod will be mostly ignored by Kubrows and Kavats.
Since Scavenge is 90% successful, as compared to this Master Thief's 40%, it is advisable to use a pet with Scavenge to open lockers instead.
**Tips:**
This mod can be very useful when trying to farm resources or Nav Coordinates since lockers have a chance of dropping them.
Can be acquired through Transmutation.
Can be acquired on all standard low and high (but not medium) level defense missions on Rotation C.
Syndicate Death Squads are also able to drop this mod
Lockers in the Orokin Void are affected by this mod. This can be helpful in finding rare Argon Crystals.
The Corpus Gas City Tileset on Jupiter has a large number of lockers in general, perfect for this mod.

## Mecha Empowered
Increased Pistol Ammo recovery.
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** naramon | **Drain:** -2 | **Compatible:** AURA | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 5

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Squad deals +25% extra Damage against a Marked Enemy
- **Rank 1:** Squad deals +50% extra Damage against a Marked Enemy
- **Rank 2:** Squad deals +75% extra Damage against a Marked Enemy
- **Rank 3:** Squad deals +100% extra Damage against a Marked Enemy
- **Rank 4:** Squad deals +125% extra Damage against a Marked Enemy
- **Rank 5:** Squad deals +150% extra Damage against a Marked Enemy

### Drop Locations
- Venus/Orb Vallis (Level 30 - 50 Orb Vallis Bounty), Rotation B — Uncommon (16.67%)
- Venus/Orb Vallis (Level 30 - 50 Orb Vallis Bounty), Rotation B — Common (33.33%)
- Corpus Trencher Target — Uncommon (5.00%)

### Wiki
Mecha Empowered is a set mod that grants multiplicative bonus damage against marked enemies.
**Notes:**
Damage bonus only applies to Weapons and Warframe abilities. Operator and Companion damage is unaffected.
The extra damage is a multiplicative bonus applied each time damage is calculated against a marked enemy.
When applying a damaging Status Effect to the targeted enemy (Electricity, Heat, Slash, Toxin, Blast or Gas), the bonus will be applied twice, for a total of 6.25 times damage with just one of four possible equipped Mecha Empowered auras.
This stacks multiplicatively with similar sources of bonus damage, such as faction damage, which also multiply damage with each calculation.
You must have a Kubrow or Predasite companion equipped to make use of the set bonus.
The extra damage also applies to enemies marked by allies' Kubrows or Predasites.
**Trivia:**
This is the first set mod to also be an Aura.
24.1

## Mecha Pulse
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** madurai | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** WARFRAME | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Killing a Marked Enemy grants +15% Armor for 5s for each enemy within 7.5m.
- **Rank 1:** Killing a Marked Enemy grants +30% Armor for 10s for each enemy within 15m.
- **Rank 2:** Killing a Marked Enemy grants +45% Armor for 15s for each enemy within 22.5m.
- **Rank 3:** Killing a Marked Enemy grants +60% Armor for 20s for each enemy within 30m.

### Drop Locations
- Venus/Orb Vallis (Level 100 - 100 Orb Vallis Bounty), Rotation B — Uncommon (16.67%)
- Venus/Orb Vallis (Level 40 - 60 Orb Vallis Bounty), Rotation B — Uncommon (16.67%)
- Venus/Orb Vallis (Level 100 - 100 Orb Vallis Bounty), Rotation B — Common (33.33%)
- Venus/Orb Vallis (Level 40 - 60 Orb Vallis Bounty), Rotation B — Common (33.33%)
- Corpus Trencher Target — Uncommon (5.00%)

### Wiki
Mecha Pulse is a set mod that grants bonus Armor when killing a marked enemy.
**Notes:**
Mecha Pulse's effect applies based on who killed the marked enemy, with companions passing the effect onto their owner. Accordingly, if an ally or a specter kills the marked enemy, they will get the Armor bonus instead of you.
The Operator can also get and benefit from the armor bonus.
Bonus is additive to the bonus from Steel Fiber.
Multiple stacks can be active at once, with each stack having its own timer.
The range of this mod is based on the killing ally's location, and not the location of the marked enemy. Despite this, it will always grant a minimum of +60% Armor, even if there are no enemies within its range.
Due to this, despite the similar range to the set effect, it cannot be used to estimate how many enemies were affected unless you specifically were directly adjacent to the marked enemy when you killed it.
If your Kubrow is equipped with Link Fiber, the bonus armor will benefit it as well.
Must have a Kubrow companion equipped to make use of the set bonus.
The marked enemy will display a blue Mecha Set icon above its head.
Does not multiply the bonus armor granted by Azure Archon Shard.

## Medi-Ray
**Rarity:** Uncommon | **Polarity:** penjaga | **Drain:** 0 | **Compatible:** ROBOTIC | **Max Rank:** 5

### Stats by Rank

- **Rank 1:** Sentinel will occasionally heal its owner, restoring 4% Health over 4s.
- **Rank 2:** Sentinel will occasionally heal its owner, restoring 6% Health over 4s.
- **Rank 3:** Sentinel will occasionally heal its owner, restoring 8% Health over 4s.
- **Rank 4:** Sentinel will occasionally heal its owner, restoring 10% Health over 4s.
- **Rank 5:** Sentinel will occasionally heal its owner, restoring 12% Health over 4s.

### Drop Locations
- Kela De Thaym — Uncommon (11.11%)

### Wiki
Medi-Ray is a Sentinel and MOA precept mod that allows either to periodically restore a percentage of its owner's health over 4 seconds.
**Notes:**
This ability has a 60 second cooldown
The total healing is based on your Warframe's maximum health, including mods. For example, with 300 maximum health, you will regenerate 36 health. With 600 health using Vitality, you will regenerate 72 health.
Since the full heal takes four seconds to complete, this mod regenerates 1/4 of the listed percentage per second.
As the health restored is percentage-based, this mod will have a more noticeable effect on Warframes with higher health pools such as Inaros.
At least 2 copies of the mod are needed in order to trade one of them.
**Trivia:**
This mod, along with the other Executioner mods, are the second set mods to feature non-generic enemies on the mod card, the first being Vengeful Revenant.

## Melee Elementalist

### Wiki
Melee Elementalist is a Melee mod that increases Status Damage and Heavy Attack Wind Up speed.
Status Damage increase is multiplicative to other types of bonus damage, like Bane of Grineer and Roar.
This mod's Status Damage is additive with other sources of increased status damage such as the "Toxin Status Effects deal +30% (+45%) more damage" bonus from the Emerald Archon Shard and the bonus Status Damage from Boreal's Contempt.

## Melee Guidance

- **Rank 2:** You lose 3s Melee Combo Duration Squadmates gain 6s Melee Combo Duration
- **Rank 3:** You lose 4s Melee Combo Duration Squadmates gain 8s Melee Combo Duration
- **Rank 4:** You lose 5s Melee Combo Duration Squadmates gain 10s Melee Combo Duration
- **Rank 5:** You lose 6s Melee Combo Duration Squadmates gain 12s Melee Combo Duration

### Drop Locations
- Arbitrations, Rotation C — Rare (3.50%)

### Wiki
Melee Guidance is an aura mod that extends the Melee Combo Counter of allies at the cost of reducing the user's Combo Counter.
* Aura mods increase the amount of Mod Capacity
**Notes:**
If all four players in a squad equip this mod, each player will get +30 seconds.
In the case of Swift Momentum, four of these equipped will give +24 seconds combo duration while giving +120% Heavy Attack Wind Up speed.

### Wiki
Melee Prowess is a mod that increases the status chance of melee weapons.
**Notes:**
Melee Prowess is additive with similar mods. For example, a Galatine with a max rank Melee Prowess and Drifting Contact will have a 20% (1 + 90% + 40%) status chance.
Can be stacked with Volcanic Edge, Vicious Frost, Voltaic Strike, Virulent Scourge, Enduring Affliction, and Drifting Contact for a 355% increase in Status Chance while Channeling. On weapons with at least 22% base Status Chance, this allows them to achieve a total Status Chance value of 100%, allowing every strike to deal Status Effect procs.
Weeping Wounds can further increase the Status Chance depending on the Melee Combo Counter
**Tips:**
The Endura, Ninkondi, Lacera,Kronen Prime, Synoid Heliocor and Telos Boltace greatly benefit from this mod as they have a status chance of 30% or higher (in the Lacera's case 45%).
**Trivia:**
Replaced the mod Staggering Force in Update 11.

## Mending Shot
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** madurai | **Drain:** 0 | **Compatible:** Rifle (No Aoe) | **Max Rank:** 10

### Acquisition
Obtainable from the Arbitrations vendor in the Arbiters of Hexis room in Relays for 30 Vitus Essence.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Shoot Health Orbs to obtain them with +10% extra effect.
- **Rank 1:** Shoot Health Orbs to obtain them with +20% extra effect.
- **Rank 2:** Shoot Health Orbs to obtain them with +30% extra effect.
- **Rank 3:** Shoot Health Orbs to obtain them with +40% extra effect.
- **Rank 4:** Shoot Health Orbs to obtain them with +50% extra effect.
- **Rank 5:** Shoot Health Orbs to obtain them with +60% extra effect.
- **Rank 6:** Shoot Health Orbs to obtain them with +70% extra effect.
- **Rank 7:** Shoot Health Orbs to obtain them with +80% extra effect.
- **Rank 8:** Shoot Health Orbs to obtain them with +90% extra effect.
- **Rank 9:** Shoot Health Orbs to obtain them with +100% extra effect.
- **Rank 10:** Shoot Health Orbs to obtain them with +110% extra effect.

### Wiki
Mending Shot is an Exilus mod for primary weapons (excluding shotguns) that allows picking up Health Orbs when shooting at them, also increasing the amount gained by doing so.
**Notes:**
This mod cannot be equipped on shotguns, Exalted Weapons, nor the following primaries (with the hidden AOE compatibility tag):
Acceltra
Amprex
Basmu
Ignis (Ignis Wraith)
Javlok
Kuva Bramma(Needs confirmation)
Kuva Chakkhurr(Needs confirmation)
Lenz
Miter
Ogris (Kuva Ogris)
Opticor (Opticor Vandal)
Penta (Secura Penta)
Shedu
Simulor (Synoid Simulor)
Tonkor (Kuva Tonkor)
Torid
Zarr
Mutually exclusive with Thunderbolt (cannot be equipped together).
Though having AoE capabilities, the Ferrox and Scourge spearguns can equip this mod. They also have different interactions with Health orbs:
Primary fire's beam/projectiles and their AoE explosions will pick up orbs.
Secondary fire will also pick up orbs if:
The speargun hits the orb directly, which will make the weapon return to the player immediately, as if their timer had expired when deployed.
The Health orbs are within the Scourge's radial explosion, but not the Ferrox's AoE.
This mod will make projectiles stop when hitting Health orbs, potentially wasting ammo. This can vary with hitscan weapons.
Weapons with Multishot can pick up Health orbs with just a single projectile.
Health orbs picked up this way will trigger on-Health-pickup effects, like Arcane Pulse, Health Conversion and Equilibrium.
Equilibrium (including Equinox's passive) does not scale off of the increased health gained.
Bonus does affect Equilibrium's conversion on Health Orbs from dispensers in Arena gamemodes.
Increased Health Orb Effectiveness is additive with similar increases from Amber Archon Shard and Restorative Bond, but multiplicative with Dagath's Passive.
Works across the material and Rift planes.

## Mending Soul
**Rarity:** Uncommon | **Polarity:** vazarin | **Drain:** 4 | **Max Rank:** 3

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** The first 1 revives are instantaneous. Additional revives are 25% faster.
- **Rank 1:** The first 2 revives are instantaneous. Additional revives are 50% faster.
- **Rank 2:** The first 3 revives are instantaneous. Additional revives are 75% faster.
- **Rank 3:** The first 4 revives are instantaneous. Additional revives are 100% faster.

## Mending Splinters
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Gara | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired by attaining the rank of Maxim under the Arbiters of Hexis, or the rank of Flawless under the New Loka, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Splinter Storm Augment: For each target affected, Splinter Storm heals 10 Health/s.
- **Rank 1:** Splinter Storm Augment: For each target affected, Splinter Storm heals 11 Health/s.
- **Rank 2:** Splinter Storm Augment: For each target affected, Splinter Storm heals 12.5 Health/s.
- **Rank 3:** Splinter Storm Augment: For each target affected, Splinter Storm heals 15 Health/s.

### Drop Locations
- Arbiters of Hexis, Maxim — Common (100.00%)
- New Loka, Flawless — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Mending Splinters is a Warframe Augment Mod for Gara's Splinter Storm that causes it to heal friendly targets it's applied to, with the healing being increased for every target (friendly or not) affected by Splinter Storm for a short time.
**Notes:**
Only friendly targets affected by Splinter Storm are eligible for healing.
Kavats and Kubrows receive healing if their owner is being healed.
Additional healing is granted regardless of whether the target is an enemy, an ally, or the user.
No additional healing is granted for enemies being damaged by Splinter Storm's area of effect damage.
Healing amount is affected by Ability Strength.
Works on Defense Objects.
Rather than health over time, Mending Splinters actually will give a flat 15 chunk of health every second, and each target has a separate timer when it was cast. As such, actual healing rate will be slightly different while still technically giving the player 15 health per target, every second.

## Merulina Guardian
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Yareli | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired by attaining the rank of Genius under Cephalon Suda, or the rank of Flawless under New Loka, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Merulina Augment: Enemies eliminated during Sea Snares heal 5% of Merulina's health. Upon healing, gain +50% Reload Speed and Fire Rate on Secondary weapons for 8s.
- **Rank 1:** Merulina Augment: Enemies eliminated during Sea Snares heal 10% of Merulina's health. Upon healing, gain +100% Reload Speed and Fire Rate on Secondary weapons for 12s.
- **Rank 2:** Merulina Augment: Enemies eliminated during Sea Snares heal 15% of Merulina's health. Upon healing, gain +150% Reload Speed and Fire Rate on Secondary weapons for 16s.
- **Rank 3:** Merulina Augment: Enemies eliminated during Sea Snares heal 20% of Merulina's health. Upon healing, gain +200% Reload Speed and Fire Rate on Secondary weapons for 20s.

### Drop Locations
- Cephalon Suda, Genius — Common (100.00%)
- New Loka, Flawless — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Merulina Guardian is a Warframe Augment Mod for Yareli's Merulina that causes enemies killed while affected by Sea Snares to heal Merulina and grant increased Reload Speed and Fire Rate to secondaries for a duration.
**Notes:**
Heal is affected by Ability Strength.
Buff duration is affected by Ability Duration.
Reload Speed and Fire Rate bonuses are not affected by mods.
Buff only triggers if Merulina isn't at full HP.
You can run out of ammo very quickly with the high fire rate and reload speed buff gained from activating the augment, especially on weapons that consume a lot of ammo/are not so ammo efficient by default, like the Atomos or the Twin Grakatas. To counteract this, it would be best to either slot Pistol Ammo Mutation/Primed Pistol Ammo Mutation in the exilus slot of your weapon, run Pistol Scavenger as your aura mod, or use Arcane Pistoleer if your weapon of choice is compatible.
Alternatively, you may consider firing your weapon in short bursts instead, to avoid potential ammo wastage.
The healing done to Merulina can trigger Archon Intensify.
Can use the aura mod Combat Discipline to trigger the augment's bonuses consistently and without risk (in case there's no damage to receive from enemies attacking Yareli while riding Merulina), as the 90% damage reduction from Merulina minimizes the mod's health loss to only 1 point per kill.

## Mesa's Waltz
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Mesa | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired by attaining the rank of General in Steel Meridian, the rank of Exalted under Red Veil, or the rank of Tempest in Conclave and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.
This mod can periodically be acquired from The Circuit.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Peacemaker Augment: Mesa can move at 35% Speed while using Peacemaker.
- **Rank 1:** Peacemaker Augment: Mesa can move at 40% Speed while using Peacemaker.
- **Rank 2:** Peacemaker Augment: Mesa can move at 45% Speed while using Peacemaker.
- **Rank 3:** Peacemaker Augment: Mesa can move at 50% Speed while using Peacemaker.

### Drop Locations
- Conclave, Tempest — Common (100.00%)
- Red Veil, Exalted — Common (100.00%)
- Steel Meridian, General — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Mesa's Waltz is a Warframe Augment/Exilus Mod usable in both PvE and Conclave that allows Mesa to move at a reduced speed while channeling Peacemaker.
**Notes:**
The mod can be used in the Exilus mod slot.
With this mod equipped, the focus ring reticle will grow back gradually when not firing.
As the ring grows the damage bonus per burst also fades.
Casting Peacemaker in mid-air with the augment equipped may incur the hard landing animation.
This can be avoided by rolling just before landing.
Mesa can be staggered while using this augment.
**Tips:**
Mesa is still able to roll while Peacemaker is active, which can be further augmented with Amalgam Barrel Diffusion or any buffs to Parkour Velocity.
**Trivia:**
With the augment equipped, Mesa no longer rapidly points her Regulators around when firing. Instead, she will continuously aim forward. An astral form of her regular firing animation will be visible however.
The augment was originally exclusive to Conclave and only allowed Mesa to roll while Peacemaker is active.
Along with Afterburn, Ice Wave Impedance, and Iron Shrapnel, this mod is one of the few Augments usable in Conclave.
Before Update 23.8, Acolyte Mods would affect Regulators if the augment was equipped.
22.18.2

- Phobos/Gulliver (Defense), Rotation C — Rare (7.69%)

- Venus/Unda (Spy), Rotation C — Rare (8.60%)
- Venus/Vesper (Spy), Rotation C — Rare (8.60%)

### Wiki
Metal Auger is a mod that increases punch through for Rifles.
Data may differ due to width variances from dynamic posing.
**Notes:**
This mod allows weapons to punch (penetrate) through targets and solid objects to the depth listed in meters.
Actual increments are at +0.35 per level, the numbers are rounded up, similar to the Dead Eye aura mod.
Can be combined with Shred for a maximum 3.3 punch through and +30% fire rate, or Primed Shred for a maximum 4.3 punch through and +55% fire rate. 
Although Shred is more efficient due to a lower cost, Metal Auger can be used where increasing the fire rate isn't a desired effect.
**Trivia:**
Before Update 11, this mod used to provide Punch-through values of +0.1 per level.
An auger is a type of drill bit used to quickly bore through materials.

## Metamorphic Magazine
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** naramon | **Drain:** 2 | **Compatible:** Gorgon | **Max Rank:** 5

### Acquisition
Available for 20 from the Nightwave Cred Offerings store on a rotational basis.
Metamorphic Magazine was originally acquired by reaching Rank 23 in Nightwave during Nora's Mix Volume 3.
Beginning with Nightwave Nora's Mix: Vol. 8, it was added to the Nightwave Cred Offerings.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +15% Magazine Capacity, +15% Ammo Maximum, Inflicting 20 cumulative attacks on an enemy will petrify them for 5s.
- **Rank 1:** +30% Magazine Capacity, +30% Ammo Maximum, Inflicting 20 cumulative attacks on an enemy will petrify them for 5s.
- **Rank 2:** +45% Magazine Capacity, +45% Ammo Maximum, Inflicting 20 cumulative attacks on an enemy will petrify them for 5s.
- **Rank 3:** +60% Magazine Capacity, +60% Ammo Maximum, Inflicting 20 cumulative attacks on an enemy will petrify them for 5s.
- **Rank 4:** +75% Magazine Capacity, +75% Ammo Maximum, Inflicting 20 cumulative attacks on an enemy will petrify them for 5s.
- **Rank 5:** +90% Magazine Capacity, +90% Ammo Maximum, Inflicting 20 cumulative attacks on an enemy will petrify them for 5s.

### Wiki
Metamorphic Magazine is a Weapon Augment Mod for Gorgon, Prisma Gorgon, and Gorgon Wraith that increases Magazine Capacity and Ammo Maximum. In addition, dealing a certain number of hits will solidify targets in a similar manner to Atlas's Petrify for a short time.
**Notes:**
Certain enemies like Demolysts and Acolytes will resist the effect with each time it's applied to the point where the duration only lasts a fraction of a second.
If the player uses Atlas, petrified enemies will also drop Rubble.
However, Metamorphic Magazine does not activate Ore Gaze or share Petrify's effects.
**Tips:**
Increasing multishot as much as possible will allow the weapon to turn enemies to stone faster

## Meticulous Aim
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** naramon | **Drain:** 4 | **Compatible:** Vulkar | **Max Rank:** 5

### Acquisition
Meticulous Aim could be obtained by reaching certain ranks from Nightwave:
Rank 23 from Series 2: The Emissary.
Rank 14 from Intermission III.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +17.5% Headshot Damage, -7.5% Bodyshot Damage
- **Rank 1:** +35% Headshot Damage, -15% Bodyshot Damage
- **Rank 2:** +52.5% Headshot Damage, -22.5% Bodyshot Damage
- **Rank 3:** +70% Headshot Damage, -30% Bodyshot Damage
- **Rank 4:** +87.5% Headshot Damage, -37.5% Bodyshot Damage
- **Rank 5:** +105% Headshot Damage, -45% Bodyshot Damage

### Wiki
Meticulous Aim is a Weapon Augment Mod for Vulkar and Vulkar Wraith that increases headshot damage at the cost of decreased bodyshot damage.
**Notes:**
Calculated additively with Vulkar's headshot zoom bonus, which then calculated multiplicatively with weapon overall damage.
At max level of zoom with Meticulous Aim equipped, the weapon deals a total of 70 + 105 = 175% headshot damage.
Despite the differing descriptions, it stacks with Target Acquired for up to 165% headshot damage.
Bodyshot damage penalty is multiplicative to weapon overall damage.
For example, using Vulkar modded with a Serration and a maxed rank Meticulous Aim will reduce body shot damage from (225 x (1 + 1.65) = 596.25 to (225 x (1 + 1.65) x (1 - 0.45)) = 327.93.
"Bodyshot" means anything but a headshot, including limbs or parts that might have higher damage multipliers such as MOA backpacks.
The -45% bodyshot damage is always applied twice to Slash, Heat, Toxin, and Blast, but will not apply to Electricity or Gas if they are from a headshot.
Both bonus damage and damage penalty still apply whether zooming while firing or not.

## Mischief
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** penjaga | **Drain:** 0 | **Compatible:** Smeeta Kavat | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
Mischief is automatically acquired upon obtaining a Smeeta Kavat.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Smeeta Kavat becomes invisible for 6s every 10s while a decoy kavat draws fire. Decoy has a 0% chance to evade damage and blinds nearby enemies on death.
- **Rank 1:** Smeeta Kavat becomes invisible for 7s every 9s while a decoy kavat draws fire. Decoy has a 5% chance to evade damage and blinds nearby enemies on death.
- **Rank 2:** Smeeta Kavat becomes invisible for 8s every 8s while a decoy kavat draws fire. Decoy has a 10% chance to evade damage and blinds nearby enemies on death.
- **Rank 3:** Smeeta Kavat becomes invisible for 9s every 7s while a decoy kavat draws fire. Decoy has a 15% chance to evade damage and blinds nearby enemies on death.

### Drop Locations
- Kavat — Rare (0.83%)

### Wiki
Mischief is a precept mod for the Smeeta Kavat that allows it to become invisible while a decoy Kavat draws fire by running toward the enemy. The decoy has a chance to evade damage and upon death it releases a radial Blind.
**Notes:**
The Smeeta Kavat can attack while cloaked and remain cloaked, allowing them to execute several attacks while invisible.
Decoy possesses 50% of the Smeeta's Health, with 100% Critical Chance and 2.0x Critical Multiplier.
As long as the decoy is active, the Smeeta Kavat is invisible and invulnerable. If the decoy dies, both effects will end.
Blind has a radius of 8 meters.
The decoy can be targeted by a Limbo's Banish, which can allow it to draw much more fire from enemies for it's duration. Note that no matter what plane the Smeeta Kavat is in, the decoy will always spawn in the material plane.

Mobilize is an Exilus mod that increases Parkour Velocity and the duration of Aim Glide and Wall Latch.
**Notes:**
Parkour velocity affects the player's Bullet Jump speed as well as rolling, sidespring, and backspring speed.
Cannot be equipped together with other Exilus mods that affect Bullet Jump, Aim Glide and Wall Latch, such as Patagium.

## Molecular Conversion
**Rarity:** Common | **Polarity:** penjaga | **Drain:** 2 | **Compatible:** Taxon | **Max Rank:** 5

### Acquisition
Molecular Conversion is automatically acquired upon obtaining Taxon.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Blast enemies within 10m, converting 50 Damage into Shields for the Warframe.
- **Rank 1:** Blast enemies within 10m, converting 75 Damage into Shields for the Warframe.
- **Rank 2:** Blast enemies within 10m, converting 100 Damage into Shields for the Warframe.
- **Rank 3:** Blast enemies within 10m, converting 125 Damage into Shields for the Warframe.
- **Rank 4:** Blast enemies within 10m, converting 150 Damage into Shields for the Warframe.
- **Rank 5:** Blast enemies within 10m, converting 200 Damage into Shields for the Warframe.

### Wiki
Molecular Conversion is a precept mod for the Taxon Sentinel that allows it to attack enemies within 10 meters with a beam that deals Radiation damage, and converts the damage it deals into Shields for the Warframe.
**Notes:**
The amount of Shields created is calculated after taking into account damage modifiers.
Molecular Conversion will create Overshields for the Warframe if its shield restoration exceeds the amount of shields that the Warframe has lost.
The overshields stack as long as the player doesn't take damage to their overshields in excess to the shields granted.
This stacking has an upper limit — 6 times the listed capacity. This is 1,200 overshields at max rank.
Has a cooldown of 20 seconds.
The beam can inflict Radiation's Status Effect.
Activates from Stealth, but only if target is already alerted.
Does not work on Inaros, Nidus, or Kullervo, as they do not have shields.
Enemies killed by the beam are disintegrated.
**Tips:**
Consider placing this mod at the upper-left mod slot so that Taxon can prioritize its use.

## Molecular Fission
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Nova | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired by attaining the rank of General under Steel Meridian, or the rank of Genius under Cephalon Suda, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.
This mod can periodically be acquired from The Circuit.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Molecular Prime Augment: Enemies hit by Null Stars are primed. When killed, primed enemies will restore a Null Star charge and have a 25% chance of restoring two.
- **Rank 1:** Molecular Prime Augment: Enemies hit by Null Stars are primed. When killed, primed enemies will restore a Null Star charge and have a 50% chance of restoring two.
- **Rank 2:** Molecular Prime Augment: Enemies hit by Null Stars are primed. When killed, primed enemies will restore a Null Star charge and have a 75% chance of restoring two.
- **Rank 3:** Molecular Prime Augment: Enemies hit by Null Stars are primed. When killed, primed enemies will restore a Null Star charge and have a 100% chance of restoring two.

### Drop Locations
- Cephalon Suda, Genius — Common (100.00%)
- Steel Meridian, General — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Molecular Fission is a Warframe Augment Mod for Nova's Molecular Prime that makes primed enemies restore Null Star charges when killed and causes Null Star particles to inflict Molecular Prime.
**Notes:**
At max rank, two charges are restored per kill.
Chance for additionally restored charges is not affected by Ability Strength.
Kills by Allies or Companions on primed enemies will replenish the null star particle count up to the cap.
Number of charges is capped at the number you would get from using Null Star, which is affected by Ability Duration.
Molecular Fission can produce a constant supply of null star particles over the duration of combat, providing a low effort addition to outgoing ability damage for as long as the player doesn't run out of active particles.
Neutron Star may be better suited for quickly replacing large numbers of null star particles than Molecular Fission, with the added benefit of spreading large amounts of Heat and Blast.
Particles will not be spawned unless at least one particle is active, requiring a minimum of one Null Star cast per mission.
Null Star particles prime enemies with slowed or sped up version of Molecular Prime, depending on which version was used last. By default it has a slow effect.
The version will be changed right after casting Molecular Prime and doesn't require recasting.
Can't be equipped on subsumed Null Star, as other Warframes don't have Molecular Prime ability.

## Momentary Pause

- **Rank 3:** On Kill: +15 Heal Rate for 10s, -25% from Health Orbs

### Drop Locations
- Conclave, Hurricane — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Momentary Pause is a Conclave-exclusive shotgun mod that gives Health regeneration for a short period after defeating an opponent, but reduces Health gained from Health orbs.
**Notes:**
Health orb debuff stacks additively with Recuperate, Calculated Victory, Recover, Vanquished Prey, and Prize Kill
Using 4 of these mods will make you unable to pickup health orbs.

## Momentous Bond
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** madurai | **Drain:** 4 | **Compatible:** COMPANION | **Max Rank:** 5

### Acquisition
The mod can be bought from The Business for 20,000 Standing after reaching Rank 3 - Doer with Solaris United.
Elemental bonus can be triggered by the player or companion killing Eximus enemies.
Bonus damage that can be triggered includes the 4 base elementals and 6 combined elemental types.
The elements from the buff do not combine with existing elements on the companion's weapon.
The buff can be stacked multiple times, and it is possible to have all elemental buffs active at the same time. Each buff has its own timer that will be hidden by new triggers.
Bonus only applies on companion's normal attack. Does not work with special abilities like Viral Quills, nor the clones from Duplex Bond.
However, it does apply to the shadow Kavats from Territorial Aggression.
Elements gained by this mod count for Manifold Bond, since they are applied to the weapon.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Killing Eximus enemies grants 20% bonus of a random Elemental Damage to your Companion for 30s and reduces Companion Recovery Time by 3s.
- **Rank 1:** Killing Eximus enemies grants 40% bonus of a random Elemental Damage to your Companion for 30s and reduces Companion Recovery Time by 6s.
- **Rank 2:** Killing Eximus enemies grants 60% bonus of a random Elemental Damage to your Companion for 30s and reduces Companion Recovery Time by 9s.
- **Rank 3:** Killing Eximus enemies grants 80% bonus of a random Elemental Damage to your Companion for 30s and reduces Companion Recovery Time by 12s.
- **Rank 4:** Killing Eximus enemies grants 100% bonus of a random Elemental Damage to your Companion for 30s and reduces Companion Recovery Time by 15s.
- **Rank 5:** Killing Eximus enemies grants 120% bonus of a random Elemental Damage to your Companion for 30s and reduces Companion Recovery Time by 18s.

### Drop Locations
- Solaris United (The Business), Doer — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Momentous Bond is a Companion mod that causes Eximus kills to grant the companion a random Elemental Damage bonus for 30 seconds and reduce the companion recovery timer.

### Wiki
Motus Setup is a set mod that briefly increases Critical Chance and Status Chance after landing from a Double Jump or Bullet Jump.
**Notes:**
Motus Setup is additive with similar mods. For example, a Strun with a max rank Motus Setup and Shotgun Savvy and Blunderbuss will have a 5% (1 + 100% + 90%) Status Chance and 7.5% (1 + 100% + 90%) Critical Chance.
Performing a Double Jump or Bullet Jump while the buff is active will refresh its duration.
Garuda triggers this mod's buff when landing from Dread Mirror's lunge or Bloodletting's jump.
**Trivia:**
It was first seen on Devstream 124, under the name Raptor Ambush.

### Wiki
Damage type dealt? Or does it inherit damage type absorbed?
Munitions Vortex is a Plexus Battle Defensive mod. It deploys a bubble that vacuums all enemy projectiles and reflects damage to enemies that pass through it, before exploding upon expiration or manual deactivation (default 1) and dealing all accumulated damage over a radius.
**Notes:**
Energy cost is not affected by Reactors or Warframe mods.
Duration is affected by Ability Duration.
Range is affected by Ability Range.
**Tips:**
Projectile crawls forward in a straight line from the Railjack for a very short distance (~50m) before stopping; this travel time counts against its maximum duration. As such, it is recommended to close the gap as much as possible before activation.
Though powerful when charged, its effect has a small effective radius. As such, it is best used when combined with Tether or Void Hole, allowing enemies to be grouped, immobilized, and struck by any orbiting projectiles before detonation.
The Vortex will pull on the missiles launched by abilities such as Void Hole or Shatter Burst, but does not act as a surface that would cause them to immediately detonate. As such, the Vortex can be used as a grouping tool for multiple missile-based effects, stacking them in one spot.
The Vortex can pull in incoming enemy fire, allowing it to be used as a shield against enemy projectiles.
Can be an effective response to incoming Ramsleds, as the projectile can be placed as a trap, charged by fire, and detonated when pods are close to the Railjack.

## Muzzle Flash
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Mesa | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
Can be acquired by attaining the rank of General under Steel Meridian, or the rank of Exalted under Red Veil, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.

- **Rank 1:** Shooting Gallery Augment: After 6 kill assists by a player with Shooting Gallery, Mesa's next shot will blind enemies within 8m for 4s.
- **Rank 2:** Shooting Gallery Augment: After 6 kill assists by a player with Shooting Gallery, Mesa's next shot will blind enemies within 10m for 5s.
- **Rank 3:** Shooting Gallery Augment: After 6 kill assists by a player with Shooting Gallery, Mesa's next shot will blind enemies within 12m for 6s.

### Drop Locations

- Steel Meridian, General — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Muzzle Flash is a Warframe Augment Mod for Mesa, that makes Shooting Gallery emit a flash that blinds enemies around Mesa when a player buffed with Shooting Gallery assists in 6 kills.
**Notes:**
Blind radius and duration affected by Ability Range and Ability Duration, respectively.
Blinded enemies are vulnerable to melee finishers.

## Mystic Bond
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** naramon | **Drain:** 4 | **Compatible:** COMPANION | **Max Rank:** 5

### Acquisition
The mod can be bought from Master Teasonai for 20,000 Standing after reaching Rank 3 - Trusted with the Ostrons.
Companion Ability List
The number of companion abilities used is not tracked with a buff icon on the UI, however a buff icon is shown when a free ability is ready.
Only one free ability use can be stored at a time.
Companion ability counter is not reset on companion death.
Other miscellaneous mods that do not trigger the effect include:
All Companions: Bond Mods, Synth Deconstruct, Synth Fiber
Beast Companions: Hunter Command, Scavenge
Kavats: Swipe, Tek Assault
Robotic: Odomedic, Self Destruct

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** After your Companion uses Abilities with cooldowns 10 times, you may cast a Warframe Ability without expending Energy.
- **Rank 1:** After your Companion uses Abilities with cooldowns 9 times, you may cast a Warframe Ability without expending Energy.
- **Rank 2:** After your Companion uses Abilities with cooldowns 8 times, you may cast a Warframe Ability without expending Energy.
- **Rank 3:** After your Companion uses Abilities with cooldowns 7 times, you may cast a Warframe Ability without expending Energy.
- **Rank 4:** After your Companion uses Abilities with cooldowns 6 times, you may cast a Warframe Ability without expending Energy.
- **Rank 5:** After your Companion uses Abilities with cooldowns 5 times, you may cast a Warframe Ability without expending Energy.

### Drop Locations
- Ostron (Master Teasonai), Trusted — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Mystic Bond is a Companion mod that allow the owner to cast an Ability at no cost after the companion has used its abilities a certain number of times.

## Napalm Grenades
Grenades leave a burning patch on impact.
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** naramon | **Drain:** 4 | **Compatible:** Penta | **Max Rank:** 5

### Acquisition
Available for 20 from the Nightwave Cred Offerings store on a rotational basis.
Napalm Grenades was originally acquired by reaching Rank 22 in Nightwave during Series 1: The Wolf of Saturn Six or reaching Rank 13 during Intermission II.
Beginning with Nightwave Nora's Mix: Vol. 6, it was added to the Nightwave Cred Offerings.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +5 Final Status Chance
- **Rank 1:** +10 Final Status Chance
- **Rank 2:** +15 Final Status Chance
- **Rank 3:** +20 Final Status Chance
- **Rank 4:** +25 Final Status Chance
- **Rank 5:** +30 Final Status Chance

### Wiki
Napalm Grenades is a Weapon Augment Mod for Penta, Secura Penta, and Carmine Penta that causes the grenades to leave a burning patch on the ground upon impact. The weapon also gains increased status chance, and its damage type is changed to purely Heat and overall damage increased by ~73%.
**Notes:**
Overall damage will increase, though two parts of the damage still remain. As an example, an unmodified Secura Penta equipped with nothing but Napalm Grenades will deal 325 Heat area of effect damage, as well as 325 Heat direct impact damage. If either procs, the enemy will be dealt procs equal to 162 Heat damage.
In addition, direct impacts will create a separate 100 Heat which procs, likely there only to guarantee that enemies hit directly will be set on fire.
Cannot be used with Tether Grenades.
The fire patches generated by this mod last about 5 seconds.
When installed, Penta's grenades are changed to detonate on contact while still maintaining the manual detonation function with Alternate Fire allowing mid-air detonation and possible headshots on humanoid targets, also resulting in fire patches spawning mid-air.
Adhesive Blast can be installed to prevent grenades from detonating on contact by making them stick to the object that they make contact with in which they can be manually detonated or will auto-detonate about 5 seconds after attachment.
Unlike similar weapons such as Torid, Pox, Mutalist Cernos and Ogris with Nightwatch Napalm, the fire patches generated by this mod do not deal direct damage, but instead proc Heat status effect when enemies enter the effect radius.

## Naramon Transmute Core
Ensures transmuted mod is of Naramon polarity and eliminates credit cost.
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** naramon | **Drain:** 0 | **Compatible:** MOD | **Max Rank:** 0

### Drop Locations
- Cephalon Simaris, Naramon Transmute Core
 — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Naramon Transmutate Core is a special Core that can be used as an ingredient in Transmutation. When transmuted alongside other Mods, the core ensures that the resulting Mod will be of Naramon Naramon Polarity. Using a Transmutation Core also removes the credit cost for that transmutation, allowing for cheaper attempts at transmuting Rare-quality Mods. Transmutation Cores do not guarantee the rarity of the resulting mod, only their polarity.
Transmutation Cores can be bought from Cephalon Simaris for 5,000 Standing each.

## Narrow Minded
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** vazarin | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** WARFRAME | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 10

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +9% Ability Duration, -6% Ability Range
- **Rank 1:** +18% Ability Duration, -12% Ability Range
- **Rank 2:** +27% Ability Duration, -18% Ability Range
- **Rank 3:** +36% Ability Duration, -24% Ability Range
- **Rank 4:** +45% Ability Duration, -30% Ability Range
- **Rank 5:** +54% Ability Duration, -36% Ability Range
- **Rank 6:** +63% Ability Duration, -42% Ability Range
- **Rank 7:** +72% Ability Duration, -48% Ability Range
- **Rank 8:** +81% Ability Duration, -54% Ability Range
- **Rank 9:** +90% Ability Duration, -60% Ability Range
- **Rank 10:** +99% Ability Duration, -66% Ability Range

### Drop Locations
- Derelict Vault — Rare (4.17%)

### Wiki
Narrow Minded is a Corrupted Mod that increases Ability Duration while reducing Ability Range of Warframe abilities.
**Tips:**
Equip this mod on frames that do not rely on Ability Range, or use Stretch and/or Overextended to compensate for the loss in range.
Recommended to use on Mesa as most of her abilities are not affected by Ability Range, but her Shooting Gallery and Shatter Shield durations are greatly increased, at the cost of a smaller stun range for Shooting Gallery. Her Peacemaker's energy drain also gets greatly reduced, but the minimum FoV will be reduced.
Required for Loki to sustain extended duration Invisibility (still vulnerable between recasts) for extended solo stealth runs.
Useful for a stealth-centered Ivara build to greatly extend the duration of her toggled Prowl.
May be useful on defensive builds for Limbo, as it allows him to defend objectives using Cataclysm for longer without interfering with other Warframes, as well as reducing the chance that a stray Nullifier will cancel out the bubble.
Highly recommended for Chroma tank builds utilizing Cold Elemental Ward and Vex Armor, as both abilities cost a great deal of energy when used simultaneously and the user can be extremely vulnerable between casts. As an added benefit, neither ability requires range if used specifically for tanking purposes.
A must in a Titania build focusing on Razorwing, as the minimization of energy drain while not sacrificing Ability Strength is the most important part of the build. Plus, the ability isn't affected by Ability Range.
Useful for a slowing Nova build as Molecular Prime size is increased in size by Duration and the amount of slow effect on enemies is caused by greater Ability Strength.

## Natural Talent
Improves Casting Speed on Warframe abilities if applicable.
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** naramon | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** WARFRAME | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +12.5% Casting Speed
- **Rank 1:** +25% Casting Speed
- **Rank 2:** +37.5% Casting Speed
- **Rank 3:** +50% Casting Speed

### Drop Locations
- Duviri/Endless: Tier 6 (Normal) — Rare (1.03%)
- Duviri/Endless: Tier 4 (Normal) — Rare (1.49%)
- Leekter — Uncommon (10.00%)
- Shik Tal — Uncommon (10.00%)
- Ceres/Cinxia (Interception), Rotation C — Rare (2.58%)
- Earth/Gaia (Interception), Rotation C — Rare (2.58%)
- Mars/Alator (Interception), Rotation C — Rare (2.58%)
- Mercury/Odin (Interception), Rotation C — Rare (2.58%)
- Venus/Cytherean (Interception), Rotation C — Rare (2.58%)
- Vem Tabook — Uncommon (5.00%)

### Wiki
Natural Talent is a mod that improves Casting Speed of Warframe abilities.
**Notes:**
This mod affects the casting animations of most abilities, including Focus powers.
It will not, however, affect the attack speed of Blade Storm or Peacemaker.
The calculation for casting time is (Base Time) (1.00 + Speed Bonus). At maximum rank, expect a ~33% reduction in casting time.
This mod stacks with Speed Drift.
**Tips:**
It synergizes well with Fleeting Expertise, Streamline and Flow, as the energy cost reduction (and/or increased energy pool) encourages the use of easily spammable abilities.
This mod is very useful for abilities with long casting times, such as Nekros' Shadows of the Dead or Mag's Crush.
**Trivia:**
Natural Talent was first leaked on Update 10 and used to have a maximum effect of +100% casting speed. It was retrieved shortly afterward and re-released in Update 12, albeit with its effects reduced.
21.1.0.1

## Necramech Augur

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** 40% Energy spent on abilities is converted to Shields.
- **Rank 1:** 80% Energy spent on abilities is converted to Shields.
- **Rank 2:** 120% Energy spent on abilities is converted to Shields.
- **Rank 3:** 160% Energy spent on abilities is converted to Shields.
- **Rank 4:** 200% Energy spent on abilities is converted to Shields.
- **Rank 5:** 240% Energy spent on abilities is converted to Shields.

- Deimos Jugulus Rex — Rare (4.42%)
- Operation: Orphix Venom, Rotation A — Rare (4.55%)
- Operation: Orphix Venom, Rotation A — Rare (6.52%)

### Wiki
Necramech Augur is a Necramech mod that restores shields based on the amount of energy used upon ability cast.
**Notes:**
Does not provide overshields like Augur Accord.

- Deimos/Cambion Drift (Level 30 - 40 Isolation Vault), Rotation A — Uncommon (12.50%)
- Deimos/Cambion Drift (Level 30 - 40 Isolation Vault), Rotation B — Uncommon (12.50%)
- Deimos/Cambion Drift (Level 30 - 40 Isolation Vault), Rotation C — Uncommon (12.50%)
- Deimos/Cambion Drift (Level 30 - 40 Arcana Isolation Vault), Rotation A — Uncommon (15.63%)
- Deimos/Cambion Drift (Level 30 - 40 Arcana Isolation Vault), Rotation B — Uncommon (15.63%)

- Deimos/Cambion Drift (Level 30 - 40 Arcana Isolation Vault), Rotation A — Uncommon (25.00%)

- Deimos/Cambion Drift (Level 30 - 40 Arcana Isolation Vault), Rotation C — Uncommon (25.00%)

### Wiki
Necramech Redirection is a Necramech mod that increases the Maximum Shield Capacity of a Necramech.
**Notes:**
The increase to shield capacity provided by Necramech Redirection is applied after rank bonuses from levelling up the necramech.
For example, if a maxed Necramech Redirection is applied to a level 30 Voidrig, the unranked shield capacity (850) is increased by the +200 Base Shield rank bonus, giving 1050. This total is multiplied by 2 (+100% Necramech Redirection shield bonus), giving a grand total of 2100 shield capacity.

## Necramech Repair
**Rarity:** Uncommon | **Polarity:** vazarin | **Drain:** 10 | **Compatible:** Necramech | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
Has a chance to drop from Deimos Jugulus Rex that spawns upon completing the Pit Monster puzzle room in Isolation Vaults.
Can be bought from Bird 3 of Cavia for 25,000 Standing, requiring Rank 3 - Colleague.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Restore 2.5% Health/s over 3s when Health drops below 5%. 60s Cooldown.
- **Rank 1:** Restore 5% Health/s over 3s when Health drops below 10%. 45s Cooldown.
- **Rank 2:** Restore 7.5% Health/s over 3s when Health drops below 15%. 30s Cooldown.
- **Rank 3:** Restore 10% Health/s over 3s when Health drops below 20%. 15s Cooldown.

### Drop Locations
- Operation: Orphix Venom, Rotation C — Uncommon (14.29%)
- Deimos Jugulus Rex — Rare (4.42%)
- Operation: Orphix Venom, Rotation A — Rare (4.55%)
- Operation: Orphix Venom, Rotation A — Rare (6.52%)

### Wiki
Necramech Repair is a Necramech mod that restores health over time once it goes below a certain threshold.
**Notes:**
If the Necramech's health is below the threshold when the cooldown finishes, Repair will activate on the next instance of damage to health.
Does not provide any invulnerability mechanic while restoring health which means you can still be killed by damage sources without the use of defensive cooldowns. Especially noticeable against high level enemies where the damage can exceed the 20% margin or be high enough to negate the healing altogether.

## Necrophagic Vigor
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** madurai | **Drain:** 2 | **Compatible:** Hema | **Max Rank:** 5

### Acquisition
Necrophagic Vigor can be obtained by reaching Rank 25 with Nightwave during Nora's Mix: Dreams of the Dead.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Reloading drains up to half your Health based on ammo created, increasing Critical Chance and Critical Damage by +10% for every 20 Health drained. Capped at 360%.
- **Rank 1:** Reloading drains up to half your Health based on ammo created, increasing Critical Chance and Critical Damage by +12% for every 20 Health drained. Capped at 360%.
- **Rank 2:** Reloading drains up to half your Health based on ammo created, increasing Critical Chance and Critical Damage by +14% for every 20 Health drained. Capped at 360%.
- **Rank 3:** Reloading drains up to half your Health based on ammo created, increasing Critical Chance and Critical Damage by +16% for every 20 Health drained. Capped at 360%.
- **Rank 4:** Reloading drains up to half your Health based on ammo created, increasing Critical Chance and Critical Damage by +18% for every 20 Health drained. Capped at 360%.
- **Rank 5:** Reloading drains up to half your Health based on ammo created, increasing Critical Chance and Critical Damage by +20% for every 20 Health drained. Capped at 360%.

### Wiki
Necrophagic Vigor is an Weapon Augment Mod for the Hema and Coda Hema that drains up to half of the user's Health upon reloading, based on the amount of ammo created, to increase Critical Chance and Critical Damage for every 20 Health drained, up to a cap.
**Notes:**
Requires a max health of 720 for the equipped Warframe, and a fully depleted magazine on reload to gain the maximum benefit.
Health consumed is proportional to the percentage of the magazine being replenished.
Having more than 720 max health will result in getting a larger benefit per amount reloaded, while having a larger magazine will result in a smaller benefit for the same flat amount of ammo reloaded.
Health consumed upon reload is based off of maximum health, NOT current health.
Having insufficient health to reload to maximum will result in a partial reload, or even the inability to reload at all if you don't have at least 20 health to lose.
Reloading will reduce you to a minimum of 2 health.
Bonus to Critical Chance and Critical Damage is additive with mods such as Point Strike and Vital Sense.

## Negate

- **Rank 2:** Sentinel prevents Status Effects from applying to its owner once every 11s.
- **Rank 3:** Sentinel prevents Status Effects from applying to its owner once every 9s.
- **Rank 4:** Sentinel prevents Status Effects from applying to its owner once every 7s.
- **Rank 5:** Sentinel prevents Status Effects from applying to its owner once every 5s.

### Drop Locations
- Cephalon Simaris, Negate
 — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Negate is a precept mod for Wyrm and Wyrm Prime that nullifies Status Effects ailing its owner's Warframe.
**Notes:**
Negate also protects from knockdowns from being grappled or Seismic Shockwave, as well as the stun after Commanders' Switch Teleport.
Negate does not work while the Wyrm/Prime is inactive, for example on Titania's Razorwing ability when active.

## Negation Armor
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Inaros | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired by attaining the rank of Maxim under the Arbiters of Hexis, or the rank of Partner under the The Perrin Sequence, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.
This mod can periodically be acquired from The Circuit.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Scarab Shell Augment: When Inaros takes fatal damage, he consumes Scarab Shell to heal with a brief invulnerability that ends with a deadly Slash Status Effect. Cooldown 60s.
- **Rank 1:** Scarab Shell Augment: When Inaros takes fatal damage, he consumes Scarab Shell to heal with a brief invulnerability that ends with a deadly Slash Status Effect. Cooldown 48s.
- **Rank 2:** Scarab Shell Augment: When Inaros takes fatal damage, he consumes Scarab Shell to heal with a brief invulnerability that ends with a deadly Slash Status Effect. Cooldown 38s.
- **Rank 3:** Scarab Shell Augment: When Inaros takes fatal damage, he consumes Scarab Shell to heal with a brief invulnerability that ends with a deadly Slash Status Effect. Cooldown 30s.

### Drop Locations
- Arbiters of Hexis, Maxim — Common (100.00%)
- The Perrin Sequence, Partner — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Negation Armor is a Warframe Augment Mod for Inaros that sacrifices an active Scarab Shell to prevent Inaros from entering Bleedout, but receives a Slash status effect once its invulnerability period expires.
**Notes:**
Cooldown begins when the effect triggers.
Upon activating, Inaros will heal 90% of his Health and become invulnerable for 3 seconds. Once the invulnerability ends, he receives a Slash proc that deals ~20% of his max health per tick, necessitating the need of healing and other invulnerability sources to survive.
The Slash proc can not be prevented, even by recasting Scarab Shell during the invulnerability period or using Arcane Deflection.
Rapid Resilience affects Negation Armor's Slash proc, making it non-lethal if Inaros's health is at least at ~20% of his max.
**Tips:**
Sandstorm can be used to make Inaros invulnerable during the slash proc period, while also allowing him to heal any health lost.

## Neutralizing Justice
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** vazarin | **Drain:** 4 | **Compatible:** Miter | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod is acquired by attaining the rank of Protector with Steel Meridian, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.
Purchased from Ergo Glast during Operation: Ambulas Reborn for 75 Animo Beacons.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Each Miter blade has a +22.5% chance to immediately destroy a Nullifier field., +0.3 'Justice'
- **Rank 1:** Each Miter blade has a +45% chance to immediately destroy a Nullifier field., +0.5 'Justice'
- **Rank 2:** Each Miter blade has a +67.5% chance to immediately destroy a Nullifier field., +0.8 'Justice'
- **Rank 3:** Each Miter blade has a +90% chance to immediately destroy a Nullifier field., +1 'Justice'

### Drop Locations
- Steel Meridian, Protector — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Neutralizing Justice is a Weapon Augment Mod exclusive to the Miter that grants its blades to have a chance to instantly destroy a Nullifier's bubble and adds a Justice effect.
**Notes:**
Miter blades from both normal and charged attacks have a chance to instantly pop a Nullifier bubble.
The augment actually destroys the floating shield generator that powers the Nullifier bubble, meaning the bubble is permanently disabled on the target enemy.
Multiple blades resulting from Multishot will continue their flight paths as they collide with a Nullifier bubble, provided that one blade procs its chance to destroy the bubble, allowing the other blade to fly through. Otherwise, both blades will bounce off the bubble.
Only affects the Nullifier shield bubbles of the Nullifier Crewman, Corrupted Nullifier, and Corpus Target when they spawn with a Nullifier generator, and has no effect on any other enemy in Warframe.
Does not affect Nullifier bubbles created by Terra Shield Osprey and Isolator Bursa.

## Neutron Star
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Nova | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired by attaining the rank of General under Steel Meridian, or the rank of Genius under Cephalon Suda, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Null Star Augment: Particles deal 120 Heat Damage with guaranteed Status effect in 4m. On recast, remaining particles seek out enemies with doubled search radius.
- **Rank 1:** Null Star Augment: Particles deal 160 Heat Damage with guaranteed Status effect in 5m. On recast, remaining particles seek out enemies with doubled search radius.
- **Rank 2:** Null Star Augment: Particles deal 200 Heat Damage with guaranteed Status effect in 6m. On recast, remaining particles seek out enemies with doubled search radius.
- **Rank 3:** Null Star Augment: Particles deal 240 Heat Damage with guaranteed Status effect in 8m. On recast, remaining particles seek out enemies with doubled search radius.

### Drop Locations
- Cephalon Suda, Genius — Common (100.00%)
- Steel Meridian, General — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Neutron Star is a Warframe Augment Mod for Nova's Null Star that allows it to inflict Heat damage with guaranteed status effect in a radius and recasting the ability will disperse currently active particles to seek out enemies before being replenished. Neutron Star's seek radius is doubled than that of Null Star's.
**Notes:**
Explosion damage is affected by Ability Strength.
Explosion radius is affected by Ability Range.
Seek radius after 5 meters and explosion require line of sight.
**Tips:**
The augment deals a separated instance of Heat damage and status from Null Star, highly benefiting from the same modifiers that can affect Null Star, such as Arcane Arachne, Holster Amp, Vigorous Swap, among others.
The guaranteed proc chance makes the augment useful for short-range crowd control.
The augment allows the user to refresh the innate damage reduction that each Null Star innately possess.

## Nidri Tauron Strike
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** vazarin | **Drain:** 4 | **Max Rank:** 3

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Summon forth Nidri from the Void and plant the noble staff into the ground, creating a titanic splash of water that becomes a lingering vortex for 20s. 20% of damage dealt to enemies caught in the vortex is also inflicted upon all other enemies within it.
- **Rank 1:** Summon forth Nidri from the Void and plant the noble staff into the ground, creating a titanic splash of water that becomes a lingering vortex for 20s. 30% of damage dealt to enemies caught in the vortex is also inflicted upon all other enemies within it.
- **Rank 2:** Summon forth Nidri from the Void and plant the noble staff into the ground, creating a titanic splash of water that becomes a lingering vortex for 20s. 40% of damage dealt to enemies caught in the vortex is also inflicted upon all other enemies within it.
- **Rank 3:** Summon forth Nidri from the Void and plant the noble staff into the ground, creating a titanic splash of water that becomes a lingering vortex for 20s. 50% of damage dealt to enemies caught in the vortex is also inflicted upon all other enemies within it.

## Night Stalker
On Hit:
**Rarity:** Uncommon | **Polarity:** naramon | **Drain:** 2 | **Compatible:** Pistol | **Max Rank:** 5

### Acquisition

### Stats by Rank

- **Rank 1:** Reveals target on Minimap for +2s.
- **Rank 2:** Reveals target on Minimap for +3s.
- **Rank 3:** Reveals target on Minimap for +4s.
- **Rank 4:** Reveals target on Minimap for +5s.
- **Rank 5:** Reveals target on Minimap for +6s.

### Drop Locations
- Conclave, Mistral — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Night Stalker is a Conclave-exclusive secondary mod which causes targets hit to be temporarily visible on the minimap.
**Notes:**
Any enemy hit will also create a thin, red line that will trail behind the moving opponent.

## Nightwatch Napalm
**Rarity:** Uncommon | **Polarity:** madurai | **Drain:** 4 | **Compatible:** Ogris | **Max Rank:** 5

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Rockets disperse napalm, inflicting 5% damage over 1s across 15% of the explosion area.
- **Rank 1:** Rockets disperse napalm, inflicting 10% damage over 2s across 30% of the explosion area.
- **Rank 2:** Rockets disperse napalm, inflicting 15% damage over 3s across 45% of the explosion area.
- **Rank 3:** Rockets disperse napalm, inflicting 20% damage over 4s across 60% of the explosion area.
- **Rank 4:** Rockets disperse napalm, inflicting 25% damage over 5s across 75% of the explosion area.
- **Rank 5:** Rockets disperse napalm, inflicting 30% damage over 6s across 90% of the explosion area.

### Drop Locations
- Kela De Thaym — Uncommon (11.11%)

### Wiki
Nightwatch Napalm is a mod for the Ogris and Kuva Ogris that, upon striking an enemy or object, will form small pockets of fire within a sphere around the impact area. The fire will deal 30% of the weapon's modified base damage as Heat damage with 68% chance to proc over 6 seconds.
**Notes:**
Rather than doing 30% of damage, it creates an AoE dealing 150 Heat damage per tick.
Damage output can only be increased by base damage mods (e.g. Serration, Heavy Caliber, Semi-Rifle Cannonade), and faction mods (e.g. Bane of Grineer, Bane of Corpus).
Damage is increased by Kuva weapon's bonus damage stat.
Napalm cannot deal critical damage via any means.
Napalm has 68% chance to proc Heat, ticking for 50% of napalm's damage per second for 3 seconds.
Heat proc damage is affected by Heat mods.
Status chance is not affected by mods.
Continuous Misery and Lavos's duration passive increase the duration of the Heat proc.
Firestorm (Primed Firestorm) increases the effective area.
Given that Nullifier Crewman bubbles will deflect the trajectory of Ogris rockets, it is advisable to aim at the nearest surface of an approaching Nullifier to allow the Napalm to spawn.
Unlike Zenistar, but similar to Torid, Napalm will not damage Nullifier bubbles themselves, but Nullifiers who walk over the flames will become damaged, even inside their bubble.
**Tips:**
Will continue to proc heat over the duration to enemies within the napalm, allowing it and further rockets to deal more and more damage to armored enemies due to Heat proc status effect of temporarily reducing current armor. This synergizes well with a Viral built Ogris, as viral procs will increase heat proc damage and further attacks will deal increased damage due to armor reductions.
Setting the Energy color to black will greatly reduce the visual effects of the Heat proc. This results in much clearer vision.
