## Null Audit
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** penjaga | **Drain:** 0 | **Compatible:** Hound | **Max Rank:** 5

### Acquisition
Null Audit is automatically acquired upon obtaining a Hound built with the Bhaira Model.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** The Hound utilizes the same Aura and Abilities as an Eximus enemy for 10s, while stripping its Overguard by 50%.
- **Rank 1:** The Hound utilizes the same Aura and Abilities as an Eximus enemy for 20s, while stripping its Overguard by 50%.
- **Rank 2:** The Hound utilizes the same Aura and Abilities as an Eximus enemy for 30s, while stripping its Overguard by 50%.
- **Rank 3:** The Hound utilizes the same Aura and Abilities as an Eximus enemy for 40s, while stripping its Overguard by 50%.
- **Rank 4:** The Hound utilizes the same Aura and Abilities as an Eximus enemy for 50s, while stripping its Overguard by 50%.
- **Rank 5:** The Hound utilizes the same Aura and Abilities as an Eximus enemy for 60s, while stripping its Overguard by 50%.

### Wiki
Null Audit is a precept mod for Hounds that copies Eximus auras for its own use, while stripping half of targeted Eximus's current Overguard.
**Notes:**
Cannot be equipped with Repo Audit and Equilibrium Audit.
Cooldown is 60 seconds from the start of the previous use at all ranks.
The Hound will locate the nearest Eximus enemy and attack it, generating a transparent bubble effect around the attacked unit, copying their unique abilities to the Hound and stripping Overguard. The Hound can only have one aura at a time. Eximus Behaviors are as follows:
Arson/Caustic Eximus gives the Hound the ability to use a fire blast to knock back enemies.
Arctic/Frozen Eximus gives the Hound a radius that slightly slows down enemies while periodically generating a Snow Globe for its own protection. The globe blocks enemy fire, while Tenno gunfire can pass through unhindered.
Shock/Bio-Electric Eximus will allow the Hound to fire off three shock orbs every 10 seconds that seek and rotate around enemies. The damage is non-existent on the Hound, compared to an enemy using this ability.
Venomous Eximus gives the Hound Toxin procs on attacks and a 3m toxin cloud surrounding it. The damage is negligible.
Guardian Eximus gives the Hound and Tenno a Damage Reduction buff, but will not inherit the rotating shields.
Blitz Eximus gives the Hound the ability to use the shockwave every 10 seconds.
Sanguine/Leech Eximus gives the Hound life steal on all attacks. Additionally, the Hound will generate the same sapping zones that the Eximus units can, which appear to do scaling damage.
Energy Leech/Parasitic Eximus gives the Hound Magnetic procs on attacks, providing 3 energy per second over 3 seconds to Tenno within 25 meters.

## Odomedic
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** penjaga | **Drain:** 2 | **Compatible:** ROBOTIC | **Max Rank:** 3

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Every 12m traversed by Wall Running regenerates +10 Health over 1s. This effect can stack up to 3x.
- **Rank 1:** Every 9m traversed by Wall Running regenerates +20 Health over 2s. This effect can stack up to 3x.
- **Rank 2:** Every 6m traversed by Wall Running regenerates +30 Health over 3s. This effect can stack up to 3x.
- **Rank 3:** Every 3m traversed by Wall Running regenerates +40 Health over 4s. This effect can stack up to 3x.

### Drop Locations
- Amalgam Arca Heqet — Uncommon (1.00%)
- Amalgam Arca Kucumatz — Uncommon (1.00%)
- Amalgam Cinder Machinist — Uncommon (1.00%)
- Amalgam Phase Moa — Uncommon (1.00%)
- Amalgam Swarm Satyr — Uncommon (1.00%)

### Wiki
Odomedic is a Sentinel or MOA mod that generates Health for their owner through wall running, stacking up to three times.
**Notes:**
Although the description implies that it heals 40 health over 4 seconds, it instead heals 40 health per second for 4 seconds, totaling for 160 health.
A second/third stack is gained after traversing the distance listed on the mod once again.
After gaining 3 stacks, any more distance will only reset the timer for the healing.
Every stack gained resets the timer and adds one stack to the counter.
Loses one stack at a time, refreshing the timer of remaining stacks.
The user also does not need to maintain constant contact with a wall to meet the healing requirements, simply performing a single wall-jump and remaining airborne long enough to cover 3m will provide one stack from the mod.
The total distance covered must be horizontal.
Aim-gliding will interrupt the total distance covered, preventing stacks from being gained.
During the Bullet Jump animation, the horizontal distance will not be counted after a wall hop. The distance before and after the animation, however, will be counted. 
A good strategy to stack the mod quickly and efficiently is to hop away from the wall, double jump, and perform a roll. This will usually give 2-3 stacks.
**Tips:**
Valkyr's Rip Line and Zephyr's Tail Wind will trigger Odomedic.

## Ore Gaze
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Atlas | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired by attaining the rank of General under Steel Meridian, or the rank of Exalted under the Red Veil, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Petrify Augment: Petrified enemies are scanned into the Codex and have a 10% chance to drop additional loot when killed.
- **Rank 1:** Petrify Augment: Petrified enemies are scanned into the Codex and have a 15% chance to drop additional loot when killed.
- **Rank 2:** Petrify Augment: Petrified enemies are scanned into the Codex and have a 20% chance to drop additional loot when killed.
- **Rank 3:** Petrify Augment: Petrified enemies are scanned into the Codex and have a 25% chance to drop additional loot when killed.

### Drop Locations
- Red Veil, Exalted — Common (100.00%)
- Steel Meridian, General — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Ore Gaze is a Warframe Augment Mod for Atlas that modifies Petrify to cause enemies turned to stone to be scanned into the Codex and have a chance to make them drop extra loot.
**Notes:**
Extra loot chance is affected by Ability Strength; requiring 400% to achieve 100% chance of loot.
Extra loot chance does not scale past 100%.
The extra loot chance does not cause enemies to drop additional Rubble.
Enemies must stay petrified for the chance at extra loot. If the enemy remains alive after the effects of Petrify expire it must be petrified again.
Enemies that have already been fully scanned into the Codex can not be scanned by Ore Gaze, making it an ineffective way to scan Kavats for their Genetic Codes.
The extra loot drop will still occur on enemies that are fully scanned into the Codex.
The mod also affects enemies petrified by Path of Statues.
Petrified
Using Desecrate on a petrified corpse will not cause Ore Gaze to drop additional items beyond what Desecrate would have created normally.

## Overextended
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** vazarin | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** WARFRAME | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 5

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +15% Ability Range, -10% Ability Strength
- **Rank 1:** +30% Ability Range, -20% Ability Strength
- **Rank 2:** +45% Ability Range, -30% Ability Strength
- **Rank 3:** +60% Ability Range, -40% Ability Strength
- **Rank 4:** +75% Ability Range, -50% Ability Strength
- **Rank 5:** +90% Ability Range, -60% Ability Strength

### Drop Locations
- Derelict Vault — Rare (4.17%)

### Wiki
Overextended is a Corrupted Mod that increases Ability Range while reducing Ability Strength of Warframe abilities.
**Notes:**
Abilities that are not affected by Ability Strength (Such as Nyx's Chaos, Nekros' Desecrate and Loki's Radial Disarm) benefit most from this mod, as they are not affected by the Ability Strength penalty.
While this mod can be combined with a max rank Intensify and upgraded to rank 2 (+45% range, -30% strength) without any negative effects, equipping a max rank Stretch instead would achieve the same range bonus without requiring a second mod or costing additional mod points.
**Tips:**
Useful for ability builds that prioritize pulling enemies into a cluster over dealing damage, such as with Nidus's Larva, Vauban's Vortex-mode Bastille, Yareli's Riptide and Gyre's detonated Coil Horizon.
Saryn's Spores can benefit from Overextended as the mod allows her boils to spread onto more enemies, as well as offsetting the Strength penalty with the sustained rate of damage-over-time increase.
Using Molt Augmented can help offset the Ability Strength penalty, given the Arcane's buff is permanent until player's death.

**Notes:**
Additional bolts are not affected by mods.
Enemies affected by pacifying bolts are not slept, they are aware of their surroundings and will become alerted by nearby Tenno.

Cannot be equipped on Sentinels.
Does not generate Shields or Overshields.
Will heal Companions on all melee attacks, including Finishers, Stealth Attacks, and staggers via melee blocking. Thus, Warframes who can easily leave enemies open to finishers can make excellent use of this mod.
Heals when hitting an enemy with bullet jump.
Notably, each individual hit of Parazon finisher attacks can trigger the effects of this mod. for example, a 'backstab' finisher on a grineer lancer triggers the effect twice, while a finisher on an ancient healer triggers it four times.
The mod's effects will also work with Exalted Weapons.
Khora's Whipclaw can trigger this mod's effect.
Atlas's Landslide can trigger this mod's effect.

## Pain Points

- **Rank 3:** On Weak Point hits with Primary Fire: +40% Weak Point Damage for 5s. Stacks up to 10x.
- **Rank 4:** On Weak Point hits with Primary Fire: +50% Weak Point Damage for 5s. Stacks up to 10x.
- **Rank 5:** On Weak Point hits with Primary Fire: +60% Weak Point Damage for 5s. Stacks up to 10x.

### Wiki
Pain Points is an Weapon Augment Mod for Athodai which gives Weak Point damage when hitting Weak Points with the primary fire.
**Notes:**
Stacks up to 10x, for a maximum of +600% Weak Point damage.
Stacks last 5 seconds, with all stacks expiring once the most recent one expires.
Bonus is additive with Pistol Acuity.

## Panzer Devolution
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** penjaga | **Drain:** 0 | **Compatible:** Panzer Vulpaphyla | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
Panzer Devolution is automatically acquired upon obtaining a Panzer Vulpaphyla.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Devolves into its larval form when downed and attacks enemies with quills, dealing 15 Viral Damage each. Regains true form after 30s.
- **Rank 1:** Devolves into its larval form when downed and attacks enemies with quills, dealing 30 Viral Damage each. Regains true form after 30s.
- **Rank 2:** Devolves into its larval form when downed and attacks enemies with quills, dealing 45 Viral Damage each. Regains true form after 30s.
- **Rank 3:** Devolves into its larval form when downed and attacks enemies with quills, dealing 60 Viral Damage each. Regains true form after 30s.

### Wiki
Panzer Devolution is a precept mod for the Panzer Vulpaphyla to devolve into its larval form upon death and attack enemies with projectiles, dealing Viral damage and infecting them with spores. The Vulpaphyla will respawn after 30 seconds.
**Notes:**
The larval form floats beside the owner, similar to a Sentinel.
Devolution prevents bleedout and thus the Vulpaphyla cannot be revived normally. Its transformation also cannot be sped up.
Damage is affected by Maul, Bite, and elemental mods. 
Causes spores from Viral Quills to de-spawn on death.
The larva spits up to 3 quills at nearby enemies every 5 seconds. Enemies that are hit are afflicted by spores, similar to Saryn's Spores, which inflict Viral damage every second and applying Viral procs.
These quills don't trigger the lifesteal-link from Hunter Recovery or modded status effects like the full-grown Viral Quills do.
Spores remain active until the Vulpaphyla respawns to its true form.
Spores can be spread by shooting them.
Saryn's Toxic Lash will also burst all spores on a target.

## Parasitic Vitality
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Nidus | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired by attaining the rank of General under Steel Meridian, or the rank of Partner under The Perrin Sequence, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Parasitic Link Augment: Nidus and any ally he's bound to gain 1% Max Health per Mutation during Parasitic Link.
- **Rank 1:** Parasitic Link Augment: Nidus and any ally he's bound to gain 2% Max Health per Mutation during Parasitic Link.
- **Rank 2:** Parasitic Link Augment: Nidus and any ally he's bound to gain 3% Max Health per Mutation during Parasitic Link.
- **Rank 3:** Parasitic Link Augment: Nidus and any ally he's bound to gain 4% Max Health per Mutation during Parasitic Link.

### Drop Locations
- Steel Meridian, General — Common (100.00%)
- The Perrin Sequence, Partner — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Parasitic Vitality is a Warframe Augment Mod for Nidus that allows Parasitic Link to grant Nidus and the ally he is linked through with the ability bonus Health, scaling with each mutation stack.
**Notes:**
Bonus health scales with mutation stacks, up to 100x at full mutation for +400% health, and scales with Ability Strength.
This can be further increased by utilizing Abundant Mutation which increases mutation stacks to 300. This increases unmodded health by up to +1200%.
Bonus is additive to other sources of percentage health increases such as Vigor and Gladiator Resolve.
Bonus scales off of base health, i.e. before any mods or other health bonuses such as Arcane Blessing or Azure Archon Shard's health bonus.
Counts as a Vitality mod, thus cannot be used in conjunction with Vitality, Archon Vitality, or Umbral Vitality.
The ally linked however, will gain the bonus health regardless if they have any of these mods equipped.
Nidus will still gain the bonus health even if linked to an enemy.
When the link breaks, there is a 5 second countdown before Parasitic Vitality's health bonus disappears until Nidus links with a new target.
When Nidus gains a mutation stack, his maximum and current health will be updated to reflect the new value. This functionally grants Nidus a full heal whenever gaining a stack.
**Tips:**
Combining this augment with Abundant Mutation can allow Nidus to gain health far into the quadruple digits. However, his natural healing will never scale enough to account for the increased health. Percentage scaling health abilities like Blood Altar or arcanes such as Arcane Grace and Arcane Victory can allow for better health regeneration.

- Mercury/Suisei (Spy), Rotation C — Rare (8.60%)
- Phobos/Grildrig (Spy), Rotation C — Rare (8.60%)
- Phobos/Shklovsky (Spy), Rotation C — Rare (8.60%)
- Venus/Unda (Spy), Rotation C — Rare (8.60%)
- Venus/Vesper (Spy), Rotation C — Rare (8.60%)

### Wiki
Parry is an Exilus Weapon Mod for melee that increases the chance to counter incoming melee attacks, opening an enemy to a melee finisher when blocking.
**Tips:**
If getting close to a ranged enemy, they will most likely melee hit you with their weapon to defend themselves, this maneuver can be parried and the player can perform a counter attack finisher on the enemy.
Finisher attacks triggered by Parry are affected by True damage.
Triggering the counter does not require the user to equip Parry or to block manually.
Equipping a fully ranked Parry will increase the chance to counter to +96% such that it will rarely fail from RNG.
Disarming enemies' ranged weapons (with effects like Loki's Radial Disarm) will force foes to switch to melee attacks that can be parried.
Certain special attacks can not be parried or trigger a counterattack finisher, though they will be blocked.
Grappling hook attacks (such as from the Grineer Scorpion or Infested Ancient Disruptor) will be blocked but not parried.
Normal knockback and knockdown rules for blocking still apply. Heavy enemy attacks like Seismic Shockwave or the Shield Lancer's charge will still trigger knockback but will not knock down a blocking player.

### Wiki
Particle Ram is a Plexus Battle Offensive mod. It creates a large shield-like projection deals Radiation damage on contact while the Railjack is moving forward, and deals increased damage during a Vector Maneuver. Deactivating the ability (default 2) will launch the ram as a projectile with even more damage.
**Notes:**
The ram inherits the Railjack's engine color.
The ram deals 150% increased damage during a Vector Maneuver, and 200% when launched.
Energy cost is not affected by Reactors or Warframe mods.
Damage per second is affected by Ability Strength.
Launch duration is affected by Ability Duration.
**Tips:**
Because of the ram's large hitbox, it can be used as an impromptu means of gathering spaceborne resources by destroying containers and deposits as the Railjack flies along.

## Partitioned Mallet
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Octavia | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This Mod can be acquired by attaining the rank of Genius under the Cephalon Suda, or the rank of Flawless under the New Loka, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Mallet Augment: Create an additional Mallet with 65% reduced range.
- **Rank 1:** Mallet Augment: Create an additional Mallet with 50% reduced range.
- **Rank 2:** Mallet Augment: Create an additional Mallet with 35% reduced range.
- **Rank 3:** Mallet Augment: Create an additional Mallet with 20% reduced range.

### Drop Locations
- Cephalon Suda, Genius — Common (100.00%)
- New Loka, Flawless — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Partitioned Mallet is a Warframe Augment Mod for Octavia that allows her to summon two Mallet with reduced range.
**Notes:**
Two Mallets cannot be placed within each other's radii.
It is, however, possible for a Resonator carrying a Mallet to enter the radius of another Mallet, or for 2 Mallets' radii to overlap if both are under the effects of Amp.
The damage ticks from both Mallets can affect the same enemies, provided they are within the radius of both.
Resonator can only carry 1 Mallet. If Resonator is summoned while both Mallets are present, it will go for the closest one.
Amp only affects the last Mallet summoned, unless the radii of both Mallets are in Octavia's sight.
If Mallet is recast while Amp is active, Amp must be recast for that Mallet to benefit from it.

## Peaceful Provocation
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Equinox | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired by attaining the rank of Maxim under the Arbiters of Hexis, or the rank of Flawless under New Loka, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Pacify & Provoke Augment: Pacify converts damage done to allies into an aura that slows enemies by 20%. Provoke converts damage done to enemies into 6% extra Ability Strength.
- **Rank 1:** Pacify & Provoke Augment: Pacify converts damage done to allies into an aura that slows enemies by 30%. Provoke converts damage done to enemies into 9% extra Ability Strength.
- **Rank 2:** Pacify & Provoke Augment: Pacify converts damage done to allies into an aura that slows enemies by 35%. Provoke converts damage done to enemies into 12% extra Ability Strength.
- **Rank 3:** Pacify & Provoke Augment: Pacify converts damage done to allies into an aura that slows enemies by 40%. Provoke converts damage done to enemies into 15% extra Ability Strength.

### Drop Locations
- Arbiters of Hexis, Maxim — Common (100.00%)
- New Loka, Flawless — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Peaceful Provocation is a Warframe Augment Mod for Equinox that increases the effectiveness of Pacify & Provoke. Pacify converts damage done to Equinox and her allies into an aura that slows enemies by up to 40%. Provoke converts damage done to enemies within the aura into up to 15% bonus Ability Strength.
**Notes:**
Pacify's maximum slow and Provoke's maximum bonus Ability Strength percentages are affected by Ability Strength.
Maximum extra Ability Strength bonus is capped at 30%.
Maximum enemy slow is capped at 80%.
Pacify's damage taken to Slow Aura conversion is as follows: Every 1% of Maximum Health + Shields lost by Equinox and allies within Pacify's range increases the slow aura by +0.25%.
Conversion is not affected by Ability Strength.
Damage to Overguard is not converted.
Provoke's damage dealt to Ability Strength conversion is as follows: Every 1% of maximum enemy Health + Shields dealt increases the bonus ability strength by +0.02%.
Conversion is not affected by Ability Strength.
Damage to Overguard is not converted.
Ability strength bonus is rounded to the nearest 1%.
Equinox retains Peaceful Provocation's effects as long as Pacify or Provoke remains active. Switching between forms will reset the bonus.
Peaceful Provocation's current percentages are displayed beside Equinox's shield and health indicators.

The mod emits a sound audible for Teammates with a radius of approximately 5 meters.

## Peculiar Bloom
Critical hits cause flowers to grow from the wounds.
**Rarity:** Uncommon | **Polarity:** naramon | **Drain:** 2 | **Compatible:** WARFRAME | **Max Rank:** 5

### Drop Locations
- Elite Sanctuary Onslaught, Rotation C — Rare (2.01%)

### Wiki
Peculiar Bloom is a Peculiar Warframe Exilus mod that causes flowers to grow on enemies when a player hits them with critical hits. Increasing the mod's rank will increase the size of flowers generated.
**Notes:**
This mod is cosmetic and does not affect damage or apply a status effect.
Only up to five flowers will spawn per enemy unit.
Landing a critical hit will produce a single flower that despawns after 30 seconds. Landing multiple critical hits in a single hit (e.g. from weapons with Multishot) will produce multiple flowers.
The position of the flower is based on where the enemy was shot, e.g. shooting an enemy's knee will produce a flower in that knee. The flowers grow from several fixed locations within the enemy, as opposed to growing from exactly where the enemy was hit.
The angle at which the flower is oriented depends on the angle at which the shot was fired.
The mod takes the same amount of Endo to upgrade as an uncommon mod and it is grouped between Amalgam and Riven Mods in the Mods Segment.
As with Riven Mods, only one peculiar mod can be equipped on a Warframe at a time.
Attacks by your Companions can also cause flowers to appear, should they crit.
**Trivia:**
Prior to Update 22.18.8, there used to be no limit to how many flowers could grow on an enemy. This means a properly modded weapon (such as an Amprex modded for low damage and high critical chance) could spawn hundreds, if not thousands, of flowers across multiple enemies, causing game-breaking performance issues or even crashing the game entirely.
In the game files the mod is named "FlowerPowerMod", which can be a reference to the popular peace slogan Flower power.

## Peculiar End
**Rarity:** Common | **Polarity:** naramon | **Drain:** 2 | **Compatible:** WARFRAME | **Max Rank:** 5

### Acquisition
This mod can be purchased unranked from Baro Ki'Teer for 1,000,001 and 228. Note however that Baro Ki'Teer's stock changes with each appearance, and may not have this item available at every time.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Finisher kills have a 50% chance to dissolve enemies in dread.
- **Rank 1:** Finisher kills have a 60% chance to dissolve enemies in dread.
- **Rank 2:** Finisher kills have a 70% chance to dissolve enemies in dread.
- **Rank 3:** Finisher kills have a 80% chance to dissolve enemies in dread.
- **Rank 4:** Finisher kills have a 90% chance to dissolve enemies in dread.
- **Rank 5:** Finisher kills have a 100% chance to dissolve enemies in dread.

### Wiki
Peculiar End is a Peculiar Warframe Exilus mod that makes Finisher kills have a chance to dissolve enemies in dread. The effect is a swirling red ephemera similar to the Ki'Teer Eskhatos Ephemera and evil sounding laughter.
**Notes:**
Nekros' Desecrate does affect targets affected by this mod, but only after the duration of the animation.

## Peculiar Growth
**Rarity:** Uncommon | **Polarity:** naramon | **Drain:** 2 | **Compatible:** WARFRAME | **Max Rank:** 5

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Damaging an enemy will inflate the body part hit for 1s.
- **Rank 1:** Damaging an enemy will inflate the body part hit for 2s.
- **Rank 2:** Damaging an enemy will inflate the body part hit for 3s.
- **Rank 3:** Damaging an enemy will inflate the body part hit for 4s.
- **Rank 4:** Damaging an enemy will inflate the body part hit for 5s.
- **Rank 5:** Damaging an enemy will inflate the body part hit for 6s.

### Drop Locations
- Elite Sanctuary Onslaught, Rotation B — Rare (2.01%)

### Wiki
Peculiar Growth is a Peculiar Warframe Exilus mod that causes hit parts of an enemy body to inflate for a short time.
It can be obtained as a drop from Elite Sanctuary Onslaught in rotation B (zone 6, 14, 22, etc.).
**Notes:**
The effect is only applied on non-critical hits.
This mod is cosmetic and does not affect damage or apply a status effect.
Although mostly cosmetic, it technically affects the hitbox size as well, which may sometimes make landing headshots easier or harder because of the head or body part hitbox of an enemy becoming blocked.
This can theoretically make this mod useful for any build requiring headshots but lacking in critical hits, as repeated headshots will be easier to land.
The effect also works on other Warframes if the player has been affected by a Radiation proc.
As with Riven Mods, only one Peculiar mod can be equipped on a Warframe at a time.
**Trivia:**
This mod was first showcased on Devstream 110.
This mod allows all ranged weapons to achieve a similar growth mechanic innately available to the Kuva Nukor Nukor.

## Perpetual Agony
**Rarity:** Common | **Polarity:** madurai | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Pistol | **Max Rank:** 5

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +15% Status Duration
- **Rank 1:** +30% Status Duration
- **Rank 2:** +45% Status Duration
- **Rank 3:** +60% Status Duration
- **Rank 4:** +75% Status Duration
- **Rank 5:** +90% Status Duration

### Drop Locations
- Arid Butcher — Common (1.14%)
- Deimos Carnis — Uncommon (1.14%)
- Deimos Jugulus — Uncommon (1.14%)
- Deimos Leaping Thrasher — Uncommon (1.14%)
- Deimos Saxum — Uncommon (1.14%)
- Frontier Butcher — Common (1.14%)
- Leaping Thrasher — Uncommon (1.14%)
- Narmer Butcher — Common (1.14%)
- Eidolon Gantulyst — Uncommon (32.21%)

### Wiki
Perpetual Agony is a mod that increases the status duration inflicted by secondary weapons.
**Notes:**
The mod's effects are only visible on Status Effects with an associated duration, either increasing the duration of the associated debuff (Puncture, Cold, Magnetic, Corrosive, Radiation, and Viral), or the number of ticks associated with DoT effects (Slash, Toxin, Gas, and Heat).
This mod will not affect the duration of crowd control effects generated by Status Effects (staggers from Impact, the panic duration of Heat, and the stun animation caused by Electricity).
However, it does affect the duration of the freeze effect applied by Cold after reaching 10 stacks.
Increased Status Duration will slow down the Armor Strip effect of Heat. For example, with +100% status duration, the armor strip will occur every second instead of every half second. As a result, the full 50% armor strip will only be achieved after 4 seconds.
Status Effects from the radial blasts of Syndicate Weapon Augments and Syndicate Weapons are not affected by this mod.
**Tips:**
Most beneficial on weapons with high status chance like the Tysis, Marelok, and Nukor, and weapons with innate status effects, such as the Acrid, Castanas and the Embolist.

## Phoenix Renewal
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Oberon | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired by attaining the rank of General under Steel Meridian, or the rank of Flawless under New Loka, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.
This mod can periodically be acquired from The Circuit.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Renewal Augment: Taking fatal damage while under the effects of Renewal will instead Heal you or allies to 20% Health. This effect triggers only once for each ally every 60s.
- **Rank 1:** Renewal Augment: Taking fatal damage while under the effects of Renewal will instead Heal you or allies to 25% Health. This effect triggers only once for each ally every 60s.
- **Rank 2:** Renewal Augment: Taking fatal damage while under the effects of Renewal will instead Heal you or allies to 35% Health. This effect triggers only once for each ally every 60s.
- **Rank 3:** Renewal Augment: Taking fatal damage while under the effects of Renewal will instead Heal you or allies to 50% Health. This effect triggers only once for each ally every 60s.

### Drop Locations
- New Loka, Flawless — Common (100.00%)
- Steel Meridian, General — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Phoenix Renewal is a Warframe Augment Mod for Oberon's Renewal that prevents affected players from entering Bleedout upon receiving fatal damage, becoming healed for a percentage of their health and are granted 5 seconds of Invulnerability with a 60 second cooldown.
**Notes:**
Prevents the down from occurring at all, so any abilities or effects relating to taking fatal damage will not trigger. Does not synergize with Quick Thinking or Gladiator Finesse, as it activates before they deplete energy.
Will affect Companions.
Companions use a separate cooldown.
Both the Warframes and Operators can be saved, but the cooldown is tied to the Tenno.
Does not work on allied NPCs.
Works in Arbitrations.
Upon triggering, a message is displayed on the screen of the player that was saved. They are then given a red Renewal icon as a debuff to indicate the cooldown.
Even though multiple Renewals stack together, the augment's cooldown is universal - multiple Oberons equipped with the augment will not provide multiple separate Phoenix Renewal triggers.

## Piercing Caliber
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** naramon | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Rifle | **Max Rank:** 5

### Acquisition
Piercing Caliber can be acquired from the Razorback Armada or the Eidolon Gantulyst.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +20% Puncture
- **Rank 1:** +40% Puncture
- **Rank 2:** +60% Puncture
- **Rank 3:** +80% Puncture
- **Rank 4:** +100% Puncture
- **Rank 5:** +120% Puncture

### Drop Locations
- Eidolon Gantulyst — Legendary (0.84%)
- Razorback — Rare (5.53%)

### Wiki
The Piercing Caliber mod increases Puncture damage of a rifle by 20% per rank for a maximum of 120% at Rank 5.
**Notes:**
Can be combined with Piercing Hit to provide a 210% increase to Puncture damage.
When calculating total raw damage, a weapon will require a Puncture damage percentage equal to 75% of its total base damage to equal the raw damage increase provided by a standard elemental mod (which increases corresponding elemental damages by 90%).
The higher the Puncture damage percentage over 75%, the more viable this mod will be as opposed to an elemental mod, and vice versa for lower values.
These calculations only pertain to raw damage, and do not take into consideration enemy armor/flesh/shield damage multipliers.
**Trivia:**
One of the few mods that depicts a Warframe with non-standard color schemes, in this case a Nyx with her Immortal skin.

## Piercing Navigator
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Ivara | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired by attaining the rank of Genius under Cephalon Suda, or the rank of Partner under The Perrin Sequence, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Navigator Augment: Each hit increases the projectile's Critical Chance by 20% up to a max of 250%. +3 Projectile Punch Through.
- **Rank 1:** Navigator Augment: Each hit increases the projectile's Critical Chance by 30% up to a max of 250%. +3 Projectile Punch Through.
- **Rank 2:** Navigator Augment: Each hit increases the projectile's Critical Chance by 40% up to a max of 250%. +3 Projectile Punch Through.
- **Rank 3:** Navigator Augment: Each hit increases the projectile's Critical Chance by 50% up to a max of 250%. +3 Projectile Punch Through.

### Drop Locations
- Cephalon Suda, Genius — Common (100.00%)
- The Perrin Sequence, Partner — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Piercing Navigator is a Warframe Augment Mod for Ivara's Navigator that increases the projectile's Critical Chance by 50% for every successful hit, up to a max of 250%, and adds Punch Through.
**Notes:**
The Critical Chance bonus applies additively to other Critical Chance mods.
For example, a Boltor with Point Strike equipped and after 5 hits controlled by Navigator will have a total of 10% (1 + 150% + 250%) critical chance.
Critical Chance is not affected by Ability Strength.
Punch Through is affected by Ability Range.
In the case of the Zenistar, the disk itself must touch the enemy in order to gain the bonus.

## Piercing Roar
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Rhino | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired by attaining the rank of General under Steel Meridian, or the rank of Partner under The Perrin Sequence, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Roar Augment: Roar gains +40% range. Enemies within 10m are knocked down and suffer 5 stacks of Puncture Status.
- **Rank 1:** Roar Augment: Roar gains +40% range. Enemies within 15m are knocked down and suffer 5 stacks of Puncture Status.
- **Rank 2:** Roar Augment: Roar gains +40% range. Enemies within 20m are knocked down and suffer 5 stacks of Puncture Status.
- **Rank 3:** Roar Augment: Roar gains +40% range. Enemies within 25m are knocked down and suffer 5 stacks of Puncture Status.

### Drop Locations
- Steel Meridian, General — Common (100.00%)
- The Perrin Sequence, Partner — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Piercing Roar is a Warframe Augment Mod for Rhino that gives Roar the ability to inflict knockdown and 5 Puncture procs on enemies. Additionally allows Roar to be recast and passively increases its range.
**Notes:**
Applies 5 Puncture stacks to all enemies in range, reducing their damage by 80% and increasing the critical chance of incoming weapon attacks by an additive 25%.
Does not require line of sight to hit enemies.
Deals 100 Puncture damage to all affected enemies. Damage does not scale with strength.
Damage is increased by the Roar buff itself however, increasing the base damage by Ability Strength50% (or Ability Strength30% for the subsumed version).
The Roar buff is applied before damage is dealt to enemies, so the damage will always be buffed by Roar.
Piercing range is affected by Ability Range.
However it does not benefit from the +40% range from the augment, which only applies to the Roar buff range.
Puncture proc duration is affected by Ability Duration.
Any existing buff by Roar gets cancelled when recasting Roar.
**Tips:**
This mod applies two different status effects (knockdown and Puncture) to nearby enemies, so it can be useful for Condition Overload.

Visual effects added during Bullet Jumps will always be the same color, regardless of Warframe Energy or Emission colors.

## Pilfering Strangledome
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Khora | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired by attaining the rank of General under the Steel Meridian, or the rank of Exalted under the Red Veil, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Strangledome Augment: Enemies held in Strangledome have a 30% chance of dropping additional loot.
- **Rank 1:** Strangledome Augment: Enemies held in Strangledome have a 35% chance of dropping additional loot.
- **Rank 2:** Strangledome Augment: Enemies held in Strangledome have a 50% chance of dropping additional loot.
- **Rank 3:** Strangledome Augment: Enemies held in Strangledome have a 65% chance of dropping additional loot.

### Drop Locations
- Red Veil, Exalted — Common (100.00%)
- Steel Meridian, General — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Pilfering Strangledome is a Warframe Augment Mod for Khora that allows enemies caught in Strangledome have a chance to drop additional loot when killed.
**Notes:**
Drop chance is not affected by mods.
Death
**Tips:**
This mod synergizes very well with anything that can help pull enemies into a cluster within the chains and thus generate a higher chance at yielding loot, such as Proboscis Cernos, Nidus's Larva, Vauban's Bastille in Vortex mode and Minelayer in Tether Coil mode, Khora's Ensnare, Mag's short-tap Magnetize, Yareli's Riptide, Gyre's detonated Coil Horizon, the Vazarin school's Void Snare, or Nautilus's Cordon precept.
While Hydroid's Pilfering Swarm can benefit with Overextended, the same mod may have a counterproductive effect on Khora's version, as having a very large Strangledome makes enemies hang too far from each other and thus can be harder to take out en masse.
While in an Excavation mission, Power Carrier enemies have a chance to drop additional Power Cells when caught by Pilfering Strangledome.
**Trivia:**
Pilfering Strangledome is the second Warframe Augment Mod to use the term Pilfering, the first being Pilfering Swarm; both share similar augmentation and apply to an ability that traps and incapacitates enemies.

## Pilfering Swarm
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Hydroid | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired by attaining the rank of Genius under Cephalon Suda, or the rank of Flawless under New Loka, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Tentacle Swarm Augment: Enemies held by tentacles have a 25% chance at additional drops.
- **Rank 1:** Tentacle Swarm Augment: Enemies held by tentacles have a 50% chance at additional drops.
- **Rank 2:** Tentacle Swarm Augment: Enemies held by tentacles have a 75% chance at additional drops.
- **Rank 3:** Tentacle Swarm Augment: Enemies held by tentacles have a 100% chance at additional drops.

### Drop Locations
- Cephalon Suda, Genius — Common (100.00%)
- New Loka, Flawless — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Pilfering Swarm is a Hydroid Warframe Augment Mod that grants Tentacle Swarm a chance to yield additional loot from enemies killed while they're ensnared by the tentacles.
**Notes:**
Enemies held by Pilfering Swarm tentacles and killed by any sources have a chance to drop additional loot.
If there are enemies that can't be picked up by the tentacles (e.g. enemies affected by Petrify or immune to crowd control), they need to be killed by the tentacles to drop additional loot.
Works against Ospreys and other flying enemies.
Death
**Tips:**
This mod synergizes very well with anything that can help pull enemies into a cluster within the tentacles and thus generate a higher chance at yielding loot, such as Proboscis Cernos, Nidus's Larva, Vauban's Bastille in Vortex mode, Khora's Ensnare, Mag's short-tap Magnetize, Yareli's Riptide, Gyre's detonated Coil Horizon, the Vazarin school's Void Snare, or Nautilus's Cordon precept.
As drop chance is unaffected by Ability Strength, players can focus on maximizing Pilfering Swarm's coverage via Overextended.
**Trivia:**
When this augment was first introduced, additional loot would only be generated if the enemies were killed by Tentacle Swarm, making this augment less effective at higher levels due to enemy health and armor scaling. Update 17.0.4 changed this to its current effect, in exchange for the additional loot chance no longer being affected by Ability Strength.
Up until Update 21.4, this augment's mod image was identical to Tentacle Swarm's.

## Pistol Acuity
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** naramon | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Pistol | **Max Rank:** 10

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +32% Weak Point Damage, +32% Weak Point Critical Chance. Multishot cannot be modified.
- **Rank 1:** +64% Weak Point Damage, +64% Weak Point Critical Chance. Multishot cannot be modified.
- **Rank 2:** +95% Weak Point Damage, +95% Weak Point Critical Chance. Multishot cannot be modified.
- **Rank 3:** +127% Weak Point Damage, +127% Weak Point Critical Chance. Multishot cannot be modified.
- **Rank 4:** +159% Weak Point Damage, +159% Weak Point Critical Chance. Multishot cannot be modified.
- **Rank 5:** +191% Weak Point Damage, +191% Weak Point Critical Chance. Multishot cannot be modified.
- **Rank 6:** +223% Weak Point Damage, +223% Weak Point Critical Chance. Multishot cannot be modified.
- **Rank 7:** +254% Weak Point Damage, +254% Weak Point Critical Chance. Multishot cannot be modified.
- **Rank 8:** +286% Weak Point Damage, +286% Weak Point Critical Chance. Multishot cannot be modified.
- **Rank 9:** +318% Weak Point Damage, +318% Weak Point Critical Chance. Multishot cannot be modified.
- **Rank 10:** +350% Weak Point Damage, +350% Weak Point Critical Chance. Multishot cannot be modified.

### Drop Locations
- Scaldra Barbican — Rare (0.08%)
- Scaldra Eradicator — Rare (0.09%)
- Techrot Skuzzi — Rare (0.13%)

### Wiki
Pistol Acuity is a mod that increases the Weak Point Damage and Weak Point Critical Chance of pistols, but prevents Multishot from being modified.
**Notes:**
Equipping this mod will set weapon's Multishot to its default ignoring other bonuses, even negative effects (e.g. Pistol Riven Mod).
The Weak Point Damage is multiplicative with bonuses to Headshot Multiplier, such as from Secondary Deadhead.
The Weak Point Damage is additive with the Weak Point Multiplier on enemies.
The values described on the mod are misleading, the actual damage bonus being 1.5x of the listed value.
For example, a typical humanoid enemy such as a Butcher has a 3x Weak Point Multiplier on its head. A rank 10 Acuity will increase this value to 3 + (3.5 * 1.5) = 8.25x
This is a relative damage increase of +175%
MOA's backpack is one exception to this, accurately adding the listed damage bonus to its Weak Point Multiplier.
Note that damage increase may vary between different types of enemies, since not all enemies will receive "Headcrit" damage, for example MOA and Lumbering Fragment.
Weapons with Area of Effect damage or other special projectiles that inherently do not deal increased weakpoint damage will not gain increased damage from this mod.
Weakspots created by Banshee's Sonar do not count as weak points
category:Acuity Mods

### Wiki
Pistol Ammo Mutation is a pistol mod that converts any unused Primary Ammo into Secondary Ammo. The affected pistol will only convert ammo while equipped, and will not convert any ammo type used by holstered primary weapons until their own ammo is full.
Converted ammo is denoted by a Mutate symbol. The final amount of ammo restored is rounded to the nearest integer.
**Tips:**
Highly recommended for ammo-inefficient secondaries such as the Dex Furis or the Azima.
Functions well with Sentinel's Vacuum or Chesa Kubrow's Retrieve, which automatically collect unused ammo as needed.

## Pistol Amp
**Rarity:** Uncommon | **Polarity:** madurai | **Drain:** -2 | **Compatible:** AURA | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 5

### Acquisition
Pistol Amp is a potential drop from the Knave Specter, available upon anointing a Silver Grove shrine with the Nightfall Apothic.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Squad receives +4.5% Pistol Damage
- **Rank 1:** Squad receives +9% Pistol Damage
- **Rank 2:** Squad receives +13.5% Pistol Damage
- **Rank 3:** Squad receives +18% Pistol Damage
- **Rank 4:** Squad receives +22.5% Pistol Damage
- **Rank 5:** Squad receives +27% Pistol Damage

### Drop Locations
- Duviri/Endless: Tier 1 (Normal) — Legendary (0.67%)
- Duviri/Endless: Tier 3 (Normal) — Legendary (0.67%)
- Knave Specter — Common (51.52%)

### Wiki
Not to be confused with Amp.
Pistol Amp is an aura mod that increases the damage dealt with Secondary Weapons.
* Aura mods increase the amount of Mod Capacity
**Notes:**
Pistol Amp is additive with base damage mods such as Hornet Strike.
For example, Lato with Pistol Amp and Hornet Strike will deal 30 (1 + 27% + 220%) damage.
This aura also affects the damage of Glory, Regulators, Dex Pixia, and Balefire Charger.
**Trivia:**
For a short time after Update 10.6, this mod could have been acquired via Transmutation. This was due to a bug where all mods, including unreleased ones such as this one, became obtainable through transmutation. This has since been fixed, but players who obtained the mod this way were allowed to keep them, though Update 11.0.6 made it so all unreleased mods were made untradeable until they were properly introduced.
Prior to Update The Silver Grove, the damage bonus was 60% at max rank.

## Pistol Pestilence
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** madurai | **Drain:** 4 | **Compatible:** Pistol | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
Originally acquired as a limited-edition reward from participating in The Cicero Crisis (50 points)
Can be acquired as a possible drop by killing Corrupted Vor in Orokin Void missions level >=40 such as Mithra and Mot
25% drop chance; it has 100% drop, but shares this with its counterparts - Malignant Force, Virulent Scourge, Toxic Barrage
It was also acquired as a drop from Razorback Armada; however, this is no longer possible.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +15% Toxin, +15% Status Chance
- **Rank 1:** +30% Toxin, +30% Status Chance
- **Rank 2:** +45% Toxin, +45% Status Chance
- **Rank 3:** +60% Toxin, +60% Status Chance

### Drop Locations
- Corrupted Vor — Uncommon (25.00%)

### Wiki
Pistol Pestilence is a mod that increases the Toxin Damage and status chance of a pistol by 15% per rank for a maximum of 60% at rank 3.
**Notes:**
Pistol Pestilence is additive with similar mods. For example, a Sicarus with a max rank Pistol Pestilence and Sure Shot will have a 6% (1 + 60% + 90%) status chance.
This mod can be combined with Jolt, Frostbite and Scorch to achieve a combined status chance bonus of 240%. With Sure Shot and Stunning Speed, this bonus can be increased to 360%. With Saxum Spittle, Jugulus Spines, and Carnis Stinger, this bonus can be increased to 540%.

### Wiki
The Point Blank mod increases the base damage of a shotgun by 15% per rank at a maximum of 90% at rank 5.
**Notes:**
Affects base damage which is applied before every other damage type. Therefore, every other damage increasing mod is benefiting from Point Blank as well.
This mod combined with Vicious Spread and Blaze give off a total damage increase of +240%.
With Primed Point Blank instead of Point Blank, this adds up for a total damage increase of +315% with Vicious Spread and Blaze.
Due to how it affects the base damage, this mod is necessary for all shotgun weapons in order to use them optimally. Few mods can replace the effectiveness of this mod.

### Wiki
Point Strike is a mod that increases a primary weapon's critical chance by 25% per rank for a maximum of 150% at rank 5.
**Notes:**
Cannot be equiped with Critical Delay.
Point Strike is additive with similar mods. For example, a Braton with a max rank Point Strike and Argon Scope will have a 12% (1 + 150% + 135%) critical chance.

## Poppin' Vert
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** naramon | **Drain:** 4 | **Compatible:** K-Drive | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
Can be purchased from Roky after reaching Rank Whozit with the Ventkids for 7,500 Standing.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +20% K-Drive Double Jump Height
- **Rank 1:** +40% K-Drive Double Jump Height
- **Rank 2:** +60% K-Drive Double Jump Height
- **Rank 3:** +80% K-Drive Double Jump Height

### Drop Locations
- Ventkids, Whozit — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Poppin' Vert is a K-Drive mod that enables it to double jump, with additional ranks increasing the K-Drive's Double Jump Height.
**Notes:**
Allows for the ability to double jump on K-drive.
With this mod equipped, any trick performed while in mid air will launch the user upwards.
It is only possible to jump once while in mid air, all further tricks will respond as usual. Touching the ground restores the ability to double jump.
There is no way to control the double jump height beyond fusing the mod. Any and all tricks will trigger the full height of the jump, regardless of velocity or height from the ground.
The mod appears to use a value of 15m for the calculation of double jump height, although the base value of double jump height is 0m.
Technically, double jump is always available even if the user does not possess this mod. However, because the double jump height is 0m, it has no effect.
24.2.0

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** The Kavat pounces at an enemy, dealing 60% Damage and stunning them for a short duration.
- **Rank 1:** The Kavat pounces at an enemy, dealing 70% Damage and stunning them for a short duration.
- **Rank 2:** The Kavat pounces at an enemy, dealing 80% Damage and stunning them for a short duration.
- **Rank 3:** The Kavat pounces at an enemy, dealing 90% Damage and stunning them for a short duration.

### Drop Locations
- Kavat — Uncommon (6.11%)

### Wiki
Pounce allows Kavats to lunge at an enemy, stunning them for a short duration.
**Notes:**
Stun lasts 3.5 seconds and opens enemy to a Finisher.
Open to Finisher for ~3.3 seconds with ~0.2 second recovery animation.
Can stun multiple enemies at once if they are within ~1 meter of each other.
Does not affect enemies with Overguard.

## Power Donation
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** madurai | **Drain:** -4 | **Compatible:** AURA | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 5

### Acquisition
Obtainable from the Arbitrations vendor in the Arbiters of Hexis room in Relays for 20 Vitus Essence.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** You lose 5% Ability Strength Squadmates gain 5% Ability Strength
- **Rank 1:** You lose 10% Ability Strength Squadmates gain 10% Ability Strength
- **Rank 2:** You lose 15% Ability Strength Squadmates gain 15% Ability Strength
- **Rank 3:** You lose 20% Ability Strength Squadmates gain 20% Ability Strength
- **Rank 4:** You lose 25% Ability Strength Squadmates gain 25% Ability Strength
- **Rank 5:** You lose 30% Ability Strength Squadmates gain 30% Ability Strength

### Wiki
Power Donation is an Aura mod that grants all allies increased Ability Strength at the cost of the user's own Ability Strength.
* Aura mods increase the amount of Mod Capacity
**Notes:**
If all 4 players in a squad equip this mod, each player will get +60% Ability Strength.
In comparison, Growing Power will yield +100% but needs status effects to be activated.
Tied with Steel Charge for the highest Aura mod capacity, granting 18 additional capacity at max rank with a matching polarity.
Does not work with Coaction Drift.bug?
If this aura is slotted into your Warframe, it will affect it exactly as intended; however, if you summon a Necramech, your mech will be treated as an Ally even while you are piloting it, and it will receive the +30% Ability Strength Bonus, rather than the -30% Ability Strength decrease. This bonus is additive with Necramech Intensify.
**Tips:**
Can be used in conjunction with Overextended to achieve 10% Ability Strength.
**Trivia:**
Going into Archwing used to double the Aura's penalty, causing negative Ability Strength values. This was fixed in Update 23.10.3.
Until Update 23.10.6, it used the outdated terminology of Power Strength instead of Ability Strength.
This is the first Aura to negatively affect the carrier to offset the gain for allies.
24.2.5

### Wiki
Update 29.5 changes and add gif showcasing what a consecutive throw looks like
Power Throw is a mod for Glaives that adds punch through when thrown and increases thrown damage and projectile speed.
Data may differ due to width variances from dynamic posing & inconsistent mechanic.
**Notes:**
Damage bonus is multiplicative to the one provided by Pressure Point and other Melee Damage buffs.
Damage bonus is additive with many Universal Damage Bonuses as it gives Weapon Damage, not Melee Damage.
Punch through applies to bodies only, but not surfaces of any kind (e.g. it cannot pass through a Shield Lancer's shield).
Consecutive throw bonus refers to "buffering" by holding the melee button before the Glaive is caught, otherwise the buff will be lost. In addition to the Power Throw buff combo counter, the throw animation will also change; the base throw is an open-handed forward throw, while consecutive throws alternate between a backspin throw and a side-flip under throw.
**Trivia:**
Prior to Update 10.2.1, the mod granted 1.0m punch through per rank.
In Dark Sector, another game published by Digital Extremes, "Power Throw" is an ability for Hayden Tenno to deal quadruple damage with his Glaive.
Update 13 gave Power Throw the ability to create explosions when hitting an object or an enemy on its last bounce. This feature has since been removed in Update 26.

## Precision Strike
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** naramon | **Drain:** 4 | **Compatible:** Tonkor | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
Available for 20 from the Nightwave Cred Offerings store on a rotational basis.
Precision Strike was originally acquired by reaching Rank 23 in Nightwave during Series 3.
Beginning with Nightwave Nora's Mix: Vol. 7, it was added to the Nightwave Cred Offerings.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Hitting an enemy directly with the grenade increases Reload Speed by 37% for 5s.
- **Rank 1:** Hitting an enemy directly with the grenade increases Reload Speed by 75% for 5s.
- **Rank 2:** Hitting an enemy directly with the grenade increases Reload Speed by 110% for 5s.
- **Rank 3:** Hitting an enemy directly with the grenade increases Reload Speed by 150% for 5s.

### Wiki
Precision Strike is an weapon augment mod for Tonkor and Kuva Tonkor that increases reload speed upon hitting an enemy directly with the grenade projectile for 5 seconds.
**Notes:**
Modded reload time is calculated as Weapon Base Reload Time (1 + Mod Reload Bonus), so with this mod at rank 3 and no other bonuses to reload speed, the reload time is 0.68s for the Tonkor and 0.6s for the Kuva Tonkor.
The reload speed is granted even if the projectile explodes as long as it hit an enemy.
The buff can be refreshed while active.
The buff is only received while you actively hold the Tonkor.

**Rarity:** Uncommon | **Polarity:** madurai | **Drain:** 6 | **Max Rank:** 5

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +8.3% Turret Critical Chance
- **Rank 1:** +16.7% Turret Critical Chance
- **Rank 2:** +25% Turret Critical Chance
- **Rank 3:** +33.3% Turret Critical Chance
- **Rank 4:** +41.7% Turret Critical Chance
- **Rank 5:** +50% Turret Critical Chance

## Preparation
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 2 | **Compatible:** WARFRAME | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 10

### Acquisition
Obtainable from the Arbitrations vendor in the Arbiters of Hexis room in Relays for 30 Vitus Essence.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +9% Maximum Energy is filled on Spawn
- **Rank 1:** +18% Maximum Energy is filled on Spawn
- **Rank 2:** +27% Maximum Energy is filled on Spawn
- **Rank 3:** +36% Maximum Energy is filled on Spawn
- **Rank 4:** +45% Maximum Energy is filled on Spawn
- **Rank 5:** +55% Maximum Energy is filled on Spawn
- **Rank 6:** +64% Maximum Energy is filled on Spawn
- **Rank 7:** +73% Maximum Energy is filled on Spawn
- **Rank 8:** +82% Maximum Energy is filled on Spawn
- **Rank 9:** +91% Maximum Energy is filled on Spawn
- **Rank 10:** +100% Maximum Energy is filled on Spawn

### Wiki
Preparation is an Exilus mod for Warframes that increases the amount of energy filled upon spawning into a mission.
**Notes:**
The bonus only applies to the Starting Energy of a Warframe when entering a mission and respawning/reviving from death.
Starting Energy Initial Energy + (5 Free Mod Capacity) + (Total Energy Mod Multiplier)
Example: A rank 30 Wisp equipped with Primed Flow has a Total Energy pool of 850 and a preset Initial Energy of 150. Let's say she has 9 Free (or available) Mod Capacity. When equipped with a rank 4 Preparation mod (+45%, or x0.45), the Warframe will have 150 + (5 * 9) + (850 * 0.45) Starting Energy.
Example 2: The same setup but with a rank 8 Preparation mod (+82%, or x0.82), the Warframe will have 150 + (5 * 9) + (850 * 0.82) Starting Energy. In this example, the surplus is effectively lost as it surpasses the 850 Max Energy cap.
As per the second example, higher ranks of this mod are redundant.
Between rank 4 and rank 6 of this mod, the increase seems to be around 9.5% instead of the linear 9% from other ranks. This makes a rank 5 Preparation actually grant a 54.5% bonus from Total Energy.
This is because the max effect of this mod (100% of energy capacity filled) is not evenly divisible by 11 mod ranks. Mods do not display values in the decimal places.
As Harrow already spawns into missions with 100% starting energy because of his Passive, he has no need for this mod.
Triggers on every entry into Sevagoth's Shadow.
Does not work on Arenas (Index, Rathuum), not even when re-spawning after death.
Falling out of bounds and reappearing does not count as spawning for the purposes of this mod.
If equipped on your Warframe the mod will apply to your Archwing's initial energy during Archwing missions.
**Tips:**
It's recommended at lower-than-max rank on Warframes with high Energy capacity and average Starting Energy.

### Wiki
Pressure Point increases damage on melee weapons by 20% per rank, at a maximum of 120% at rank 5.
**Notes:**
Affects base damage which is applied before every other damage type. Therefore, every other damage increasing mod is benefiting from Pressure Point as well.
Affects damage dealt by regular attacks, slide attacks, wall attacks and jump attacks (not to be confused with the AoE damage that is dealt simultaneously with a jump attack which is considered slam damage).
Stacks with Spoiled Strike for +220% attack damage.
**Tips:**
Pressure Point is very useful for making weapons with low damage but high attack rates more useful.
This mod also makes weapons, such as the Amphis, compare more with other heavy weapons, like the Fragor, in respect to damage.
**Trivia:**
As of Update 9, this mod was buffed from 7% increase per rank to 20% per rank.
Pressure Point is the first mod to receive two different upgrades variants, namely Primed Pressure Point and Sacrificial Pressure.

## Pressurized Magazine
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** madurai | **Drain:** 4 | **Compatible:** Pistol | **Max Rank:** 5

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** On Reload: +15% Fire Rate when Aiming for 2s
- **Rank 1:** On Reload: +30% Fire Rate when Aiming for 3s
- **Rank 2:** On Reload: +45% Fire Rate when Aiming for 5s
- **Rank 3:** On Reload: +60% Fire Rate when Aiming for 6s
- **Rank 4:** On Reload: +75% Fire Rate when Aiming for 8s
- **Rank 5:** On Reload: +90% Fire Rate when Aiming for 9s

### Drop Locations
- Duviri/Endless: Tier 6 (Normal) — Rare (1.03%)
- Duviri/Endless: Tier 4 (Normal) — Rare (1.49%)
- Deimos/Cambion Drift (Level 15 - 25 Cambion Drift Bounty), Rotation C — Uncommon (22.73%)
- Deimos/Cambion Drift (Level 15 - 25 Cambion Drift Bounty), Rotation C — Common (40.00%)

### Wiki
Pressurized Magazine is a rare Pistol mod that increases fire rate while aiming for a brief time upon reloading.
**Notes:**
If activated, Pressurized Magazine will display an icon timer beside the player's health UI, and the Warframe will be briefly surrounded by a flashing energy ribbon.
The Fire Rate bonus is granted upon the end of the reload animation.
On event launch, Pressurized Magazine's duration was not only affected by rank, but also by the amount of Fusion energy in each rank.
Can be combined with Gunslinger, Lethal Torrent, Anemic Agility and Arcane Velocity for a total of +432% fire rate while aiming. This used on the Twin Grakatas will result in a fire rate of almost 173 rounds per second, making it possible to empty its entire 120-round magazine in 0.35 seconds.
**Tips:**
The fire rate buff is activated on the animation start which can be interrupted with a quick melee, meaning the buff can be activated almost on demand for high magazine/low fire rate weapons.

## Primal Rage
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Wukong | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired by attaining the rank of Maxim under the Arbiters of Hexis, or the rank of Flawless under New Loka, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Primal Fury Augment: Killing an enemy increases Critical Chance by 8%. The increase decays by 1%/s
- **Rank 1:** Primal Fury Augment: Killing an enemy increases Critical Chance by 10%. The increase decays by 1%/s
- **Rank 2:** Primal Fury Augment: Killing an enemy increases Critical Chance by 12%. The increase decays by 1%/s
- **Rank 3:** Primal Fury Augment: Killing an enemy increases Critical Chance by 15%. The increase decays by 1%/s

### Drop Locations
- Arbiters of Hexis, Maxim — Common (100.00%)
- New Loka, Flawless — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Primal Rage is a Warframe Augment Mod for Wukong that increases the critical chance of Primal Fury with every kill, up to a maximum of 150%. The additional critical chance gradually falls off after Wukong stops killing for 5 seconds.
**Notes:**
Primal Rage has a bonus cap of +150% critical chance. At full stacks Iron Staff's critical chance will be 25% * (1+150%).
This Augment only provides bonus critical chance to Iron Staff.
The critical chance bonus is additive with critical chance mods.
For example, with a max-ranked True Steel and Primal Rage's bonus at 150%, Iron Staff will have:Base Critical Chance (1 + Critical Chance Mods + Primal Fury)3 critical chance
Critical chance per kill is affected by Ability Strength while the decay rate is affected by Ability Duration.
The maximum bonus critical chance cap of 150% is not affected by mods.
For example, with a max-ranked Intensify and Primal Rage, Iron Staff will gain:Base Critical Chance per Kill (1 + Strength Mods) 15% (1 + 0.3) critical chance per kill
With a max-ranked Continuity, the critical chance decay rate becomes:Base Decay Rate (1 + Duration Mods) 4/sec
Wukong retains Primal Rage's effects as long as Primal Fury remains active, and the bonus will reset if Primal Fury is deactivated by any means.
Primal Rage's current percentages are displayed beside Wukong's shield and health indicators.

## Primary Acuity
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** naramon | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Rifle | **Max Rank:** 10

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +32% Weak Point Damage, +32% Weak Point Critical Chance. Multishot cannot be modified.
- **Rank 1:** +64% Weak Point Damage, +64% Weak Point Critical Chance. Multishot cannot be modified.
- **Rank 2:** +95% Weak Point Damage, +95% Weak Point Critical Chance. Multishot cannot be modified.
- **Rank 3:** +127% Weak Point Damage, +127% Weak Point Critical Chance. Multishot cannot be modified.
- **Rank 4:** +159% Weak Point Damage, +159% Weak Point Critical Chance. Multishot cannot be modified.
- **Rank 5:** +191% Weak Point Damage, +191% Weak Point Critical Chance. Multishot cannot be modified.
- **Rank 6:** +223% Weak Point Damage, +223% Weak Point Critical Chance. Multishot cannot be modified.
- **Rank 7:** +254% Weak Point Damage, +254% Weak Point Critical Chance. Multishot cannot be modified.
- **Rank 8:** +286% Weak Point Damage, +286% Weak Point Critical Chance. Multishot cannot be modified.
- **Rank 9:** +318% Weak Point Damage, +318% Weak Point Critical Chance. Multishot cannot be modified.
- **Rank 10:** +350% Weak Point Damage, +350% Weak Point Critical Chance. Multishot cannot be modified.

### Drop Locations
- Scaldra Jaeger — Rare (0.10%)
- Scaldra Dedicant — Rare (0.19%)

### Wiki
Primary Acuity is a mod that increases the Weak Point Damage and Weak Point Critical Chance of rifles, but prevents Multishot from being modified.
**Notes:**
Equipping this mod will set weapon's Multishot to its default ignoring other bonuses, even negative effects (e.g. Rifle Riven Mod).
The Weak Point Damage is additive with the Weak Point Multiplier on enemies.
The values described on the mod are misleading, the actual damage bonus being 1.5x of the listed value.
For example, a typical humanoid enemy such as a Butcher has a 3x Weak Point Multiplier on its head. A rank 10 Acuity will increase this value to 3 + (3.5 * 1.5) = 8.25x
This is a relative damage increase of +175%
MOA's backpack is one exception to this, accurately adding the listed damage bonus to its Weak Point Multiplier.
Note that damage increase may vary between different types of enemies, since not all enemies will receive "Headcrit" damage, for example MOA and Lumbering Fragment.
Weapons with Area of Effect damage or other special projectiles that inherently do not deal increased weakpoint damage will not gain increased damage from this mod.
The Weak Point Damage is multiplicative with bonuses to Headshot Multiplier, such as from Primary Deadhead.
For example, equipping both Primary Acuity and Primary Deadhead and hitting a 3x weakpoint yields a 1.3*(3+(3.5*1.5))=10.725x multiplier.
The bonus Critical Chance stacks additively with other mods like Point Strike.
Weakspots created by Banshee's Sonar and Citrine's Crystallize do not count as weak points.

**Notes:**

## Primed Ammo Stock
**Rarity:** Legendary | **Polarity:** naramon | **Drain:** 2 | **Compatible:** Shotgun | **Prime** | **Max Rank:** 10

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +10% Magazine Capacity
- **Rank 1:** +20% Magazine Capacity
- **Rank 2:** +30% Magazine Capacity
- **Rank 3:** +40% Magazine Capacity
- **Rank 4:** +50% Magazine Capacity
- **Rank 5:** +60% Magazine Capacity
- **Rank 6:** +70% Magazine Capacity
- **Rank 7:** +80% Magazine Capacity
- **Rank 8:** +90% Magazine Capacity
- **Rank 9:** +100% Magazine Capacity
- **Rank 10:** +110% Magazine Capacity

### Wiki
Primed Ammo Stock is the Primed version of Ammo Stock, increasing magazine capacity for shotguns.
**Tips:**
Works particularly well with the Kuva Hek, as it grants the gun an additional four rounds of ammunition and allows a quick succession of its heavy burst alt-fire shots.
Works also well with the Tigris shotgun series, allowing a quick fire of four rounds within a second and increasing overall DPS due to its fairly quick reload speed.
**Trivia:**
Primed Ammo Stock was an unintentionally released and briefly replaced all standard copies of Ammo Stock between Update 16.4 and Update 16.4.1, however aside from its Legendary rarity, the two mods were otherwise identical.
When paired with Burdened Magazine, this mod allows the Exergis to reach a magazine size of 3 without use of Riven Mods.

## Primed Chamber
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** madurai | **Drain:** 4 | **Compatible:** Sniper | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod can be purchased unranked from Baro Ki'Teer for 2,995 and 1,000,000. Note however that Baro Ki'Teer's stock changes with each appearance, and may not have this item available every time.
Historical
This was originally distributed to the top 100 ranked players in the Informant Event, based on the number of Informer kills.
For a short time after Update 10.6 it could have been acquired via transmutation. This was due to a bug where all mods (including unreleased ones) became obtainable through transmutation. This has since been fixed, but players who obtained the mod this way were allowed to keep them.
During Devstream 17, 18, 43, 49, 59, 94, 107, 112 and 116 one lucky winner was given a Primed Chamber mod through random selection. Two winners were randomly selected during Devstream 121.
A single Primed Chamber was given out as the first place reward for the Mirror the Mod Contest.
A Primed Chamber was given out upon reaching $4,000.00 during the WarframeFanChannels Extra-Life event in February 2017.
The option of being given either a Primed Chamber or a Legendary Core was given by [DE]Sheldon at TennoCon 2017 to five people who caught MTG cards he threw out into the audience. He later signed these cards as proof of the prize.
A Primed Chamber was given to an attendee of the TennoVIP 2018 in Cologne, Germany. The winner was selected randomly from a list of attendees by [DE]Rebecca and [DE]Megan.
Two Primed Chambers were given out during QuietteShy's Twitch stream via random selection on January 16, 2019.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +25% Damage on first shot in Magazine
- **Rank 1:** +50% Damage on first shot in Magazine
- **Rank 2:** +75% Damage on first shot in Magazine
- **Rank 3:** +100% Damage on first shot in Magazine

### Wiki
Primed Chamber is a sniper rifle mod that increases the damage from the first shot fired from a fresh magazine.

## Primed Charged Shell
**Rarity:** Legendary | **Polarity:** naramon | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Shotgun | **Prime** | **Max Rank:** 10

### Acquisition
This mod can be purchased unranked from the Void Trader Baro Ki'Teer for 200,000 and 350. Note however that Baro Ki'Teer's stock changes with each appearance, and may not have this item available at every time.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +15% Electricity
- **Rank 1:** +30% Electricity
- **Rank 2:** +45% Electricity
- **Rank 3:** +60% Electricity
- **Rank 4:** +75% Electricity
- **Rank 5:** +90% Electricity
- **Rank 6:** +105% Electricity
- **Rank 7:** +120% Electricity
- **Rank 8:** +135% Electricity
- **Rank 9:** +150% Electricity
- **Rank 10:** +165% Electricity

### Wiki
Primed Charged Shell is the Primed version of the Charged Shell mod, which adds Electricity damage to shotguns.
**Notes:**
Primed Charged Shell is essentially the Charged Shell mod with Legendary rarity and 5 more ranks. It is considered the same mod as its Uncommon counterpart, and thus both mods cannot be equipped together.
Combining Primed Charged Shell with Primed Chilling Grasp - another primed elemental damage mod for shotguns - grants the guns very impressive damage outputs, but at the expense of far greater mod capacity requirements.

## Primed Chilling Grasp
**Rarity:** Legendary | **Polarity:** vazarin | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Shotgun | **Prime** | **Max Rank:** 10

### Acquisition
This mod can be purchased unranked from the Void Trader Baro Ki'Teer for 125,000 and 350. Note however that Baro Ki'Teer's stock changes with each appearance, and may not have this item available at every time.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +15% Cold
- **Rank 1:** +30% Cold
- **Rank 2:** +45% Cold
- **Rank 3:** +60% Cold
- **Rank 4:** +75% Cold
- **Rank 5:** +90% Cold
- **Rank 6:** +105% Cold
- **Rank 7:** +120% Cold
- **Rank 8:** +135% Cold
- **Rank 9:** +150% Cold
- **Rank 10:** +165% Cold

### Wiki
Primed Chilling Grasp is the Primed version of the Chilling Grasp mod, which adds Cold damage to shotguns.
**Notes:**
Primed Chilling Grasp is essentially the Chilling Grasp mod with Legendary rarity and 5 more ranks. It is considered the same mod as its Uncommon counterpart, and thus both mods cannot be equipped together.
**Trivia:**
Primed Chilling Grasp is the first primed elemental mod to break exclusivity of one primed elemental mod for each weapon category, in this case with Primed Charged Shell.
Combining it with Primed Charged Shell grants shotguns very impressive damage outputs, but at the expense of far greater mod capacity requirements.

## Primed Cleanse Corpus
**Rarity:** Legendary | **Polarity:** madurai | **Drain:** 4 | **Compatible:** Shotgun | **Prime** | **Max Rank:** 10

### Acquisition
This mod can be purchased unranked from Baro Ki'Teer for 140,000 and 350. Note however that Baro Ki'Teer's stock changes with each appearance, and may not have this item available at every time.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** x1.05 Damage to Corpus
- **Rank 1:** x1.1 Damage to Corpus
- **Rank 2:** x1.15 Damage to Corpus
- **Rank 3:** x1.2 Damage to Corpus
- **Rank 4:** x1.25 Damage to Corpus
- **Rank 5:** x1.3 Damage to Corpus
- **Rank 6:** x1.35 Damage to Corpus
- **Rank 7:** x1.4 Damage to Corpus
- **Rank 8:** x1.45 Damage to Corpus
- **Rank 9:** x1.5 Damage to Corpus
- **Rank 10:** x1.55 Damage to Corpus

### Wiki
Primed Cleanse Corpus is the Primed counterpart of Cleanse Corpus which increases the Faction Damage of shotguns against Corpus.
**Notes:**
Has no effect on Corrupted and Narmer Corpus (e.g. Corrupted Crewman, Narmer Crewman).
Despite being listed as a multiplier, it will stack additively with other sources of Damage to Corpus.
For example, if you had a Riven Mod with x0.5 Damage to Corpus and a max rank Primed Cleanse Corpus with x1.55 Damage to Corpus, you would have a 1 - 0.5 + 0.55 = x1.05 multiplier for damage against Corpus with both mods equipped.

### Wiki

For example, combining Rhino's Roar (+50% faction damage bonus to all factions) and a max rank Primed Cleanse Orokin with x1.55 Damage to Corrupted grants a 1 + 0.5 + 0.55 = x2.05 multiplier for damage against Corrupted.

- **Rank 0:** On Reload From Empty: +20% Damage for 2s
- **Rank 1:** On Reload From Empty: +40% Damage for 3s
- **Rank 2:** On Reload From Empty: +60% Damage for 5s
- **Rank 3:** On Reload From Empty: +80% Damage for 6s
- **Rank 4:** On Reload From Empty: +100% Damage for 8s
- **Rank 5:** On Reload From Empty: +120% Damage for 9s
- **Rank 6:** On Reload From Empty: +140% Damage for 11s
- **Rank 7:** On Reload From Empty: +160% Damage for 12s

### Wiki

**Notes:**
Stacks additively with other base damage bonuses such as Rubedo-Lined Barrel.
This mod has a buff icon which appears next to the player's health and shield display to indicate it is in effect.
As Archguns only reload when used on foot with a Gravimag, this mod does not trigger in space combat or when driving a Necramech.

## Primed Expel The Murmur
**Rarity:** Legendary | **Polarity:** madurai | **Drain:** 4 | **Compatible:** Pistol | **Prime** | **Max Rank:** 10

### Acquisition
This mod can be purchased unranked from Baro Ki'Teer for 130,000 and 375. Note however that Baro Ki'Teer's stock changes with each appearance, and may not have this item available at every time.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** x1.05 Damage to Murmur
- **Rank 1:** x1.1 Damage to Murmur
- **Rank 2:** x1.15 Damage to Murmur
- **Rank 3:** x1.2 Damage to Murmur
- **Rank 4:** x1.25 Damage to Murmur
- **Rank 5:** x1.3 Damage to Murmur
- **Rank 6:** x1.35 Damage to Murmur
- **Rank 7:** x1.4 Damage to Murmur
- **Rank 8:** x1.45 Damage to Murmur
- **Rank 9:** x1.5 Damage to Murmur
- **Rank 10:** x1.55 Damage to Murmur

### Wiki
Primed Expel The Murmur increases the Primed counterpart of Expel The Murmur which increases Faction Damage of pistols against The Murmur.
**Notes:**
This mod currently cannot be linked in the in-game chat at the time of this note. Hotfix 39.0.7

- **Rank 6:** +105% Shield Recharge, -35% Shield Recharge Delay
- **Rank 7:** +120% Shield Recharge, -40% Shield Recharge Delay
- **Rank 8:** +135% Shield Recharge, -45% Shield Recharge Delay

## Primed Fast Hands
**Rarity:** Legendary | **Polarity:** naramon | **Drain:** 2 | **Compatible:** Rifle | **Prime** | **Max Rank:** 10

### Acquisition
This mod can be purchased unranked from the Void Trader Baro Ki'Teer for 120,000 and 375. Note that Baro Ki'Teer's stock changes with each visit, and he will not have this mod available on every appearance.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +5% Reload Speed
- **Rank 1:** +10% Reload Speed
- **Rank 2:** +15% Reload Speed
- **Rank 3:** +20% Reload Speed
- **Rank 4:** +25% Reload Speed
- **Rank 5:** +30% Reload Speed
- **Rank 6:** +35% Reload Speed
- **Rank 7:** +40% Reload Speed
- **Rank 8:** +45% Reload Speed
- **Rank 9:** +50% Reload Speed
- **Rank 10:** +55% Reload Speed

### Wiki
Primed Fast Hands is the Primed version of the Fast Hands mod, which increases the reload speed of rifles.
**Notes:**
Primed Fast Hands is essentially the Fast Hands mod with Legendary rarity and 5 more ranks. It is considered the same mod as its Common counterpart, and thus both mods cannot be equipped together.
As with its common version, this mod can be used on Rifles, Launchers, Bows and Sniper Rifles.
The calculation for reload time using reload speed is Base Reload Time (1 + Reload Speed Bonus).
At max rank, this mod will give a ~35.5% reduction to reload times.
**Trivia:**
Prior to Update 16.7, this mod was usable in PvP games until it was replaced by its own specific PvP mod along with a number of other mods.

## Primed Fulmination
Improves the Blast Radius of weapons with Radial Attacks.
**Rarity:** Legendary | **Polarity:** madurai | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Pistol | **Prime** | **Max Rank:** 10

### Acquisition
This mod can be purchased unranked from the Void Trader Baro Ki'Teer for 175,000 and 350. Note however that Baro Ki'Teer's stock changes with each appearance, and may not have this item available at every time.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +4% Blast Range
- **Rank 1:** +8% Blast Range
- **Rank 2:** +12% Blast Range
- **Rank 3:** +16% Blast Range
- **Rank 4:** +20% Blast Range
- **Rank 5:** +24% Blast Range
- **Rank 6:** +28% Blast Range
- **Rank 7:** +32% Blast Range
- **Rank 8:** +36% Blast Range
- **Rank 9:** +40% Blast Range
- **Rank 10:** +44% Blast Range

### Wiki
Primed Fulmination is the Primed version of Fulmination, a secondary mod that increases the blast radius of weapons with radial attacks.
**Notes:**
While it can be installed on any secondary weapon, Primed Fulmination only has noticeable effects on the following explosive weaponsAegrit
Akarius (Akarius Prime)
Angstrum (Prisma Angstrum)
Incarnon mode of the Atomos
Balefire Charger
Castanas (Sancti Castanas)
Catabolyst
Cyanex
Incarnon mode of the Despair
Epitaph (Epitaph Prime)
Kompressa
Kulstar
Kuva Seer
Incarnon mode of the Laetum
Pox
Sepulcrum
Sonicor
Sporelacer
Staticor
Stug
Tenet Spirex
Thrown weapons equipped with Concealed Explosives
Tombfinger
Talons
Zakti (Zakti Prime)
Zymos
An increase of 44% in blast radius adds up to a (100% + 44%) ^ 2 - 100% increase in the total blast area and a (100% + 44%) ^ 3 - 100% increase in total blast volume.
Due to the range increase, this mod lessens the damage falloff from the epicenter of the explosion.
This mod increases the range of self-stagger.

## Primed Fury
**Rarity:** Legendary | **Polarity:** madurai | **Drain:** 4 | **Compatible:** Melee | **Prime** | **Max Rank:** 10

### Acquisition
This mod is exclusive to the Daily Tribute system. It will become available at day 200, 400, 600 and 900, until chosen as the Milestone reward, among Primed Vigor, Primed Shred, and (day 400+) Primed Sure Footed.
As of Update Lunaro 4, this mod cannot be traded between players, primarily to prevent farming Daily Tribute-rewarded mods by creating multiple accounts.
Cannot be equipped with Berserker Fury.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +5% Attack Speed
- **Rank 1:** +10% Attack Speed
- **Rank 2:** +15% Attack Speed
- **Rank 3:** +20% Attack Speed
- **Rank 4:** +25% Attack Speed
- **Rank 5:** +30% Attack Speed
- **Rank 6:** +35% Attack Speed
- **Rank 7:** +40% Attack Speed
- **Rank 8:** +45% Attack Speed
- **Rank 9:** +50% Attack Speed
- **Rank 10:** +55% Attack Speed

### Wiki
Primed Fury is the Primed version of Fury, which increases the attack speed of a melee weapon.
