## Primed Heavy Trauma
**Rarity:** Legendary | **Polarity:** naramon | **Drain:** 2 | **Compatible:** Melee | **Prime** | **Max Rank:** 10

### Acquisition
This mod can be purchased unranked from the Void Trader Baro Ki'Teer for 100,000 and 350. Note however that Baro Ki'Teer's stock changes with each appearance, and may not have this item available at every time.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +15% Impact
- **Rank 1:** +30% Impact
- **Rank 2:** +45% Impact
- **Rank 3:** +60% Impact
- **Rank 4:** +75% Impact
- **Rank 5:** +90% Impact
- **Rank 6:** +105% Impact
- **Rank 7:** +120% Impact
- **Rank 8:** +135% Impact
- **Rank 9:** +150% Impact
- **Rank 10:** +165% Impact

### Wiki
Primed Heavy Trauma is the Primed version of the Heavy Trauma mod, which increases the Impact damage of melee weapons.
**Notes:**
Primed Heavy Trauma is essentially the Heavy Trauma mod with Legendary rarity and 5 more ranks. It is considered the same mod as its Rare counterpart, and thus both mods cannot be equipped together.
Can be combined with Collision Force to provide a 285% total increase to Impact damage.
Compared to a base elemental mod (+90%) in terms of raw damage, Primed Heavy Trauma becomes more effective at rank 9 when a weapon's Impact damage exceeds 60% of total weapon damage. This does not take damage type multipliers into consideration.
Compared to Primed Pressure Point, they share the same % bonus, but Primed Heavy Trauma uses 12 mod capacity instead of 14, making it more cost efficient only if more than 85.7% of a weapon's base damage is Impact damage.
Bo Prime, Mk1-Bo, Bo, Shaku, Cobra & Crane, and Ruvox meet this requirement.
Atlas' Landslide and Baruuk's Serene Storm (without Reactive Storm augment) benefit from this mod. Valkyr's Hysteria benefits more from Primed Pressure Point.

## Primed Magazine Warp
**Rarity:** Legendary | **Polarity:** naramon | **Drain:** 4 | **Compatible:** Rifle | **Prime** | **Max Rank:** 10

### Acquisition
This mod can be purchased unranked from Baro Ki'Teer for 200,000 and 280. Note however that Baro Ki'Teer's stock changes with each appearance, and may not have this item available every time.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +5% Magazine Capacity
- **Rank 1:** +10% Magazine Capacity
- **Rank 2:** +15% Magazine Capacity
- **Rank 3:** +20% Magazine Capacity
- **Rank 4:** +25% Magazine Capacity
- **Rank 5:** +30% Magazine Capacity
- **Rank 6:** +35% Magazine Capacity
- **Rank 7:** +40% Magazine Capacity
- **Rank 8:** +45% Magazine Capacity
- **Rank 9:** +50% Magazine Capacity
- **Rank 10:** +55% Magazine Capacity

### Wiki
Primed Magazine Warp is the primed version of Magazine Warp, increasing the magazine capacity of rifles.
**Notes:**
For any non-integer magazine values the magazine size is simply rounded to the nearest whole number. For example, a Braton modded with a max rank will have a calculated magazine size of 45 (1 + 55%), which will simply round to 45 (1 + 55%).
A weapon with only 1 base magazine capacity (such as Vectis and Tonkor (Kuva Tonkor)) will have 1 (1 + 55%), rounded to 2 magazine capacity.
Stacks with Tainted Mag (+66%) and Wildfire (+20%) for a total of 141% magazine capacity.

## Primed Pack Leader
Heal your Companion with Melee hits. Excess healing grants Overguard.
**Rarity:** Legendary | **Polarity:** madurai | **Drain:** 4 | **Compatible:** COMPANION | **Prime** | **Max Rank:** 10

### Acquisition
This mod can be purchased unranked from the Void Trader Baro Ki'Teer for 200,000 and 300. Note however that Baro Ki'Teer's stock changes with each appearance, and may not have this item available at every time.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +17 Health per hit, +200 Overguard Max
- **Rank 1:** +33 Health per hit, +400 Overguard Max
- **Rank 2:** +50 Health per hit, +600 Overguard Max
- **Rank 3:** +67 Health per hit, +800 Overguard Max
- **Rank 4:** +83 Health per hit, +1,000 Overguard Max
- **Rank 5:** +100 Health per hit, +1,200 Overguard Max
- **Rank 6:** +117 Health per hit, +1,400 Overguard Max
- **Rank 7:** +133 Health per hit, +1,600 Overguard Max
- **Rank 8:** +150 Health per hit, +1,800 Overguard Max
- **Rank 9:** +167 Health per hit, +2,000 Overguard Max
- **Rank 10:** +183 Health per hit, +2,200 Overguard Max

### Wiki
Primed Pack Leader is the Primed variant of Pack Leader that heals a Companion a set amount with Warframe Melee hits. Excess healing grants Overguard.
**Notes:**
Cannot be equipped on Sentinels.
Will heal Companions on all melee attacks, including Finishers, Stealth Attacks, and staggers via melee blocking. Thus, Warframes who can easily leave enemies open to finishers can make excellent use of this mod.
Notably, each individual hit of Parazon finisher attacks can trigger the effects of this mod. for example, a 'backstab' finisher on a grineer lancer triggers the effect twice, while a finisher on an ancient healer triggers it four times.
The mod's effects will also work with Exalted Weapons.
Khora's Whipclaw can trigger this mod's effect.
Atlas's Landslide can trigger this mod's effect.
**Trivia:**
This mod was accidentally released early on consoles. After discovering the mistake, DE hot-fixed the PC version of the game to add Primed Pack Leader to Baro Ki'Teer's offerings for the week (since the mod had not yet been released on PC).
This makes Primed Pack Leader one of very few pieces of content that was made available to consoles before PC.

## Primed Pistol Ammo Mutation
**Rarity:** Legendary | **Polarity:** naramon | **Drain:** 0 | **Compatible:** Pistol | **Prime** | **Max Rank:** 10

### Acquisition
This mod can be purchased unranked from the Void Trader Baro Ki'Teer for 140,000 and 400. Note however that Baro Ki'Teer's stock changes with each appearance, and may not have this item available at every time.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Converts Primary ammo pickups to 8% of Ammo Pick Up.
- **Rank 1:** Converts Primary ammo pickups to 16% of Ammo Pick Up.
- **Rank 2:** Converts Primary ammo pickups to 25% of Ammo Pick Up.
- **Rank 3:** Converts Primary ammo pickups to 33% of Ammo Pick Up.
- **Rank 4:** Converts Primary ammo pickups to 42% of Ammo Pick Up.
- **Rank 5:** Converts Primary ammo pickups to 50% of Ammo Pick Up.
- **Rank 6:** Converts Primary ammo pickups to 58% of Ammo Pick Up.
- **Rank 7:** Converts Primary ammo pickups to 67% of Ammo Pick Up.
- **Rank 8:** Converts Primary ammo pickups to 75% of Ammo Pick Up.
- **Rank 9:** Converts Primary ammo pickups to 84% of Ammo Pick Up.
- **Rank 10:** Converts Primary ammo pickups to 92% of Ammo Pick Up.

### Wiki
Primed Pistol Ammo Mutation is the Primed version of the Pistol Ammo Mutation mod for secondaries, which converts any unused Primary Ammo into Secondary Ammo. The affected pistol will only convert ammo while equipped, and will not convert any ammo type used by holstered primary weapons until their own ammo is full.
Converted ammo is denoted by a Mutate symbol. The final amount of ammo restored is rounded to the nearest integer.
**Tips:**
Highly recommended for ammo inefficient weapons such as the Dex Furis or Twin Vipers Wraith.
Functions well with Robotic companion's Vacuum, Chesa Kubrow's Retrieve, and/or Beast companion's Fetch, which automatically collect unused ammo as needed.

## Primed Pistol Gambit
**Rarity:** Legendary | **Polarity:** madurai | **Drain:** 2 | **Compatible:** Pistol | **Prime** | **Max Rank:** 10

### Acquisition
This mod can be purchased unranked from the Void Trader Baro Ki'Teer for 220,000 and 400. Note however that Baro Ki'Teer's stock changes with each appearance, and may not have this item available at every time.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +17% Critical Chance
- **Rank 1:** +34% Critical Chance
- **Rank 2:** +51% Critical Chance
- **Rank 3:** +68% Critical Chance
- **Rank 4:** +85% Critical Chance
- **Rank 5:** +102% Critical Chance
- **Rank 6:** +119% Critical Chance
- **Rank 7:** +136% Critical Chance
- **Rank 8:** +153% Critical Chance
- **Rank 9:** +170% Critical Chance
- **Rank 10:** +187% Critical Chance

### Wiki
Primed Pistol Gambit is the primed version of the Pistol Gambit mod, which increases the critical chance of secondary weapons.
**Notes:**
is additive with similar mods. For example, a Sicarus with a max rank and Hydraulic Crosshairs will have a 16% (1 + 187% + 135%) critical chance.
Cannot be equipped with Creeping Bullseye.
Unlike most Primed mods, Primed Pistol Gambit offers less critical chance per rank than its normal version, giving only 17% per rank compared to Pistol Gambit's 20%. However, Primed Pistol Gambit has a higher maximum rank along with a lower overall mod cost for similar benefits.
At the cost of needing more Endo in order to upgrade this mod, Primed Pistol Gambit boasts a better critical chance to drain ratio.
For example, at base rank, Pistol Gambit supplies 20% critical chance for 4 drain while Primed Pistol Gambit at rank 2 grants 51% critical chance at the same 4 drain.
Had Primed Pistol Gambit followed Pistol Gambit's format, it would have had 220% critical chance at the cost of 14 drain.
**Tips:**
Can be combined with Hydraulic Crosshairs for a total increase of +322% after getting a shot to an enemy's head. This allows the Akmagnus, Lex Prime, Spira, and Twin Grakatas to gain a critical chance of 105.5%, guaranteeing crits.

## Primed Pressure Point
**Rarity:** Legendary | **Polarity:** madurai | **Drain:** 4 | **Compatible:** Melee | **Prime** | **Max Rank:** 10

### Acquisition
This mod can be purchased unranked from Baro Ki'Teer for 300,000 and 385. Note however that Baro Ki'Teer's stock changes with each appearance, and may not have this item available at every time.
Primed Pressure Point is considered to be of the same type as the Pressure Point and Sacrificial Pressure, and none of these mods can be equipped together.
This mod has less damage increase per rank than its Common counterpart, making it less effective up to and including rank 7 because of higher capacity cost. For comparison, Pressure Point increases damage by 120% at rank 5, while Primed Pressure Point only increases damage by 90% at the same rank and cost.
Even at max rank, Primed Pressure Point is less effective compared to Pressure Point in Damage / Cost, but still remains more effective than Elemental mods, while also boosting them.
The difference in effectiveness is minimized, though not eliminated, if a Madurai-polarized slot is used, because Pressure Point's max rank (9) is an odd number, whereas Primed Pressure Point's max rank (14) is even.
On a weapon modded with no other base damage-increasing mods (e.g. Spoiled Strike), swapping out Pressure Point for Primed Pressure Point represents an increase of 2.65 2.20 1 20.45%.
Primed Pistol Gambit, the only other Primed mod with a reduced effect per rank, also has reduced drain to balance out the lower gain, thus being more effective than its normal counterpart for any given mod cost.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +15% Melee Damage
- **Rank 1:** +30% Melee Damage
- **Rank 2:** +45% Melee Damage
- **Rank 3:** +60% Melee Damage
- **Rank 4:** +75% Melee Damage
- **Rank 5:** +90% Melee Damage
- **Rank 6:** +105% Melee Damage
- **Rank 7:** +120% Melee Damage
- **Rank 8:** +135% Melee Damage
- **Rank 9:** +150% Melee Damage
- **Rank 10:** +165% Melee Damage

### Wiki
Primed Pressure Point is the Primed version of the Pressure Point mod that increases a Melee Weapon's base damage by 15% per rank, to a maximum of 165% at rank 10.
**Trivia:**
During Devstream 76, Scott McGregor revealed that this mod was never meant to be released, but it was added into the game without his knowledge by Sheldon Carter.

## Primed Ravage
**Rarity:** Legendary | **Polarity:** madurai | **Drain:** 4 | **Compatible:** Shotgun | **Prime** | **Max Rank:** 10

### Acquisition
This mod can be purchased unranked from the Void Trader Baro Ki'Teer for 100,000 and 280. Remember however that Baro Ki'Teer's stock changes with each appearance, and may not have this item available at every time.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +10% Critical Damage
- **Rank 1:** +20% Critical Damage
- **Rank 2:** +30% Critical Damage
- **Rank 3:** +40% Critical Damage

- **Rank 5:** +60% Critical Damage

Primed Ravage is essentially the Ravage mod with Legendary rarity and 5 more ranks. It is considered the same mod as its Rare counterpart, and thus both mods cannot be equipped together.
Primed Ravage increases the critical damage output from 60 percent to 110 percent, putting it nearly on-par with its rifle counterpart, Vital Sense.

## Primed Reach
**Rarity:** Legendary | **Polarity:** madurai | **Drain:** 4 | **Compatible:** Melee | **Prime** | **Max Rank:** 10

### Acquisition
Primed Reach can be purchased unranked from the Void Trader Baro Ki'Teer for 220,000 and 300 during his limited tours, if the mod is available during his current stock rotation.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +0.3 Range
- **Rank 1:** +0.5 Range
- **Rank 2:** +0.8 Range
- **Rank 3:** +1.1 Range
- **Rank 4:** +1.4 Range
- **Rank 5:** +1.6 Range
- **Rank 6:** +1.9 Range
- **Rank 7:** +2.2 Range
- **Rank 8:** +2.5 Range
- **Rank 9:** +2.7 Range
- **Rank 10:** +3 Range

### Wiki
Primed Reach is the Primed version of the Reach mod, which extends the attack range of Melee weapons.
**Notes:**
Mods cannot be equipped alongside variants of themselves (e.g. Reach).
Primed Reach does not increase the radius of slam radial attacks.
Primed Reach does not increase the range limit of thrown melee weapons, but instead grants these weapons a larger hitbox.
Primed Reach does not increase the fall-off range for gunblade melee weapons.
**Trivia:**
Primed Reach was the first Primed mod which, before the mod rank restructuring, had a maximum rank of 3 rather than 5 as with other Primed mods at the time.
It was also the first Primed mod that more than doubled the original mod's effect.

## Primed Redirection
**Rarity:** Legendary | **Polarity:** vazarin | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** WARFRAME | **Augment** | **Prime** | **Max Rank:** 10

### Acquisition
This mod can be purchased unranked from Baro Ki'Teer for 225,000 and 350. Note however that Baro Ki'Teer's stock changes with each appearance, and may not have this item available at every time.

### Stats by Rank

- **Rank 1:** +33% Shield Capacity
- **Rank 2:** +49% Shield Capacity
- **Rank 3:** +65% Shield Capacity
- **Rank 4:** +82% Shield Capacity
- **Rank 5:** +98% Shield Capacity
- **Rank 6:** +115% Shield Capacity
- **Rank 7:** +131% Shield Capacity
- **Rank 8:** +147% Shield Capacity
- **Rank 9:** +164% Shield Capacity
- **Rank 10:** +180% Shield Capacity

### Wiki
Primed Redirection is the Primed version of Redirection, which increases the Maximum Shield Capacity of a Warframe.
**Trivia:**
Primed Redirection is the first Primed mod variant of which the base mod (Redirection) can also be ranked up to a maximum of ten, and thus acts as a completely direct upgrade of the original.

## Primed Regen

- **Rank 4:** Sentinel recovery time reduced by 17s. Revives with 10s of invulnerability.
- **Rank 5:** Sentinel recovery time reduced by 20s. Revives with 10s of invulnerability.

## Primed Rifle Ammo Mutation
**Rarity:** Legendary | **Polarity:** naramon | **Drain:** 0 | **Compatible:** Assault Rifle | **Prime** | **Max Rank:** 10

### Acquisition
This mod can be purchased unranked from the Void Trader Baro Ki'Teer for 140,000 and 400. Note however that Baro Ki'Teer's stock changes with each appearance, and may not have this item available at every time.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Converts Secondary ammo pickups to 8% of Ammo Pick Up.
- **Rank 1:** Converts Secondary ammo pickups to 16% of Ammo Pick Up.
- **Rank 2:** Converts Secondary ammo pickups to 25% of Ammo Pick Up.
- **Rank 3:** Converts Secondary ammo pickups to 33% of Ammo Pick Up.
- **Rank 4:** Converts Secondary ammo pickups to 42% of Ammo Pick Up.
- **Rank 5:** Converts Secondary ammo pickups to 50% of Ammo Pick Up.
- **Rank 6:** Converts Secondary ammo pickups to 58% of Ammo Pick Up.
- **Rank 7:** Converts Secondary ammo pickups to 67% of Ammo Pick Up.
- **Rank 8:** Converts Secondary ammo pickups to 75% of Ammo Pick Up.
- **Rank 9:** Converts Secondary ammo pickups to 84% of Ammo Pick Up.
- **Rank 10:** Converts Secondary ammo pickups to 92% of Ammo Pick Up.

### Wiki
Primed Rifle Ammo Mutation is the Primed version of the Rifle Ammo Mutation mod for assault rifles, which converts any unused Secondary Ammo into Primary Ammo. The affected rifle will only convert ammo while equipped, and will not convert any ammo type used by holstered secondary weapons until their own ammo is full.
Converted ammo is denoted by a Mutate symbol. The final amount of ammo restored is rounded to the nearest integer.
**Tips:**
Good for weapons with poor ammo economy, such as the Amprex or Grakata.
Functions well with Robotic companion's Vacuum, Chesa Kubrow's Retrieve, and/or Beast companion's Fetch, which automatically collect unused ammo as needed.

## Primed Shred
**Rarity:** Legendary | **Polarity:** madurai | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Rifle | **Prime** | **Max Rank:** 10

### Acquisition
This mod is exclusive to the Daily Tribute system. It will become available at day 200, 400, 600 and 900, until chosen as the Milestone reward, among Primed Vigor, Primed Fury, and (day 400+) Primed Sure Footed.
This mod first became available on July 24, 2017 UTC.
As with other Daily Tribute rewards, it can not be traded.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +5% Fire Rate (x2 for Bows), +0.2 Punch Through
- **Rank 1:** +10% Fire Rate (x2 for Bows), +0.4 Punch Through
- **Rank 2:** +15% Fire Rate (x2 for Bows), +0.6 Punch Through
- **Rank 3:** +20% Fire Rate (x2 for Bows), +0.8 Punch Through
- **Rank 4:** +25% Fire Rate (x2 for Bows), +1 Punch Through
- **Rank 5:** +30% Fire Rate (x2 for Bows), +1.2 Punch Through
- **Rank 6:** +35% Fire Rate (x2 for Bows), +1.4 Punch Through
- **Rank 7:** +40% Fire Rate (x2 for Bows), +1.6 Punch Through
- **Rank 8:** +45% Fire Rate (x2 for Bows), +1.8 Punch Through
- **Rank 9:** +50% Fire Rate (x2 for Bows), +2 Punch Through
- **Rank 10:** +55% Fire Rate (x2 for Bows), +2.2 Punch Through

### Wiki
Primed Shred is the Primed version of Shred, which increases both fire rate of Primary weapons by 5% and punch through by 0.2 meter per rank for a maximum of 55% and 2.2 meter at Rank 10, respectively.
Data may differ due to width variances from dynamic posing.
**Notes:**
This mod is significantly more powerful than its normal counterpart when stacking a maxed Metal Auger, increasing overall punch-through up to 4.3.
The fire rate boost increases charging speed of chargeable weapons and the drawing speed of Bow class weapons.
Bows receive double the fire rate increase from this mod unlike other primary weapons.
Primed Shred costs slightly more than Metal Auger, but gives a slightly higher punch through bonus along with a fire rate bonus.
In a matching polarity mod slot, both Metal Auger (Naramon) and Primed Shred (Madurai) will cost 8 capacity.
**Trivia:**
This is the second Primed mod to be based on a Nightmare Mod, following Primed Vigor which is also limited to the Daily Tribute system.

## Primed Slip Magazine
**Rarity:** Legendary | **Polarity:** naramon | **Drain:** 4 | **Compatible:** Pistol | **Prime** | **Max Rank:** 10

### Acquisition
This mod can be purchased unranked from the Void Trader Baro Ki'Teer for 200,000 and 280. Note, however, that Baro Ki'Teer's stock changes with each visit, and he may not have this item available next time he appears.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +5% Magazine Capacity
- **Rank 1:** +10% Magazine Capacity
- **Rank 2:** +15% Magazine Capacity
- **Rank 3:** +20% Magazine Capacity
- **Rank 4:** +25% Magazine Capacity
- **Rank 5:** +30% Magazine Capacity
- **Rank 6:** +35% Magazine Capacity
- **Rank 7:** +40% Magazine Capacity
- **Rank 8:** +45% Magazine Capacity
- **Rank 9:** +50% Magazine Capacity
- **Rank 10:** +55% Magazine Capacity

### Wiki
Primed Slip Magazine is the Primed version of the Slip Magazine mod, which increases the magazine capacity of pistols.
**Notes:**
For any non-integer magazine values the magazine size is simply rounded to the nearest whole number. For example, a Furis modded with a max rank will have a calculated magazine size of 35 (1 + 55%), which will simply round to 54.
is essentially the Slip Magazine mod with Legendary rarity and 5 more ranks. It is considered the same mod as its Common counterpart, and thus both mods cannot be equipped together.

## Primed Smite Corpus
**Rarity:** Legendary | **Polarity:** madurai | **Drain:** 4 | **Compatible:** Melee | **Prime** | **Max Rank:** 10

### Acquisition
This mod can be purchased unranked from Baro Ki'Teer for 140,000 and 350. Note however that Baro Ki'Teer's stock changes with each appearance, and may not have this item available at every time.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** x1.05 Damage to Corpus
- **Rank 1:** x1.1 Damage to Corpus
- **Rank 2:** x1.15 Damage to Corpus
- **Rank 3:** x1.2 Damage to Corpus
- **Rank 4:** x1.25 Damage to Corpus
- **Rank 5:** x1.3 Damage to Corpus
- **Rank 6:** x1.35 Damage to Corpus
- **Rank 7:** x1.4 Damage to Corpus
- **Rank 8:** x1.45 Damage to Corpus
- **Rank 9:** x1.5 Damage to Corpus
- **Rank 10:** x1.55 Damage to Corpus

### Wiki
Primed Smite Corpus is the Primed counterpart of Smite Corpus which increases the Faction Damage of melee weapons against Corpus.
**Notes:**
Has no effect on Corrupted and Narmer Corpus (e.g. Corrupted Crewman, Narmer Crewman).
Despite being listed as a multiplier, it will stack additively with other sources of Damage to Corpus.
For example, if you had a Riven Mod with x0.5 Damage to Corpus and a max rank Primed Smite Corpus with x1.55 Damage to Corpus, you would have a 1 - 0.5 + 0.55 = x1.05 multiplier for damage against Corpus with both mods equipped.

## Primed Sniper Ammo Mutation
**Rarity:** Legendary | **Polarity:** naramon | **Drain:** 0 | **Compatible:** Sniper | **Prime** | **Max Rank:** 10

### Acquisition
This mod can be purchased unranked from Baro Ki'Teer for 140,000 and 400. Note however that Baro Ki'Teer's stock changes with each appearance, and may not have this item available at every time.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Converts Secondary ammo pickups to 8% of Ammo Pick Up.
- **Rank 1:** Converts Secondary ammo pickups to 16% of Ammo Pick Up.
- **Rank 2:** Converts Secondary ammo pickups to 25% of Ammo Pick Up.
- **Rank 3:** Converts Secondary ammo pickups to 33% of Ammo Pick Up.
- **Rank 4:** Converts Secondary ammo pickups to 42% of Ammo Pick Up.
- **Rank 5:** Converts Secondary ammo pickups to 50% of Ammo Pick Up.
- **Rank 6:** Converts Secondary ammo pickups to 58% of Ammo Pick Up.
- **Rank 7:** Converts Secondary ammo pickups to 67% of Ammo Pick Up.
- **Rank 8:** Converts Secondary ammo pickups to 75% of Ammo Pick Up.
- **Rank 9:** Converts Secondary ammo pickups to 84% of Ammo Pick Up.
- **Rank 10:** Converts Secondary ammo pickups to 92% of Ammo Pick Up.

### Wiki
Primed Sniper Ammo Mutation is a sniper rifle mod that converts any unused ammo type into Primary Ammo. The affected weapon will only convert ammo while equipped, and will not convert any ammo type used by holstered secondary weapons until their own ammo is full.
Converted ammo is denoted by a Mutate symbol. The final amount of ammo restored is rounded to the nearest integer.
**Tips:**
Functions well with Robotic companion's Vacuum,Chesa Kubrow's Retrieve, and/or Beast companion's Fetch, which automatically collect unused ammo as needed.
Mutated ammo always rounds up, resulting in converted ammo even with Primed Sniper Ammo Mutation being completely unranked, saving all capacity cost. This means the effect of unranked Primed Sniper Ammo Mutation is equivalent to fully ranked on weapons with 1 ammo pickup.
Rifle Scavenger applies to mutated ammo pickups in addition to normal pickups, and counts after ammo mutation's rounding. For example, on Rubico Prime, it increases all ammo pickups from primary and secondary pickups to provide 38 and 35 ammo respectively, with max ranked Primed Sniper Ammo Mutation.

## Primed Steady Hands
**Rarity:** Legendary | **Polarity:** naramon | **Drain:** 2 | **Compatible:** Pistol | **Prime** | **Max Rank:** 10

### Acquisition
This mod can be purchased unranked from the Void Trader Baro Ki'Teer for 220,000 and 300. Note however that Baro Ki'Teer's stock changes with each appearance, and may not have this item available at every time.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** -7.7% Weapon Recoil
- **Rank 1:** -15.5% Weapon Recoil
- **Rank 2:** -23.2% Weapon Recoil
- **Rank 3:** -30.9% Weapon Recoil
- **Rank 4:** -38.6% Weapon Recoil
- **Rank 5:** -46.4% Weapon Recoil
- **Rank 6:** -54.1% Weapon Recoil
- **Rank 7:** -61.8% Weapon Recoil
- **Rank 8:** -69.5% Weapon Recoil
- **Rank 9:** -77.3% Weapon Recoil
- **Rank 10:** -85% Weapon Recoil

### Wiki
Primed Steady Hands is the Primed version of Steady Hands secondary mod that reduces recoil.
**Notes:**
Bonus stacks additively with Secondary Deadhead and Strafing Slide's recoil reduction.
Can add with other sources to go beyond -100% recoil, which can be useful to negate Riven mod effect.
This mod reduces the distance the aiming reticle is shaken when fired, however, it doesn't help increase gun base accuracy.
Affects vertical, horizontal, and other recoil patterns.
**Tips:**
In rough order, the Akjagara, Aklex, Akvasto, Lex, Magnus, Seer, Vasto and Zakti benefit effectively from this mod, especially when increasing their fire rate with mods.
Has no effect on the Spectra, Embolist, or other beam-type weapons, as they have no recoil.

## Primed Sure Footed
**Rarity:** Legendary | **Polarity:** vazarin | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** WARFRAME | **Augment** | **Prime** | **Max Rank:** 10

### Acquisition
This mod is exclusive to the Daily Tribute system. It will become available at day 400, 600 and 900, until chosen as the Milestone reward, among Primed Vigor, Primed Shred, and Primed Fury.
This mod first became available on May 20, 2018 UTC.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +9% Chance to Resist Knockdown
- **Rank 1:** +18% Chance to Resist Knockdown
- **Rank 2:** +27% Chance to Resist Knockdown
- **Rank 3:** +36% Chance to Resist Knockdown
- **Rank 4:** +45% Chance to Resist Knockdown
- **Rank 5:** +55% Chance to Resist Knockdown
- **Rank 6:** +64% Chance to Resist Knockdown
- **Rank 7:** +73% Chance to Resist Knockdown
- **Rank 8:** +82% Chance to Resist Knockdown
- **Rank 9:** +91% Chance to Resist Knockdown
- **Rank 10:** +100% Chance to Resist Knockdown

### Wiki
Primed Sure Footed is the Primed version of Sure Footed, increasing a Warframe's chance to resist knockdowns and staggers.
Knockdown and stagger animations will not be played when a player successfully resists a knockdown or stagger.
**Notes:**
Provides a chance to resist Stagger, Big Stagger, Stun, Ragdoll, pull, and Knockdown effects from:
Player explosive weapons that deal area-of-effect damage such as Lenz and Kuva Bramma.
Knockdown attacks from units such as Shield Lancers, Heavy Gunners, and MOAs.
Pull attacks from units such as Ancients and Scorpions.
Knockdowns from running through Laser Barriers.
Does not provide protection from:
High-damage stagger when using Quick Thinking or Gladiator Finesse.
Momentary recovery needed from a Hard Landing.
**Trivia:**
This is the first Primed mod that can be put in the Exilus slot.
Prior to Update 32.1, the actual knockdown resist chance for the last rank is 99.99% with the mod simply displaying the rounded value of 100%.

**Notes:**
The calculation of reload time using reload speed is: Reload Time Base Reload Time (1 + Reload Speed Bonus)
For example, Tigris' new reload time with a max-ranked Primed Tactical Pump would be 1.8s / (1 + 100%)s

## Prismatic Companion
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Citrine | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired by attaining the rank of General under Steel Meridian, or the rank of Exalted under the Red Veil, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Prismatic Gem Augment: The gem now attaches to your free-moving companion. Passive : +20% Prismatic Gem Duration.
- **Rank 1:** Prismatic Gem Augment: The gem now attaches to your free-moving companion. Passive : +30% Prismatic Gem Duration.
- **Rank 2:** Prismatic Gem Augment: The gem now attaches to your free-moving companion. Passive : +40% Prismatic Gem Duration.
- **Rank 3:** Prismatic Gem Augment: The gem now attaches to your free-moving companion. Passive : +50% Prismatic Gem Duration.

### Wiki
Prismatic Companion is a Warframe Augment Mod for Citrine that attaches Prismatic Gem to a free-moving Companion while also passively increasing its Ability Duration.
**Notes:**
This mod's duration bonus is multiplicative with typical ability duration increases. Functionally, it increases the default duration to 45 seconds.
"Free-moving" refers to Kubrows, Kavats, MOAs, Predasites, Vulpaphyla, and Hounds. The gem will not attach to Sentinels or Vulpaphylas in their larval state (Sly Devolution, Crescent Devolution, or Panzer Devolution), and will instead cast normally.
Diversified Denial will interfere with the augment, negating the activation.
This augment used to trigger Melee Influence in spite of not being a melee trigger related mod.

Health orb debuff stacks additively with Recuperate, Calculated Victory, Recover, Vanquished Prey and Momentary Pause
Using 4 of these mods will make you unable to pickup health orbs.

## Prolonged Paralysis
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Valkyr | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
Can be acquired by attaining the rank of Partner under The Perrin Sequence, or the rank of Flawless under New Loka, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Paralysis Augment: Affected enemies are pulled towards Valkyr and the stun duration is increased by 2s.
- **Rank 1:** Paralysis Augment: Affected enemies are pulled towards Valkyr and the stun duration is increased by 3s.
- **Rank 2:** Paralysis Augment: Affected enemies are pulled towards Valkyr and the stun duration is increased by 4s.
- **Rank 3:** Paralysis Augment: Affected enemies are pulled towards Valkyr and the stun duration is increased by 5s.

### Drop Locations
- New Loka, Flawless — Common (100.00%)
- The Perrin Sequence, Partner — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Prolonged Paralysis is a Warframe Augment Mod for Valkyr that causes Paralysis to pull affected enemies towards her and inflict Knockdown.
**Notes:**
Duration increase is affected by Ability Strength.
The pull strength is affected by Ability Strength.
The base pull range of 10 meters is affected by Ability Range.
The enemy pull effect is gradual and not instantaneous.
Enemies pulled in are vulnerable to grounded melee Finishers.
**Trivia:**
At the time of its release, Prolonged Paralysis left enemies vulnerable to Melee Finishers as opposed to Ground Finishers. This was changed in Update 15.5, likely to prevent players from clipping into map geometry during Finisher animations, which either resulted in getting stuck or falling out of the map (as this augment was introduced prior to the introduction of the "/unstuck" command in Update 16.2). This was reverted in Update 32.2.

## Protect
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** penjaga | **Drain:** 0 | **Compatible:** Raksa Kubrow | **Max Rank:** 5

### Acquisition
Protect is automatically acquired upon obtaining a Raksa Kubrow.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** The kubrow comes to the defense of its master, replenishing both of their shields by 5% of its maximum shields and adding its shield regeneration rate to its master's for 3s.
- **Rank 1:** The kubrow comes to the defense of its master, replenishing both of their shields by 10% of its maximum shields and adding its shield regeneration rate to its master's for 3s.
- **Rank 2:** The kubrow comes to the defense of its master, replenishing both of their shields by 15% of its maximum shields and adding its shield regeneration rate to its master's for 3s.
- **Rank 3:** The kubrow comes to the defense of its master, replenishing both of their shields by 20% of its maximum shields and adding its shield regeneration rate to its master's for 3s.
- **Rank 4:** The kubrow comes to the defense of its master, replenishing both of their shields by 25% of its maximum shields and adding its shield regeneration rate to its master's for 3s.
- **Rank 5:** The kubrow comes to the defense of its master, replenishing both of their shields by 30% of its maximum shields and adding its shield regeneration rate to its master's for 3s.

### Drop Locations
- Corrupted Drahk — Legendary (0.01%)
- Drahk — Legendary (0.01%)
- Kubrow — Legendary (0.01%)
- Kuva Drahk — Legendary (0.01%)
- Tamm — Legendary (0.01%)
- Techrot Scaart — Rare (0.03%)
- Techrot Matmas — Rare (0.09%)
- Techrot Babau — Rare (0.11%)
- Techrot Obsolyte — Rare (0.11%)

### Wiki
Protect is a precept mod for the Raksa Kubrow that comes to its master's defense, replenishing both of their Shields and adding its own shield regeneration to its master's.
**Notes:**
Protect recovers shields, similar to Guardian.
Unlike its Sentinel counterpart, Protect can be activated as long as the shield capacity of the Kubrow's master is not full, as well as replenish the Kubrow's shields.
The following patterns have been observed with max rank Protect after forcing over 70 casts:
Shields replenished from Protect roll over into Overshields.
Cooldown is inconsistent; Kubrow can use Protect twice over 1~2s before entering a 5s cooldown. If used extensively (after ~15 cooldowns in immediate succession), Protect enters a downtime of ~10s before it can activate again.
Kubrow will perform a howling animation on activation, making it temporarily vulnerable against oncoming attacks.
The first shield replenish will occur at the start of the animation and the second near the end.
Protect replenishes shields every 6 seconds
It will initially restore 15% of Kubrow's shields, followed by the other 15% shields after 1.5 seconds. After shield replenishment, it will take 4.5 seconds until Protect can restore shields again.
**Trivia:**
Shares the same picture as Shelter, only with a golden border.

## Proton Pulse
**Rarity:** Common | **Polarity:** naramon | **Drain:** 4 | **Compatible:** WARFRAME | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Wall Dashing grants +25% Bullet Jump Speed.
- **Rank 1:** Wall Dashing grants +50% Bullet Jump Speed.
- **Rank 2:** Wall Dashing grants +75% Bullet Jump Speed.
- **Rank 3:** Wall Dashing grants +100% Bullet Jump Speed.

### Drop Locations
- Jupiter/Ganymede (Disruption), Rotation A — Uncommon (11.11%)
- Amalgam Alkonost — Uncommon (3.27%)
- Amalgam Arca Heqet — Uncommon (3.27%)
- Amalgam Arca Kucumatz — Uncommon (3.27%)
- Amalgam Cinder Machinist — Uncommon (3.27%)
- Amalgam Heqet — Uncommon (3.27%)
- Amalgam Kucumatz — Uncommon (3.27%)
- Amalgam Machinist — Uncommon (3.27%)
- Amalgam Moa — Uncommon (3.27%)
- Amalgam Osprey — Uncommon (3.27%)
- Amalgam Phase Moa — Uncommon (3.27%)
- Amalgam Satyr — Uncommon (3.27%)
- Amalgam Swarm Satyr — Uncommon (3.27%)
- Aurax Culveri Moa — Uncommon (3.27%)
- Aurax Polaris Moa — Uncommon (3.27%)
- Demolyst Heqet — Uncommon (3.27%)
- Demolyst Machinist — Uncommon (3.27%)
- Demolyst Moa — Uncommon (3.27%)
- Demolyst Osprey — Uncommon (3.27%)
- Demolyst Satyr — Uncommon (3.27%)

### Wiki
Proton Pulse is a set mod mod that grants an increased Bullet Jump and Rolling speed buff after performing a Wall Latch (despite the description saying Wall Dash) until a Bullet Jump is used or contact with the ground is made.
**Notes:**
Despite the mod's description mentioning Wall Dashing, the effect is currently activated after Wall Latching. It is not required to end the wall latch by wall dashing (although this also triggers the effect); simply cancelling the latch is enough to trigger it.
The effect lasts until a Bullet Jump is used or the player touches the ground.
Affects Rolling despite not mentioning it in the description.
The bonus is additive with other Parkour Velocity bonuses.
**Tips:**
Uriel's Infernalis flight speed scales with Parkour Velocity, allowing Proton Pulse to drastically increase its speed.
The ability snapshots Uriel's current Parkour Velocity when he enters flight, so the buff will continue to affect his speed even if he touches the ground and loses the buff while he is flying, so long as the flight is not cancelled.
The buff will not be removed by Uriel's flight, so he can enter and exit flight mode as many times as desired with the buff still active on each new flight, so long as he does not make contact with the ground at any point.
**Trivia:**
It was first seen on Devstream 124, under the name Spider Leap.
25.1.2

## Proton Snap
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** madurai | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Melee | **Max Rank:** 3

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Hold Wall Latch for 2s to gain +25% Toxin Damage, and +12.5% Status Chance for 5s.
- **Rank 1:** Hold Wall Latch for 2s to gain +50% Toxin Damage, and +25% Status Chance for 10s.
- **Rank 2:** Hold Wall Latch for 2s to gain +75% Toxin Damage, and +37.5% Status Chance for 15s.
- **Rank 3:** Hold Wall Latch for 2s to gain +100% Toxin Damage, and +50% Status Chance for 20s.

### Drop Locations
- Amalgam Alkonost — Rare (0.22%)
- Amalgam Arca Heqet — Rare (0.22%)
- Amalgam Arca Kucumatz — Rare (0.22%)
- Amalgam Cinder Machinist — Rare (0.22%)
- Amalgam Heqet — Rare (0.22%)
- Amalgam Kucumatz — Rare (0.22%)
- Amalgam Machinist — Rare (0.22%)
- Amalgam Moa — Rare (0.22%)
- Amalgam Osprey — Rare (0.22%)
- Amalgam Phase Moa — Rare (0.22%)
- Amalgam Satyr — Rare (0.22%)
- Amalgam Swarm Satyr — Rare (0.22%)
- Aurax Culveri Moa — Rare (0.22%)
- Aurax Polaris Moa — Rare (0.22%)
- Demolyst Heqet — Rare (0.22%)
- Demolyst Machinist — Rare (0.22%)
- Demolyst Moa — Rare (0.22%)
- Demolyst Osprey — Rare (0.22%)
- Demolyst Satyr — Rare (0.22%)
- Jupiter/Ganymede (Disruption), Rotation C — Uncommon (10.00%)

### Wiki
Proton Snap is a set mod that grants Toxin damage and Status Chance to your melee weapon after holding a Wall Latch for two seconds.
**Notes:**
Proton Snap's Status Chance bonus is additive rather than multiplicative.
Total Status Chance Base Status Chance (1 + Normal Status Chance Mods) + Proton Snap
For example, a Galatine with a max rank Melee Prowess, Proton Snap, and Weeping Wounds at a 2.0x combo mulitplier will have a 20% (1 + 90% + 40% (2.0 - 1) ) + 50% status chance.
Must be equipped to Exalted Weapons to apply to them. 
Fully applies to pseudo-exalted melee skills including Landslide, Whipclaw, Shattered Lash, and Slash Dash.
Since the bonus is additive, this mod gives the latter two the ability to inflict status effects when they normally cannot, however the Status Chance bonus doesn't extend to detonating Mass Vitrify.
In rare cases where stances multiply modded status chance on attacks, such as Iron Phoenix's first hit of Wings and Beak, they will not multiply Proton Snap's bonus.
Total Status Chance Base Status Chance (1 + Normal Status Chance Mods) (1 + Stance Status Chance Bonus) + Proton Snap
The Toxin will combine with other elements according to Proton Snap's mod placement. Because this only applies while Proton Snap is active, it is possible to change elements in the middle of play by activating and deactivating the effect.
If equipped alongside a Toxin mod or on a weapon with innate Toxin damage, Proton Snap will not allow for changing of elements, and will instead work based on where the first Toxin mod is placed (including Proton Snap).
Proton Snap does not extend its effects to weapons or abilities it is not equipped on.
Cannot be equipped on Melee Robotic Weapons (Deconstructor (Deconstructor Prime), Akaten, Batoten, and Lacerten).

Provoked is a mod that increases the damage dealt by a Warframe while in bleedout.

## Pummel
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** naramon | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Pistol | **Max Rank:** 5

### Acquisition
This mod was originally acquired by getting 1000 Points in the Operation: False Profit Event.
It is now offered as a periodic item for sale by Baro Ki'Teer, costing 300 and 150,000 to purchase. Note that Baro Ki'Teer's stock changes with each appearance, and this mod may not be available on a succeeding appearance.
After the addition of the Plains of Eidolon, the mod may also be obtained by killing the Eidolon Teralyst.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +20% Impact
- **Rank 1:** +40% Impact
- **Rank 2:** +60% Impact
- **Rank 3:** +80% Impact
- **Rank 4:** +100% Impact
- **Rank 5:** +120% Impact

### Drop Locations
- Eidolon Teralyst — Rare (8.33%)

### Wiki
The Pummel mod increases Impact damage of a pistol by 20% per rank for a maximum of 120% at rank 5.
**Notes:**
Can be combined with Concussion Rounds and Saxum Spittle to provide a 300% increase to Impact damage.
When calculating total raw damage, a weapon will require an Impact damage percentage equal to 75% of its total base damage to equal the raw damage increase provided by a standard elemental mod (which increases corresponding elemental damages by 90%).
The higher the Impact damage percentage over 75%, the more viable this mod will be as opposed to an elemental mod, and vice versa for lower values.
These calculations only pertain to raw damage, and do not take into consideration enemy armor/flesh/shield damage multipliers.

### Drop Locations

Purging Slash is a PvE and Conclave Warframe Augment Mod for Excalibur's Slash Dash that removes allies' debuffs and restores their shields when Slash Dashed into.
**Notes:**
Allies do not include the player that casted the ability, their companions, or defense objects.
Amount of debuffs removed is affected by Ability Strength.
Shield restoration amount is not affected by mods.

## Pyroclastic Flow
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Nezha | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired by attaining the rank of General under the Steel Meridian, or the rank of Genius under Cephalon Suda, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Fire Walker Augment: Accumulate 100% of the damage Fire Walker deals, unleashing it in a trail of fire that lasts 6s.
- **Rank 1:** Fire Walker Augment: Accumulate 150% of the damage Fire Walker deals, unleashing it in a trail of fire that lasts 7s.
- **Rank 2:** Fire Walker Augment: Accumulate 200% of the damage Fire Walker deals, unleashing it in a trail of fire that lasts 8s.
- **Rank 3:** Fire Walker Augment: Accumulate 250% of the damage Fire Walker deals, unleashing it in a trail of fire that lasts 10s.

### Drop Locations
- Cephalon Suda, Genius — Common (100.00%)
- Steel Meridian, General — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Pyroclastic Flow is a Warframe Augment Mod for Nezha and Nezha Prime that accumulates the damage Fire Walker deals and unleashes it in a trail of fire upon reactivating the ability.
**Notes:**
Nezha kicks a trail 20 meters in the direction he is aiming. Unlike Fire Walker's basic casting animation, Pyroclastic Flow will not interrupt charging.
Only the ability damage itself from Fire Walker accumulates damage, thereforeHeat status damage does not contribute.
Damage accumulation multiplier and trail range are not affected by mods.
Trail duration is affected by Ability Duration.
Pyroclastic Flow inflicts a guaranteed Heat proc.
The damage is stored before armor and resistances are taken into account.
Damage stored is shown in the HUD below the respective ability icon and applies once every 0.5 seconds effectively doubling the damage per second from the ability one might expect.
Accumulated damage will stay stored even after Fire Walker's duration expires and will not be removed by being dispelled by Nullifier Crewmen or falling out of bounds. Casting Fire Walker after it has expired will activate Pyroclastic Flow in addition to reactivating Fire Walker.
Flames that linger beyond Fire Walker timing out or being dispelled will not accumulate damage.

## Quasar Drill
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** naramon | **Drain:** 2 | **Compatible:** Archgun | **Max Rank:** 5

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +15% Puncture
- **Rank 1:** +30% Puncture
- **Rank 2:** +45% Puncture
- **Rank 3:** +60% Puncture
- **Rank 4:** +75% Puncture
- **Rank 5:** +90% Puncture

### Drop Locations
- Lektro Commander — Uncommon (3.00%)
- Pyr Captain — Uncommon (3.00%)
- Earth/Erpo (Mobile Defense) — Rare (3.76%)
- Europa/Gamygyn (Mobile Defense) — Rare (3.76%)
- Jupiter/Galilea (Sabotage) — Rare (3.76%)
- Neptune/Salacia (Mobile Defense) — Rare (3.76%)

### Wiki
Quasar Drill is a mod that increases Puncture damage of Archguns by 15% per rank for maximum of 90% at Rank 5.
**Trivia:**
Upon release, this mod displayed as dual-stat (damage and status chance), however, this was later stated to be an outdated tooltip.
Used to add puncture damage to a weapon based on the total base damage. Therefore, a weapon with very little puncture could be boosted heavily, such as the Fluctus becoming puncture based.
It also used to add puncture damage to weapons without physical damage, like the Velocitus.
This mod's damage is no longer based on total damage, instead modifying only the weapons puncture damage, if any.
Used to add Impact damage instead of Puncture.

## Quick Thinking
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** vazarin | **Drain:** 10 | **Compatible:** WARFRAME | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 5

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Drains Energy to stop Lethal Damage with 40% Efficiency.
- **Rank 1:** Drains Energy to stop Lethal Damage with 80% Efficiency.
- **Rank 2:** Drains Energy to stop Lethal Damage with 120% Efficiency.
- **Rank 3:** Drains Energy to stop Lethal Damage with 160% Efficiency.
- **Rank 4:** Drains Energy to stop Lethal Damage with 200% Efficiency.
- **Rank 5:** Drains Energy to stop Lethal Damage with 240% Efficiency.

### Drop Locations
- Corrupted Ancient — Rare (0.03%)
- Duviri/Endless: Tier 6 (Normal) — Rare (1.03%)
- Duviri/Endless: Tier 4 (Normal) — Rare (1.49%)

### Wiki
Quick Thinking is a mod that uses the Warframe's residual energy pool as an emergency buffer to prevent death whenever a player drops to 2 Health.
**Notes:**
Stacks with Gladiator Finesse for a total of 300% efficiency.
Ability Efficiency has no effect on the amount of energy lost while under the effects of Quick Thinking.
The Warframe's Armor and any sources of damage reduction will still reduce incoming damage and as such, the amount of energy drain.
Damage taken while Quick Thinking is in effect does not count as taking Health damage; as such, Rage and Hunter Adrenaline do not convert incoming damage into energy, and Vex Armor and Vexing Retaliation do not trigger their effects.
While under the effects of Quick Thinking (2 HP and losing energy instead), strong hits will cause the Warframe to stagger. Be wary, as repeated strong hits can effectively stun-lock you in place until you die. Weak hits will not induce this effect.
Pain Threshold drastically reduces the stagger duration, allowing one to avoid being stun-locked.
A max rank Primed Sure Footed will not affect the stagger.
Quick Thinking has the lowest priority of almost all death prevention effects, such as Nidus' passive or Phoenix Renewal. Quick Thinking will only start draining energy after all other means of preventing lethal damage are exhausted.
Wukong's passive will only trigger once Quick Thinking runs out of energy to drain.
If the player would take enough damage to lose both all their health and energy, the player will instead enter bleedout immediately and not lose any energy.
Will not work on Hildryn or Lavos since they do not have energy capacity.
Rage and Hunter Adrenaline will not replenish energy if a portion of health is lost during a Magnetic proc.
Will not work if player is in Operator mode while channeled abilities are active.

### Wiki
Quickdraw is a pistol mod that increases reload speed.
**Notes:**
The calculation of reload time using reload speed is: Reload Time Base Reload Time (1 + Reload Speed Bonus)
At max rank, expect a 33% reduction to reload times. 1/(1 + 0.48) ~ (2/3 of original reload speed)
Stacking this with Stunning Speed should reduce reload times by 47% (or 53% of base reload speed)
**Trivia:**
Prior to Update 16.7, this mod was usable in PvP games until it was replaced by its own specific PvP mods along with a number of other mods: Fast Hands, Slip Magazine, Tactical Pump, Steady Hands, Stabilizer, Burdened Magazine, Magazine Warp and Terminal Velocity.

### Wiki
Quickening is a mod that increases the attack speed of melee weapons while granting a chance of additional combo count per hit.
**Notes:**
Stacks with Gladiator Vice and 2-stack Berserker Fury for a maximum of of (1+30%+40%+70%) attack speed.
This mod is a straight upgrade from the non-primed version of Fury, adding 10% more attack speed and 20% combo count chance for the same cost.
**Trivia:**
Unlike most mods, the picture shows an Excalibur wearing the Pendragon helmet, his Immortal Skin, as well as a different color scheme.
This is a rare instance of a mod depicting a non-default character loadout.
The other examples are Life Strike, Second Wind, True Punishment, Enduring Strike, Warrior's Grip, Killing Blow and Corrupt Charge, all of which used to be channeling mods.
Coincidentally, all of said mods depict Excalibur wielding a Nikana.
The only difference in each appearance (besides positioning) is the energy color used.
As of Update 15.5, there are four new mods that also depict non-default characters. These mods are Bore, Piercing Caliber, Breach Loader, and Auger Strike, all of which depict a Nyx Warframe with her Immortal Skin and Menticide helmet.
Prior to Update 26, this mod provided bonus attack speed upon Channeling while reducing Channeling Efficiency.

## Radiant Finish

### Acquisition

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Radial Blind Augment: Blinded enemies take 150% more Finisher Damage.
- **Rank 1:** Radial Blind Augment: Blinded enemies take 200% more Finisher Damage.
- **Rank 2:** Radial Blind Augment: Blinded enemies take 250% more Finisher Damage.
- **Rank 3:** Radial Blind Augment: Blinded enemies take 300% more Finisher Damage.

### Drop Locations

### Wiki

**Notes:**

Multiplicative to Finishing Touch.
**Tips:**

- Operation: Orphix Venom, Rotation A — Rare (6.52%)

### Wiki
Rage is a Warframe mod that converts a percentage of Health damage received into Energy. If the user has no Shields, damage taken to Overguard granted from an ally is converted into Energy.
**Notes:**
Rage and Hunter Adrenaline have the same effect, stacking for a total of 85% damage conversion.
The amount of Energy gained from damage taken to Health is affected by Warframe Damage Reduction.
Applies to Status Effects that deal damage over time and damage that bypasses Shields.
Damage taken to Overguard only applies energy if the user does not have Shields (such as Inaros and Nidus).
The Overguard must also come from an ally. As such, Kullervo's Recompense does not receive this benefit despite his lack of shields.
Warframes whose shields have been removed via subsumed Parasitic Armor (while active), or Arcane Persistence will benefit from energy conversion from Overguard.
Does not apply to the following sources of damage:
Self-inflicted damage, such as Nekros's Despoil or Combat Discipline.
Laser traps encountered in the Orokin Void and Orokin Derelicts.
Health lost due to the Vampire Nightmare Mode modifier, or health drained in Survival missions by falling to 0% life support.
Energy is gained even with active Channeled Abilities.
Nourish affects the amount of energy conversion.

### Wiki
Range Advantage is a Weapon Augment Mod for Akjagara and Akjagara Prime that increases Damage if no enemy is within 10m of the player.
**Notes:**
Damage increase is additive with other damage mods like Hornet Strike.
**Tips:**
Synergizes well with an Ability Range-focused Sevagoth's Gloom, as it helps in keeping enemies well outside the minimum distance requirement for the mod to trigger its damage bonus for the akimbo pistols.

### Wiki
Rapid Resilience is a mod that reduces the status duration of status effects upon the player.
**Notes:**
This mod does not affect knockdown or stagger recovery time.
Most status effects are reduced to 1.5 seconds. This effectively makes damaging status effects only 1 second. The table below shows the which damage ticks go through for each status type.
The 120 total energy drained from a Magnetic proc is reduced to 30 energy.
A Magnetic proc on Tenno normally lasts 3 seconds. With Rapid Resilience this is reduced to 0.75 seconds, because the energy drain has no delay it is not completely nullified.
The UI distortion effect is also reduced to 0.75 seconds.
Blast procs will deal damage after a delay of 0.37 seconds.
A shield gate duration of more than 1 second will block all damaging status effects from damaging your health, except for Toxin, and during the shield gate invulnerability.

## Razor Mortar
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Caliban | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired by attaining the rank of Genius under Cephalon Suda, or the rank of Partner under the The Perrin Sequence, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Razor Gyre Augment: Groups enemies together, while Lethal Progeny's Ortholysts focus fire on those enemies with +40% Electricity Damage and Fire Rate for 3s.
- **Rank 1:** Razor Gyre Augment: Groups enemies together, while Lethal Progeny's Ortholysts focus fire on those enemies with +50% Electricity Damage and Fire Rate for 4s.
- **Rank 2:** Razor Gyre Augment: Groups enemies together, while Lethal Progeny's Ortholysts focus fire on those enemies with +60% Electricity Damage and Fire Rate for 5s.
- **Rank 3:** Razor Gyre Augment: Groups enemies together, while Lethal Progeny's Ortholysts focus fire on those enemies with +70% Electricity Damage and Fire Rate for 6s.

### Wiki
Razor Mortar is a Warframe Augment Mod for Caliban's Razor Gyre that groups enemies while causing Lethal Progeny's Ortholysts to focus fire on them with increased Electricity damage and Fire Rate.
**Notes:**
In addition to grouping enemies hit by the ability, all Ortholysts will teleport close to the enemies that were grouped by Razor Gyre.
The teleportation effect and buffs will only activate if Razor Gyre successfully hits 1 or more enemies.
Duration of the buffs are affected by Ability Duration.
Fire Rate andElectricity damage buffs are affected by Ability Strength.
The pull has an unlisted base range of 5 meters, affected by Ability Range.
The Electricity damage dealt by Ortholysts proc Archon Stretch, per Update 39.1.

Razor Munitions is a Conclave exclusive pistol mod that converts an amount of damage to Slash damage.

A weapon may only have one damage conversion mod equipped at a time.
**Trivia:**
Despite it being a Slash mod, the Magnus deals Impact damage.

### Wiki
Razor Shot is a mod that increases the Slash damage of Pistols.
**Notes:**
Increases effectiveness against unarmored enemies.
Recommended to use with weapons with a higher Slash damage compared to other damage types, like the Pyrana and Vasto.
Unlike Elemental damage modules, only increases the % of that damage type of the weapon, not based on the total of all base damages.
Also does not increase the damage of other modules that calculate from these original values either.
Can be combined with Maim and Carnis Stinger to provide a 300% increase to Slash damage.

## Razorwing Blitz
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Titania | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired by attaining the rank of Flawless under New Loka, or the rank of Exalted under Red Veil, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.
This mod can periodically be acquired from The Circuit.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Razorwing Augment: Flight Speed increased by 25% and Fire Rate increased by 25% for 2s when using abilities. Stacks up to 4x.
- **Rank 1:** Razorwing Augment: Flight Speed increased by 25% and Fire Rate increased by 25% for 4s when using abilities. Stacks up to 4x.
- **Rank 2:** Razorwing Augment: Flight Speed increased by 25% and Fire Rate increased by 25% for 6s when using abilities. Stacks up to 4x.
- **Rank 3:** Razorwing Augment: Flight Speed increased by 25% and Fire Rate increased by 25% for 8s when using abilities. Stacks up to 4x.

### Drop Locations
- New Loka, Flawless — Common (100.00%)
- Red Veil, Exalted — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Razorwing Blitz is a Warframe Augment Mod for Titania's Razorwing, which boosts the speed of some Warframe and weapon actions when an Ability is cast while the former is channeled.
**Notes:**
All effects (including Duration) additively stack up to a cap of 4; further casts will only re-add Duration (but still won't exceed 4x base).
When the bonuses are active, an aura showing the percentage of the buff and remaining duration is visible on the top right corner next to the health and shields bar.
Effects are only gained if a Warframe Ability (including those infused via Helminth) is cast while Razorwing is active, and will end if Razorwing does.
"Flight Speed" refers to both Movement Speed and Sprint Speed.
Movement Speed bonus stacks additvely with other buffs such as Dispatch Overdrive.
Sprint Speed bonus stacks additively with other buffs such as Rush.
Fire Rate buff boosts both Dex Pixia's Fire Rate and Diwata's Attack Speed.
Fire Rate bonus stacks additively with other buffs, such as Gunslinger and (Primed Fury) Fury, respectively.
Visually, the Flight Speed buff is limited to 1200% on the in game HUD
Movement Speed, Sprint Speed, and Fire Rate bonuses are affected by Ability Strength, and effect duration is affected by Ability Duration.
Affected by Precision Intensify.
Buff values are determined by Titania's Ability Strength when Razorwing is cast, not when the buff itself is triggered.
This means that conditional increases like Growing Power and Empower should ideally be activated beforehand, and that more time-consuming or unpredictable sources (like Molt Augmented or a Void Fissure's corruption, respectively) may require recasting of Razorwing, if the ability was already in-use before obtaining the buff.

### Wiki
Reach is a mod that increases the range of a Melee weapon's attacks. Glaives gain increased projectile size, while throw distance remains unchanged.
For the robotic companion Helios, Reach increases the range at which it can attack enemies if installed on Deconstructor.
**Notes:**
Reach does not increase the radius of slam radial attacks.
Reach does not increase the range limit of thrown melee weapons, but instead grants these weapons a larger hitbox.
Reach does not increase the fall-off range for gunblade melee weapons.
**Tips:**
The effects of Reach are not seen in the energy trails that appear while swinging a weapon, but the trails themselves determine which angles would the attacks land as the new range of the equipped weapon.
Can be installed on Helios' Deconstructor to extend its attack range.

## Reactive Storm
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Baruuk | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired by attaining the rank of Flawless under New Loka, or the rank of Maxim under the Arbiters of Hexis, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.
This mod can periodically be acquired from The Circuit.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Serene Storm Augment: Desert Wind is granted +100% Status Chance and changes its damage type to match enemy weaknesses.
- **Rank 1:** Serene Storm Augment: Desert Wind is granted +150% Status Chance and changes its damage type to match enemy weaknesses.
- **Rank 2:** Serene Storm Augment: Desert Wind is granted +200% Status Chance and changes its damage type to match enemy weaknesses.
- **Rank 3:** Serene Storm Augment: Desert Wind is granted +250% Status Chance and changes its damage type to match enemy weaknesses.

### Drop Locations
- Arbiters of Hexis, Maxim — Common (100.00%)
- New Loka, Flawless — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Reactive Storm is a Warframe Augment Mod for Baruuk's Serene Storm to increase Desert Wind's status chance and alters its damage type to match enemy weaknesses.
**Notes:**
The status chance increase granted by this augment is affected by Ability Strength and additive with any other status chance mods.
For example, using a maxed Intensify, Drifting Contact, and Reactive Storm results in:Base status chance [1 + (Reactive Storm Bonus (1 + Strength Mods)) + Status Mods] 0.15 [1 + (2.5 1.3) + 0.4] 69.7% status chance
To achieve 100% status chance without any status chance mods, Ability Strength must be at least 227%.
Only the base Impact damage is altered to match as enemies weakest damage type.
If damage becomes a primary element, it will not combine with other modded primary elements.
Impact damage mods no longer have any effect after changing.
To adapt damage types, Baruuk can strike with both his fists/feet at close range or Desert Wind's long-range waves.
Once set (by the last enemy struck by fists), the new damage type will affect the waves.
Damage type will reset to Impact when Serene Storm deactivates.
Damage type will prioritize combined and non-physical elements and the first element a given faction is vulnerable to based on the in-game UI, limiting its potential damage conversions to the following:
Heat (Infested)
Toxin (Narmer)
Corrosive (Grineer, Kuva Grineer, and Scaldra)
Magnetic (Corpus and Corpus Amalgam)
Viral (Orokin)
Radiation (Sentient and The Murmur)
Blast (Infested Deimos)
Gas (Techrot)
Damage Resistance reduction by Magus Accelerant, Magus Destruct or Petrify will influence the new damage type.
Damage type does not change in response to Sentient damage adaptation.

## Reaping Chakram
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Nezha | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired by attaining the rank of General under the Steel Meridian, or the rank of Genius under Cephalon Suda, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Blazing Chakram Augment: Each enemy hit increases the ring's Damage by 1.25x and the chance for enemies to drop Health Orbs on death by +0.10x.
- **Rank 1:** Blazing Chakram Augment: Each enemy hit increases the ring's Damage by 1.5x and the chance for enemies to drop Health Orbs on death by +0.15x.
- **Rank 2:** Blazing Chakram Augment: Each enemy hit increases the ring's Damage by 1.75x and the chance for enemies to drop Health Orbs on death by +0.20x.
- **Rank 3:** Blazing Chakram Augment: Each enemy hit increases the ring's Damage by 2x and the chance for enemies to drop Health Orbs on death by +0.25x.

### Drop Locations
- Cephalon Suda, Genius — Common (100.00%)
- Steel Meridian, General — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Reaping Chakram is a Warframe Augment Mod for Nezha which increases Blazing Chakram's damage for each enemy hit by the attack. Additionally it increases the spawn percentage for health orbs for each enemy hit.
**Notes:**
As the augment increases the chance for health orbs above 100%, it can cause enemies to drop multiple health orbs.
Passing through the same enemy multiple times will still count towards the multiplier.
The multiplier affects the damage after Ability Strength is taken into account.
Multiplier itself is not affected by Ability Strength.
Multiplier affects total damage, making the scaling (1.25 / 1.5 / 1.75 / 2 )n, where n is the number of times Blazing Chakram passes through an enemy.
Multiplier is also applied to the Heat proc done by the chakram.
There is no cap to the possible damage boost.
**Trivia:**
Reaping Chakram originally had the additional effect of giving Blazing Chakram Punch Through against enemies, replacing its normal bounce effect. This was later made part of Nezha's base kit (through the ability to charge Blazing Chakram) in Update 23.8.

### Wiki
Recover is a Conclave-exclusive rifle mod that gives Health regeneration for a short period after defeating an opponent, but reduces Health gained from Health orbs.
**Notes:**
Health orb debuff stacks additively with Recuperate, Calculated Victory, Vanquished Prey, Momentary Pause and Prize Kill
Using 4 of these mods will make you unable to pickup health orbs.

## Recrystalize
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Citrine | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired by attaining the rank of General under Steel Meridian, or the rank of Exalted under the Red Veil, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Crystallize Augment: Enemies killed by crystalline growths stagger and spread the crystals to enemies within 4m.
- **Rank 1:** Crystallize Augment: Enemies killed by crystalline growths stagger and spread the crystals to enemies within 8m.
- **Rank 2:** Crystallize Augment: Enemies killed by crystalline growths stagger and spread the crystals to enemies within 12m.
- **Rank 3:** Crystallize Augment: Enemies killed by crystalline growths stagger and spread the crystals to enemies within 16m.

### Drop Locations
- Red Veil, Exalted — Common (100.00%)
- Steel Meridian, General — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Recrystalize is a Warframe Augment Mod for Citrine that allows Crystallize to spread to additional enemies when crystalized foes are killed by shooting the growths.
**Notes:**
Enemies killed while affected by a Crystal Growth will instantly spread Crystals to all unaffected enemies within Ability Range16 meters.
The killing blow does not have to be landed on an enemy's Crystal in order to trigger the effect.
The spread range does not require Line of Sight.
Enemies affected by Recrystallize will trigger the effect on their death as well, allowing the effect to trigger multiple times from a single ability cast.
Enemies affected by Recrystallize will NOT be frozen in place, but instead dealt Ability Strength500 Impact damage with a forced proc and stagger.
Each newly crystallized enemy will have the full Ability Duration8 second duration of Crystalize, regardless of the remaining duration on the killed enemy that spread the effect.
This can enable the effect to last indefinitely, so long as there are enough new enemies to spread Crystals to.
Crystallized enemies will not have their Crystallize duration reset if Recrystallize is triggered again nearby.
**Trivia:**
The augment mod spells Crystallize in "Recrystalize" with one L, while the ability uses two L's.

### Wiki
Redirection increases the Maximum Shield Capacity of a Warframe. It is the first normal Mod that all new players receive in Warframe, where it is automatically given upon activating the Orbiter's Mods console.
**Notes:**
The increase to shield capacity provided by Redirection is applied after rank bonuses from levelling up a warframe.
For example, if a maxed Redirection is applied to a level 30 Frost Prime, the base shield capacity (550) is increased by the +100 base shield rank bonus, giving 650. This total is multiplied by 2 (+100% Shield bonus from redirection), giving a grand total of 1300 shield capacity.

### Wiki
Reflex Coil is a mod that increases your melee weapon's Heavy Attack Efficiency by 10% per rank, at a maximum of 60% at rank 5.
**Notes:**
Heavy Attack Efficiency determines the percentage of combo used up whenever the player performs a Heavy Attack.
**Trivia:**
The mod still shows a Lancer armed with a Braton, as opposed to the Grakata that Lancers currently use. This is because the Grakata was not introduced to the game until Update 7.8 and the Braton was used as a placeholder until then.
Prior to Update 26, this mod increased Channeling efficiency.
At maximum rank, Reflex Coil can reduce Melee Channel's energy cost per strike to just 2 Energy.
While Reflex Coil and Focus Energy combined will total 100% channeling efficiency, Energy Channel has a minimum energy cost of 1 Energy per strike, translating to an 80% Power Efficiency cap.
This combination, however, can be still fully utilized to offset any channeling efficiency penalties such as those caused by Life Strike.

## Reflex Denial
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** penjaga | **Drain:** 0 | **Compatible:** Hound | **Max Rank:** 5

### Acquisition
Reflex Denial is automatically acquired upon obtaining a Hound built with the Cela Bracket.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Generates a shield that reflects 15% Damage taken in pulses over 5s as Magnetic Damage in a 2m radius.
- **Rank 1:** Generates a shield that reflects 30% Damage taken in pulses over 6s as Magnetic Damage in a 4m radius.
- **Rank 2:** Generates a shield that reflects 45% Damage taken in pulses over 7s as Magnetic Damage in a 6m radius.
- **Rank 3:** Generates a shield that reflects 60% Damage taken in pulses over 8s as Magnetic Damage in a 8m radius.
- **Rank 4:** Generates a shield that reflects 75% Damage taken in pulses over 9s as Magnetic Damage in a 10m radius.
- **Rank 5:** Generates a shield that reflects 90% Damage taken in pulses over 10s as Magnetic Damage in a 12m radius.

### Wiki
Reflex Denial is a precept mod for Hounds that creates a shield that reflects a portion of the damage received, releasing pulses that deal radial Magnetic damage.
**Notes:**
Cooldown of 10s.
The shield has a 4m radius.
Allied damage is ignored, and will pass through the shield.
Less effective against melee Infested units, as they seem to prioritize attacking the player rather than the Hound.
Cannot be equipped with Diversified Denial and Evasive Denial.

### Wiki
Reflex Draw is a pistol mod usable in both PvE and PvP reduces recoil and increases accuracy for 8 seconds upon holstering a weapon. This effect works for both primary and secondary weapons.
**Notes:**
Both recoil and spread reduction apply to all weapons, not just the one equipped with this mod.
Buff is refreshed, whenever a weapon is equipped.
Buff stacks additively with those from Twitch and Soft Hands.
Mesa/Prime exalted weapon Regulators, if equipped with Reflex Draw can be activated while you do not have the Peacemaker ability active.

## Regenerative Molt
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Saryn | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired by attaining the rank of General under Steel Meridian, or the rank of Exalted under Red Veil, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Molt Augment: After casting Molt, Saryn regenerates 20 Health/s for 10s.
- **Rank 1:** Molt Augment: After casting Molt, Saryn regenerates 30 Health/s for 10s.
- **Rank 2:** Molt Augment: After casting Molt, Saryn regenerates 40 Health/s for 10s.
- **Rank 3:** Molt Augment: After casting Molt, Saryn regenerates 50 Health/s for 10s.

### Drop Locations
- Red Veil, Exalted — Common (100.00%)
- Steel Meridian, General — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Regenerative Molt is a Warframe Augment Mod for Saryn that allows her to regenerate health over a span of time after casting Molt.
**Notes:**
Saryn heals an amount of health per tick for 10 ticks over 10 seconds.
Amount healed per tick is affected by Ability Strength.
Is not affected by Ability Duration.
The regeneration effect does not end early if Molt's decoy is destroyed by damage. However, it does end when Molt expires naturally if the decoy duration drops below 9 seconds.
Regenerative Molt restores at a rate of one tick per second. The total number of healing ticks is equal to healing duration in seconds, rounded up to a maximum of 10.
Examples:
If Molt's duration is 20 seconds long, Regenerative Molt will heal for 10 ticks.
If Molt's duration is 9.1 seconds long, Regenerative Molt will still heal for 10 ticks.
If Molt's duration is 5.6 seconds long, Regenerative Molt will heal for only 6 ticks.
The minimum threshold to recieve all 10 ticks is 23% Ability Duration.
Regenerative Molt Ability Strength scaling is 0.5 hp per tick for every additional 1% ability strength. (rounded down)
A maxed Intensify giving 30% extra Ability Strength will give the base 50+15 hp per tick.
A rank 8 Transient Fortitude with 45% Ability Strength will grant the base 50+22.5 hp per tick - In-game it seems to be rounded down because you will get 22 instead of 23 extra hp per tick.
Although Molt's base decoy health should reflect the health restored by 10 ticks at max rank, the actual value may be lower due to rounding.
**Trivia:**
The name of this augment used to be Regenerative Moult, using the British English spelling.

## Reinforced Bond
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** naramon | **Drain:** 4 | **Compatible:** COMPANION | **Max Rank:** 5

### Acquisition
The mod can be bought from The Business for 20,000 Standing after reaching Rank 3 - Doer with Solaris United.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** If the Companion exceeds 1200 Max Shields or Overshields then your fire rate is increased 10%. Reloading restores 25 Overshields to your companion.
- **Rank 1:** If the Companion exceeds 1200 Max Shields or Overshields then your fire rate is increased 20%. Reloading restores 50 Overshields to your companion.
- **Rank 2:** If the Companion exceeds 1200 Max Shields or Overshields then your fire rate is increased 30%. Reloading restores 75 Overshields to your companion.
- **Rank 3:** If the Companion exceeds 1200 Max Shields or Overshields then your fire rate is increased 40%. Reloading restores 100 Overshields to your companion.
- **Rank 4:** If the Companion exceeds 1200 Max Shields or Overshields then your fire rate is increased 50%. Reloading restores 125 Overshields to your companion.
- **Rank 5:** If the Companion exceeds 1200 Max Shields or Overshields then your fire rate is increased 60%. Reloading restores 150 Overshields to your companion.

### Drop Locations
- Solaris United (The Business), Doer — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Reinforced Bond is a Companion mod that increases the owner's Fire Rate when the companion's maximum Shields or current Shields (including Overshield) exceed 1200. Reloading will also restore shields to the companion.
**Notes:**
As noted above, the buff activates if the companion possesses over 1200 maximum shields through modding, and will remain active regardless of their actual moment-to-moment shield strength. However, the buff will temporarily deactivate if the companion is incapacitated.
Alternatively, as reloading restores up to 150 shields, maintaining companion's shields (including Overshield) over 1200 will activate the buff. Note that Battery Weapons won't restore shields.
In order to get at least 1200 maximum shields with the Calculated Redirection mod at maximum rank, your companion will need a minimum 343 base shield. This can only be accomplished by the following companions:
Wyrm Prime
Nautilus Prime
Huras Kubrow
Raksa Kubrow
Sunika Kubrow
Any breed of Vulpaphyla or Predasite
Any MOA or Hound with the right modular components. See Final MOA Stats and Final Hound Stats.
Using a max-rank Link Redirection to reach the required 1200 maximum shields on beast companions is heavily dependent on your selected Warframe's maximum shields.

## Rejuvenation
**Rarity:** Uncommon | **Polarity:** vazarin | **Drain:** -2 | **Compatible:** AURA | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 5

### Acquisition
Available for 20 creds from the Nightwave Cred Offerings store on a rotational basis.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Squad receives +0.5 Health Regen/s
- **Rank 1:** Squad receives +1 Health Regen/s
- **Rank 2:** Squad receives +1.5 Health Regen/s
- **Rank 3:** Squad receives +2 Health Regen/s
- **Rank 4:** Squad receives +2.5 Health Regen/s
- **Rank 5:** Squad receives +3 Health Regen/s

### Drop Locations
- Duviri/Endless: Tier 1 (Normal) — Legendary (0.67%)
- Duviri/Endless: Tier 3 (Normal) — Legendary (0.67%)

### Wiki
Rejuvenation is an aura mod that regenerates all team members' health every second.
* Aura mods increase the amount of Mod Capacity
**Notes:**
Can be stacked with other teammates' for a total of 12 health regen per second in a typical 4-person Squad.
Affected allies includes all Warframes, Companions, Eidolon Lures, summoned allied units such as Specters, allied Invasion units, Hostages, Kavor Defectors, Sortie and Arbitrations Defense Operatives, and Defense Objects.
**Tips:**
This mod is extremely useful in Nightmare mode:
In the "No shield" challenge you will be able to gradually recover from health damage.
The Vampiric Drain challenge does not count as damage and will not reset the rejuvenation counter, allowing you to regen health to counteract the draining. Note that the drain is a % of your total health, while the rejuvenation is a constant value. Thus, depending on your total health, rejuvenation may or may not entirely counter the effects of Vampiric Drain.
