### Wiki
Relentless Assault is a Conclave-exclusive melee mod which regenerates a small amount of energy on each melee kill for a short period of time.
**Trivia:**
This mod featured an image of Volt Prime before he was released, making this the third case of a mod previewing future content. The first two mods to do so were Aviator showing off Zephyr, and the Operation Breeding Grounds mods set with Mirage.
Can be equipped with Martial Fury.

## Relentless Combination
**Rarity:** Uncommon | **Polarity:** naramon | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Melee | **Max Rank:** 3

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +25% chance to increase Melee Combo Counter when Slash Status deals damage.
- **Rank 1:** +50% chance to increase Melee Combo Counter when Slash Status deals damage.
- **Rank 2:** +75% chance to increase Melee Combo Counter when Slash Status deals damage.
- **Rank 3:** +100% chance to increase Melee Combo Counter when Slash Status deals damage.

### Drop Locations
- Ancient Disruptor — Uncommon (0.33%)
- Deimos Therid — Uncommon (0.33%)
- Tar Mutalist Moa — Uncommon (0.33%)
- Duviri/Endless: Tier 1 (Normal) — Legendary (0.67%)
- Duviri/Endless: Tier 3 (Normal) — Legendary (0.67%)

### Wiki
Relentless Combination grants a chance to deal Combo Counter hits when a target is dealt damage from a Slash proc caused by the player's melee strikes.
**Notes:**
At maximum rank, Relentless Combination increases the Melee Combo Counter by +1 for every Slash proc inflicted per enemy. A combo hit is applied for each application of Slash and additional +1 hits thereafter for every tick of Bleed damage.
If the enemy dies while under the Bleed proc or Bleed runs out, you will stop gaining additional hits from that enemy.
Only Slash procs caused by melee weapons count; Slash procs from primary or secondary weapons, Warframe abilities (including abilities that are affected by melee weapon mods or the Combo Counter, such as Shattered Lash or Blade Storm), or Rending Turn will not increase the Combo Counter.
Exalted Blade, Diwata, Iron Staff, Valkyr Talons, and Garuda Talons can equip the mod and benefit from it.
Slash procs from Exalted Blade's energy waves, including ones cast from Slash Dash while Exalted Blade is active, add hits to the combo counter.
Slash procs from thrown Glaives also add hits, even though the combo counter does not interact with Glaives.
Note that several melee weapons are incapable of inflicting Bleed due to not dealing any Slash damage, but can still equip the mod for no benefits. This can be partially rectified by using Stance combos with forced Slash procs.
Benefits from "Additional Combo Count Chance" mods such as True Punishment and Quickening.
**Tips:**
Most Stances have a combo in which an attack has a guaranteed status proc; these can be used to take advantage of this mod regardless of the weapons status chance.
Since additional hits are gained from the Bleed DoT, the mod's effectiveness is more noticeable on durable enemies.
Lasting Sting, as well as Ash's and Saryn's passive abilities increase the status duration, allowing more ticks to occur.

## Rending Crane
Downward cuts with an impact combo.
**Rarity:** Uncommon | **Polarity:** madurai | **Drain:** -2 | **Compatible:** Heavy Blade | **Max Rank:** 3

### Drop Locations
- Kuva Napalm — Rare (0.17%)
- Napalm — Rare (0.17%)

- Tusk Napalm — Rare (0.17%)

### Wiki
Rending Crane is a stance mod for Heavy Blade melee weapons, which specializes in overhead slashes with powerful knockdown finishers.
**Notes:**
The second hit of Lashing Tempest's first attack does 100% bonus Slash damage, while the third and fourth attack does 25% bonus Slash damage.

## Rending Strike
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** naramon | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Melee | **Max Rank:** 3

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +15% Slash, +20% Puncture
- **Rank 1:** +30% Slash, +40% Puncture
- **Rank 2:** +45% Slash, +60% Puncture
- **Rank 3:** +60% Slash, +80% Puncture

### Drop Locations
- Nightmare Mode Rewards, Rotation B — Uncommon (15.49%)

### Wiki
Rending Strike is a mod that adds an additional 20% Puncture per rank and 15% Slash per rank for a maximum of 80% and 60% at rank 3, respectively.
**Notes:**
Stacks with Sundering Strike and Auger Strike for a maximum of 290% Puncture damage, or Jagged Edge and Buzz Kill for a maximum of 270% Slash damage.
Slash bonus has no effect on the damage of the bleed status effect.
Endura, Okina, Dehtat, Balla, Mios, Dual Raza and Valkyr Talons make the best use of this mod, as their damages are relatively balanced between Slash and Puncture.
**Trivia:**
Previously, the in-game description of this mod incorrectly stated:"Convert 15% of energy used to Bonus Damage on next Melee Attack. Store up to +0.2 Bonus Damage"This was fixed in Update 9.3.
Rending Strike used to give a bonus of 60% more Charge damage alongside its Puncture damage, but since Update 13 removed Charge attacks, it has been replaced with Slash damage.
This is the only physical damage mod that increases two damage types instead of one.

Also increases the distance/speed of rolls.
At max rank, Rending Turn adds a slicing effect that deals 160 Slash damage with a 200% status chance within a 3-meter radius as well as a guaranteed knockdown within a 0.75-meter radius from where a player initiates a Bullet Jump.
Visual effects added during Bullet Jumps will always be the same color, regardless of Warframe Energy or Emission colors.

## Repair Dispensary
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Protea | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired by attaining the rank of Maxim under Arbiters of Hexis, or the rank of Partner under The Perrin Sequence, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Dispensary Augment: Dispensary also creates pick ups that heal companions for 5% of their maximum health and reduce their incapacitation timer by 3 sec.
- **Rank 1:** Dispensary Augment: Dispensary also creates pick ups that heal companions for 10% of their maximum health and reduce their incapacitation timer by 6 sec.
- **Rank 2:** Dispensary Augment: Dispensary also creates pick ups that heal companions for 15% of their maximum health and reduce their incapacitation timer by 9 sec.
- **Rank 3:** Dispensary Augment: Dispensary also creates pick ups that heal companions for 20% of their maximum health and reduce their incapacitation timer by 12 sec.

### Drop Locations
- Arbiters of Hexis, Maxim — Common (100.00%)
- The Perrin Sequence, Partner — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Repair Dispensary is a Warframe Augment Mod/Exilus Mod for Protea that causes Dispensary to drop special pickups that heals Companions and reduces their respawn timer.
**Notes:**
The Repair Dispensary, after creating Energy Orbs, will generate special Health Orb-looking pickups that apply their benefits only to Companions.
Despite their appearance, they do not function as a traditional Health Orb and provide no healing for Warframes.
Repair Dispensary projects a holographic Dethcube above itself.
Since Repair Dispensary adds a new pickup to the Dispensary rotation, it will also increase the spawn rate of all other individual pickups by 2 seconds (e.g. Instead of Energy Orbs spawning at 6/12/18... seconds, they will instead spawn at 6/14/22... seconds)

## Repair Kit
**Rarity:** Common | **Polarity:** vazarin | **Drain:** 2 | **Compatible:** Sentinel | **Max Rank:** 5

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +3 Companion Health Regen/s
- **Rank 1:** +6 Companion Health Regen/s
- **Rank 2:** +9 Companion Health Regen/s
- **Rank 3:** +12 Companion Health Regen/s
- **Rank 4:** +15 Companion Health Regen/s
- **Rank 5:** +18 Companion Health Regen/s

### Wiki
Repair Kit is a companion mod that provides health regeneration to Sentinels.
It is the only occasional drop from the Domestik Drone on Corpus Gas Cities and Corpus Ships.
Farming Tips
The mod can be easily acquired from the Ropalolyst mission. On entry, one drone always spawns in front and 2 others in the hallways left and right of the spawn point. If you don't get the drop simply abandon the mission and try again. If you cannot yet access the Ropalolyst, running capture missions where the drone can spawn such as Skyresh on Phobos or Ananke on Jupiter will allow for quick extraction after the mod drops.
**Notes:**
Its functionality can be compared to Medi-Pet Kit, which heals only 12 health/second for companions (Kubrows, Kavats, MOAs and Hounds) but also reduces companion recovery time.
**Trivia:**
This is the first mod that exclusively drops from an object's drop table (while there are mod drops from the special containers in the Void, those can also be acquired elsewhere).
On release the mod did not have any assigned polarity. Interestingly despite such a "universal" polarity, it would still double its cost when equipped on a polarized slot, as if it was a "wrong" polarity.
The image is actually the depiction of Medi-Ray, healing the Valkyr as she uses Warcry against a Vapos MOA.
Neither the Domestik Drone nor Repair Kit are listed in the official Warframe Drop Tables, but the in-game codex correctly lists Repair Kit as a drop from the drone.

## Resolute Focus
**Rarity:** Uncommon | **Polarity:** naramon | **Drain:** 4 | **Compatible:** Archgun | **Max Rank:** 5

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +17% Chance to Resist Staggers/Knockdowns when Aiming, +8% Accuracy when Aiming
- **Rank 1:** +33% Chance to Resist Staggers/Knockdowns when Aiming, +17% Accuracy when Aiming
- **Rank 2:** +50% Chance to Resist Staggers/Knockdowns when Aiming, +25% Accuracy when Aiming
- **Rank 3:** +67% Chance to Resist Staggers/Knockdowns when Aiming, +33% Accuracy when Aiming
- **Rank 4:** +84% Chance to Resist Staggers/Knockdowns when Aiming, +42% Accuracy when Aiming
- **Rank 5:** +100% Chance to Resist Staggers/Knockdowns when Aiming, +50% Accuracy when Aiming

### Drop Locations
- Gyre Butcher — Rare (0.26%)
- Gyre Elite Lancer — Rare (0.26%)
- Exo Raider Carver — Rare (0.37%)
- Exo Raider Eviscerator — Rare (0.37%)
- Exo Raider — Rare (0.37%)
- Venus/Orb Vallis (Level 40 - 60 PROFIT-TAKER - PHASE 2), Rotation C — Uncommon (15.00%)

### Wiki
Resolute Focus is a mod that increases stagger/knockdown resistance and increases the accuracy of Archguns while aiming.
**Notes:**
The stagger and knockdown resistance only benefits atmospheric use of Archguns via the Gravimag. The spread decrease, on the other hand, affects both atmospheric and Archwing use of the heavy guns.
The stagger resistance from Resolute Focus does NOT prevent the stagger caused by Quick Thinking when the player receives a "strong hit".
The stagger resistance also does NOT prevent the short knockback from firing a charged shot with the Velocitus and Corvas (Corvas Prime).
**Tips:**
This mod is particularly suited when fighting the Profit-Taker Orb to prevent interruptions by the various Corpus mobs with jump attacks, as well as the field boss's knockdown attacks from the legs and the repelling shields.
Note that the mod only prevents knockdown and stagger, not knockbacks, but being knocked back is still generally preferable as it is easier to recover from it than a stagger or a knockdown.
This mod works well at focusing the otherwise highly scattered shots from the Grattler (Kuva Grattler).
24.2.2

## Resonance
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Banshee | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired by attaining the rank of Genius under Cephalon Suda, or the rank of Partner under The Perrin Sequence, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.
Resonance Pulse Cooldown
In this section:
The initial cast of Sonar will be referred to as a Sonar pulse.
The subsequent pulse created by killing an enemy's highlighted weakspot will be referred to as a Resonance pulse.
When a Sonar marked enemy is killed and a Resonance pulse is released, Resonance cannot be triggered again until the currently active Resonance pulse reaches its maximum range. Because the Resonance pulse has a fixed 20 m/s wave propagation speed, this means that increasing range will also increase the cooldown between Resonance pulses, as the pulse has further to travel before reaching its maximum range.
Resonance pulses have the same Ability Range35 meter range as Sonar, giving Resonance an effective base cooldown of 1.75 seconds, scaling inversely with range. For example, 34% range will give a cooldown of 0.6s between Resonance pulses, while 280% range would give a cooldown of 4.9s.
Although there is no limit on the number of possible Sonar pulses active at once, there can only be one active instance of Resonance pulse.
A Resonance pulse can still trigger even if the Sonar pulse from the initial cast of Sonar is still active.
At times, the Resonance cooldown does not seem to apply if the two consecutive triggers are made by different players.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Sonar Augment: Killing an enemy by shooting their weak spot will trigger another Sonar for 50% of remaining duration.
- **Rank 1:** Sonar Augment: Killing an enemy by shooting their weak spot will trigger another Sonar for 65% of remaining duration.
- **Rank 2:** Sonar Augment: Killing an enemy by shooting their weak spot will trigger another Sonar for 80% of remaining duration.
- **Rank 3:** Sonar Augment: Killing an enemy by shooting their weak spot will trigger another Sonar for 100% of remaining duration.

### Drop Locations
- Cephalon Suda, Genius — Common (100.00%)
- The Perrin Sequence, Partner — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Resonance pulse is a community term. It is a mechanically separate pulse from the initial pulse when casting the Sonar ability, which are referred to in this article as a Sonar pulse.
Resonance is a Warframe Augment Mod for Banshee's Sonar that triggers a secondary pulse with a fraction of the initial Sonar pulse's remaining duration if an enemy is killed in a highlighted weakspot.

## Resonating Quake
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Banshee | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired by attaining the rank of Genius under Cephalon Suda, or the rank of Partner under The Perrin Sequence, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Sound Quake Augment: Forgoes channeling to create a shockwave that deals 10x Damage at the epicenter, gradually weakening as it expands out.
- **Rank 1:** Sound Quake Augment: Forgoes channeling to create a shockwave that deals 12x Damage at the epicenter, gradually weakening as it expands out.
- **Rank 2:** Sound Quake Augment: Forgoes channeling to create a shockwave that deals 15x Damage at the epicenter, gradually weakening as it expands out.
- **Rank 3:** Sound Quake Augment: Forgoes channeling to create a shockwave that deals 20x Damage at the epicenter, gradually weakening as it expands out.

### Drop Locations
- Cephalon Suda, Genius — Common (100.00%)
- The Perrin Sequence, Partner — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Resonating Quake is an Warframe Augment Mod for Banshee that replaces Sound Quake's channeling feature for a single, large shockwave that deals bonus damage at the epicenter, but decreases damage as the shockwave spreads.
**Notes:**
This mod changes Sound Quake from a channeled ability into a single cast ability with a base energy cost of 100.
As the ability is no longer channeled, Ability Duration does not affect the energy drain.
Increases base range from Ability Range20m to Ability Range35m.
Does not require line of sight to damage enemies.
Base damage is Ability Strength200 with a 20x damage multiplier, for an effective base damage of Ability Strength4000.
Damage falls off over distance, decreasing very sharply within just a few meters, then decreasing more slowly down to a minimum of 10% of the listed damage at the maximum range.
Falloff is based on the distance between Banshee and the target at the time of the ability hitting, not the distance the shockwave has traveled.
Moving closer to the target while the wave is traveling will increase the damage dealt, and vice versa.
Approximately follows the formula: Damage Multiplier = 0.1 * (distance / max range)-0.5
For some examples, the damage is reduced by 50% after traveling only 4% of its total range, and reduced by 75% after 16%.
Damage is bugged and fluctuates somewhat randomly. For example, an enemy further away from Banshee may take more damage than one closer to her, or two equally distant enemies may take noticeably different damage.
The quake only hits enemies once per cast and pushes enemies back.
The quake expands out at a set speed of ~17.5 meters per second, unaffected by mods.

## Resourceful Retriever
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** naramon | **Drain:** 2 | **Compatible:** BEAST | **Max Rank:** 5

### Acquisition
Resourceful Retriever can be purchased from Son in the Necralisk for:
1 Sly Vulpaphyla Tag
1 Purple Velocipod Tag
1 Burrowing Cryptilex Tag
1 Common Avichaea Tag
1 Amethyst Nexifera Tag
1 Vizier Predasite Tag
1 Umber Undazoa Tag

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** 5.5% chance to double Resource pickups
- **Rank 1:** 8% chance to double Resource pickups
- **Rank 2:** 10.5% chance to double Resource pickups
- **Rank 3:** 13% chance to double Resource pickups
- **Rank 4:** 15.5% chance to double Resource pickups
- **Rank 5:** 18% chance to double Resource pickups

### Wiki
Resourceful Retriever is a Beast mod that grants a chance at doubled Resource pickups.
**Notes:**
Doubles Void Trace pick-ups.
Continues to function even while companion is downed.
Khora's Venari can not equip Resourceful Retriever.
Cannot be equipped alongside Loyal Retriever or Prosperous Retriever.
Has the potential to individually affect each ore type in a vein when hitting the special narrow brackets. This will be indicated with the "2x" icon near the name of the affected ore after mining the vein. The same applies to fishing and capturing wildlife, potentially giving you 2x fish or 2x/4x/6x tags on bad/good/perfect wildlife captures. Also may yield double the plants from harvesting.
Effect doubles Cryotic excavator earnings.
Certain resources, such as Orokin Cell, Riven Sliver, Kuva, etc., which appear on the bottom left of the screen when picked up, do not show a "2x" icon but are still doubled.

Retribution is a Exilus mod that grants a chance to deal Electricity damage to enemies when they deal shield damage to a Warframe via a melee attack.

While an enemy is stunned, a finisher attack may be executed on it.

## Revealing Spores
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Saryn | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired by attaining the rank of General under Steel Meridian, or the rank of Exalted under Red Veil, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Spores Augment: Infected enemies within 10m appear on the minimap. +10 Enemy Radar.
- **Rank 1:** Spores Augment: Infected enemies within 20m appear on the minimap. +20 Enemy Radar.
- **Rank 2:** Spores Augment: Infected enemies within 30m appear on the minimap. +30 Enemy Radar.
- **Rank 3:** Spores Augment: Infected enemies within 40m appear on the minimap. +40 Enemy Radar.

### Drop Locations
- Red Veil, Exalted — Common (100.00%)
- Steel Meridian, General — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Revealing Spores is an Exilus Warframe Augment Mod for Saryn's Spores that causes enemies affected the ability to show up on the mini map.
**Notes:**
Detection range is affected by Ability Range.
Enemy Radar is not affected by mods.
Unlike other enemy radar mods like Enemy Radar or Animal Instinct, Revealing Spores will mark enemies with spores as green arrows instead of red.
Cannot be equipped at the same time as Venom Dose.
Radar is personal, not shared with teammates.
The Operator can see enemies in the mini map with Revealing Spores, while they don't get the same benefit from mods with Enemy Radar attribute.
Enemies afflicted with spores stay visible on the map, when the map is jammed with "Signal Disruption" from Regulators and similar effects, even though the normal enemy radar does not function under these conditions.
**Tips:**
With Revealing Spores in combination with Enemy Radar, the player can keep track of infected and uninfected enemies in the mini map, and spread spores accordingly for optimal area coverage.
When number of infected enemies is too low, Revealing Spores could help in pinpointing them, so the player can attack/kill them to spread more spores, thus make it easier to maintain accumulated damage meter.

### Wiki
Revenge is a precept mod that allows Shade, Prisma Shade, and Shade Prime to attack enemies but only if they attacked its owner.
**Notes:**
Upgrading increases the range this skill can be activated from (i.e. Shade will be able to retaliate against enemies that attack from a longer range).
Without this mod equipped, the Shade will not attack.
Revenge will only trigger if the Sentinel's master receives actual damage (invulnerability such as Iron Skin does not count). This does not also include the Sentinel itself taking damage.
Used to display Wyrm in the picture until update 10.5.

## Reverse Rotorswell

- **Rank 1:** Rotorswell Augment: 25% of damage received is redirected toward the instigator as Electricity Damage.
- **Rank 2:** Rotorswell Augment: 30% of damage received is redirected toward the instigator as Electricity Damage.
- **Rank 3:** Rotorswell Augment: 35% of damage received is redirected toward the instigator as Electricity Damage.

### Wiki
Reverse Rotorswell is a Warframe Augment Mod for Gyre's Rotorswell that grants her Damage Reduction, and deals Electricity damage to her attackers proportional to the reduced damage.
**Notes:**
Despite claiming in its description that this mod offers Damage Redirection, the mod actually offers Damage Reduction, which does not affect Overguard, nor provides Status Effect immunity, alongside the damaging effect.
Damage Reduction caps at 75%, achieved with 215% Ability Strength.

### Wiki
Rifle Ammo Mutation is an assault rifle mod that converts any unused Secondary Ammo into Primary Ammo. The affected rifle will only convert ammo while equipped, and will not convert any ammo type used by holstered secondary weapons until their own ammo is full.
Converted ammo is denoted by a Mutate symbol. The final amount of ammo restored is rounded to the nearest integer.
**Tips:**
Good for weapons with poor ammo economy, such as the Grakata.
Functions well with Sentinel's Vacuum or Chesa Kubrow's Retrieve, which automatically collect unused ammo as needed.

## Rifle Amp
**Rarity:** Uncommon | **Polarity:** madurai | **Drain:** -2 | **Compatible:** AURA | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 5

### Acquisition
Available for 20 creds from the Nightwave Cred Offerings store on a rotational basis.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Squad receives +4.5% Rifle Damage
- **Rank 1:** Squad receives +9% Rifle Damage
- **Rank 2:** Squad receives +13.5% Rifle Damage
- **Rank 3:** Squad receives +18% Rifle Damage
- **Rank 4:** Squad receives +22.5% Rifle Damage
- **Rank 5:** Squad receives +27% Rifle Damage

### Drop Locations
- Duviri/Endless: Tier 1 (Normal) — Legendary (0.67%)
- Duviri/Endless: Tier 3 (Normal) — Legendary (0.67%)

### Wiki
Not to be confused with Amp.
Rifle Amp is an aura mod that increases the damage dealt with rifles.
* Aura mods increase the amount of Mod Capacity
**Notes:**
As stated on the mod description Rifle Amp increases rifle damage, but it also affects bows, sniper rifles and launchers (Ogris, Penta, etc.). Shotguns do not benefit from this mod, Shotgun Amp.
Rifle Amp adds to the base damage as Serration and Heavy Caliber do.
Rifle Damage Base Damage (1 + Serration + Heavy Caliber + Rifle Amp)
Therefore, the actual bonus from a maxed rifle amp is only +(27 (1 + Damage Bonus ))%, where Damage Bonus is the damage bonus from Serration and Heavy Caliber as a decimal fraction. That's +12.3% for a 7/10 Serration, +10.2% for a maxed Serration, and +6.3% for a maxed Serration and maxed Heavy Caliber.
Assuming a maxed Serration and no Heavy Caliber is equipped on the rifle, without damage type modifiers, against an armored enemy, it will deal more damage with Corrosive Projection than with Rifle Amp if the target's armor is higher than 134. For a damage type that has a +75% bonus against the target's armor type, Corrosive Projection surpasses Rifle Amp if armor is higher than 536.
Can be stacked with other members using this aura, for a total of +108% additive damage bonus.

## Rifle Scavenger
**Rarity:** Uncommon | **Polarity:** naramon | **Drain:** -2 | **Compatible:** AURA | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 5

### Acquisition
Available for 20 creds from the Nightwave Cred Offerings store on a rotational basis.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Squad receives +25% Rifle Ammo Recovery
- **Rank 1:** Squad receives +50% Rifle Ammo Recovery
- **Rank 2:** Squad receives +75% Rifle Ammo Recovery
- **Rank 3:** Squad receives +100% Rifle Ammo Recovery
- **Rank 4:** Squad receives +125% Rifle Ammo Recovery
- **Rank 5:** Squad receives +150% Rifle Ammo Recovery

### Drop Locations
- Duviri/Endless: Tier 1 (Normal) — Legendary (0.67%)
- Duviri/Endless: Tier 3 (Normal) — Legendary (0.67%)

### Wiki
Rifle Scavenger is an aura mod that increases the amount of primary ammo recovered per drop.
* Aura mods increase the amount of Mod Capacity
**Notes:**
Applies to all primary weapons, not just rifles.
Does not apply to Kuva Bramma.
Applies to ammo converted by ammo mutation mods, such as Rifle Ammo Mutation.
Converted ammo always rounds down and preserves no decimals, unlike ammo efficiency bonuses.
For example, Kuva Zarr getting two ammo pickups will get 1 250% = 2.5 = 2 rounded down from each, rather than 1 extra ammo on every other pickup from the 0.5 remainder.
Can be stacked with other teammates' to increase the amount of primary ammo gained by up to 600%.
**Trivia:**
Although Saryn is the Warframe in the image, the polarity of her Aura slot (Vazarin) doesn't match that of Rifle Scavenger's.

## Rift Haven
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Limbo | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired by attaining the rank of Maxim under the Arbiters of Hexis, or the rank of Genius under Cephalon Suda, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.
This mod can periodically be acquired from The Circuit.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Banish Augment: Allies banished to the rift will have 10% of their Maximum Health restored every second.
- **Rank 1:** Banish Augment: Allies banished to the rift will have 15% of their Maximum Health restored every second.
- **Rank 2:** Banish Augment: Allies banished to the rift will have 20% of their Maximum Health restored every second.
- **Rank 3:** Banish Augment: Allies banished to the rift will have 25% of their Maximum Health restored every second.

### Drop Locations
- Arbiters of Hexis, Maxim — Common (100.00%)
- Cephalon Suda, Genius — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Rift Haven is a Warframe Augment Mod for Limbo that makes Banish restore a portion of an injured ally's health over time.
**Notes:**
This augment allows Banish to restore health on all banished Warframes, summoned allied units such as Specters, allied Invasion units, Hostages, Kavor Defectors, and Sortie/Arbitration Defense Operatives.Confirmation Needed
This cannot be used to heal Companions or Defense Objects, due to Banish being unable to target them.
This cannot heal Eidolon Lures.
Healing percentage is affected by Ability Strength.
Healing applies only when ally target is banished to the Rift Plane.

## Rift Strike
**Rarity:** Uncommon | **Polarity:** naramon | **Drain:** 2 | **Compatible:** Twin Basolk | **Max Rank:** 5

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Heavy attacks now step through the rift to attack enemies up to 5m away
- **Rank 1:** Heavy attacks now step through the rift to attack enemies up to 9m away
- **Rank 2:** Heavy attacks now step through the rift to attack enemies up to 13m away
- **Rank 3:** Heavy attacks now step through the rift to attack enemies up to 17m away
- **Rank 4:** Heavy attacks now step through the rift to attack enemies up to 21m away
- **Rank 5:** Heavy attacks now step through the rift to attack enemies up to 25m away

### Drop Locations
- Kela De Thaym — Uncommon (11.11%)

### Wiki
Rift Strike is a mod exclusive to the Twin Basolk that causes Heavy Attacks to teleport the user directly in melee range of an enemy, provided the user is aiming at them when the heavy attack is completed, and the user is standing within the mod's listed range.
**Notes:**
These attacks do not gain a stealth damage bonus while invisible and striking from a distance. It is unknown if this is intended.
These attacks can be used to teleport to enemy corpses, given that they are still visible.
Heavy attacks executed on unalerted enemies backs will cause you to do a back finisher attack.
However heavy attacks on other parts of the enemy while they are unalerted will not cause any type of finisher attack.
The mod suggests that the Rift Plane is being used to achieve the instantaneous travel to an enemy's location, however in practical terms it is strictly a teleportation mod.
The user is not granted any Rift effects or visual indicators for any part of the heavy attack.
The teleport works similarly to Amar's Set, but does not stack with it.
Reach does not increase the teleportation range.
Can be used in conjunction with Nyx's Assimilate augment to provide an alternative form of mobility.
Teleporting using this mod may result in getting stuck, though this mod may also be used to get free again.
**Trivia:**
This mod, along with the other Executioner mods, are the second set mods to feature non-generic enemies on the mod card, the first being Vengeful Revenant.

## Rift Torrent
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Limbo | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired by attaining the rank of Maxim under the Arbiters of Hexis, or the rank of Genius under Cephalon Suda, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Rift Surge Augment: Limbo deals 15% Extra Damage for each enemy affected by Rift Surge while in the rift.
- **Rank 1:** Rift Surge Augment: Limbo deals 20% Extra Damage for each enemy affected by Rift Surge while in the rift.
- **Rank 2:** Rift Surge Augment: Limbo deals 25% Extra Damage for each enemy affected by Rift Surge while in the rift.
- **Rank 3:** Rift Surge Augment: Limbo deals 30% Extra Damage for each enemy affected by Rift Surge while in the rift.

### Drop Locations
- Arbiters of Hexis, Maxim — Common (100.00%)
- Cephalon Suda, Genius — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Rift Torrent is a Warframe Augment Mod for Limbo's Rift Surge that increases Limbo's damage while in the Rift Plane for each enemy affected by Rift Surge.
**Notes:**
Damage bonus is affected by Ability Strength.
Limbo will lose the buff when exiting but will instantly regain it upon entering the Rift Plane.
Each enemy affected by Rift Surge will increase Limbo's damage by 30% at max Rank. The damage is displayed as a buff with the current percentage.
If an enemy affected by Rift Surge dies, the bonus will be updated to the current amount of enemies affected.
Enemy does not need to be in the rift for the buff to apply.
If more than one Limbo with Rift Torrent is in a squad all players will share and stack each other's bonus'. For example: 1 Player with 20 Surged enemies gives 2,000% damage, an allied limbo will receive it upon entering the Rift. However if that allied Limbo Surges an enemy, and is given 50% damage. The bonus' will stack with each other for 2,050%.
The damage buff is additive to mods like Serration and Hornet Strike.
The modified damage can be calculated using the following formula:
Modified Damage Base Damage (1 + Rift Torrent Bonus (1 + Ability Strength) Number of Affected Enemies + Damage Mods)
For example, with a max rank Serration and Intensify, 5 enemies, and a weapon that deals 100 damage, the total modified damage will be:
100 (1 + 0.3 (1 + 0.3) 5 + 1.65)
With Exodia Contagion and Glaive explosions Rift Torrent is applied twice. It is additive to mods like Power Throw or Vigorous Swap and it is additive to base damage mods like Serration.

## Rime Rounds
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** madurai | **Drain:** 4 | **Compatible:** Rifle | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired as a random end-of mission reward for a successful Spy mission with all 3 Data Vaults hacked.
Drop Locations (Spy Missions Rotation C):
Pavlov
Originally this mod could be acquired from Operation Cryotic Front by scoring more than 1000 points in a single mission.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +15% Cold, +15% Status Chance
- **Rank 1:** +30% Cold, +30% Status Chance
- **Rank 2:** +45% Cold, +45% Status Chance
- **Rank 3:** +60% Cold, +60% Status Chance

### Drop Locations
- Lua/Pavlov (Spy), Rotation C — Uncommon (11.06%)
- Ceres/Bode (Spy), Rotation C — Rare (7.74%)
- Europa/Valac (Spy), Rotation C — Rare (7.74%)
- Jupiter/Amalthea (Spy), Rotation C — Rare (7.74%)
- Saturn/Aegaeon (Spy), Rotation C — Rare (7.74%)
- Saturn/Dione (Spy), Rotation C — Rare (7.74%)

### Wiki
Rime Rounds is a mod that increases both the Cold damage and status chance of rifles by 15% per rank for a maximum of 60% at rank 3.
**Notes:**
Rime Rounds is additive with similar mods. For example, a Snipetron with a max rank Rime Rounds and Rifle Aptitude will have a 12% (1 + 60% + 90%) status chance.
This mod can be combined with High Voltage, Thermite Rounds, and Malignant Force to achieve a combined status chance bonus of 240%. With Rifle Aptitude and Hammer Shot, this bonus can be increased to 410%, more than quadrupling the weapon's status chance.
**Trivia:**
Rime refers to a coating of ice formed on objects when water droplets quickly freeze on a cold object.
The mod card shows the Convectrix, which is a shotgun, not a rifle.

This mod can acquired by reaching the rank of Mistral from the Conclave Syndicate, and spending 2,000 Standing to purchase.

### Wiki
Rising Steel is a Conclave-exclusive stance mod for longswords, designed to immobilize and knockdown opponents with controlled blade swings.
Rising Steel
**Notes:**
The heavy attack, Perfect Cut, can inflict Impair onto opponents.
The first attack, Forward Edge, can give a small dash of momentum.

## Rising Storm
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Ash | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired by attaining the rank of Maxim under the Arbiters of Hexis, or the rank of Exalted under the Red Veil, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Blade Storm Augment: Blade Storm attacks increase your Ability Combo Counter by 1. Passive: +10s Combo Duration
- **Rank 1:** Blade Storm Augment: Blade Storm attacks increase your Ability Combo Counter by 2. Passive: +10s Combo Duration
- **Rank 2:** Blade Storm Augment: Blade Storm attacks increase your Ability Combo Counter by 3. Passive: +10s Combo Duration
- **Rank 3:** Blade Storm Augment: Blade Storm attacks increase your Ability Combo Counter by 4. Passive: +10s Combo Duration

### Drop Locations
- Arbiters of Hexis, Maxim — Common (100.00%)
- Red Veil, Exalted — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Rising Storm is an Ash Warframe Augment Mod that allows the attacks by clones generated by Blade Storm to give additional Combo points to the Melee Combo Counter, as well as increases the duration the Melee Combo Counter lasts.
**Notes:**
Bonus hits are affected by both Ability Strength and melee combo count chance.
The augment confers the extra ten second Melee Combo Counter duration regardless of its rank. This bonus duration is not affected by Ability Duration mods.
Synergizes well with Drifting Contact, Body Count, and Gladiator Rush for a total combo counter duration of 41 seconds at max rank.

## Rolling Guard
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** vazarin | **Drain:** 2 | **Compatible:** WARFRAME | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 10

### Acquisition
Obtainable from the Arbitrations vendor in the Arbiters of Hexis room in Relays for 20 Vitus Essence.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** On Dodge: Become invulnerable for 2s and remove all Status Effects. 12s cooldown.
- **Rank 1:** On Dodge: Become invulnerable for 2.1s and remove all Status Effects. 11.5s cooldown.
- **Rank 2:** On Dodge: Become invulnerable for 2.2s and remove all Status Effects. 11s cooldown.
- **Rank 3:** On Dodge: Become invulnerable for 2.3s and remove all Status Effects. 10.5s cooldown.
- **Rank 4:** On Dodge: Become invulnerable for 2.4s and remove all Status Effects. 10s cooldown.
- **Rank 5:** On Dodge: Become invulnerable for 2.5s and remove all Status Effects. 9.5s cooldown.
- **Rank 6:** On Dodge: Become invulnerable for 2.6s and remove all Status Effects. 9s cooldown.
- **Rank 7:** On Dodge: Become invulnerable for 2.7s and remove all Status Effects. 8.5s cooldown.
- **Rank 8:** On Dodge: Become invulnerable for 2.8s and remove all Status Effects. 8s cooldown.
- **Rank 9:** On Dodge: Become invulnerable for 2.9s and remove all Status Effects. 7.5s cooldown.
- **Rank 10:** On Dodge: Become invulnerable for 3s and remove all Status Effects. 7s cooldown.

### Wiki
Rolling Guard is a Warframe mod that grants a brief period of invulnerability and removes all Status Effects when rolling, with a cooldown period between uses.
**Notes:**
The invulnerability status is indicated by the Warframe's health bar being greyed out.
Cooldown starts after the invulnerability period ends, shown by a timer with an inverted red triangle symbol.
This mod works while in Archwing in Atmospheric mode, provided it is equipped on the active Warframe, and Titania in Razorwing mode. It can be activated by the dash move in all six cardinal directions (forward, back, left, right, up and down).
This mod allows low durability frames such as Loki, Ember, or Nova to have a small window of invulnerability to cast their abilities in the open.
Allows Limbo to remain safe while exiting the rift, allowing the player to maintain continuous protection as long as they re-enter the rift before the invincibility duration ends.
It also synergizes particularly well with Mirage, as she lacks a defense against area-of-effect attacks, and has low durability. In addition, her passive ability letting her roll much more quickly gives her more time to act while invulnerable.
The invulnerability period will also give her ample time to cast Prism, killing and/or blinding enemies to keep them from attacking after Rolling Guard's effects end.
For similar reasons to Mirage's passive, it also synergizes well with Amalgam Barrel Diffusion, due to its effect of speeding up rolling animations.
Can be used to avoid getting Magnetic procs from Eidolon's Energy Spike attacks if rolls are timed correctly.
It can be used to remove magnetic procs that are already underway, reducing the amount of energy loss from the proc.
The mod also grants full Knockdown resistance during the invulnerability period.
The mod works for Yareli while rolling on Merulina.

## Rousing Plunder

### Wiki
Rousing Plunder is a Warframe Augment Mod for Hydroid that allows Plunder to heal Hydroid and his allies within Affinity Range, and increases the Corrosive damage and armor bonus from Plunder.
**Notes:**
Heals Hydroid and allies in affinity range by Ability Strength50 health per enemy in line of sight within Ability Range25 meters, plus an additional Ability Strength50 health for each Corrosive proc removed by Plunder.
Heal is affected by Ability Strength.
Increases max Corrosive damage buff by a flat 100%, applied after Ability Strength modifiers.
Corrosive damage buff increase is not affected by Ability Strength
Increases max Armor buff by 375, applied after Ability Strength modifiers.
Armor buff increase is not affected by Ability Strength

## Rubble Heap
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Atlas | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired by attaining the rank of General under Steel Meridian, or the rank of Exalted under the Red Veil, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.
This mod can periodically be acquired from The Circuit.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Passive Augment: When above 1400 Rubble, Landslide costs no Energy, deals 1.25x Damage, and travels 1.25x faster.
- **Rank 1:** Passive Augment: When above 1400 Rubble, Landslide costs no Energy, deals 1.5x Damage, and travels 1.5x faster.
- **Rank 2:** Passive Augment: When above 1400 Rubble, Landslide costs no Energy, deals 1.75x Damage, and travels 1.75x faster.
- **Rank 3:** Passive Augment: When above 1400 Rubble, Landslide costs no Energy, deals 2x Damage, and travels 2x faster.

### Drop Locations
- Red Veil, Exalted — Common (100.00%)
- Steel Meridian, General — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Rubble Heap is a Warframe Augment Mod for Atlas' passive that makes Landslide cost no energy, increases its damage, and increases its speed when his Rubble count is above a threshold of 1400.
**Notes:**
Damage bonus is additive to the one provided by Ability Strength (equivalent to +100% Ability Strength).
This diminishes the effectiveness of modding for Ability Strength alongside the Augment.
Landslide dash range is extended 2x further, targeting enemies up to 24m away.
Speed is doubled, so travel time is the same.
Rubble requirement, damage bonus and dash range are not affected by mods.
Landslide attack speed and cast time is unchanged.
Energy cost becomes zero, and no energy is needed to cast landslide.
Synergizes well with Path of Statues, which eliminates the need to recast Petrify in target-rich environments. This can be used to remove any reliance on your energy pool.

Tenet Spirex can equip this mod since it has the BEAM tag despite it not being a beam weapon.

## Rumbled
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Atlas | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired by attaining the rank of Tempest in Conclave and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.
Beginning in Series 3, this augment can be purchased as a Nightwave Cred Offering for 20.
Atlas will be encased in stone armor and unequips all weapons. The player's primary fire button will throw boulders, while melee attacking performs a ground slam.
Health multiplier of the Overguard is affected by Ability Strength.
Atlas's movement is limited to walking, sprinting and rolling. He cannot jump, crouch, or slide.
He can, however, aim glide if he was placed in a high position or in midair.
The ability lasts for 45 seconds or until the Overguard has been destroyed.
Duration is affected by Ability Duration.
The visual stone armor will chip off as the Overguard depletes.
Atlas can cancel the ability by casting it again.
Atlas is immune to Status Effects while protected by Rumbled's Overguard.
Thrown boulders deal 100 Impact damage, with a fixed blast radius of 3 meters.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Rumblers Augment: Atlas becomes a Rumbler with Rock Armor that grants Overguard and absorbs up to 200% of damage to Max Health.
- **Rank 1:** Rumblers Augment: Atlas becomes a Rumbler with Rock Armor that grants Overguard and absorbs up to 234% of damage to Max Health.
- **Rank 2:** Rumblers Augment: Atlas becomes a Rumbler with Rock Armor that grants Overguard and absorbs up to 265% of damage to Max Health.
- **Rank 3:** Rumblers Augment: Atlas becomes a Rumbler with Rock Armor that grants Overguard and absorbs up to 300% of damage to Max Health.

### Drop Locations
- Conclave, Tempest — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Rumbled is a Warframe Augment Mod for Atlas's Rumblers in PvE and Conclave that transforms Atlas into a Rumbler and grants Overguard that scales off Atlas' modded health.

## Runtime
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** universal | **Drain:** 0 | **Compatible:** Parazon | **Max Rank:** 0

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +75% Sprint Speed for 15s after Hacking

### Drop Locations
- Kuva Fortress/Pago (Spy), Rotation B — Uncommon (10.00%)

### Wiki
Runtime is a Parazon mod that increases sprint speed for a duration after successfully Hacking a console.
**Notes:**
Successfully hacking a console while the speed buff is already active will refresh the buff timer.
Useful for spy missions for when you need to travel from one vault to another with the vaults usually being so far away.
The name of this mod is a wordplay on runtime, the time that a computer program is actively running.

Rush is an Exilus mod that increases the sprint speed of a Warframe. Rush also increases the distance that both vertical and horizontal wall running maneuvers can cover.

The increase depends on the Warframe's base sprint speed. For example, since Nova's base speed is higher than that of a Rhino, Nova will receive a much higher benefit from this mod than a Rhino would. See table above for more detailed sprint speeds.
On the table, values for Locust Ash, Backdraft Ember, and Gauss Mag are rounded to the nearest hundredth.

## Sabot Rounds
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** naramon | **Drain:** 10 | **Compatible:** Archgun | **Max Rank:** 5

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +10% Damage, +0.5 Punch Through
- **Rank 1:** +20% Damage, +1 Punch Through
- **Rank 2:** +30% Damage, +1.5 Punch Through
- **Rank 3:** +40% Damage, +2 Punch Through
- **Rank 4:** +50% Damage, +2.5 Punch Through
- **Rank 5:** +60% Damage, +3 Punch Through

### Drop Locations
- Gyre Raider Carver — Rare (0.10%)
- Gyre Raider Eviscerator — Rare (0.10%)
- Gyre Raider — Rare (0.10%)
- Venus/Orb Vallis (Level 40 - 60 PROFIT-TAKER - PHASE 3), Rotation C — Rare (1.25%)

### Wiki
Sabot Rounds is a mod that increases the damage and Punch Through of Archguns.
**Notes:**
Does little benefit to Archguns with infinite punch through such as the Fluctus and the Cortege, save for the bonus damage.
**Tips:**
Useful on Archguns which have very large rounds such as the Corvas (Corvas Prime) so they can maximize their crowd control potential by hitting as many enemies as possible.
The Larkspur and Larkspur Prime's overall DPS also improves with Sabot Rounds, as the mod allows the gun's energy beam to chain to more enemies similar to how the Atomos or Tenet Cycron benefit from punch-through mods.
**Trivia:**
A sabot is the term for padding used around bullets or artillery shells of smaller caliber to the gun barrel they are going to be loaded into, ensuring they still get the maximum potential kinetic energy once they exit the muzzle.
Curiously, the mod's image shows a Vauban wielding a Gravimag-loaded Fluctus, which is an Archgun that already has infinite punch-through.
Sabot Rounds is the first and currently only Archgun mod that provides punch-through.

## Sacrifice
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** penjaga | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Sentinel | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
During Operation: Plague Star: obtained from Nakak in Cetus by trading 1,500 Standing Operational Supply standing and 3,500.
During Operation: Ambulas Reborn: obtained from Ergo Glast by trading 75 Animo Beacons.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Sentinel incapacitates itself to revive its downed owner to 25% health and shields in 7s. Sentinel Recovery timer increases by 15s., +15s Companion Recovery Time
- **Rank 1:** Sentinel incapacitates itself to revive its downed owner to 50% health and shields in 6s. Sentinel Recovery timer increases by 15s., +15s Companion Recovery Time
- **Rank 2:** Sentinel incapacitates itself to revive its downed owner to 75% health and shields in 5s. Sentinel Recovery timer increases by 15s., +15s Companion Recovery Time
- **Rank 3:** Sentinel incapacitates itself to revive its downed owner to 100% health and shields in 4s. Sentinel Recovery timer increases by 15s., +15s Companion Recovery Time

### Drop Locations
- Operational Supply, Neutral — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Sacrifice is a mod that allows a Sentinel to revive its owner from Bleedout, at the cost of putting the Sentinel into Incapacitation and increases recovery time.
**Notes:**
Sacrifice triggers immediately after the master being downed during solo play, or when the Bleedout timer reaches zero when playing in a squad.
To trigger, Sacrifice must be equipped in a higher-priority (more upper left) slot than Assault Mode, otherwise with any enemies nearby Sentinel will continue attacking them, causing Sacrifice to be suppressed and the master dying without an attempt to be revived.
More generally, due to the nature of Precept mods, it is best to place Sacrifice in the very upper left slot, so the sentinel will not prioritize using other skills such as Crowd Dispersion or Ghost over reviving the player. For example, if a Shade has been modded to prioritize Ghost first, it cannot use Sacrifice to revive its owner until Ghost ends. Further, if Ghost comes off cooldown while Shade is reviving the player, the revive will be interrupted by Ghost activating.
Sacrifice does not work during Arbitrations.
Sacrifice does not trigger Sanctuary or Mending Soul.
Sacrifice will immediately trigger when activating Last Gasp.
As enemies lose aggro to players in bleedout, the Sentinel is very prone to getting incapacitated while trying to revive the master.
Sacrifice cannot be traded unless the player offering the item owns more than one copy (much like other Sentinel Precept mods).

## Sacrificesetmod

## Sacrificial Pressure
**Rarity:** Legendary | **Polarity:** umbra | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Melee | **Max Rank:** 10

### Acquisition
This mod can be obtained at the penultimate mission of The Sacrifice, automatically ranked to level 5. Additional unranked mods can be bought from Cephalon Simaris for 100,000 Standing.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +10% Melee Damage, x1.03 Damage to Sentients
- **Rank 1:** +20% Melee Damage, x1.06 Damage to Sentients
- **Rank 2:** +30% Melee Damage, x1.09 Damage to Sentients
- **Rank 3:** +40% Melee Damage, x1.12 Damage to Sentients
- **Rank 4:** +50% Melee Damage, x1.15 Damage to Sentients
- **Rank 5:** +60% Melee Damage, x1.18 Damage to Sentients
- **Rank 6:** +70% Melee Damage, x1.21 Damage to Sentients
- **Rank 7:** +80% Melee Damage, x1.24 Damage to Sentients
- **Rank 8:** +90% Melee Damage, x1.27 Damage to Sentients
- **Rank 9:** +100% Melee Damage, x1.3 Damage to Sentients
- **Rank 10:** +110% Melee Damage, x1.33 Damage to Sentients

### Drop Locations
- Cephalon Simaris, Complete The Sacrifice — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Sacrificial Pressure is the Umbra variant of Pressure Point, increasing a melee weapon's base Damage and damage to Sentient.
Sacrificial Set: Enhances the primary values of other mods in the set.
**Notes:**
Sacrificial Pressure is essentially the Pressure Point mod with Legendary rarity and 5 more ranks. It is considered the same mod as its Common counterpart, and thus both mods cannot be equipped together. The same is true for Primed Pressure Point.
The set bonus for Sacrificial Pressure increases all stats given by the mod by 25% of mod's value per set pieces. At max rank with full set bonus, Sacrificial Pressure grants a melee damage bonus of +137.5% and a Sentient damage bonus of +41.3%.
This mod only surpasses Pressure Point at rank 9 with both set mods but fails to surpass Primed Pressure Point even at rank 10.
However, the significant boost to Critical Chance from Sacrificial Steel putting it over double of True Steel can make up for this.
Umbra mods have a unique Umbra Umbra polarity, whose matching polarity can currently only be found on Excalibur Umbra, Exalted Umbra Blade, and the Skiajati. It is possible to (re-)add an Umbra Umbra polarity to a mod slot but only through the use of Umbra Forma, which are unique rare rewards from Nightwave, so it is advised not to re-Forma any mod slot with an Umbra Umbra polarity.
Similar to other Faction Damage Mods (i.e. Smite Grineer), the bonus damage against Sentient gets applied on top of the base damage and any added elemental damage of the equipped melee weapon (see Damage for complete mechanics).

## Sacrificial Steel
**Rarity:** Legendary | **Polarity:** umbra | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Melee | **Max Rank:** 10

### Acquisition
This mod can be obtained at the penultimate mission of The Sacrifice, automatically ranked to level 5. Additional unranked mods can be bought from Cephalon Simaris for 100,000 Standing.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +20% Critical Chance (x2 for Heavy Attacks), x1.03 Damage to Sentients
- **Rank 1:** +40% Critical Chance (x2 for Heavy Attacks), x1.06 Damage to Sentients
- **Rank 2:** +60% Critical Chance (x2 for Heavy Attacks), x1.09 Damage to Sentients
- **Rank 3:** +80% Critical Chance (x2 for Heavy Attacks), x1.12 Damage to Sentients
- **Rank 4:** +100% Critical Chance (x2 for Heavy Attacks), x1.15 Damage to Sentients
- **Rank 5:** +120% Critical Chance (x2 for Heavy Attacks), x1.18 Damage to Sentients
- **Rank 6:** +140% Critical Chance (x2 for Heavy Attacks), x1.21 Damage to Sentients
- **Rank 7:** +160% Critical Chance (x2 for Heavy Attacks), x1.24 Damage to Sentients
- **Rank 8:** +180% Critical Chance (x2 for Heavy Attacks), x1.27 Damage to Sentients
- **Rank 9:** +200% Critical Chance (x2 for Heavy Attacks), x1.3 Damage to Sentients
- **Rank 10:** +220% Critical Chance (x2 for Heavy Attacks), x1.33 Damage to Sentients

### Drop Locations
- Cephalon Simaris, Complete The Sacrifice — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Sacrificial Steel is the Umbra variant of True Steel, increasing melee Critical Chance and damage to Sentient.
Sacrificial Set: Enhances the primary values of other mods in the set.
**Notes:**
Sacrificial Steel is additive with similar mods.
For example, a Galatine with a max rank Sacrificial Steel (full set bonus) and Maiming Strike will have a 10% (1 + 275% + 150%) Critical Chance on Slide Attacks.
Sacrificial Steel is essentially the True Steel mod with Legendary rarity and 5 more ranks. It is considered the same mod as its Common counterpart, and thus both mods cannot be equipped together.
The set bonus for Sacrificial Steel increases all stats given by the mod by 25% of mod's value per set pieces. At max rank with full set bonus, Sacrificial Steel grants a Critical Chance bonus of +275% and a Sentient damage bonus of +41.3%.
Sacrificial Steel is equal to True Steel at rank 5 when using 1 set piece. It outperforms at rank 4 when using 2 set pieces.
Umbra Mods have a unique Umbra Umbra polarity, whose matching polarity can currently only be found on Excalibur Umbra, Exalted Umbra Blade, and the Skiajati. It is possible to (re-)add an Umbra Umbra polarity to a mod slot but only through the use of Umbra Forma, which are unique rare rewards from Nightwave, so it is advised not to re-Forma any mod slot with an Umbra Umbra polarity.
Similar to other faction damage mods (i.e. Smite Grineer), the bonus damage against Sentient gets applied on top of the base damage and any added elemental damage of the equipped melee weapon (see Damage for complete mechanics).

## Safeguard
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Nezha | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired by attaining the rank of General under the Steel Meridian, or the rank of Genius under Cephalon Suda, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Warding Halo Augment: Can now be cast on allies with 20% effectiveness.
- **Rank 1:** Warding Halo Augment: Can now be cast on allies with 30% effectiveness.
- **Rank 2:** Warding Halo Augment: Can now be cast on allies with 40% effectiveness.
- **Rank 3:** Warding Halo Augment: Can now be cast on allies with 50% effectiveness.

### Drop Locations
- Arbiters of Hexis, Maxim — Common (100.00%)
- Cephalon Suda, Genius — Common (100.00%)
- Red Veil, Exalted — Common (100.00%)
- Steel Meridian, General — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Safeguard is a Warframe Augment Mod for Nezha that allows him to cast Warding Halo on allies, albeit with reduced durability.
**Notes:**
This augment allows Warding Halo to be cast on allied Warframes, Companions, Eidolon Lures, summoned allied units such as Specters, allied Invasion units, Hostages, Kavor Defectors, Sortie and Arbitrations Defense Operatives.
The modified Warding Halo only takes into account Nezha's Armor value and Ability Strength at the time of casting, which in turn can be affected by Arcane Enhancements, abilities like Metronome and Symphony of Mercy, and mods such as Health Conversion or Growing Power.
Multiple instances can be active simultaneously.
Remaining durability on players can be seen next to the squad UI, however there is no way of knowing the health of rings cast on other units.
Just like Safeguard's health, the absorbed damage that can be stacked up in the first 3 seconds is halved, either directly from enemies or environmental hazards
The "effectiveness" in the augment's description refers only to the Warding Halo's health. Allies still receive the full 90% Damage Reduction while Warding Halo is active on them.
Casting Warding Halo on friendly units has no range limit but requires line of sight.
Players under the effects of Safeguard will use the visual effects associated with Warding Halo, using the energy color of the Nezha who cast it.
Warding Halo does not get removed from allies when the Nezha who cast it goes inside a Nullifier field.
Going out of bounds and resetting will remove Warding Halo from players but will not remove it from Companions.

## Safeguard Switch
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Loki | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired by attaining the rank of Maxim under the Arbiters of Hexis, or the rank of Exalted under the Red Veil, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Switch Teleport Augment: Switch with an enemy for 1.5s invulnerability. Switch with an ally to give ally 3s invulnerability. Switch also removes negative status effects on Loki and allies.
- **Rank 1:** Switch Teleport Augment: Switch with an enemy for 2s invulnerability. Switch with an ally to give ally 4s invulnerability. Switch also removes negative status effects on Loki and allies.
- **Rank 2:** Switch Teleport Augment: Switch with an enemy for 2.5s invulnerability. Switch with an ally to give ally 5s invulnerability. Switch also removes negative status effects on Loki and allies.
- **Rank 3:** Switch Teleport Augment: Switch with an enemy for 3s invulnerability. Switch with an ally to give ally 6s invulnerability. Switch also removes negative status effects on Loki and allies.

### Drop Locations
- Arbiters of Hexis, Maxim — Common (100.00%)
- Red Veil, Exalted — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Safeguard Switch is a Warframe Augment Mod for Loki that makes him briefly invulnerable on switching with enemies, gives allies invulnerability when switching with them, and cleanses Status Effects from himself and switched allies.
**Notes:**
Invulnerability period is affected by Ability Duration mods. Duration can be refreshed while active. Can affect multiple allies simultaneously. There is no cooldown to re-cast the ability.
Loki is only granted the invincibility if he swaps position with an enemy; swapping positions with an ally only clears status effects while giving the ally increased invincibility.
Casting range is affected by Range mods.
Casting time is affected by Casting Speed mods.
Works on other players, companions, allied NPCs, and Loki's own Decoy.
Loki must cast the ability either on an enemy, an enemy corpse, or even an enemy as it's in its death animation in order to receive the invulnerability.
Can be cast on some bosses like The Jackal but not on Archons. Can also be cast on Demolysts/Demolishers, Acolytes, Liches, etc including on Eximus units with their Overguard up.
The invulnerability period is only granted to Loki upon completion of the teleport. So then if Loki receives fatal damage before the animation is complete, Loki will still teleport with the target but then will immediately be downed/killed. This can happen either through receiving fatal damage directly during the first portion of the animation (as Loki begins to teleport) or via a status proc where the tick delivers fatal damage during the teleport animation.

## Savage Silence
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Banshee | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired by attaining the rank of Genius under Cephalon Suda, or the rank of Partner under The Perrin Sequence, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Silence Augment: Silence staggers enemies and Finisher damage is increased by 150%
- **Rank 1:** Silence Augment: Silence staggers enemies and Finisher damage is increased by 200%
- **Rank 2:** Silence Augment: Silence staggers enemies and Finisher damage is increased by 250%
- **Rank 3:** Silence Augment: Silence staggers enemies and Finisher damage is increased by 300%

### Drop Locations
- Cephalon Suda, Genius — Common (100.00%)
- The Perrin Sequence, Partner — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Savage Silence is a Warframe Augment Mod for Banshee's Silence that increases enemies Damage Vulnerability to Finisher damage.
**Notes:**
Damage vulnerability is affected by Ability Strength.
Damage vulnerability is multiplicative to Finishing Touch.
Causes Silence's stagger to open enemies to standing finisher attacks. Silence without the Augment will not set up (alerted) enemies for finisher attacks.
Staggering effects that set enemies up for finisher attacks also makes them take increased damage from regular melee attacks due to stealth damage bonus.
Enemies affected by Savage Silence also take increased Finisher damage from Warframe abilities that deal Finisher damage, notably Blade Storm.
Approaching stunned enemies upfront and killing them won't break the stealth affinity multiplier.
In similar fashion, bumping into stunned enemies won't break the multiplier either.
**Tips:**
Synergizes well with the Zakti pistol, as the Gas explosions will open enemies to finishers, allowing Banshee to simply have Silence active to deal increased Finisher damage on any enemy without needing low range or duration to re-stun enemies.
Can allow for consistent proccing of on-finisher effects, such as Arcane Trickery and Arcane Ultimatum.
ev2rL6dwUw0

## Savagery
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** penjaga | **Drain:** 0 | **Compatible:** Sunika Kubrow | **Max Rank:** 5

### Acquisition
Savagery is automatically acquired upon obtaining a Sunika Kubrow.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** The Kubrow rushes at 3 enemies within 5m, dealing damage and knocking them over.
- **Rank 1:** The Kubrow rushes at 4 enemies within 6m, dealing damage and knocking them over.
- **Rank 2:** The Kubrow rushes at 5 enemies within 7m, dealing damage and knocking them over.
- **Rank 3:** The Kubrow rushes at 6 enemies within 8m, dealing damage and knocking them over.
- **Rank 4:** The Kubrow rushes at 7 enemies within 9m, dealing damage and knocking them over.
- **Rank 5:** The Kubrow rushes at 8 enemies within 10m, dealing damage and knocking them over.

### Drop Locations
- Corrupted Drahk — Legendary (0.01%)
- Drahk — Legendary (0.01%)
- Kubrow — Legendary (0.01%)
- Kuva Drahk — Legendary (0.01%)
- Tamm — Legendary (0.01%)

### Wiki
Savagery is a precept mod for the Sunika Kubrow to quickly rush a number of enemies within a radius, inflicting damage and stunning them. At the end of the rush, all attacked enemies will be afflicted by Knockdown.
**Notes:**
Generates combo with Tandem Bond. This can be useful for melee builds that need to quickly generate combo repeatedly, such as Heavy Attack builds with little/no Heavy Attack Efficiency.
The cooldown is approximately 20 seconds.
**Trivia:**
Along with Ferocity, this is the other Kubrow ability mod that is transmutable.

## Savior Decoy
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** zenurik | **Drain:** 6 | **Compatible:** Loki | **Augment** | **Max Rank:** 3

### Acquisition
This mod can be acquired by attaining the rank of Maxim under the Arbiters of Hexis, or the rank of Exalted under the Red Veil, and spending 25,000 Standing to purchase.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Decoy Augment: If Loki takes fatal damage, Decoy absorbs the damage and swaps locations. Also increases Casting Speed of Decoy by 20%
- **Rank 1:** Decoy Augment: If Loki takes fatal damage, Decoy absorbs the damage and swaps locations. Also increases Casting Speed of Decoy by 25%
- **Rank 2:** Decoy Augment: If Loki takes fatal damage, Decoy absorbs the damage and swaps locations. Also increases Casting Speed of Decoy by 35%
- **Rank 3:** Decoy Augment: If Loki takes fatal damage, Decoy absorbs the damage and swaps locations. Also increases Casting Speed of Decoy by 50%

### Drop Locations
- Arbiters of Hexis, Maxim — Common (100.00%)
- Red Veil, Exalted — Common (100.00%)

### Wiki
Savior Decoy is a Warframe Augment Mod for Loki that allows Decoy to absorb any fatal damage dealt to the player before switching places, along with passively increasing Decoy's Casting Speed.
**Notes:**
After switching places with the player, Savior Decoy has a 60-second cooldown during which it will be unable to absorb any fatal damage dealt.
Grants 3s of Invulnerability
Works beyond the range of Switch Teleport (probably infinite) and does not require direct line of sight.
**Tips:**
Synergizes very well with Limbo, as his Banish can protect the decoy from harm allowing a guaranteed activation of the augment.
Enemies will surround and target the decoy if left out in the open. This is especially difficult to utilize against Infested who will rush the decoy and destroy it in just a few attacks. This makes the augment rather counterintuitive since if Loki himself takes fatal damage, and assuming his decoy is still alive, he will be teleported into a group of enemies with only a tiny bit of health.
Because of this, it's recommended to place the decoy in a hidden place far from enemies. However, finding a spot where enemies both cannot attack it can be very difficult, as the game will prevent it from being obstructed and most enemies can jump to almost any spot in the game.

**Rarity:** Common | **Polarity:** naramon | **Drain:** 2 | **Compatible:** Rifle | **Max Rank:** 3

Only increases the Slash damage of rifles. Has no effect on the other damage types.
Can be combined with Fanged Fusillade to provide a 210% increase to Slash damage.
**Trivia:**
As contrary in its description above, giving bullets Sawtooth characteristics is actually the supposed Serration process, which is to create jagged spikes, in order to deal crippling blows as these bullets pass through their targets, slicing their internal organs while puncturing them simultaneously.

## Saxum Spittle
**Rarity:** Rare | **Polarity:** naramon | **Drain:** 4 | **Compatible:** Pistol | **Max Rank:** 5

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** +15% Impact, +10% Status Chance
- **Rank 1:** +30% Impact, +20% Status Chance
- **Rank 2:** +45% Impact, +30% Status Chance
- **Rank 3:** +60% Impact, +40% Status Chance
- **Rank 4:** +75% Impact, +50% Status Chance
- **Rank 5:** +90% Impact, +60% Status Chance

### Drop Locations
- Deimos Saxum Rex — Rare (0.20%)
- Deimos Saxum — Rare (0.20%)
- Deimos/Cambion Drift (Level 25 - 30 Cambion Drift Bounty), Rotation B — Rare (1.70%)
- Deimos/Cambion Drift (Level 25 - 30 Cambion Drift Bounty), Rotation C — Rare (1.72%)
- Deimos/Cambion Drift (Level 25 - 30 Cambion Drift Bounty), Rotation A — Rare (1.75%)
- Deimos/Cambion Drift (Level 25 - 30 Cambion Drift Bounty), Rotation B — Rare (2.50%)

- Deimos/Cambion Drift (Level 25 - 30 Cambion Drift Bounty), Rotation A — Rare (2.56%)

### Wiki
Saxum Spittle is a set mod that increases both the Impact Damage and Status Chance of secondary weapons.
**Notes:**
The Impact damage of a Pistol weapon must be at least 70% of the total for the raw damage gained from this mod to be comparable to an elemental damage counterpart like Scorch.
Pistols with high impact damage and status chance include;
Kuva Kraken
Bronco (Bronco Prime)
Akbronco Prime
Twin Vipers Wraith
Has a very high percentage of Impact damage, and the relatively low Status Chance is offset by the extremely high Fire Rate.
Vaykor Marelok and Akstiletto Prime
They are also solid choices if you're more interested in Status Chance and the Saxum Set bonus than raw damage.
This is accentuated further by the Shrapnel Rounds augment for Marelok.
Can be combined with Concussion Rounds and Pummel to provide a 300% increase to Impact damage.

- Ceres/Bode (Spy), Rotation C — Rare (7.74%)
- Europa/Valac (Spy), Rotation C — Rare (7.74%)
- Jupiter/Amalthea (Spy), Rotation C — Rare (7.74%)
- Saturn/Aegaeon (Spy), Rotation C — Rare (7.74%)
- Saturn/Dione (Spy), Rotation C — Rare (7.74%)

### Wiki
Scattering Inferno is a mod that increases both the Heat damage and status chance of a shotgun by 15% per rank for a maximum of 60% at rank 3.
**Notes:**
Scattering Inferno is additive with similar mods. For example, a Strun with a max rank Scattering Inferno and Shotgun Savvy will have a 20% (1 + 60% + 90%) status chance.
This mod can be combined with Toxic Barrage, Shell Shock and Frigid Blast to achieve a combined status chance bonus of 240%. With Shotgun Savvy, this bonus can be increased to 330%.
**Trivia:**
Unlike the previous Cicero and Tethra mod sets that depict Warframes already available at the time of the event, the cards from Operation Breeding Grounds depicted Mirage, a Warframe that at the time was yet to be revealed or released.

### Wiki
Scavenge mod gives Kubrows and Kavats a chance to unlock lockers.
**Notes:**
When a Kubrow or Kavat fails, the locker goes from red (lock) to off (black) and cannot be unlocked anymore.
Lockers that Master Thief fails to unlock cannot be tried by your Kubrow or Kavat.
**Trivia:**
The image used for the mod shows the Corpus lockers used before Update 24.2 instead of the newer ones.

### Wiki
Grineer
Scorches are medium Grineer units resembling Troopers with red and yellow armor. Scorches wield the Ignis flamethrower, dealing massive continuous damage in short range as long as the victim is in range. They can deal continuous damage and also set the player on fire, making them incredibly lethal in a successful ambush or when working with units such as Scorpions and Rollers that can inhibit player movement.
Farming Locations
These are based on opinions and may not be 100% true. These should be viewed as advice for finding the enemies until better facts are proven.
Normal Scorch
Anthe
Tactics
Scorches tend to take cover behind doorways or next to pillars so they can ambush unsuspecting targets who pass by. This makes them a threat to solo players who are unaware of their presence.
Their Ignis has AoE, which makes "dodging" flames almost impossible.
The AoE of this weapon often causes Sentinel damage. Approach with caution.
Scorches tend to hide more frequently than other Grineer units.
This means that they move to different spots more frequently.
They also are the ranged unit most likely to use terminals.
When players deploy decoys in their field of view, they will simply track closer targets instead of the decoys, even if that target is behind the Scorch while the decoy is in front.
As of Update 11, Scorches have a yellow and red color scheme, making them somewhat easier to differentiate from a Trooper.

### Wiki
Searing Steel is a mod that increases Heat damage and status chance on Archmelee weapons by 15% per rank for a maximum of 60% at rank 3.
**Notes:**
Searing Steel is additive with similar mods. For example, an Kaszas with a max rank Searing Steel and Sudden Impact will have a 20% (1 + 60% + 60%) status chance.
Can be stacked with Blazing Steel for a total of 180% Heat damage.
**Trivia:**

It is available as a potential end of mission reward during a Razorback Armada encounter.

## Security Override
**Rarity:** Common | **Polarity:** penjaga | **Drain:** 2 | **Compatible:** Moa | **Max Rank:** 5

### Acquisition
Security Override is automatically acquired upon obtaining a MOA built with the Oloro Model.

### Stats by Rank
- **Rank 0:** Auto-hacks consoles over 12s. 5% chance to mind control basic robotic enemies and turrets in 30m for 4s after Hacking.
- **Rank 1:** Auto-hacks consoles over 10s. 10% chance to mind control basic robotic enemies and turrets in 30m for 6s after Hacking.
- **Rank 2:** Auto-hacks consoles over 8s. 15% chance to mind control basic robotic enemies and turrets in 30m for 8s after Hacking.
- **Rank 3:** Auto-hacks consoles over 6s. 20% chance to mind control basic robotic enemies and turrets in 30m for 10s after Hacking.
- **Rank 4:** Auto-hacks consoles over 4s. 25% chance to mind control basic robotic enemies and turrets in 30m for 12s after Hacking.
- **Rank 5:** Auto-hacks consoles over 2s. 30% chance to mind control basic robotic enemies and turrets in 30m for 14s after Hacking.

### Wiki
Security Override is a precept mod for MOAs to hack consoles, with a chance to mind-control robotic enemies within 30m after a successful hack.
**Notes:**
If it manages to hack a MOA Cabinet Spawner, two friendly Shockwave MOAs will be produced instead of one.
If it hacks a Spy mission console when alarms are on, it will still count as undetected hack. It will also make Lotus say both hack outcome voice lines
Before Update 24.0.7, trying to use it with Vacuum equipped would prevent the MOA from hacking anything.
While it was assumed Vacuum was the issue, the hack rarely happened even with the mod un-equipped.

### Wiki
Grineer
Seekers are tactical Grineer units with yellow-green armor and darker markings. Aside from using the Kraken pistol, they will also deploy Latchers. This ability makes them unlikely to rush into the front lines like any regular Grineer unit. More durable than regular Grineer Lancers, these can be versatile opponents.
Behaviour
Due to their habit of hiding away from actual combat, they can be more likely to use terminals in order to sound alarms and cause lockdowns than other units.
This also means that you will often find yourself constantly bombarded with Latchers from Seekers that you cannot see.
Use these time slots to your advantage as the Seeker is incapable of retaliating effectively, preferable to kill with a strong damage-per-bullet weapon to avoid being killed by their Kraken/Viper.
Armed with a Kraken pistol.
**Notes:**
Seekers affected by Nyx's Mind Control or Chaos will deploy Latchers which are allied, causing enemies to become distracted by them if they come nearby. Although weak to a single shot by any Grineer, a mind-controlled Seeker with duration can effectively spawn many Latchers, creating many possible entities that can redirect enemy fire.
Shadow Seekers do not deploy Latchers.
Farming Locations
These are based on opinions and may not be 100% true. These should be viewed as advice for finding the enemies until better facts are proven. For endless missions, players may need to stay in-mission for a while until these enemies start spawning.
Normal Seeker
Adaro
Arid Seeker
Ara
Frontier Seeker
E Prime
Drekar Seeker
Ophelia
**Trivia:**
The Seeker was added to the game in the 2013 Valentine's Day update, Update 6.3.
The Frontier Seeker was originally a Seeker with its visor always up and wearing all-grey armor.
The Latchers deployed by the Seeker can jump up ledges, like a normal unit would, using the same pause where the jumping animation would be, much to the amusement of any watching players.

Body Units Punctured
